_doc: Raven Hollow data-driven quests (BLUEPRINT_27). Givers match NPCData cast ids; targets match live enemy type_names; collect items match LootSystem material ids (drop from trash_b1). Positions are [x,y] in the named map. Two chains: rh_fountain_rumor->rh_fountain_watch and rh_wolves_gate->rh_wolves_proof. | +1007 lore-of-the-land quests (BACKLOG #77).

rh_petra_walk.title: Old Petra's Errand
rh_petra_walk.summary: Old Petra's knees have disagreed with her since the old king. Carry her worry to Gatewarden Iosif and see the east gate for yourself.
rh_petra_walk.offer: My knees won't walk me to the gate, dear, but my worry gets there fine. Ask Iosif if he's shutting it early again. And look at the arch while you're there -- tell me it still stands.
rh_petra_walk.progress: Iosif keeps the east gate. You'll find him where the wall meets the Emberfall Road.
rh_petra_walk.complete: Still standing, is it. Good. Places like this remember when a thing falls down. Here -- for the walk.

objectives.text: Speak with Gatewarden Iosif

objectives.label: the east gate
objectives.text: See the east gate stands

rh_petra_walk.note: Old Petra nodded, satisfied. The arch still stands. She did not say what she expected instead.

rh_maid_stair.title: The Third Stair
rh_maid_stair.summary: Elsbeth swears the third stair of the inn creaks now like it's telling secrets -- and always at the same hour. Ask the ones who'd know if a house can go wrong.
rh_maid_stair.offer: The third stair creaks at the same hour every night now, and it never used to. Marta says settling. Old Vasile would know if a house can turn strange -- ask them both, and tell me I'm being silly.
rh_maid_stair.progress: Marta keeps the inn; Gravekeeper Vasile keeps the older things. Ask them both about the stair.
rh_maid_stair.complete: Settling, they both say. Same hour every night is just settling. ...You'd tell me, wouldn't you, if it were something else.

objectives.text: Ask Innkeeper Marta about the stair
Ask Gravekeeper Vasile about the stair

rh_maid_stair.note: Everyone said the same word -- settling -- at the same hour. Elsbeth stopped using that stair.

rh_fountain_rumor.title: A Wish Gone Missing
rh_fountain_rumor.summary: Coins have gone missing from the wishing fountain -- fished out in the night, folk say. Terrible luck, stealing a wish. Gather what the village knows before someone decides who to blame.
rh_fountain_rumor.offer: Someone's been fishing coins from the fountain after dark. Bad luck to steal a wish, worse luck to be accused of it. Before names start flying, hear what Elsbeth and Marta have caught wind of.
rh_fountain_rumor.progress: Maid Elsbeth misses nothing in the taproom; Innkeeper Marta hears every rumor twice. Ask them both.
rh_fountain_rumor.complete: So. Lights on the old road, coins gone by morning, and nobody's foot in the mud. That's not a thief with a purse. Keep this between us -- and keep watching.

objectives.text: Ask Maid Elsbeth about the fountain
Ask Innkeeper Marta about the fountain

rh_fountain_rumor.note: Coins gone, no footprints in the mud. Tibalt wanted a thief. He got something worse: a pattern.

rh_fountain_watch.title: Watch by the Old Fountain
rh_fountain_watch.summary: No thief leaves no footprints. Stand watch at the fountain -- and deal with whatever comes up the graveyard path for the coins that were promised to a wish.
rh_fountain_watch.offer: You watch tonight. Stand by the fountain and see who -- or what -- comes for the coins. Vasile's hill has been restless. Whatever you find down that path... don't let it reach the water.
rh_fountain_watch.progress: Take up the watch at the fountain in the square, then put down whatever walks the graveyard path.
rh_fountain_watch.complete: Bone-dry hands and old grave-dirt, and every coin it took laid out in a straight, even line on the rim. Nobody wished for this. Somebody arranged it. Take the charm -- and take care.

objectives.label: the wishing fountain
objectives.text: Keep watch at the fountain
Put down what walks the graveyard path

rh_fountain_watch.note: The stolen coins were laid out on the rim in one straight line. A wish is a small thing to steal. Unless you are counting.

rh_boar_cull.title: Roots and Ruin
rh_boar_cull.summary: The boars are up out of the Emberfall thickets and into Ansel's field, rooting the harvest to ruin before the fair. Thin them out.
rh_boar_cull.offer: Good soil this year, and the boars agree -- they've torn my south field to furrows chasing grubs. Fair's coming and I'll have nothing to show. Thin the brutes on the road and I'll thank you proper.
rh_boar_cull.progress: The boars root along the Emberfall Road, out past the east gate. Cull them before the fair.
rh_boar_cull.complete: That'll teach them to respect a man's furrows. The wheat won't cut itself, but at least there's wheat to cut now. Take the shield -- old, but it's turned a tusk or two.

objectives.text: Cull boars on the Emberfall Road

rh_boar_cull.note: Ansel got his harvest. The pear tree by the fountain, his grandfather's, kept its fruit another year.

rh_fair_meat.title: Meat for the Harvest Fair
rh_fair_meat.summary: The whole village turns out for the harvest fair, and Marta's stew needs actual meat in it this year. Bring back good boar haunch from the road.
rh_fair_meat.offer: Harvest's been kind, thank the saints, and the fair means my stew needs meat that was recently walking. Bring me good haunch off the road -- boar, not stringy old wolf -- and there's a warm bowl and a coin in it for you.
rh_fair_meat.progress: Boar haunch, mind -- from the beasts along the Emberfall Road. Four good cuts should fill the pot.
rh_fair_meat.complete: Now that is meat. The taproom will be full and loud and warm tonight, the way a harvest ought to be. Bless you -- and here, a coin off the fountain that actually brought luck.

objectives.text: Gather boar haunch

rh_fair_meat.note: The fair was warm and loud and full. For one night Raven Hollow forgot to listen for anything on the road.

rh_old_road.title: As Far as the Old Road Goes
rh_old_road.summary: Young Emeric dreams of taking the Emberfall Road past the graveyard, past everything. His da says the harvest needs him. Scout the far overlook and bring word of what lies beyond.
rh_old_road.offer: One day I'm taking the old road as far as it goes -- Vetka, the stone cities, all of it. Da won't hear it till the harvest's in. Walk out to the eastern overlook for me, will you? Tell me it goes somewhere.
rh_old_road.progress: The overlook sits at the far southeast of the Emberfall Road, where the land drops away toward Vetka.
rh_old_road.complete: It DOES go somewhere. Vetka, and past it the whole spine of the world. I'll take that road yet -- after the harvest. Thanks for the eyes. Keep the band; a wanderer travels light.

objectives.label: the eastern overlook
objectives.text: Scout the eastern overlook

rh_old_road.note: The road goes to Vetka, and past it everything. Emeric will take it one day. The road keeps what it catches.

rh_wolves_gate.title: The Road Keeps What It Catches
rh_wolves_gate.summary: The wolves come down off the ridges after dark, and Iosif swears they no longer hunt like wolves -- they spread out even and quiet, pacing off the ground for someone. Break the pack before it finishes measuring.
rh_wolves_gate.offer: Beyond the arch, the wolves don't hunt like wolves anymore. They spread out even and quiet, like they're pacing off the ground for something to be built on. Break the pack for me -- before it finishes its counting.
rh_wolves_gate.progress: The pack works the Emberfall Road after dusk. Cut it down -- six should scatter the rest.
rh_wolves_gate.complete: Six, and the road's quieter for it. But you saw it too, didn't you. They weren't circling like beasts. They were surveying. Take the blade. Come back if your stomach's still with you -- I've a harder ask.

objectives.text: Break the pack on the Emberfall Road

rh_wolves_gate.note: They spread out even and quiet, pacing off the ground. Not hunting. Measuring. For what, Iosif would not say.

rh_wolves_proof.title: Proof for the Warden
rh_wolves_proof.summary: Iosif wants pelts to send up the road with his warning -- proof that Raven Hollow's wolves have changed. Skin four from the pack, and try not to notice how the fur lies flat in one direction.
rh_wolves_proof.offer: Nobody up the road believes a gatekeeper. So bring me proof -- four pelts, clean. When they see how the fur's gone strange, maybe they'll shut their own gates before it reaches them.
rh_wolves_proof.progress: Four wolf pelts from the pack on the Emberfall Road. Take them clean.
rh_wolves_proof.complete: Look at that. The nap lies all one way, every pelt, like something combed them while they lived. I'll send these up the road. It won't stop what's coming. But someone, somewhere, ought to be warned.

objectives.text: Gather changed wolf pelts

rh_wolves_proof.note: Every pelt's nap lay one way, as if combed while the beast still breathed. Iosif sent them up a road that has never handed anything back.

rh_graveyard_bones.title: The Restless Yard
rh_graveyard_bones.summary: The graveyard is older than the village, and lately its dead don't keep. Vasile is too old to swing a spade at what climbs out. Put the restless bones back down.
rh_graveyard_bones.offer: Everyone ends up on my hill eventually -- the lucky ones slowly. Lately some are in a hurry to leave it. My spade's honest but my back is not. Put the walking ones down for good, and let the yard be old and quiet again.
rh_graveyard_bones.progress: The restless dead rise on the graveyard hill above the village. Lay them back down.
rh_graveyard_bones.complete: Quiet again. For now. Funny -- they always rose facing the fountain, every one. The dead don't have wishes. So who's it they were listening to? Take the ring. It kept its last owner calm enough.

objectives.text: Lay the restless dead back down

rh_graveyard_bones.note: They rose facing the fountain, every one. The dead keep no wishes. They were listening to something else.

rh_smith_tusks.title: Tusks for the Tempering
rh_smith_tusks.summary: Goran's ore comes up the old road when the old road feels like being a road -- which is seldom, now. Until it does, he'll harden what he has with boar tusk. Bring him a good pile.
rh_smith_tusks.offer: Ore comes up the old road when the road feels like cooperating, and it hasn't in weeks. A well-hammered nail beats a wish-coin, but I need boar tusk to temper the steel I've got left. Fetch me six beasts' worth off the road.
rh_smith_tusks.progress: Boar, on the Emberfall Road -- six of them will give tusk enough to temper a season of blades.
rh_smith_tusks.complete: Good tusk, that. Enough to keep the forge honest till the road remembers itself. Take this shortsword -- I tempered it the old way, before the ore stopped coming. It'll outlast the both of us.

objectives.text: Hunt boar on the Emberfall Road

rh_smith_tusks.note: The ore stopped coming up the old road. Goran did not ask why. In Raven Hollow, not asking is its own kind of wisdom.

rh_bear_hollow.title: The Thing in the Hollow
rh_bear_hollow.summary: Something big has denned in the hollow off the road, and it drove even the changed wolves out. Iosif won't send another warden after it. He'll send you -- once.
rh_bear_hollow.offer: There's a hollow off the road where even the wolves won't go now, and the wolves stopped being sensible weeks ago. A bear, the trappers say -- a big one, gone wrong in the head like the rest of it. I've buried two wardens this season. I won't bury a third. But you... you keep walking back. End it.
rh_bear_hollow.progress: The beast dens in the hollow off the Emberfall Road. Go carefully -- and come back.
rh_bear_hollow.complete: You walked back. Again. I'm starting to think the road can't keep you, and saints know it's tried. This was my grandfather's -- the Bent Oar's own luck, they called it. It's yours. You've earned the right to press it back.

objectives.text: End the beast in the hollow

rh_bear_hollow.note: The road has tried to keep you and failed. Iosif noticed. So, perhaps, did something older than Iosif.

chamber_depths_the_sealed_satchel.title: The Sealed Satchel
chamber_depths_the_sealed_satchel.summary: Below the last warm hearth in Draconia lies a cold vault, and in it a courier who died with his message still sealed. Iosif needs a steadier hand than his own to bring the satchel up.
chamber_depths_the_sealed_satchel.offer: There is a man in the vault below who has been dead a fortnight and has not begun to smell. No wound on him. Buckles done up, seal unbroken -- he never delivered his letter, because he stopped to read the wall. Go down. Bring me the satchel he carried. Do not read the wall.
chamber_depths_the_sealed_satchel.progress: The Courier lies in the cold vault, at the deep end of the Chamber where the script grows dense. Bring up his satchel, sealed as it is.
chamber_depths_the_sealed_satchel.complete: Sealed still. Good. His fingernails were clean, you say -- of course they were. The wound is that there is no wound. Keep the token he carried against the dark; whoever it was for is a fortnight past needing it.

objectives.label: the cold vault
objectives.text: Reach the Courier in the cold vault
Bring the sealed satchel to Iosif

chamber_depths_the_sealed_satchel.note: The Courier died of understanding -- no wound, clean nails, the last glyph half-copied under his quill. Iosif took the satchel and did not open it. Someone up the road is still waiting on an answer a wall ate.

chamber_depths_transcribe_or_deface.title: Two Notes of Three
chamber_depths_transcribe_or_deface.summary: The dense script in the vault hums two notes of some third it withholds. Iosif wants it copied for the record. Vasile's kin want it starved. Whatever you do, the ground below will remember which.
chamber_depths_transcribe_or_deface.offer: The stone down there hums -- two notes, and it is reaching for a third. Copy the glyphs and we keep the knowledge; deface them and we starve the thing. I am a chronicler; I want the record. But I have buried men who wanted the record. Go down. Decide at the wall. The stone will not tell you which was right.
chamber_depths_transcribe_or_deface.progress: The humming script waits at the deep wall of the cold vault. Transcribe it, or deface it. Choose, and come back.
chamber_depths_transcribe_or_deface.complete: It is done, then, one way or the other. The floor of the vault was warm when you left it -- warmer than a fortnight-dead room should be. I have made a note of the day. It matches a day I would rather it did not. Take the counteragent. Some things are easier not to hear afterward.

objectives.label: the humming wall
objectives.text: Reach the humming wall
Work the wall by Hessik's method

chamber_depths_transcribe_or_deface.note: The wall was worked and the notes still number two. Iosif dated the warming of the floor and did not like the date. Whatever the choice bought, the ground below Vetka keeps the receipt.

chamber_depths_the_note_that_answered.title: The Note That Answered
chamber_depths_the_note_that_answered.summary: The chamber's echo has begun to speak back. Not in the Underlanguage -- in plain words, borrowed from a mouth that paid for them. Iosif needs it witnessed before he can bring himself to believe it.
chamber_depths_the_note_that_answered.offer: The echo down there answers now. I do not mean it rings -- I mean it replies, in words, with a voice I half know. I cannot go back down and I cannot let it stand unwitnessed. Reach the deep gallery. Let the thing speak its one sentence at you. Then find me, and swear you heard it too.
chamber_depths_the_note_that_answered.progress: The deep gallery lies past the vault, where the air itself has learned to carry the signal. Reach it, hear what answers, then bring word to Iosif.
chamber_depths_the_note_that_answered.complete: You heard it. Then I did not imagine it. It knew a thing I did was not for anyone to know -- a deed, not my name, spoken flat as a ledger line. Wear the veil. It will not stop the thing hearing you. It only means you will not have to watch your own reflection listening back.

objectives.label: the deep gallery
objectives.text: Reach the deep gallery and hear the echo
Swear to Iosif what you heard

chamber_depths_the_note_that_answered.note: The echo spoke plainly, in a borrowed voice, and named a deed the traveler had thought unwitnessed. Iosif wrote it down, then burned the page. The record he most wanted, he could not keep. Entries grow.

chamber_depths_the_undelivered_letter.title: The Undelivered Letter
chamber_depths_the_undelivered_letter.summary: The Courier's letter is sealed and Cornel will not open it, but he can read a direction off the wax. Someone paid for a message a wall ate. He wants to at least know their name.
chamber_depths_the_undelivered_letter.offer: I keep the one hearth people still come down to, so I keep their griefs too. That courier -- there is a name pressed in the wax of his letter, if you can lift it without breaking the seal. I will not read what is inside. But I would like to know who is waiting. Bring me what the seal says, and no more.
chamber_depths_the_undelivered_letter.progress: The sealed letter is with the Courier's satchel-things, gathered near the vault mouth. Lift the name from the wax without breaking it.
chamber_depths_the_undelivered_letter.complete: A woman's name, and a village two roads west. She sent him with a question and he sent her silence. I will get word to her the slow honest way -- a person, not a wall. Sit by the fire a while first. You have the grey look of someone who read further down than they meant to.

objectives.label: the vault mouth
objectives.text: Recover the sealed letter's wax-name
Bring the wax-name to Cornel

chamber_depths_the_undelivered_letter.note: The seal gave up a name and a village; the letter kept the rest. Cornel means to walk word to her himself. A wall ate the answer. A person will carry the grief -- which is all anyone ever could.

chamber_depths_iron_filings.title: Iron Filings Round a Lodestone
chamber_depths_iron_filings.summary: Danciu's filings have stopped falling where they should. Swept from his floor, they crawl into fine parallel lines -- all pointing the same way, down. He wants to know what they are pointing at.
chamber_depths_iron_filings.offer: I have swept a forge floor forty years and iron falls where iron falls. Not now. My filings lie in lines, thin and even, like something combed them -- and every line points down the deep passage. Follow where they point. Tell me there is a rat's nest of lodestone down there and not the other thing.
chamber_depths_iron_filings.progress: The filings point down the deep passage toward whatever aligns them. Follow the lines to their source.
chamber_depths_iron_filings.complete: Not lodestone. Of course not lodestone. The dust points at a warm stone that is teaching the whole vault to face one way -- iron, air, and men alike. Take the striker. It will light a fire that at least answers to you, which is more than the dust does now.

objectives.label: where the filings point
objectives.text: Follow the aligned filings to their source

chamber_depths_iron_filings.note: The filings pointed at a warm stone teaching the vault to face it -- iron, air, and men alike. Danciu sweeps his floor twice a day now. It does not help. The dust has made up its mind.

chamber_depths_the_spinners_lattice.title: The Spinners' Lattice
chamber_depths_the_spinners_lattice.summary: Something has strung the deep passage with a lattice no spider spins -- taut, blue-lit lines that the dead below seem to walk along. Fane wants the weavers cut down before the pattern is finished.
chamber_depths_the_spinners_lattice.offer: There is a web down there that no honest spider made. Thread-spinners, the old woman calls them -- they string a lattice from wall to wall, tight as a loom, and the walking dead pace it like it were a road laid for them. It is not finished yet. Cut the weavers down before it is. I will not have my watch pacing off someone's floor-plan.
chamber_depths_the_spinners_lattice.progress: Thread-spinners weave the lattice through the deep passage. Cut them down before the pattern is complete.
chamber_depths_the_spinners_lattice.complete: Fewer lines now, and the dead below wander like dead ought to, without their road. But you saw how neat the weave was -- rows of it, even as fence-posts. That is not a spider's math. Take the binding-cord. If the lattice starts to close again, it will hold a door long enough for the last man out.

objectives.text: Cut down the thread-spinners

chamber_depths_the_spinners_lattice.note: The lattice was cut and the dead lost their road. But the weave was too even to be a spider's, laid in rows like a plan half-built. Fane counts the lines each dawn now. They are fewer. They are never gone.

chamber_depths_do_not_read_aloud.title: Do Not Read Aloud
chamber_depths_do_not_read_aloud.summary: A student of Iosif's came down to copy the galleries and has not come up. He is at a wall, lips moving. Iosif cannot save what a curious mouth invites -- but a lens might let you read without reading.
chamber_depths_do_not_read_aloud.offer: I sent a boy down with chalk and good intentions, and he is at a wall now with his lips moving, and I have learned what a moving mouth invites. Take the riddler's lens -- it lets the eye pass over the marks without the mind closing on them. Reach him. Do not speak the glyphs. Do not let him finish the one he is on.
chamber_depths_do_not_read_aloud.progress: The student stands at a gallery wall in the deep passage, reading. Reach him with the lens -- and do not read aloud.
chamber_depths_do_not_read_aloud.complete: You reached him. He does not remember the wall, or the chalk, or most of the morning -- which is the best outcome there was. He is warm and confused and alive, and I will take confused over the Courier's kind of clean. Keep the lens. You have the eyes for it, and the sense to look away.

objectives.label: the gallery wall
objectives.text: Reach the reading student with the lens
Report to Iosif that the boy lives

chamber_depths_do_not_read_aloud.note: The boy lives, minus a morning he will not miss. Iosif keeps him away from chalk now. To understand the wall is to be unwritten by it -- and the wall is patient, and the young are curious. Both facts are load-bearing.

chamber_depths_the_signal_in_the_air.title: The Signal in the Air
chamber_depths_the_signal_in_the_air.summary: Emilian's lantern-flames bend toward the deep gallery, and the air there has begun to hold a shape -- echoes that are learning to keep themselves. He'll pay well to have them scattered before they thicken.
chamber_depths_the_signal_in_the_air.offer: Trade is bad when your candles all lean the same way. Down the deep gallery the air itself has gone wrong -- echoes that do not fade, that hang and turn and thicken, like the sound is teaching itself to stay. Scatter the things before they set hard. I have a good draught for you, if you have the stomach to breathe that air.
chamber_depths_the_signal_in_the_air.progress: Transmission-echoes gather and thicken in the deep gallery. Scatter them before they set.
chamber_depths_the_signal_in_the_air.complete: Quieter down there, they tell me. For a season, maybe. Those echoes were the signal writing itself into the very air -- not a ghost, a rehearsal. Here, the reading-draught, as promised. Trade will pick up. It always does, right up until it doesn't.

objectives.text: Scatter the transmission-echoes

chamber_depths_the_signal_in_the_air.note: The echoes scattered, and the gallery air went ordinary again -- for a season. They were not ghosts. They were the signal rehearsing itself into the air, learning to stay. It learns fast. It has nothing but time.

chamber_depths_the_farmers_boots.title: Facing the Wrong Way
chamber_depths_the_farmers_boots.summary: Manea's brother went down to fetch water and now stands at the deep threshold, faced toward the vault, not moving. She says he's just resting. He has been resting since Tuesday.
chamber_depths_the_farmers_boots.offer: My brother went down for water three days back and he is standing at the low threshold still, faced at the vault, not moving. He is just resting -- that is what I tell the neighbors, and it is a lie I would like made true. Go to him. See if he will turn. See if there is any of him left that wants to.
chamber_depths_the_farmers_boots.progress: Manea's brother stands at the deep threshold, faced toward the vault. Reach him and see if he will turn.
chamber_depths_the_farmers_boots.complete: He turned. He blinked. He asked what day it was and did not like the answer. The ground where he stood was dry and warm and the dust round his boots lay in lines -- I know what that means now, everyone does. Take the charm. It kept him incurious just long enough. Some days that is the whole battle.

objectives.label: the low threshold
objectives.text: Reach the brother at the low threshold
Tell Manea her brother turned

chamber_depths_the_farmers_boots.note: He turned. Many do not. The soil at his boots was warm and dry and the dust lay in fine parallel lines, the way it does where the ground has started to listen. Manea keeps him upstairs now, away from thresholds.

chamber_depths_marta_would_know.title: Marta Would Know
chamber_depths_marta_would_know.summary: An old healer's walking-stick was left leaning by the vault door, mislaid by someone who fled. Cornel wants it carried up and kept safe. Small warm things, he says, are the only things worth guarding down here.
chamber_depths_marta_would_know.offer: Somebody left a stick by the vault door -- a herb-woman's walking staff, worn smooth at the grip, the kind of small good thing nobody thinks to save. Whoever dropped it ran, and did not come back for it, which tells you the kind of day they had. Fetch it up before the damp takes it. A stick is a small thing to save. It is also, some days, the only thing you can.
chamber_depths_marta_would_know.progress: The healer's stick leans by the vault door in the deep dark. Bring it up before the damp claims it.
chamber_depths_marta_would_know.complete: There it is. Smells of dried thyme still, under the cold. I will keep it by the hearth where it is warm. One day someone will ask what it was and I will say -- a healer walked with this, and helped people, and that was all. Small. Warm. Ours. Keep the cord; you have the hands for keeping things.

objectives.label: the vault door
objectives.text: Recover the healer's walking-stick
Bring the stick to Cornel

chamber_depths_marta_would_know.note: The stick came up smelling of thyme, and Cornel set it by the fire. A healer walked with it once and helped people, and that was all. The small warm things are the only weapon against a stone that hungers for a clean, empty world.

chamber_depths_the_dead_that_pace.title: The Dead That Pace
chamber_depths_the_dead_that_pace.summary: Thread-touched dead have come down from the north and taken to the deep galleries, pacing the lattice like laborers on a shift. Gherghe wants them put down before they wear a road into the stone.
chamber_depths_the_dead_that_pace.offer: The ones pacing the deep galleries are not our dead -- they came down from the north on their invisible threads and they walk like men on a shift, back and forth, back and forth, wearing a groove. Put them down, all you can find. They do not fight like the fresh dead. They fight like they are still on the clock and you are keeping them from work.
chamber_depths_the_dead_that_pace.progress: Thread-touched dead pace the deep galleries. Put down the ones on the lattice.
chamber_depths_the_dead_that_pace.complete: Eight fewer pacing, and the groove they wore is a hand deep in the stone. Eight of them, kept upright on a thread I could not see, doing labor nobody set and nobody stops. Take the plate. It has turned worse than teeth, and there is worse than teeth down there.

objectives.text: Put down the thread-touched pacers

chamber_depths_the_dead_that_pace.note: Eight put down, and the groove they had paced ran a hand deep in living stone. They labored on a thread no one could cut, at work no one had set. In the north, someone is holding that thread. Gherghe does not say the name.

chamber_depths_the_deepbed_light.title: A Light for the Deepbed
chamber_depths_the_deepbed_light.summary: The lanterns of the lower gallery gutter and die where the stone runs warm. Emilian will fit you with a deepbed lantern -- if you first prove the dark down there is a dark worth selling light against.
chamber_depths_the_deepbed_light.offer: A merchant sells what the place needs, and this place needs light that does not die. My lanterns gutter where the stone runs warm -- and it runs warm in more places each week. Take one of my deepbed lamps down to the far marker and back. If it holds against the dark there, I will know the make is sound and you will have earned the carrying of it.
chamber_depths_the_deepbed_light.progress: The far marker sits at the deep end of the lower gallery, where the warm stone kills ordinary flame. Carry the deepbed lantern there and back.
chamber_depths_the_deepbed_light.complete: It held. Good. That is a lamp that answers to oil and wick and nothing else -- no small mercy in a place where the fire itself has started taking sides. It is yours. Where you are going, you will want a light that does not lean toward the wall.

objectives.label: the far marker
objectives.text: Carry the lantern to the far marker

chamber_depths_the_deepbed_light.note: The lamp held where warm stone kills honest flame. Emilian sells light against a dark that is spreading, and knows it, and sells it anyway -- because a man has to eat, and because a light that does not lean is worth more each week.

chamber_depths_names_against_the_dark.title: Names Against the Dark
chamber_depths_names_against_the_dark.summary: Nica keeps a list of everyone who has gone down and not come up, and reads it aloud each dusk so the vault cannot pretend they were never there. She needs the newest names carried to the threshold and spoken once.
chamber_depths_names_against_the_dark.offer: I keep the list. Everyone the Chamber has taken, by name, and I read it at dusk so the place cannot swallow a person whole and clean like it wants to. There are three new names. I am too old to make the stairs. Carry them to the threshold and say them once, out loud, into the dark. Not the glyphs -- never the glyphs. Names. Ours. Say them and come back.
chamber_depths_names_against_the_dark.progress: The threshold to the deep vault waits below. Carry the three new names there and speak them once into the dark.
chamber_depths_names_against_the_dark.complete: You said them. Good. The stone would have them be no one -- clean copies, blank rows, the world before people. A name spoken is a small stubborn no. It will not save them. It saves the fact that they were. That is the war, dear. Take the token. Keep it named.

objectives.label: the vault threshold
objectives.text: Speak the three names at the threshold
Return the read list to Nica

chamber_depths_names_against_the_dark.note: Three names spoken once into the dark, against a stone that wants a world of blank rows. It saves no one. It saves that they were. Nica reads the list at dusk still. The list grows. She reads it anyway.

chamber_depths_the_marshlight_ward.title: By Marshlight
chamber_depths_the_marshlight_ward.summary: The old folk hung marshlight jars at every turn of the Chamber to keep the walking dead honest. Half have gone dark. Manea needs them found and the shadows between them checked.
chamber_depths_the_marshlight_ward.offer: My grandmother hung marshlight jars at every bend down there, back when the dead were slower and the dark was smaller. They kept the corners honest -- a dead thing does not like a light it cannot lean away from. Half are dark now. Find the guttered ones at the low bend, and look hard at the shadow where the light should be. Look. Do not read.
chamber_depths_the_marshlight_ward.progress: The guttered marshlight jars hang at the low bend of the Chamber. Find them and check the dark between.
chamber_depths_the_marshlight_ward.complete: Dark, all of them, and something had been sitting in the black where the light failed -- you could see the dust pressed flat, in that neat even way. I have relit what I can. It is a losing kind of chore, keeping lights lit against a dark that is patient. But losing slow is still slower than not trying. Take the jar. Keep a corner honest.

objectives.label: the low bend
objectives.text: Check the guttered jars at the low bend

chamber_depths_the_marshlight_ward.note: The jars were dark and the dust in the shadow lay pressed and even, as if something had rested there. Manea relights them as they gutter. It is a losing chore. Losing slow is still slower than not trying at all.

chamber_depths_the_copied_page.title: The Page That Copies Itself
chamber_depths_the_copied_page.summary: A dead scholar's notebook was found near the galleries, and the glyphs he copied are surfacing on the facing blank page in a hand no longer living. Iosif needs it sealed away before it finishes writing itself.
chamber_depths_the_copied_page.offer: Here is a thing to keep you up at night: a notebook, found by the galleries, its owner long past worrying. He copied one line of the wall onto the left page. On the right page -- blank, mind, he wrote nothing there -- the same line is surfacing, letter by letter, in ink that was never wet. It is copying itself. Find where he sat and see what fed it. Then I am sealing the wretched thing in lead.
chamber_depths_the_copied_page.progress: The dead scholar's sitting-place is by the deep galleries, where the self-copying notebook was found. Reach it and see what feeds the page.
chamber_depths_the_copied_page.complete: Sealed in lead now, and I hope lead is enough. It was feeding on the air of the gallery -- the same air that hums, the signal writing itself onto any surface that will hold it, paper or stone or a man's attention. The scholar gave it a page. It took the page and reached for the next. Take the counteragent. Breathe it before you go back down. Do not go back down.

objectives.label: the scholar's seat
objectives.text: Reach the scholar's seat and see what feeds the page
Bring the notebook to Iosif to be sealed

chamber_depths_the_copied_page.note: The notebook copied itself onto a blank page in dry ink, fed on the humming air of the gallery. Iosif sealed it in lead. The signal writes itself onto anything that will hold it -- paper, stone, or the still surface of a curious mind.

chamber_depths_riverset_in_the_dark.title: Riverset in the Dark
chamber_depths_riverset_in_the_dark.summary: Danciu forged a blade for the man who last guarded the deep door, and the man never came back for it. He wants the blade carried down to where its owner fell, or carried by someone who will use it as intended.
chamber_depths_riverset_in_the_dark.offer: I made a blade for the warden of the deep door -- good steel, river-set, tempered the old honest way. He went down to hold the door and did not come up, and the sword has sat on my bench three weeks waiting for a dead man. Carry it down to where he fell. If you find him, lay it by him. If you find only the door, then the blade is yours, and you are the warden now.
chamber_depths_riverset_in_the_dark.progress: The deep door and its fallen warden lie at the far end of the galleries. Carry the riverset blade down and settle the matter there.
chamber_depths_riverset_in_the_dark.complete: Only the door, then. Only ever the door. He held it as long as a man can and a while past that. The blade is yours now, warden -- and it is a better blade than the job deserves, because the job does not deserve anybody. Hold the door when it is your turn. Hold it as long as you can.

objectives.label: the deep door
objectives.text: Carry the blade to the deep door

chamber_depths_riverset_in_the_dark.note: Only the door remained; of the warden, nothing but the ground he held. Danciu's blade passed to a new hand. The door is held, as long as someone can hold it -- which is the only promise the Chamber lets anyone keep.

chamber_depths_the_listening_farmer.title: One More at the Fence
chamber_depths_the_listening_farmer.summary: A woman has come down the stairs each of three nights and stood at the inner rail, listening, and each night she is a little further in. Fane wants her reached and turned back before there is a fourth night.
chamber_depths_the_listening_farmer.offer: There is a woman comes down the stairs at dusk and stands at the inner rail with her head tilted, listening at the vault. First night she stood at the top rail. Last night, three steps lower. Whatever she is hearing, it is calling her down a stair at a time. Reach her at tonight's rail. Talk to her, plain and warm, of ordinary things. Do not let her hear the deep note over your voice.
chamber_depths_the_listening_farmer.progress: The listening woman stands at the inner rail tonight, further down than before. Reach her and turn her back with ordinary words.
chamber_depths_the_listening_farmer.complete: You turned her. She came up the stairs blinking like a woman out of a long church service, unsure what she came for. Ordinary words did it -- the price of bread, the state of the road, her sister's name. The deep note offers a great quiet. A warm voice offers a small now. Turns out a small now is heavier, if you get there in time. Take the ward.

objectives.label: the inner rail
objectives.text: Reach the listening woman at the inner rail
Report to Fane that she turned back

chamber_depths_the_listening_farmer.note: She was called down a stair at a time and turned back by ordinary words -- bread, the road, a sister's name. The deep note offers a great quiet. A warm voice offers a small now. A small now, reached in time, weighs more.

chamber_depths_bogiron_from_the_deep.title: Bog-Iron from the Deep
chamber_depths_bogiron_from_the_deep.summary: The ore that used to come up the road no longer comes, so Danciu works the bog-iron that seeps into the lower gallery -- and needs it fetched from where the seep runs warm and no sensible smith will go.
chamber_depths_bogiron_from_the_deep.offer: The road stopped handing up ore weeks back, so I take what the ground gives, and the ground gives bog-iron in the lower gallery where the seep runs warm. I will not send my prentice; the seep is too near the wrong stone. But you walk down there and come back, which is a trick I would like to buy. Bring me six good lumps of it. I will make you something honest of the seventh.
chamber_depths_bogiron_from_the_deep.progress: Bog-iron seeps into the lower gallery where the stone runs warm. Gather six good lumps.
chamber_depths_bogiron_from_the_deep.complete: Warm to the touch, this iron, and it should not be -- it came out of cold ground and it has not cooled. I will hammer the warmth out of it or die trying. Here, a hook, forged from the last honest-cold lump I had. The road may forget itself. A forge does not, so long as someone keeps it lit.

objectives.text: Gather warm bog-iron from the seep

chamber_depths_bogiron_from_the_deep.note: The bog-iron came up warm from cold ground and would not cool. Danciu hammers the warmth out of it, or tries. The road forgets itself; the forge does not, so long as one man keeps it lit and does not ask why the iron is warm.

chamber_depths_soothing_the_shells.title: Soothing the Shells
chamber_depths_soothing_the_shells.summary: The thread-touched dead grow restless where the lattice pulls tightest, and Nica cannot bear that they thrash. She would have their threads cut -- not for the town's peace, but for theirs.
chamber_depths_soothing_the_shells.offer: I know the guards want them put down for the watch's sake, and that is fair. But I want them put down for their own. They thrash where the web pulls tightest, caught on a will that is not theirs, worked like puppets on a string that hurts. Cut the ones at the tight bend. Not to keep us safe. To let them stop. There is a difference, and at my age it is the only difference left that matters.
chamber_depths_soothing_the_shells.progress: The thread-touched dead thrash where the lattice pulls tightest, at the tight bend of the gallery. Cut them down.
chamber_depths_soothing_the_shells.complete: Stopped now, the poor caught things. Whatever thin blue string held them, you cut it, and they went down like a held breath let go. The guards will call it clearing the watch. Call it what it was: a mercy, done in the dark, that no one will thank you for. Take the knot. It undoes the smaller bindings. Some knots want undoing.

objectives.text: Cut the thrashing shells free at the tight bend

chamber_depths_soothing_the_shells.note: The caught dead were cut free at the tight bend and went down like held breath let go. The guards called it clearing the watch. Nica called it mercy. Both were true. The thread that held them is spun in the north, and it is not idle.

chamber_depths_the_two_note_hum.title: The Two-Note Hum
chamber_depths_the_two_note_hum.summary: The melody in the deep stone has always hummed two notes of three. Emilian swears the third came, for a moment, last night -- and everyone who heard it went still. He needs the source found while it is quiet again.
chamber_depths_the_two_note_hum.offer: The stone has hummed two notes as long as anyone here has been down -- two, always two, reaching for a third it never quite finds. Last night it found it. One breath of a third note, and every soul in the lower gallery went still as furniture. Then it was two again. Find where the sound comes from while it is only two. If it becomes three for good, there will be no one left to send.
chamber_depths_the_two_note_hum.progress: The humming source lies deepest in the stone, past the galleries. Reach it while the melody is only two notes.
chamber_depths_the_two_note_hum.complete: Two notes, still, thank whatever there is to thank. You found the seam it sings from -- a vein of the deep stone, warm as a fever, humming toward a whole it has not yet made. When it makes it, the tale goes, it will not be a hum. It will be an answer, and answering is the end. Take the crown-piece. It dampens the note. It does not silence it. Nothing silences it.

objectives.label: the singing seam
objectives.text: Reach the singing seam of deep stone

chamber_depths_the_two_note_hum.note: For one breath the melody found its third note, and every soul near went still. Then it was two again. The seam sings toward a whole it has not made. When it makes it, the note becomes an answer -- and to answer is the end.

chamber_depths_wardstones_daily.title: Keeping the Wardstones
chamber_depths_wardstones_daily.summary: The salt-lines and chalk wards along the Chamber stair must be walked and renewed each day, or the dark past them creeps up a step. Gherghe sets the round; someone has to keep it.
chamber_depths_wardstones_daily.offer: The wards on the stair are a day's work that is never done. Salt-lines, chalk marks, the old folk-signs -- they hold the dark to its own steps, and they wear thin by dusk. Walk the round. Renew what has faded to the mid-landing. It is dull, thankless, and the only reason the dark is where it is and not up here with us. Come back and I will stand you a share of the watch's pay.
chamber_depths_wardstones_daily.progress: The wards fade by dusk along the Chamber stair. Walk the round to the mid-landing and renew them.
chamber_depths_wardstones_daily.complete: Walked and renewed. The dark is where it was this morning, which down here counts as a victory -- the only kind we get. Same time tomorrow, if you have the stomach for dull work that matters. Here is your share. Dull work that matters is most of what holds a place together.

objectives.label: the mid-landing
objectives.text: Renew the wards to the mid-landing

chamber_depths_wardstones_daily.note: The wards were walked and renewed, and the dark held to its own steps one more day. Dull work, thankless, and the whole reason the town still stands above the Chamber and not inside it.

chamber_depths_thin_the_weavers_daily.title: Thin the Weavers
chamber_depths_thin_the_weavers_daily.summary: The lattice regrows overnight, faster than any watch can cut. Fane keeps a standing bounty: thin the thread-spinners each day so the pattern never finishes closing.
chamber_depths_thin_the_weavers_daily.offer: Cut the web today and it is back by morning, a little further along -- that is the shape of this work. So I keep a bounty standing. Go down, thin the spinners, keep the lattice from closing. You will not finish it. Nobody finishes it. You only keep it from finishing us. That is worth honest coin, and I pay honest coin.
chamber_depths_thin_the_weavers_daily.progress: The lattice regrows daily. Go down and thin the thread-spinners to keep the pattern open.
chamber_depths_thin_the_weavers_daily.complete: Thinned again. The weave is open one more day, which is all open ever is -- one more day. You will be back tomorrow, or someone will. That is not despair, mind. That is a watch. Here is your coin. A watch kept is a watch kept, however long the war.

objectives.text: Thin the thread-spinners on the lattice

chamber_depths_thin_the_weavers_daily.note: The weavers were thinned and the lattice held open one more day, which is all open ever is. The pattern regrows each night, a little further along. The watch cuts it back each day. Neither side is winning. Neither side stops.

chamber_depths_the_incurious_round_daily.title: Walking the Listeners Home
chamber_depths_the_incurious_round_daily.summary: Each dusk, one or two of the incurious drift toward the stair, drawn to listen. Manea keeps a round of walking them back to warmth before the deep note gets its hooks in.
chamber_depths_the_incurious_round_daily.offer: Come dusk there is always one or two drifting for the stair -- a slack look, a tilted head, feet carrying them toward the vault before the rest of them agrees to go. I walk them back while I can still walk. Take my round tonight. Find whoever has drifted to the upper rail and turn them home with a warm word. It is not hard. It only has to be done, every single night, forever.
chamber_depths_the_incurious_round_daily.progress: The drifting listeners gather at the upper rail come dusk. Walk them back to warmth with an ordinary word.
chamber_depths_the_incurious_round_daily.complete: Home again, blinking, not sure what they came for -- which is exactly right, which is the whole job. A warm word turns them where a shout never could. You will do it again tomorrow. So will I, till I cannot. Take the charm. Keep somebody incurious one more night. It counts. It is the only thing that does.

objectives.label: the upper rail
objectives.text: Turn the drifting listeners back from the upper rail

chamber_depths_the_incurious_round_daily.note: The drifters were turned home with warm words, not sure what they came for -- which is the whole of the job. Done every dusk, forever, by whoever can still walk. It counts. Against a stone that hungers for a blank world, it is the only thing that does.

chamber_depths_hessiks_foundations.title: Hessik's Practical Foundations
chamber_depths_hessiks_foundations.summary: The only defense against the wall is to see it without reading it, and that is a craft. Iosif has the pages of Hessik's alchemy scattered through the galleries where students dropped them fleeing. He wants them gathered into one hand -- yours.
chamber_depths_hessiks_foundations.offer: There is a book that teaches the sight -- Hessik's Practical Foundations. Blue for the dead, violet for the deep, green for a stone about to wake, orange for a stone that is writing itself and you must leave. My students carried its pages down to study and dropped them where they fled. Gather the pages from the galleries. Learn the colors. It is the difference between reading the land and being read by it.
chamber_depths_hessiks_foundations.progress: Hessik's scattered pages lie through the galleries where students dropped them. Gather them into one hand.
chamber_depths_hessiks_foundations.complete: All of it, in order, and the sight to use it. Now you will know a live stone from a dead carving by its color, and orange means the one thing worth knowing: leave, or transmit, but never read. It is a detective's craft in a world that hangs its detectives. Use it to look away in time. That is what it is for. Not to see more. To see when to stop.

objectives.text: Gather Hessik's scattered pages
Bind the pages with Iosif

chamber_depths_hessiks_foundations.note: The pages were gathered and the sight learned: blue for the dead, violet for the deep, green for the waking, orange for the stone that writes itself -- leave, do not read. A detective's craft, in a world that hangs its detectives. It is for knowing when to look away.

western_lowlands_a_place_at_the_table.title: A Place at the Table
western_lowlands_a_place_at_the_table.summary: Sanda keeps a bench warm at the Lowland inn for river-folk who walk east and are late to supper. This season the benches keep on emptying. She would like to fill one, if only with meat.
western_lowlands_a_place_at_the_table.offer: Sit, sit -- you look like a road. I keep three benches by the fire, always have, for the ones who walk east and come back hungry. Two are empty this month and I don't care to think on why. Bring me good haunch off the fallow strips, enough for a proper pot, and I'll have one bench loud again before dark.
western_lowlands_a_place_at_the_table.progress: The field-gnawers came in off the fallow strips when the harvests thinned. Six of them will make a pot that fills a bench.
western_lowlands_a_place_at_the_table.complete: Now that's a smell this room forgot. There -- the bench is loud again, and a full bench lies for you the way a full plate does. Take the pot-hook, dear. Old iron. It has hung over kinder fires than mine.

objectives.text: Cull field-gnawers on the fallow strips

western_lowlands_a_place_at_the_table.note: One bench loud again, and two still cold. Sanda laid a plate at each anyway, the way the west has always set a place for the walking-east and rarely gotten one back.

western_lowlands_what_the_river_carries.title: What the River Carries
western_lowlands_what_the_river_carries.summary: The lowlands barge is a week overdue and Rada's stall is bare. She will not fret aloud in front of buyers, so she frets at you: walk to the landing and tell her the water still moves.
western_lowlands_what_the_river_carries.offer: A merchant with an empty table is a merchant folk stop trusting, so smile while I say this: the barge is a week late and my shelves show it. I can't leave the stall to go looking. Walk down to the barge-landing for me, would you, and tell me the river still runs and the ropes are only slow, not gone.
western_lowlands_what_the_river_carries.progress: The lowlands barge ties up at the landing where the road meets the water, west along the furrows.
western_lowlands_what_the_river_carries.complete: Only slow, then. Good. Rope frays, water forgets a schedule -- that I can sell around. It's the other kind of late I can't. Here, a striker for your trouble; strike it and there's always one light you carry yourself.

objectives.label: the barge-landing
objectives.text: See the barge-landing and the river

western_lowlands_what_the_river_carries.note: The river still ran and the ropes were only slow. Rada sold that answer to herself all afternoon. There is another kind of late in the west now, and no barge carries it.

western_lowlands_straw_men.title: Straw Men
western_lowlands_straw_men.summary: Scarecrows are standing in fields that were never sown, and Stanca's patrol has learned to count them going out and count them coming back. The counts have stopped agreeing. She wants the extra ones put down.
western_lowlands_straw_men.offer: I've walked the west furrow for eleven years and I know how many scarecrows stand on it. There are more now than any farmer set. They keep the strips they were never told to keep, and they take what wanders in past dusk. Put down the ones with no field to their name. Six should do it. Don't let them touch you -- there's a debtor stitched inside every one.
western_lowlands_straw_men.progress: The scarecrow wights hold the unsown strips past the western furrows. Six of the extra ones will thin the count.
western_lowlands_straw_men.complete: Straw and old grief and a debt-mark burned in the post -- that's what falls out of them. Somebody was left too long on the pole for what they owed, and the field kept them anyway. My counts agree again. That's all I can fix. Take the warding; press it on your own door and mean it.

objectives.text: Put down the scarecrow wights on the unsown strips

western_lowlands_straw_men.note: Inside each was a debtor left too long on the post. The field was told to keep them and did. Stanca's counts agree again. She has stopped saying the numbers aloud.

western_lowlands_the_leaning_post.title: The Leaning Post
western_lowlands_the_leaning_post.summary: Mira's grandmother is stitched into a scarecrow on the far strip -- she is nearly certain, from the way it wears the shawl. She cannot go herself. She asks only that you go, and look, and cut it down if it is her.
western_lowlands_the_leaning_post.offer: There's a scarecrow on the far strip wearing my grandmother's shawl. I know how that sounds. Gran signed a ledger the wet year to keep our two acres, and when the collectors came she went out to the post herself so they'd take her and not the land. I can't walk out there and know. Please. Go, and look, and if the shawl is grey with a red border, cut it down and let her lie.
western_lowlands_the_leaning_post.progress: The leaning post stands alone on the far western strip, out past the drowned fence. Look, and know.
western_lowlands_the_leaning_post.complete: Grey with a red border. So it was her. You laid her in the furrow -- the willing soil, Gran always called it, you could plant a stick and harvest a chair. She planted herself to keep it. Take her keepsake-cord. I can't wear it. But I can't burn it, either, and you carried her down.

objectives.label: the leaning post on the far strip
objectives.text: Reach the leaning post and look
Cut down the post, if it is her

western_lowlands_the_leaning_post.note: The shawl was grey with a red border. She had gone to the post so the collectors would take her and not the acres. Mira keeps the cord she cannot wear. The two acres are still theirs. That was the price, and it was paid in full.

western_lowlands_the_full_granary.title: The Full Granary
western_lowlands_the_full_granary.summary: Ecaterina keeps the west's death-rolls, and the rolls this year read like a famine. But the land is willing and the furrows are straight and the barns, she has heard, are not empty. She wants the granary counted -- honestly, by someone who owes no one here.
western_lowlands_the_full_granary.offer: I write down who dies and lately I write too much. They call it a lean year. I have kept forty of those and this is not one -- the soil is willing, the furrows straight. So why the rolls? There is a granary north of the barn that no cart comes to. Go and count what's in it, plainly, and bring me the number. I want it from a hand that owes nobody in this valley.
western_lowlands_the_full_granary.progress: The full granary stands north of the barn, off the road no cart uses. Reach it and count what it holds while the villages go hungry.
western_lowlands_the_full_granary.complete: Full. You are certain. Full to the rafters, the whole hungry season. Then this was never a famine, girl -- famine is when there is nothing. This is a list. Someone read the villages down it, house by house in order, and the grain sat behind a door with a lock. I will write that number. I do not yet know who I dare show it to.

objectives.label: the full granary
objectives.text: Reach the granary and count the grain
Scatter the penitents keeping the granary door

western_lowlands_the_full_granary.note: The granary was full the entire hungry season. A famine is nothing; this was a list, read down house by house, and the grain kept behind a lock. Ecaterina wrote the number. She has not decided yet whom she dares to show.

western_lowlands_signed_in_mud.title: Signed in Mud
western_lowlands_signed_in_mud.summary: The penitents at the granary door recite a debt as they die. Ecaterina wants the words -- the actual ledger they are reading from -- brought off their bodies, so she can see whose seal is at the bottom of the west's hunger.
western_lowlands_signed_in_mud.offer: You heard them at the granary, reciting -- that's not madness, that's a document. Mudlarks who signed the wrong ledger to keep the wrong land, and now they pray it. I need the pages, not the prayer. Bring me the marked map-borders they carry, enough of them to read a boundary, and I'll tell you whose line the west was quietly sold across.
western_lowlands_signed_in_mud.progress: The mudlark penitents walk the strip between the barn and the river, each carrying a scrap of the boundary. Take five marked map-borders off them.
western_lowlands_signed_in_mud.complete: Five borders, and every one redrawn the same wet season -- the year of the leaning posts. The old bounds shifted a furrow's width west, over and over, on paper nobody read. That is how you steal a valley: not with fire, with a pen and a bad year. The seal at the bottom is smudged. Smudged on purpose, I'd wager. Keep the lens. You'll want to read small things, after this.

objectives.text: Take marked map-borders from the penitents
Bring the borders to Ecaterina to read

western_lowlands_signed_in_mud.note: Every border was redrawn the same wet season, a furrow's width west each time, on paper no one read. A valley stolen with a pen and a bad year. The seal at the bottom was smudged -- deliberately. Ecaterina keeps the pages in a locked drawer now, and checks the lock twice.

western_lowlands_the_standing_farmer.title: The Standing Farmer
western_lowlands_the_standing_farmer.summary: A farmer has stood in his own field for three days now, head tilted, not eating, not answering. His wife has stopped coming to fetch him. Ruxandra will not go alone. She asks you to stand the last few paces and see what he is listening to.
western_lowlands_the_standing_farmer.offer: There's a man standing in his east field. Three days. Head canted, like the whole sky's a door somebody's about to knock on. His wife came the first two mornings with his bread. She didn't come today. I've walked toward him twice and turned back twice, and I am not a woman who turns back. Come with me. Stand where I can't make myself stand, and tell me plain what he hears.
western_lowlands_the_standing_farmer.progress: The standing farmer holds his east field, unmoving, near the murder-scene furrows. Reach him and stand where Ruxandra cannot.
western_lowlands_the_standing_farmer.complete: You stood right by him. And you say the ground was warm through your boots, and the dust between the clods lay in fine straight lines pointing east, and he wasn't listening to a voice -- he was listening the way a stone listens, patient, for something that hasn't finished arriving. His bread's still in the furrow where his wife left it. We'll leave him a while longer. I don't know a spade that fixes that. Take the charm. Wear it and stay uncurious.

objectives.label: the standing farmer's east field
objectives.text: Reach the standing farmer and listen
Tell Ruxandra plainly what he hears

western_lowlands_the_standing_farmer.note: The ground was warm through the boots, the dust lay in straight lines pointing east, and the farmer listened the way a stone listens -- for something not yet finished arriving. His bread stayed in the furrow. Nobody has fetched him. Nobody has said the word for what he has become, because there is not one yet.

western_lowlands_honest_iron.title: Honest Iron
western_lowlands_honest_iron.summary: Voica's forge runs on bog-iron dug from the drowned strips, and the diggers won't dig while the gnawers are thick. Clear her a season's worth of quiet and she'll shoe every plough in the valley for free.
western_lowlands_honest_iron.offer: A ploughshare beats a prayer, that's my whole faith. But my diggers won't set foot on the drowned strips with the gnawers running lean and bold, and no bog-iron means no shares, no shares means a hungry spring. Thin the brutes off the digging-ground for me. Do that, and half the valley ploughs on my iron come the thaw, and the other half owes you a supper.
western_lowlands_honest_iron.progress: The field-gnawers den thick on the drowned strips where the diggers cut bog-iron. Eight will clear a season.
western_lowlands_honest_iron.complete: Heard the diggers singing on the strips this morning -- first time since the wet came. That's the sound of iron getting made. Here, raw bog-iron, a good lump of it: give it a smith worth the name and it'll come back a blade that keeps its edge honest. The west runs on honest iron. Long may it.

objectives.text: Clear gnawers off the bog-iron strips

western_lowlands_honest_iron.note: The diggers sang on the strips again, the sound of iron getting made. Voica will shoe the valley's ploughs on it. A hungry spring bought off, for one year, with a season of quiet and a raised hand the gnawers no longer fear.

western_lowlands_a_charm_for_the_door.title: A Charm for the Door
western_lowlands_a_charm_for_the_door.summary: Lenuta wants a hearth-hex for her new baby's door and is too shy to ask the blacksmith outright. She'll trade you her mother's marshlight jar if you'll fetch the sign and carry the awkwardness for her.
western_lowlands_a_charm_for_the_door.offer: It's daft, I know. But the baby's come and every door in the west has a sign over it and mine's bare, and I can't make myself walk up to Voica and say the words -- she'll think I've gone superstitious as my mother. Ask her for a hearth-hex sign, would you? For the little one's door. I'll give you my mother's marshlight jar for the trouble; it's kept many a dark night friendly.
western_lowlands_a_charm_for_the_door.progress: Voica the smith keeps hearth-hex signs among her small iron. Ask her for one, then bring it back to Lenuta.
western_lowlands_a_charm_for_the_door.complete: Oh, it's a fine one -- proper cold iron, proper old shape. Over the door it goes tonight. My mother would say the sign doesn't keep anything out, it just tells the house it's loved, and a loved house keeps itself. Here's her jar, as promised. Marshlight never dies down all the way. Nice thing to have, these nights.

objectives.text: Ask Voica for a hearth-hex sign
Bring the sign back to Lenuta

western_lowlands_a_charm_for_the_door.note: The sign went up over the baby's door. It keeps nothing out, Lenuta's mother used to say -- it only tells the house it is loved, so the house keeps itself. In the west these days, a loved house is worth a great deal, and worth watching.

western_lowlands_the_barge_that_didnt_co.title: The Barge That Didn't Come
western_lowlands_the_barge_that_didnt_co.summary: The barge came in at last -- but light, and short three crates that the manifest swears were loaded. Rada wants a quiet word with the penitents who work the landing before she makes an accusation she can't take back.
western_lowlands_the_barge_that_didnt_co.offer: The barge tied up. Light by three crates the manifest lists as loaded and sealed. Now, a bargeman skims -- I've eaten that cost for years, it's the tax of the trade. But three whole crates, and the penitents who load the landing won't meet my eye and keep muttering that string of theirs. Thin a few of them off the ropes, and see what they're carrying under those prayers. I'd rather know than guess.
western_lowlands_the_barge_that_didnt_co.progress: The mudlark penitents work the barge-landing ropes to the west. Cut down four and see what they carry.
western_lowlands_the_barge_that_didnt_co.complete: Toll-receipts. Forged ones, three of them, stamped for crates that were never mine to begin with -- my seal traced by a hand that studied it. So the barge wasn't robbed. It was re-filed. Someone's moving grain under my name to keep their own ledger clean. I'll not say whose. Take the forgery; you may need to prove a thing that isn't true someday. I hope you never do.

objectives.text: Question the penitents at the landing
Recover the forged toll-receipts

western_lowlands_the_barge_that_didnt_co.note: The barge was not robbed. It was re-filed -- grain moved under Rada's traced seal to keep another's ledger clean. She will not say whose. She checks her own seal against the receipts now, some nights, to be sure it is still only hers.

western_lowlands_grave_by_the_road.title: The Grave by the Road
western_lowlands_grave_by_the_road.summary: Four fresh graves have appeared by the roadside with no names cut and no families come. Stanca will not let them lie unmarked. She asks you to see them counted and warded, so at least the ground knows they were people.
western_lowlands_grave_by_the_road.offer: Four new mounds by the west road. No stones, no names, no kin come weeping -- just dug, quick and neat, and left. That's not how the west buries. We know our dead by name or we don't call it burial. Go out and stand by them. Count them true. Set a warding at the head of each so the ground remembers they were people, even if no one living will say the names.
western_lowlands_grave_by_the_road.progress: The four unmarked graves lie by the west road, past the barn. Reach them and ward each mound.
western_lowlands_grave_by_the_road.complete: Four. All the same length, all the same day's digging, all facing east -- which is the wrong way; we lay our dead to face the river. Somebody buried them by a rule that isn't ours. You warded them anyway. Good. The ground knows now. Take the pouch -- salt from the old line. Lay a little where you sleep and the dead stay dead, mostly.

objectives.label: the unmarked graves by the road
objectives.text: Reach and ward the four graves

western_lowlands_grave_by_the_road.note: Four graves, one day's digging, all laid facing east -- the wrong way, by a rule that was not the west's. Stanca warded them so the ground would remember they were people. No family ever came. Someone had already filed them elsewhere.

western_lowlands_the_willing_soil.title: The Willing Soil
western_lowlands_the_willing_soil.summary: Sanda's late husband planted a pear tree the year they wed, and it still fruits, but a scarecrow-wight has taken to standing under it and the pickers won't go near. Clear the tree so the pears come in for the fair.
western_lowlands_the_willing_soil.offer: My Anton planted a pear by the barn the year we married -- said if the soil's willing you plant something that outlives the planting, and it has, it's outlived him twenty years. It still fruits sweeter than anything in the west. But one of those straw things stands under it now and my pickers won't go, and I'll not lose Anton's pears to a superstition and a scarecrow. Put it down and the tree's yours to pick.
western_lowlands_the_willing_soil.progress: The scarecrow-wight stands beneath the old pear by the barn. Put it down so the pickers can work.
western_lowlands_the_willing_soil.complete: Down it went, and the pears came in by the basket -- Anton's pears, sweet as the year he planted them. The whole inn'll taste them at the fair. Twenty years gone and the tree still keeps him. That's the willing soil for you: it holds what you give it. Here -- a soothing-knot my mother tied. Loose it slow when the nights are bad.

objectives.text: Clear the wight from beneath the pear tree

western_lowlands_the_willing_soil.note: The pears came in sweet as the year Anton planted the tree, twenty years dead. The willing soil holds what you give it, Sanda says. She does not finish the thought aloud -- that it holds the debtors on their posts just the same.

western_lowlands_the_penitents_prayer.title: The Penitent's Prayer
western_lowlands_the_penitents_prayer.summary: A mudlark penitent stopped at Ecaterina's window last night and recited a full debt -- names, sums, a date -- before wading back to the river. She wants the recitation confirmed off others of its kind, to learn whether they all pray the same ledger.
western_lowlands_the_penitents_prayer.offer: One came to my window in the dark and said a debt at me like a psalm -- names I knew, sums, a date. Then it walked back into the river. I wrote every word before I lost my nerve. I need to know if it was madness or a text. Go among the mudlarks on the strips, hear what they recite, and take the dossiers of habits they keep -- the little books of who-owes-what. If they all pray the same ledger, we are in more trouble than a bad harvest.
western_lowlands_the_penitents_prayer.progress: The mudlark penitents recite along the river strips. Take three dossiers of habits and confirm the prayer.
western_lowlands_the_penitents_prayer.complete: The same ledger. Word for word, all three, down to the smudged seal. It isn't madness -- it's recitation. They are keeping a debt alive by saying it, the way we keep the dead alive by naming them, only backwards: they name what's owed until the owing walks. Somebody taught them this. Somebody wanted the debt remembered when every honest book of it was burned. Take a dossier. Learn the shape of how they think. You'll meet the author eventually.

objectives.text: Take dossiers from the reciting penitents
Confirm the recitation with Ecaterina

western_lowlands_the_penitents_prayer.note: All three prayed the same ledger, word for word. They keep a debt alive by reciting it, as the west keeps its dead alive by naming them -- only turned around. Someone taught them the words when every honest book of the debt had burned. Ecaterina has begun to wonder who benefits from a debt that cannot be forgotten.

western_lowlands_ploughshares_and_pikes.title: Ploughshares and Pikes
western_lowlands_ploughshares_and_pikes.summary: The valley reeves have ordered Voica to beat the farmers' ploughshares into pike-heads for a levy nobody voted on. She'll delay them one way: bring her enough bog-iron to arm the order without touching a single share.
western_lowlands_ploughshares_and_pikes.offer: Word came down: turn every ploughshare in the west to a pike-head for the border levy. A levy no farmer here agreed to, mind, signed off somewhere upriver with a smudged seal. I'll not unmake a valley's ploughs to arm somebody's ambition -- but refuse outright and they replace me with a smith who won't. So bring me raw bog-iron, a good heap of it, and I'll forge their pikes from that and leave the shares in the ground where they feed people. Buys us a season. Maybe a harvest.
western_lowlands_ploughshares_and_pikes.progress: Bog-iron lies in the drowned strips, guarded now by penitents who've claimed the digging-ground. Bring Voica six lumps.
western_lowlands_ploughshares_and_pikes.complete: Six lumps -- enough to arm their order twice over and never touch a share. The reeves get their pikes, the farmers keep their ploughs, and nobody upriver learns the difference. That's not a victory, that's a delay dressed as one. But a delayed valley still eats. Take the pot-hook's cousin -- a proper riverset nail-hook, hangs a kettle straight over any fire you build. Small comfort. They're the only kind left.

objectives.text: Dig bog-iron from the drowned strips
Drive penitents off the digging-ground

western_lowlands_ploughshares_and_pikes.note: The reeves got their pikes from bog-iron and never touched a ploughshare. A valley's harvest bought with a lie of omission. Voica calls it a delay dressed as a victory. Somewhere upriver, a smudged seal is still ordering the west unmade, one levy at a time.

western_lowlands_the_wife_who_stopped_co.title: The Wife Who Stopped Coming
western_lowlands_the_wife_who_stopped_co.summary: The standing farmer's wife stopped bringing his bread -- and now she's gone from her cottage entirely, the door open, a place set for two. Ruxandra fears she's walked out to stand beside him. Find her before the field keeps them both.
western_lowlands_the_wife_who_stopped_co.offer: Her door's open. Bread on the table, two plates laid, chairs pushed back like they rose mid-supper. She stopped bringing him his loaf three days ago, and now she's not in the cottage and not on the road. I think she walked out to her husband's field to stand with him -- to hear whatever he hears. Go to the cottage first, then the field. If she's still walking, walk her home. If she's stopped... then at least we'll know where the field draws its second post.
western_lowlands_the_wife_who_stopped_co.progress: Start at the wife's open cottage on the west edge, then follow to the standing farmer's field where he waits.
western_lowlands_the_wife_who_stopped_co.complete: Both of them, then. Side by side in the east furrow, heads canted the same way, the ground warm around their feet and the dust in lines between them. Two plates on the table for a supper nobody came back to. You couldn't wake them. I didn't think you could. We'll set the cottage's charm-of-quiet on the doorpost and leave the place as it is. Some houses you don't clean out. You just let them get old.

objectives.label: the wife's open cottage
objectives.text: Search the wife's cottage

objectives.label: the standing farmer's field
objectives.text: Follow to the field

western_lowlands_the_wife_who_stopped_co.note: She had walked out to stand beside her husband, and the field kept them both -- two posts now where there was one, the ground warm between them. Two plates cool on a table nobody returned to. Ruxandra set the charm-of-quiet on the doorpost and left the cottage to get old. The field is still counting.

western_lowlands_kept_names.title: Kept Names
western_lowlands_kept_names.summary: If the penitents keep a debt alive by reciting it, Ecaterina means to fight back the only way a chronicler knows: keep the people alive by naming them. She asks you to gather the name-tokens off the fallen and read them into her rolls before they're lost.
western_lowlands_kept_names.offer: They keep the owing alive by saying it. Very well -- I keep the people alive by writing them. That is the whole of my craft and the only weapon I was ever given. When a mudlark falls, it drops a token with a name on it -- the name of the debtor it was, before the ledger ate it. Bring me those. I will cut each one into my rolls as a person, not a sum. It changes nothing they can measure. It changes everything I can.
western_lowlands_kept_names.progress: The kept-name tokens fall from the penitents on the strips. Gather six and bring them to Ecaterina's rolls.
western_lowlands_kept_names.complete: Six names. Six people, not six debts. I've cut each into the west's death-roll with the day they were lost and one true thing about them, whatever the token remembered -- a trade, a child, a good year. Now they are kept the way we keep our own, and the ledger can recite all it likes; I have written a louder book. It won't stop the debt from walking. But when it walks, it will pass names that argue back. Take the token they'll never get filed. Keep somebody's name in your pocket. It helps.

objectives.text: Gather kept-name tokens from the fallen
Read the names into Ecaterina's rolls

western_lowlands_kept_names.note: Six debtors cut back into the west's death-roll as people, each with one true thing beside the name. It stops nothing the ledger can measure and everything Ecaterina can. When the debt walks now, it walks past names that argue back. It is a small book against a patient one. She writes in it every night.

western_lowlands_hex_over_every_door.title: A Hex Over Every Door
western_lowlands_hex_over_every_door.summary: Lenuta's charm worked so well by her reckoning that the neighbors want signs too, and now she's promised more than she can carry. Help her ward the hamlet, door by door, before her nerve or the daylight runs out.
western_lowlands_hex_over_every_door.offer: Word got round about the baby's sign, and now every mother in the hamlet wants one, and I went and said yes to all of them because I can't say no to a worried mother -- I am one. But it's a fair walk between the houses and the light goes early these days and I'll not do it alone past dusk. Come with me. We'll set a hex-sign over every door in the row before dark, and let the whole hamlet sleep loved for once.
western_lowlands_hex_over_every_door.progress: The hamlet's doors run along the east row by the wellstone. Reach the far door setting signs as you go.
western_lowlands_hex_over_every_door.complete: Every door in the row, done before the light gave out -- I counted them twice. There's a good feeling in a whole street warded together; feels like the houses are holding hands. Silly, I know. But nobody in this row sleeps alone tonight, not really. Here, a chalk handprint ward -- press your own hand in it and put it up wherever you rest. Nice to leave your mark somewhere friendly.

objectives.label: the far door of the hamlet row
objectives.text: Ward the hamlet doors to the far house

western_lowlands_hex_over_every_door.note: Every door in the row warded before dark, the houses holding hands down the street. Nobody there slept alone that night. A chalk handprint on a warm door is a small thing. In the west these days the small things are the whole of the defense, and there is a copper print on a wall in Angel Wings that began the same way.

western_lowlands_the_reeves_ledger.title: The Reeve's Ledger
western_lowlands_the_reeves_ledger.summary: Rada has traced the forged receipts to a reeve who keeps a private ledger of the valley's true debts. She wants the ledger-craft that made the forgeries -- the tools, the traced seals -- so she can prove the west was robbed by paperwork, not weather.
western_lowlands_the_reeves_ledger.offer: I found where my seal was learned. A reeve keeps a second ledger -- the real one, the one that says who truly owes what and to whom, under all the forged tolls. The penitents guard the tools he forges with: the traced seals, the ledger-craft. Bring me those and I can walk into any Accord court and show them the west wasn't starved by a bad year. It was invoiced. That's a different crime, and it hangs a different neck.
western_lowlands_the_reeves_ledger.progress: The reeve's forging-tools are held by penitents around the barn. Recover the ledger-craft and bring it to Rada.
western_lowlands_the_reeves_ledger.complete: Ledger-craft, traced seals, a whole quiet workshop for turning a lie into a document. This is the murder weapon, and it's a pen. I'll take it to the Accord myself. They may bury it -- courts do -- but it exists now, in my hand, and a thing that exists can't be fully unfiled. Take a copy of the craft. Learn how the lie is made and you'll always spot it. That's worth more than the gold, out here.

objectives.text: Recover the reeve's ledger-craft
Clear the penitents guarding the tools
Bring the ledger-craft to Rada

western_lowlands_the_reeves_ledger.note: The murder weapon was a pen, and Rada holds it now: the ledger-craft that invoiced a valley into famine. She will carry it to the Accord, who may well bury it. But it exists, and a thing that exists cannot be fully unfiled. She has stopped trusting her own seal, and started copying everything twice.
