The bog'll grow more by tomorrow -- it always does, it's the one thing round here that's reliable. Reliable and trying to kill us. That's Vetka in three words. Come back tomorrow.

objectives.text: Thin the wet ground for the standing bounty

vetka_widow_bounty_daily.note: Paid by the head, no questions on the count. Vlaicu empties the small purse nightly rather than fill one more grave; those are the only two options left him. The bog grows more by morning -- reliable, and trying to kill them. That is Vetka in three words.

vetka_a_warm_night.title: One Warm Night at the Oar
vetka_a_warm_night.summary: Petru means to give Vetka one warm loud night before the season worsens -- a fire, a full pot, the whole low town crowded in. He is short on honest meat. Bring him boar off the road, so the stew is stew and not a symptom.
vetka_a_warm_night.offer: Here's a foolish thing I want to do before the winter's teeth come in: one warm night. Fire built high, pot full, every door in the low town open and the whole tired lot of us crowded in loud enough to drown the moor. But you can't crowd sick folk into a warm room and hope -- half the low houses are coughing. Thin the moor off the near road so the old ones can walk in safe, and bring me poultices from Magda's shelf for the ones the damp's already got. Then we'll have our night.
vetka_a_warm_night.progress: Clear the moor off the near road, and gather poultices from Magda's shelf for the coughing folk.
vetka_a_warm_night.complete: Road's clear and the poultices are handed round, and by dark this room'll be so warm and so loud that not one soul in it hears the moor. That's the whole trick out here -- you can't stop the night. You can make one room too warm and too full for it to get in. Sit. Eat. For one night, Vetka forgets to listen. That's worth more than coin, but here's coin too.

objectives.text: Clear the moor off the near road
Gather poultices for the coughing folk

vetka_a_warm_night.note: For one warm loud night the low town crowded into the Bent Oar and forgot to listen. You cannot stop the night out here. You can make one room too warm and too full for it to get in. Everything good is temporary. That is what makes it good.

black_night_the_quiet_market.title: The Quiet Market
black_night_the_quiet_market.summary: The Iele stand their market as they have for lifetimes, buying nothing, selling less. A living guard wants a living pair of eyes to count them before the count means something.
black_night_the_quiet_market.offer: You breathe. Good. I've watched the market square since the thaw and I've stopped trusting my own count. Walk it slow and tell me how they stand — the shells, I mean. Not the number. The shape of the number. Then come back while you still want to.
black_night_the_quiet_market.progress: The market square sits at the heart of the lower city. Walk its rows and mark how the shells are arranged.
black_night_the_quiet_market.complete: Rows of twelve. You saw it too. That's a butcher's count, an execution ledger's count — not a market's. Nobody set them so. Nobody has to, anymore. Take the salt. It won't help. It'll remind you there's a door.

objectives.label: the market square
objectives.text: Walk the market square and mark the rows
Bring Sava the shape of the count

black_night_the_quiet_market.note: They stand in rows of twelve, the grammar of a killing. No hand arranges them now. The Thread and the ledger were always the same tongue.

black_night_still_holds_a_lantern.title: A Hand That Still Holds a Lantern
black_night_still_holds_a_lantern.summary: Oprea keeps the only warm room in a city of the cold-blooded, and lately the lantern at his door goes out at the same hour every night — snuffed, he says, not spent. He'd like a witness before he stops relighting it.
black_night_still_holds_a_lantern.offer: I keep the last warm room north of the Vein, and I keep a lantern lit at the door for the living who wander in. It goes dark at the third bell now — snuffed clean, not burned down. My wife says leave it dark. Stand the door with me one night and tell me whether she's right.
black_night_still_holds_a_lantern.progress: Return to the inn door at the north edge of the square and keep the lantern-watch until the third bell.
black_night_still_holds_a_lantern.complete: There. You felt the cold come up before the flame died, same as me. Not wind. A tidiness. Something walks this street putting out lights it thinks are asking questions. Keep the striker — small, honest, ours. Light what you like. I'll keep relighting the door. Somebody living has to be stubborn about it.

objectives.label: the inn door
objectives.text: Keep the lantern-watch till the third bell

black_night_still_holds_a_lantern.note: The lantern was snuffed, not spent — a tidiness, not a wind. Oprea relights the door each dusk. In a city that does not breathe, stubbornness is a kind of prayer.

black_night_the_pale_between_stones.title: The Pale Between the Stones
black_night_the_pale_between_stones.summary: Pale-Stalkers have come down the thread-lit alleys into the living quarter, thin and patient, taking the ones who wander alone. The captain's watch is dead men who cannot be spared. The living must be.
black_night_the_pale_between_stones.offer: I command a watch of the dead — obedient, tireless, and no use at all when the Pale-Stalkers come, because the shells won't lift a hand against their own weather. They've come down the west alleys, thin things, quiet as held breath, and three of my living clerks are gone. Cut them back. Ten should teach the alleys to keep their own.
black_night_the_pale_between_stones.progress: Pale-Stalkers haunt the thread-lit alleys of the west quarter. Cut them back before another living soul is taken.
black_night_the_pale_between_stones.complete: Ten, and the alleys breathe easier. My dead watch will hold them a season. But you saw how they move — spread even, unhurried, pacing the ground like they were measuring it for something to be built. Not hunting. Surveying. I have buried that thought where I bury everything. Take the coin and the cord. You earned the warm side of both.

objectives.text: Cut back the Pale-Stalkers in the west alleys

black_night_the_pale_between_stones.note: The Pale-Stalkers pace even and unhurried, surveying more than hunting. Aurel buried the thought. In Black Night, not-thinking is the only discipline the dead cannot take from you.

black_night_the_listening_ones.title: Those Who Have Started Listening
black_night_the_listening_ones.summary: Living pilgrims walked north and now stand in the streets, heads tilted, listening. Cornel brews a counteragent that pulls a few of them back — for a while. He needs the makings, and a witness who understands that this is not a cure but a delay.
black_night_the_listening_ones.offer: They came for answers and the answers are eating them — pilgrims, standing in my street, heads tilted, listening to a thing the rest of us are lucky not to hear. I can brew a quiet back into a few of them. It does not last. Bring me warded thread and salted linen from my supply on the north stair, and stand with me while I wake the closest one. You should see what waking costs.
black_night_the_listening_ones.progress: Gather Cornel's makings — soothing knots and salt-line pouches from his stores — then return so he can wake a listener while you witness it.
black_night_the_listening_ones.complete: There. She blinked. She asked for her mother, who died in the Grey Marches, and I did not correct her. In an hour she will tilt her head again and I will have spent all this to buy her one hour of being a person. Take the counteragent I set aside. Carry it for the next one. There is always a next one.

objectives.text: Gather soothing knots from Cornel's stores
Gather salt-line pouches
Witness Cornel wake a listener

black_night_the_listening_ones.note: The waking bought her one hour of being herself. Comprehension is erasure, and the counteragent only rents against it. Cornel says there is always a next one. He is not wrong, and that is the whole horror.

black_night_the_incurious.title: The Value of Not Wondering
black_night_the_incurious.summary: Cornel has learned the northern trick the pilgrims never do: a charm that means nothing, and protects because it means nothing. He'll teach the making, if you carry cedar and chalk up from the living traders and ask him no questions about the thread it's tied on.
black_night_the_incurious.offer: You want to keep your own head north of the Vein? Then learn to want less. The pilgrims died of curiosity; the wise die old and dull. I tie a charm that means nothing on purpose — a hook the signal cannot catch, because there is nothing on it to read. Fetch me cedar shavings and a chalk-nub charm from the traders. Bring them, and ask me nothing about the thread.
black_night_the_incurious.progress: The living traders keep cedar and folk-wards by the south gate. Bring Cornel an incurious charm and a hearth hex-sign for the making.
black_night_the_incurious.complete: Good. You didn't ask. That's the first half of the craft. It means nothing — that is the whole defense. Wear it and be dull, traveler. The clever ones up here are all standing in the market now, listening. Dullness is the only cleverness the North rewards.

objectives.text: Bring Cornel an incurious charm
Bring a hearth hex-sign

black_night_the_incurious.note: A charm that means nothing cannot be read, and so cannot be caught. Cornel calls it the only cleverness the North rewards. The pilgrims who were cleverer are still standing in the market.

black_night_the_thread_that_frays.title: The Thread That Frays
black_night_the_thread_that_frays.summary: A Councillor of the Six wants the Thread's frayed ends mapped where the shells drop inert — and wants it done by a living hand the Council can disown. Radovan is asking the same question, for reasons the Councillor will not name.
black_night_the_thread_that_frays.offer: The Council of Six speaks with one voice when the doors are shut and six when they open, and today I speak for none of them but myself. The Thread is thinning in the deep quarter — shells dropping where the filament fails, dead as dropped gloves. I need it mapped, and I need it mapped by someone the Council never sent. Walk the deep quarter to the fallen shells. Note where the light gives out. Tell me — only me — and not Radovan, whatever he offers you.
black_night_the_thread_that_frays.progress: Reach the deep quarter where the shells have dropped inert, and mark where the Thread's light fails. Report to Danciu, and to no one else.
black_night_the_thread_that_frays.complete: So it fails in a line, not a scatter. A line points somewhere. You understand what that means and I will thank you not to say it aloud in this room, where the walls have opinions. Radovan already knows, of course — he knows a day ahead of the rest of us, always, and I have stopped believing it is cleverness. Take the reading-draught. Look before you trust. It is the only advice I have left that costs nothing.

objectives.label: the deep quarter
objectives.text: Map where the Thread's light fails
Report the failing line to Danciu alone

black_night_the_thread_that_frays.note: The Thread fails in a line, and a line points somewhere. Danciu forbade the saying of it aloud. Radovan already knew — a day ahead, as always. The Councillor has stopped calling that cleverness.

black_night_gravediggers_humor.title: Steady Work
black_night_gravediggers_humor.summary: Vintila digs graves in a city where the dead won't stay in them, and has made a grim peace with the joke of it. His spade's cracked; he'd trade a good story and a warm flask for a hand hauling the day's stones.
black_night_gravediggers_humor.offer: Best job in Black Night, mine — steady work, guaranteed. The dead here don't rot and they don't rest, so I dig 'em, they walk out, I dig 'em again. Job security, my old mother would've called it. My spade's split and my back's worse. Haul me a few kerb-stones from the yard down the row and I'll stand you a flask and the only laugh you'll get north of the river.
black_night_gravediggers_humor.progress: Vintila works the grave-rows east of the square. Haul kerb-stones from the yard to mend his day's work.
black_night_gravediggers_humor.complete: Ha! Knew you had a strong back and a weak enough head to come north. There — flask's yours, and the laugh was free. You know the worst of it? I like the company. Even the walking ones. At least they show up. Warms an old digger's heart, being needed, even by the likes of them.

objectives.text: Haul kerb-stones to Vintila's grave-rows

black_night_gravediggers_humor.note: Vintila digs graves the dead refuse to keep, and calls it job security. He laughed, and meant it. In the quietest capital on the map, one warm fool laughs on purpose. It is the loudest sound for a league.

black_night_the_camp_at_the_edge.title: The Camp at the City's Edge
black_night_the_camp_at_the_edge.summary: A living family made camp at the north edge and did not come in. Tudor found three bedrolls, cold stew, three sets of footprints leading in and none leading out. He wants someone to walk it before he has to write it down.
black_night_the_camp_at_the_edge.offer: There's a camp at the north edge — a living family, come up the pilgrim road. Three bedrolls, a pot of stew gone cold, and I counted the footprints twice: three sets in, none out. The stew's not been touched. I have to file it, and I can't make the words go right. Walk out and see it yourself. Maybe you'll count differently. I hope you count differently.
black_night_the_camp_at_the_edge.progress: Reach the abandoned family camp at the north edge of the city and see what Tudor cannot bring himself to file.
black_night_the_camp_at_the_edge.complete: You counted the same. Three in, none out, and nothing there is hungry anymore. No blood. No sign of a fight. They just... stopped needing to be anywhere. I'll file it as 'lost on the road.' It's a kinder lie than the truth, and the truth doesn't fit the form. Take the ward. Salt won't stop it. But it'll remind you you're still the kind of thing that can leave.

objectives.label: the family's camp
objectives.text: See the camp at the city's edge

black_night_the_camp_at_the_edge.note: Three sets of footprints led in; none led out. The stew was never eaten. Nothing there was hungry anymore. Tudor filed it as lost on the road — a kinder lie, and the only one the form had room for.

black_night_the_cedar_smell.title: The Cedar That Should Not Be
black_night_the_cedar_smell.summary: The chronicler keeps the record of a city that would rather forget its foundation. He wants the old tomb-stone read and its measure copied true — the one cut in the Buried Queen's own hand, where the scent of cedar still hangs after six hundred years.
black_night_the_cedar_smell.offer: I keep the record no one north of the Vein wants kept. There is a stone near the pit-edge, cut in a hand worn nearly smooth — the Queen's own, they say, from before she was what she is now. I am too old to make the walk and too honest to invent what I cannot read. Go to it. Copy the measure true. And traveler — you will smell cedar there. Do not tell me it is nothing. I have smelled it my whole life and it is not nothing.
black_night_the_cedar_smell.progress: Reach the Queen's tomb-stone at the pit-edge and copy its worn inscription true for the record.
black_night_the_cedar_smell.complete: 'Forty-seven years. I gave them forty-seven just years, and a cedar throne. They gave me a pit.' You smelled it, then. Six hundred years, and the cedar holds. She built a throne of it and they buried her under this. Everything she made after — the Thread, the dark, the whole grim North — she made from that. Rage kept fresh as the cedar. Set it in the record. Someone should remember she was owed better, even if remembering changes nothing.

objectives.label: the Queen's tomb-stone
objectives.text: Copy the worn inscription true
Give Ursu the measure for the record

black_night_the_cedar_smell.note: Forty-seven just years, a cedar throne, and then a pit. The scent holds after six centuries. Everything the North became was built from a grief kept as fresh as that wood. Ursu set it in the record. Nothing changed. He set it down anyway.

black_night_state_property.title: State Property
black_night_state_property.summary: The one living merchant north of the Vein deals in warded thread under an allowance he is not permitted to exceed. His month's spools have gone missing off the requisition floor, and the paperwork will hang him before the theft does.
black_night_state_property.offer: I sell thread-filament by allowance — three spools a month, sign here, do not tell me what for. Iele state property, every strand. This month's ran short off the requisition floor, and the ledger does not care whether I stole it or lost it; it only cares that the count is wrong. A wrong count up here is not a fine. It is a filing. Find where my spools went before the clerk finishes the form.
black_night_state_property.progress: Search the requisition floor by the merchant stalls and recover Radomir's missing spools before the count is filed.
black_night_state_property.complete: Behind the shelf, tangled — no thief, just a shell that dropped inert while carrying them, and nobody logged the drop. The Thread thins, the count goes wrong, and the living merchant hangs for the grave's own accounting. That is the North entire, in three spools. Take a knot for your trouble. I made it myself, off the books. Do not tell the clerk.

objectives.label: the requisition floor
objectives.text: Recover Radomir's missing spools

black_night_state_property.note: A shell dropped inert mid-errand and nobody logged the fall; the living merchant nearly hung for the grave's arithmetic. The Thread thins, the count goes wrong, a person pays. That is the whole North, in three spools of stolen thread.

black_night_names_kept_against_the_dark.title: Names Kept Against the Dark
black_night_names_kept_against_the_dark.summary: Among the living of Black Night there is a quiet rite: to speak the true name of a shell you knew before it dropped, so that some thread of the person is kept. Zaharia has a list of names and no courage left. She asks you to carry the speaking for her.
black_night_names_kept_against_the_dark.offer: Before they were shells, some of them were ours. My brother among them. There's a thing the living do here, quiet, that the Council frowns on and cannot forbid: you go to the ones you knew and you say their true name over them, out loud, so a thread of who they were is kept. I have the list. I have not the courage — my voice breaks at his and then I've said nothing at all. Go to the market shells. Say the names for me. Say them like you mean them.
black_night_names_kept_against_the_dark.progress: Reach the still shells in the market and speak the names on Zaharia's list aloud, so some thread of them is kept.
black_night_names_kept_against_the_dark.complete: You said them all. Even his. Thank you — I heard, from here, and it was better than my silence. They didn't answer. They never do. But keeping isn't for them, is it. It's for us, so we don't become the kind of people who let a person go quiet without a word. Take the cord. It's tied on his thread. He'd want a stubborn traveler to carry it.

objectives.label: the still market shells
objectives.text: Speak the kept names aloud over the shells
Return the list to Zaharia

black_night_names_kept_against_the_dark.note: The shells did not answer their names, as they never do. Keeping is not for the dead. It is so the living do not become the kind who let a person go silent unremarked. Zaharia heard, from a distance, and it was better than her silence.

black_night_the_revenant_road.title: What Walks the Revenant Road
black_night_the_revenant_road.summary: Black Night Revenants — shells the Thread should hold and no longer does — have started walking the pit road with purpose, all in the same direction. The captain wants them put down and their bearing marked. He suspects the bearing matters more than the killing.
black_night_the_revenant_road.offer: My dead watch will not touch them, and now I know why: the Revenants walk on the same Thread. But something's gone wrong in them — they don't drift, they march, and they all march the one way. Toward the pit. Put down eight on the pit road. And watch which way the ninth faces when it falls. I have a cold feeling the direction is the whole message.
black_night_the_revenant_road.progress: Break the Black Night Revenants marching the pit road, and mark the bearing they hold.
black_night_the_revenant_road.complete: Eight down, and every one of them pointed the same way falling — down, toward the pit, like iron filings that found their lodestone. They aren't wandering. They're being drawn. Something under this city has started calling its own back to it, and the Thread that was meant to be its cage is carrying the summons. I'll say none of that in my report. Take the blade. You've the stomach the report won't have.

objectives.text: Break the Revenants on the pit road

objectives.label: the pit road
objectives.text: Mark the bearing the fallen hold

black_night_the_revenant_road.note: The Revenants fell all pointing one way — toward the pit, like filings to a lodestone. They are not wandering; they are being called back. The Thread built to cage the stone now carries its summons. Aurel put none of this in his report.

black_night_a_reading_of_the_pit.title: A Reading of the Pit
black_night_a_reading_of_the_pit.summary: The Councillor needs the pit-edge read under alchemical sight, and needs a living reader who understands the grammar of the colors — and understands that the wrong color, seen and reported, is how the first ones died. This is the reading the Council fears to take itself.
black_night_a_reading_of_the_pit.offer: You have carried the small errands well, and now I ask the one no shell can do and no living Councillor will. Take alchemical sight to the pit-edge and read what the stone shows. Blue is the dead. Violet is the deep. Green is a stone waking. And if it reads orange — shifting orange — you say nothing, you write nothing, you look at your boots and you walk out. Reading the orange is how the pilgrims began to listen. I must know the color. I cannot afford to understand it. Neither can you.
black_night_a_reading_of_the_pit.progress: Reach the pit-edge and take a reading under alchemical sight. Note the color. Do not linger to understand it.
black_night_a_reading_of_the_pit.complete: You looked away in time. Good. Then you already know, without my saying, and I will not make either of us say it. It reads orange, and shifting — the signal, writing itself, in the one place we swore it never would. The stone is not sealed. It is broadcasting, and the cage sings along. Say this to no one but the Accord, and say it fast, before the North that guards it learns what it has been guarding. Take the veil. Wear it out of here. Do not look back at the light.

objectives.label: the pit-edge
objectives.text: Read the pit-edge, then look away
Bring Danciu the color, not the meaning

black_night_a_reading_of_the_pit.note: The pit reads shifting orange — the signal writing itself, in the one place the North swore it never would. The stone is not sealed; it is broadcasting, and the cage sings along. Danciu forbade understanding it. That refusal is the only wall left standing.

black_night_the_forgemaster_of_the_dead.title: The Forge That Does Not Ring
black_night_the_forgemaster_of_the_dead.summary: The living smith forges cold-iron the dead cannot bear to touch — the one metal that resists the Thread. His stock of cold-iron shard is stolen by shells who cannot say why they took it. He wants the cache found before the Council notices its own dead disobeying.
black_night_the_forgemaster_of_the_dead.offer: I forge cold-iron, the one metal the dead can't abide — it never rings, and the Thread can't sing down it. Good business, in theory. In practice, my stock keeps walking off in dead hands. Shells, taking what should burn them to hold, and dropping it in a heap they can't explain because they've no will to explain with. Find the cache. Quiet-like. If the Council learns its own puppets are hoarding the one thing that cuts their strings, someone gets filed, and it won't be a puppet.
black_night_the_forgemaster_of_the_dead.progress: Track the cold-iron the shells have hoarded to a cache in the smith-quarter cellars.
black_night_the_forgemaster_of_the_dead.complete: There it is — a heap of cold-iron, stacked almost neat, in a cellar no living hand keeps. Stacked. By things with no will to stack. Something is reaching through the Thread and telling them to gather the one metal that can cut it — gathering it up where it can be watched, or spent. I don't like a puppet that hoards its own scissors. Take the striker. And Pavel says nothing to the Council. Pavel likes his neck unfiled.

objectives.label: the smith-quarter cellars
objectives.text: Find the shells' cold-iron cache

black_night_the_forgemaster_of_the_dead.note: The shells hoarded cold-iron — the one metal that cuts the Thread — stacked neat by hands with no will to stack. Something reaches through the leash to gather up its own scissors. Pavel told the Council nothing. He is fond of his unfiled neck.

black_night_the_apprentice_who_read.title: The Apprentice Who Read
black_night_the_apprentice_who_read.summary: Cornel's living apprentice copied a pit-edge inscription 'to study the cure' and has begun to tilt his head. Cornel cannot bring himself to do what must be done. He asks you to reach the boy, and to try the counteragent while it can still mean anything.
black_night_the_apprentice_who_read.offer: My apprentice — Emilian's boy, sharp, too sharp — he copied a mark from the pit-edge, to help me study the entrancement. To help. He thought he was helping. Now he stands at my back window and tilts his head, and each hour the tilt goes deeper. I have brewed the counteragent strong as it will go. I cannot walk to him. My legs won't. Go to the boy. Give it to him. And if it's too late — if he's more listening than lad — you'll know, and I am asking you to be kinder than I could be.
black_night_the_apprentice_who_read.progress: Reach Cornel's apprentice at the back window and give him the counteragent before the listening takes him.
black_night_the_apprentice_who_read.complete: He drank it. He came back — for an afternoon, he came back, and he wept, and he understood exactly what he'd done to himself, which was the cruelest gift the cure gives. By dusk he tilted again. I've sent him south under guard, to the Accord's people, who study these things without loving them. That's the mercy left: to hand what you love to strangers who won't grieve it into a worse mistake. Take the draught. Read before you copy. Always read the color first.

objectives.label: the apothecary's back window
objectives.text: Reach the apprentice with the counteragent
Tell Cornel what became of the boy

black_night_the_apprentice_who_read.note: The boy copied a mark to help, and comprehension began its slow erasure. The counteragent gave him one afternoon to understand exactly what he had done — the cruelest gift it gives. Cornel handed what he loved to strangers who would not grieve it into a worse mercy.

black_night_a_pardon_that_will_not_come.title: A Pardon That Will Not Come
black_night_a_pardon_that_will_not_come.summary: Emilian's boy was taken by the listening while apprenticed to Cornel, and Emilian will not accept that no crime was done and no one can be blamed. He wants the Council's clerk petitioned for a pardon — for a boy who broke no law but curiosity.
black_night_a_pardon_that_will_not_come.offer: They tell me my son did nothing wrong and that is somehow worse. He read a mark. He was learning a cure. And now he tilts his head at nothing and they've sent him off to be studied by the Accord like a specimen in salt. There must be a pardon. A paper. Something a father can hold. Take my petition to the Council's clerk. Make them stamp something. I don't care what it says. I care that my hands aren't empty when I go home to his mother.
black_night_a_pardon_that_will_not_come.progress: Carry Emilian's petition to the Council clerk and ask for a pardon for a boy who broke only curiosity.
black_night_a_pardon_that_will_not_come.complete: Stamped. 'No offence recorded; no pardon required.' That's what they gave me. No offence — as if the not-having-a-crime undoes the losing of him. But it's paper, and it's stamped, and I'll set it on the shelf where his mother can see I tried. You did what could be done, which was nothing shaped like something. Take this. It was his warding-charm. He won't need it where the salt keeps him now.

objectives.text: Petition the Council clerk for a pardon

black_night_a_pardon_that_will_not_come.note: The clerk stamped it: no offence recorded, no pardon required. As if having committed no crime undid the loss of the boy. Emilian set the paper where his wife could see he tried. It was the only shape nothing could be made to take.

black_night_the_watch_fires_of_the_livin.title: The Watch-Fires of the Living
black_night_the_watch_fires_of_the_livin.summary: The living quarter keeps small fires burning at its bounds each night — not for warmth, but because the Pale-Stalkers keep to the dark and the fires mark the edge of where the living may still be found. Fane needs them fed and the takers of the dark cut back nightly.
black_night_the_watch_fires_of_the_livin.offer: Same ask as every night, friend. The living quarter burns a ring of little fires at its edge — not for warmth, this cold laughs at warmth, but because the pale things keep to the dark and a fire says here, at least, is still the living's ground. The fires want feeding and the dark wants thinning. Cut the stalkers back off the fire-line. It's never finished. That's the job. That's the North.
black_night_the_watch_fires_of_the_livin.progress: Feed the watch-fires along the living quarter's edge and cut back the Pale-Stalkers that gather at the dark.
black_night_the_watch_fires_of_the_livin.complete: Ring's holding. Dark's thinner. It'll be thick again by the second bell and we'll do it again tomorrow, and the day after, until one of us doesn't. That's not despair, mind — that's just the shape of it. The living hold a line every night in a city that stopped needing lines. Somebody has to. Here's your due. Sleep near a fire.

objectives.text: Cut the Pale-Stalkers back off the fire-line

objectives.label: the fire-line
objectives.text: Feed the watch-fires at the quarter's edge

black_night_the_watch_fires_of_the_livin.note: Each night the living feed a ring of small fires at their quarter's edge, and each night the dark comes back thick by the second bell. Fane calls it not despair but the shape of the thing. Somebody holds the line. Somebody always has.

black_night_the_councils_errand.title: The Council's Quiet Errand
black_night_the_councils_errand.summary: The Council clerk files the North's endless watch-readings, and the deep quarter must be surveyed fresh each day for shells that have dropped where the Thread failed — an inventory of the leash's small daily failures.
black_night_the_councils_errand.offer: The Council keeps a count, and the count keeps me. Each day a shell or two drops in the deep quarter where the Thread wears thin, and each drop must be walked, marked, and filed before dusk, or the tally's wrong and a wrong tally is a long night of forms. No shell will do it — they can't note their own falling. Walk the deep quarter. Mark the day's dropped. Bring me the count. Same tomorrow.
black_night_the_councils_errand.progress: Survey the deep quarter for the day's dropped shells and bring the clerk an honest count.
black_night_the_councils_errand.complete: Four today. More than yesterday, fewer than I fear. The Council reads these tallies and calls them stable, because a rising line is a thing they'd have to act on, and acting means admitting the leash is fraying. So the number stays 'stable' no matter what I write. I write it true anyway. It's the one honest column in the whole Archive-to-be. Your due — and my thanks, which the form has no line for.

objectives.label: the deep quarter
objectives.text: Mark the day's dropped shells for the count
Bring the clerk the day's honest tally

black_night_the_councils_errand.note: Each day the clerk counts the shells the Thread lets fall, and each day the Council calls the rising number stable — because a rising line would demand an answer. He files it true regardless. It is the one honest column in the Archive the North will become.

black_night_the_blackglass_seer.title: The Blackglass Seer
black_night_the_blackglass_seer.summary: A Strigoi glass-trader down from Blestem left a blackglass razor as surety on a debt and vanished north toward the pit. Radomir wants the debt-glass recovered — and wants to know what a Blestem spy was doing walking willingly toward the one thing his city trades in early warnings about.
black_night_the_blackglass_seer.offer: A glass-trader out of Blestem left me a blackglass razor against a debt — good glass, takes an edge once and never needs a second — then walked north. Toward the pit. A Strigoi, walking toward the stone on purpose, which no Strigoi does, because they fear it worse than we do. I want my surety back and I want to know what turned a Blestem man into a pilgrim. Follow his road to the pit-approach. Bring me the glass, and whatever's left of the man.
black_night_the_blackglass_seer.progress: Follow the Strigoi trader's road to the pit-approach and recover the blackglass razor left as surety.
black_night_the_blackglass_seer.complete: The razor, yes — dropped clean, no blood on it. And the man? Standing at the approach, head tilted, listening, same as the pilgrims. A six-hundred-year-old spy who feared this stone above all things, and it called him north like a farm-boy anyway. Blestem will want to know their best noticing-man got noticed by the thing itself. Or they'll want it buried. Take the razor. You cut better with it than he ever will again.

objectives.label: the pit-approach
objectives.text: Recover the surety and find the trader

black_night_the_blackglass_seer.note: The Strigoi trader — six centuries of feared, careful noticing — was called north like a farm-boy and now stands listening at the pit-approach. His razor lay dropped and unbloodied. The stone reaches even the ones who fear it most. Especially those.

black_night_the_thing_that_digs.title: The Thing That Digs
black_night_the_thing_that_digs.summary: A four-clawed thing has been pulling the living from the dark at the pit's approach — the stone's advance-work made flesh, digging toward something. Sava has lost too many to it. He wants it ended, and he wants a living witness to what it was digging for.
black_night_the_thing_that_digs.offer: There's a thing at the pit-approach that isn't Iele and isn't Pale — four claws, and it pulls the throat out of whatever it catches, then goes back to digging. It's been at the living. Digging toward something down there like it's the stone's own hands, sent up ahead to make ready. I've sent my dead watch and they won't cross to it — even the shells know their better. Put down what haunts the approach. And look in the hole it's dug. I need to know what it thinks is coming.
black_night_the_thing_that_digs.progress: Reach the pit-approach, put down the digging herald's brood, and look into what it has been digging toward.
black_night_the_thing_that_digs.complete: Dead, and the hole it dug — you saw. Not down. Up. It wasn't digging to reach the stone. It was clearing a path for the stone to reach the surface. Advance-work, like a road crew before a procession, and the procession is the thing under the city waking all the way up. I'll tell the captain. He'll bury the telling, same as I would. Take the ward — the good one, the Thinning-veil. If anything up here has earned it, it's the one who looked in the hole.

objectives.text: Put down what haunts the pit-approach

objectives.label: the dug hole
objectives.text: Look into what the thing was digging toward

black_night_the_thing_that_digs.note: The digging herald was not tunnelling down to the stone — it was clearing a path up, for the stone to reach the surface. Advance-work, a road crew before a procession, and the procession is the thing under the city coming all the way awake.

black_night_a_word_for_a_word.title: A Word for a Word
black_night_a_word_for_a_word.summary: Radovan — the most dangerous of the Six — has been seen at the pit-edge, alone, at the hour the readings run orange. The Councillor wants him watched by a living eye that can be denied, and warns that Radovan already knows more than a servant of a sealed stone should.
black_night_a_word_for_a_word.offer: I told you Radovan knows things a day ahead. I have learned where he learns them. He goes to the pit-edge alone, at the third bell, when even the shells keep away — and he comes back with knowledge the rest of the Six only guess at. I need to know whether he reads the stone or the stone reads him. Watch him from the dark of the near arch. Do not be seen. And whatever he says at the edge — do not repeat it to yourself later. That is how it gets in.
black_night_a_word_for_a_word.progress: Reach the near arch above the pit-edge and watch Radovan's midnight reading unseen. Report only to Danciu.
black_night_a_word_for_a_word.complete: He spoke to it. Not a reading — a conversation, his half of one, and he waited for answers in the pauses like a man who's had them before. He is not guarding the stone. He is negotiating with it, and his terms do not include Lilith, or the Council, or the Accord surviving the bargain. I feared a traitor and found something worse: a Councillor who thinks he can out-clerk the ledger itself. Say this to the Accord. Only the Accord. And burn the way here out of your memory, if you can. Take the veil. Wear it home.

objectives.label: the near arch
objectives.text: Watch Radovan's reading from the dark
Report what Radovan does to Danciu alone

black_night_a_word_for_a_word.note: Radovan does not guard the stone; he negotiates with it, his terms excluding Lilith, the Council, and the Accord's survival both. A Councillor who believes he can out-clerk the ledger itself. Danciu feared a traitor and found something worse: an ambitious man the stone is happy to let win.

black_night_cold_stew.title: Cold Stew
black_night_cold_stew.summary: Oprea sets a full table each night for the living pilgrims who wander in — fewer come every week, and lately the bowls go cold untouched because those who arrive have already stopped being hungry. He wants to know how many of the missing simply walked to the pit.
black_night_cold_stew.offer: I set the long table every night — bread, stew, a warm seat for any living soul that finds the door. Used to fill it. Now the bowls go cold because the ones who come in have already stopped being hungry; they sit, they tilt their heads, they don't lift the spoon. I keep a tally of who came and never left the city living. Walk the pilgrim road from my door to the pit-approach and mark where the last of them stopped. I want to know if they walked there. I need to know they walked.
black_night_cold_stew.progress: Walk the pilgrim road from the inn to the pit-approach and mark where the vanished pilgrims last stopped.
black_night_cold_stew.complete: They walked. Every marker on your map, a person who sat at my table, then rose in the night and walked to the edge on their own two feet. It shouldn't comfort me. It does, a little — that they weren't dragged, that the last thing they did was choose, even if the choosing was the stone's whisper wearing their want. I'll keep setting the table. Warm stew's a small stubborn thing, but small stubborn things are what we've got. Take a bowl before you go. You're still hungry. Stay that way.

objectives.label: the pilgrim road
objectives.text: Mark where the vanished pilgrims last stopped
Bring Oprea word of the road

black_night_cold_stew.note: The pilgrims walked to the pit on their own two feet — not dragged, but choosing, the stone's whisper wearing their own want. Oprea takes a bitter comfort in it and keeps setting the long table. A warm bowl is a small stubborn thing. Small stubborn things are what the living have.

black_night_the_readers_grief.title: The Reader's Grief
black_night_the_readers_grief.summary: The chronicler kept a book of the North's true history and now fears his own record is a hook — that to write clearly of the stone is to seed another transmission. He wants the dangerous pages carried out of the city to the Accord, unread, before curiosity finds them.
black_night_the_readers_grief.offer: I have spent my life writing the North true, and I have come to a terrible thought: that a clear account of the stone is itself a door. That the pilgrims who 'listen' are only reading, and my honest book is the plainest reading in the city. I cannot burn it — burning the truth is the North's oldest sin. But I can send the worst pages south, to the Accord, who keep dangerous knowledge behind salt and never let it mean anything. Carry them. Do not read them. Look at your boots the whole road out.
black_night_the_readers_grief.progress: Carry the chronicler's dangerous pages from his desk to the south gate, and do not read them on the way.
black_night_the_readers_grief.complete: You didn't read them. I can see it in you — the ones who read go quiet at the eyes, and yours are still asking questions. Thank you. They're the Accord's problem now, and they're built for problems that bite the hand that studies them. Keep the lens. It's ground to correct for the truth, they say — which is a jeweler's joke and a warning both. See clearly, traveler. Just never clearly enough to understand. That's the whole art of surviving the North.

objectives.label: the south gate
objectives.text: Carry the pages out, unread
Confirm to Ursu the pages went unread

black_night_the_readers_grief.note: The chronicler feared his own honest record was a hook, the pilgrims' listening only a plainer reading of what he wrote. He would not burn the truth and could not keep it, so he sent the worst pages south to be kept behind salt. To survive the North: see clearly, never clearly enough to understand.

black_night_the_wells_gone_warm.title: Where Even the Wells Went Quiet
black_night_the_wells_gone_warm.summary: The living quarter's wells have started running warm in a city where nothing is warm — the oldest symptom, come north at last. Vintila the gravedigger, who jokes at everything, cannot joke at this. He wants them tested and warded before the others notice.
black_night_the_wells_gone_warm.offer: I make light of most things — dead that won't stay dug, the whole grim circus of the place. But I can't laugh at this one. The living quarter's wells have gone warm. Warm, traveler, in Black Night, where the air itself is bitter and glad of it. My old mother down in Vetka used to say when the ground warms, the rest follows — copper, dead bread, all of it. Test the three wells. Ward them. And for once, don't tell me a joke back. I've none left for this.
black_night_the_wells_gone_warm.progress: Test and ward the three wells of the living quarter, which have begun — impossibly — to run warm.
black_night_the_wells_gone_warm.complete: All three, warm. The first symptom, come north to the coldest city on the map. You know what that means as well as I do: the ground's made up its mind here too, same as everywhere the stone reaches, and Black Night was supposed to be the lid. If the lid's warming... no. I said no joke and I meant it. Ward's set. Take the knot. And Vintila's going to go dig something, because digging's the only thing that's ever made sense, and it's about to make a great deal more.

objectives.label: the living quarter's wells
objectives.text: Test and ward the three warm wells
Ward each well with a salt-line

black_night_the_wells_gone_warm.note: The wells run warm in the coldest city on the map — the first symptom, come north to the lid that was meant to hold it down. Vintila, who jokes at everything, had no joke for this. The ground has made up its mind here too. Black Night was supposed to be where it could not.

black_night_the_captains_last_living_man.title: The Captain's Last Living Man
black_night_the_captains_last_living_man.summary: Aurel's last living sergeant went into the deep quarter to trace a failing thread and did not come back. The captain cannot send the dead after the living. He asks you to find the man — and to be honest about which he is when you do.
black_night_the_captains_last_living_man.offer: Every man under me is dead but one, and that one — my sergeant, Serban's boy — walked into the deep quarter yesterday to trace a thread gone thin, and hasn't walked out. I can't send shells; they'll bring me back a body and call the errand done whether he's in it or not. Go find him. And when you do, look close, and be honest with me and yourself about whether the man came back or just the shape of him. I'd rather a hard truth than a kind shell wearing his face.
black_night_the_captains_last_living_man.progress: Search the deep quarter for Aurel's missing sergeant, and look closely at what you find.
black_night_the_captains_last_living_man.complete: You found him breathing. Breathing, and himself — thread-scared and half-frozen but his eyes still asking questions, still living behind them. I've had a hard year of hoping and being wrong, and you've handed me the first right hope in it. He'll not go back down there; I'll chain him to a warm fire first. Take the cord — I tie one for each of mine, and I'll not need his, thank every cold saint. Bring the living home when you can, traveler. Up here, it's the whole of the work.

objectives.label: the deep quarter
objectives.text: Find Aurel's missing sergeant
Tell Aurel whether the man came back

black_night_the_captains_last_living_man.note: The sergeant came back breathing and himself — the first right hope in Aurel's hard year of being wrong. He will not go down again; the captain will chain him to a warm fire first. Bringing the living home is, in Black Night, the whole of the work.

black_night_the_last_warm_room.title: The Last Warm Room
black_night_the_last_warm_room.summary: With the wells gone warm and the pilgrims gone quiet, Oprea has decided to close the inn and lead the last of the living south while a road still lets them. He asks you to walk the quarter one last time and gather the ones who can still choose to leave.
black_night_the_last_warm_room.offer: I've kept the last warm room north of the Vein for eleven winters, and tonight I close it. The wells are warm, the pilgrims don't eat, and a man who keeps a light too long starts to think the dark he's holding it against is the natural state of things. It isn't. So I'm taking the living south while the road's still a road. Walk the quarter. Find the ones who can still choose their own feet. Bring them to my door. We leave at the third bell, together, or we don't leave at all.
black_night_the_last_warm_room.progress: Walk the living quarter and gather those who can still choose to leave, then bring them to the inn door before the third bell.
black_night_the_last_warm_room.complete: Nine. Nine still had feet that were theirs and the will to use them. Not the number I hoped. More than I feared. We'll walk south together, nine and a stubborn innkeeper and a traveler with warm hands, and behind us the last warm room goes dark by choice instead of by snuffing — there's a dignity in that the lantern never let me have. Take the ward. Wear it out with us. Black Night keeps the dead well enough. It was never any place for the living to stay.

objectives.label: the living quarter
objectives.text: Gather the living who can still choose to go
Bring the last of the living to Oprea's door

black_night_the_last_warm_room.note: Oprea closed the last warm room north of the Vein and led nine living souls south while a road still let them. The room went dark by choice, not by snuffing — a dignity the long vigil never allowed. Black Night keeps the dead well. It was never a place for the living to stay.

black_night_the_word_kept_back.title: The Word Kept Back
black_night_the_word_kept_back.summary: The Councillor, having sent his warnings south, prepares the North for what the Accord may decide — and asks one last errand: to carry a sealed thread-token to the road north, a message the North dares not speak aloud, only bind.
black_night_the_word_kept_back.offer: I have said all I dare to the Accord, and now I wait to learn whether saying it was wisdom or the last mistake of a Council of ghosts. One thing remains. There is a message the North must send Gravemark — a warning we dare not write, for a written thing can be read, and reading is how it spreads. So we bound it, into a thread-token, a knot only the lodge-warden's hands can untie. Carry it to the north road, toward the Gravemark lodge. Do not try to read the knot. There is nothing in it a living mind should hold and keep its own counsel.
black_night_the_word_kept_back.progress: Carry the sealed thread-token to the north road toward the Gravemark lodge. Do not attempt to untie or read the knot.
black_night_the_word_kept_back.complete: You carried it whole and untied — I can tell, for you still meet my eye. Good. It is Gravemark's burden now, and mine is only to have sent it. Whatever the Accord decides, whatever Radovan does at his edge in the dark, the North has done the one thing the North was ever good for: it noticed, it warned, and it did not read the warning aloud. Go on, then. The road north is worse than this city, and this city is the worst place I know. Take the token's twin. It's a kept name. Choose whose, when you're ready. Choose out loud.

objectives.label: the north road to Gravemark
objectives.text: Bear the sealed token toward the north road
Confirm to Danciu the knot went untied

black_night_the_word_kept_back.note: The North bound its warning into a knot rather than write it, for a written thing can be read and reading is how it spreads. Danciu sent it north and kept his counsel. The North did the one thing it was ever good for: it noticed, it warned, and it did not read the warning aloud.

whisper_passes_the_last_warm_door.title: The Last Warm Door
whisper_passes_the_last_warm_door.summary: Ruxandra keeps the only lit hearth on the pass road, and lately more travelers walk in than walk out. She wants her woodpile stacked before the fog settles for the night.
whisper_passes_the_last_warm_door.offer: This is the last warm door between the grave-country and the ridges, and folk have started noticing which direction they don't come back from. My woodpile's low and my back's older than the pass. Split me a stack and there's stew and a bed in it -- warm ones, while they last.
whisper_passes_the_last_warm_door.progress: The wood's in the lean-to by the eastern wall of the inn yard. Stack it high; the fog comes early up here.
whisper_passes_the_last_warm_door.complete: Would you look at that -- a full pile and the smoke going straight up for once. Sit. Eat. There's not much warm left in these passes, and I mean to keep this door counted among it as long as I'm able.

objectives.label: the inn woodpile
objectives.text: Stack Ruxandra's woodpile

whisper_passes_the_last_warm_door.note: The woodpile stands full and the door stays warm. Ruxandra counts the travelers in and, quieter, counts them out. The second number is the smaller one.

whisper_passes_what_the_wind_carries.title: What the Wind Carries
whisper_passes_what_the_wind_carries.summary: Zamfira keeps the pass-book, and she has stopped writing down what the wind says at the high notch. She wants a witness to stand where the sound gathers and tell her it is only wind.
whisper_passes_what_the_wind_carries.offer: Every pass has a note it sings when the wind comes through -- I've kept this book forty years and I know the passes' voices like my own kin. This one changed its song a season back. I need a steadier pair of ears than mine. Walk up to the high notch, stand in it, and come tell me it's only wind.
whisper_passes_what_the_wind_carries.progress: The high notch is up the north spur, where the walls narrow and the fog stands to your shoulders. Stand in it. Listen. Then come back to me.
whisper_passes_what_the_wind_carries.complete: You waited before you answered. Good -- that's the right instinct, and the reason I asked you and not one of the eager young guards. It's not only wind. We both know it. I'll write it in the book plainly, and I'll write your name beside it, so someone remembers who stood there and kept their own thoughts.

objectives.label: the high notch
objectives.text: Stand in the high notch and listen
Bring word back to Zamfira

whisper_passes_what_the_wind_carries.note: Zamfira wrote it plainly in the pass-book: the high notch no longer sings the wind's old note. She did not write what note it sings instead. Some things you keep out of a book on purpose.

whisper_passes_stalkers_on_the_high_road.title: Stalkers on the High Road
whisper_passes_stalkers_on_the_high_road.summary: The cliff-stalkers have grown bold enough to take travelers off the road in daylight. Lenuta wants the pack that works the switchbacks broken before the caravans stop coming through at all.
whisper_passes_stalkers_on_the_high_road.offer: Time was the stalkers kept to the crags and took what the fog gave them. Now they come down to the road itself, in the grey of noon, and pull folk off the switchbacks screaming. I've lost two carters this month. Break the pack that works the lower stair -- before the caravans decide the pass isn't worth the toll.
whisper_passes_stalkers_on_the_high_road.progress: The stalkers den along the lower switchbacks, where the road doubles back on itself. Thin them -- eight should scatter the rest into the crags.
whisper_passes_stalkers_on_the_high_road.complete: Eight down and the switchbacks quiet. The carters might come back now, for a season anyway. Take this -- a striker off a caravan we couldn't save the first time. Might as well go to someone who came back.

objectives.text: Break the stalker pack on the switchbacks

whisper_passes_stalkers_on_the_high_road.note: The switchbacks are quiet, for a season. Lenuta counts the seasons the way a woman counts coin she knows is running out. She has stopped saying which season she thinks is the last.

whisper_passes_the_pilgrim_camp.title: The Pilgrim Camp
whisper_passes_the_pilgrim_camp.summary: A pilgrim camp on the north spur has gone silent -- three fires, cold; footprints going in, none coming out. Otilia will not send her own people up. She sends you, and asks that whatever you find, you do not read what they left.
whisper_passes_the_pilgrim_camp.offer: There's a camp on the north spur -- pilgrims bound east for the ridges, the way they always come through, drawn like the fog draws. It's been three days silent. I sent Anca's boy up to look and he came back grey and won't speak of it. Go up. Count them. And whatever's written up there -- and there's always something written -- you leave it face-down. Promise me that.
whisper_passes_the_pilgrim_camp.progress: The camp sits on the north spur, above the switchbacks. Reach it, take account of what's there -- and keep your eyes on your boots when it comes to the writing.
whisper_passes_the_pilgrim_camp.complete: Three bedrolls, cold stew, footprints in and none out, and a letter left folded on a stone -- addressed, you said, to whoever reads this. You didn't read it. You did the hard thing and I won't forget it. There'll be someone up there who wants me to send it on. There always is. I'll not.

objectives.label: the silent pilgrim camp
objectives.text: Take account of the pilgrim camp
Report to Otilia -- unread

whisper_passes_the_pilgrim_camp.note: Three bedrolls, cold stew, footprints going in and none coming out. A letter addressed to whoever reads this, left folded on a stone. You left it face-down. The pass keeps its own account; you refused to add your name to it.

whisper_passes_the_choir_in_the_gallery.title: The Choir in the Gallery
whisper_passes_the_choir_in_the_gallery.summary: The sealed watch-gallery under the high notch is full of the Aligned -- pilgrims who finished listening, standing in their last postures under a shell of packed grey dust. Zamfira wants the door opened and the choir put down before the hum spreads to the living on the road.
whisper_passes_the_choir_in_the_gallery.offer: Under the high notch there's an old watch-gallery, sealed since the fog got bad -- a governor bricked it up to keep his people safe inside. Safe. I've stood at that wall and heard them humming through the stone, all together, one note. That's not safe. That's a tomb that kept its tenants standing. Open it. Break the shells before their song reaches the road, and forgive me for asking it of anyone.
whisper_passes_the_choir_in_the_gallery.progress: The gallery door is under the high notch, on the north spur. Break the sealed wall, and put down the choir inside before the hum takes hold.
whisper_passes_the_choir_in_the_gallery.complete: You came back walking straight and thinking your own thoughts, which is more than the last man who went in. You broke the shells -- and I know what you'll not say, that breaking the shell freed the hum, and it was worse for a moment than the standing was. It's quiet now. The door stays open from here. Better a cold room than a sealed one. We learned that too late for them.

objectives.label: the sealed watch-gallery
objectives.text: Open the sealed watch-gallery
Break the shells of the Aligned

whisper_passes_the_choir_in_the_gallery.note: A governor bricked a room shut to keep his people safe, and the safe became a shell, and the shell became a choir. The gallery stands open now, and cold, and empty. Zamfira keeps its door propped with a stone. Some doors you never seal again.

whisper_passes_the_toll_that_wasnt.title: The Toll That Wasn't
whisper_passes_the_toll_that_wasnt.summary: A forged toll-receipt is circulating on the pass, waving caravans through unwatched. Mira wants to know whose hand made it -- and who benefits from carts crossing without being counted.
whisper_passes_the_toll_that_wasnt.offer: Someone's passing a forged receipt through my stall -- good work, near enough to fool the guards, and every cart that carries one crosses the pass without a soul writing it down. Now who wants carts crossing uncounted, that's the question, and it's not a smuggler's question. Bring me one of the forgeries. I know every honest chisel on this road; I'll know the dishonest one too.
whisper_passes_the_toll_that_wasnt.progress: The forgeries move with the eastbound carters on the lower road. Get your hands on one and bring it to my stall.
whisper_passes_the_toll_that_wasnt.complete: Ah. This ink, this fold -- that's a Blestem hand, or a hand taught by Blestem. They don't want the toll-coin. They want the road going east to leave no record of who crossed it. A thing that crosses unwritten can't be traced, and a thing that can't be traced was never here. Keep this second forgery. You never know when you'll want to be a cart that was never counted.

objectives.text: Recover a forged toll-receipt
Bring the forgery to Mira

whisper_passes_the_toll_that_wasnt.note: The forgery was a Blestem hand. Someone wants the eastbound road to keep no record of who crosses it. Mira filed the thought where she files all dangerous ones -- unwritten, in her own head, where no ledger can be seized.

whisper_passes_salt_for_the_crossings.title: Salt for the Crossings
whisper_passes_salt_for_the_crossings.summary: Anca lays salt-lines at every doorway on the pass, the old warding her grandmother swore by. Her pouches have run dry, and the traders won't climb this high. She needs the fixings gathered so the lines can hold.
whisper_passes_salt_for_the_crossings.offer: My grandmother laid salt at every threshold in these passes, and folk laughed at her right up until the year they didn't. I keep the lines now -- doorways, well-mouths, the graves. But I'm out of the makings and no trader'll climb this far in the fog. Help me gather what a pouch wants, and I'll teach you the laying. Costs nothing to keep a line. Costs everything not to.
whisper_passes_salt_for_the_crossings.progress: The makings are up along the crag paths where the salt-crust gathers. Bring back enough for a proper pouch, and I'll show you the old way of it.
whisper_passes_salt_for_the_crossings.complete: There now -- enough salt for a season of thresholds. It's a small thing, a white line at a door. My grandmother said the small things are the only ones you get to choose. Take a pouch of your own. Lay a line wherever you sleep on this road. It won't stop the worst of it. But it'll make the worst of it knock first.

objectives.text: Gather the makings for salt-lines

whisper_passes_salt_for_the_crossings.note: Anca lays a white line at every threshold on the pass, the way her grandmother taught her. It stops nothing. It only makes the worst of it knock first. On this road, that is counted a mercy, and she means to keep it counted.

whisper_passes_the_bricked_governor.title: The Bricked Governor
whisper_passes_the_bricked_governor.summary: The governor who sealed the watch-gallery left a marked map behind, its border scrawled with which rooms he closed and in what order. Zamfira wants it recovered from his old station before the pass forgets which doors are tombs.
whisper_passes_the_bricked_governor.offer: The man who bricked that gallery kept a map -- I've seen it once, years back, before he stopped coming down to the inn. He marked the border of it with every room he sealed and the day he sealed it, like a man keeping a household ledger of his own mercy-killings. His old watch-station's up the east spur, empty now. Bring me that map. If we know which doors he shut, we know which ones still have someone standing behind them.
whisper_passes_the_bricked_governor.progress: The governor's watch-station is on the east spur, above the eastbound road. Search it for the marked map.
whisper_passes_the_bricked_governor.complete: This is it. Look at the border -- he numbered them. Eleven rooms, dated, each one a door he shut on people he'd sworn to keep. And this last line, undated: 'and the last for myself, when the hum comes.' We never found his body. Now I know why. He's behind a door of his own, somewhere on this pass, and he numbered it so no one would open it. I'll keep the map. I'll not open that one.

objectives.text: Recover the governor's marked map
Bring the map to Zamfira

whisper_passes_the_bricked_governor.note: The governor numbered eleven doors he sealed on the people he swore to keep, and a twelfth, undated, for himself, for when the hum came. Zamfira keeps the map. She has marked the twelfth door with a stroke that means: leave this one shut.

whisper_passes_iron_for_the_ridge.title: Iron for the Ridge
whisper_passes_iron_for_the_ridge.summary: Ecaterina's forge runs on bog-iron dragged up from the low country, and the last mule-train never arrived. She needs the raw iron gathered from the crag-paths so the pass's blades don't go soft.
whisper_passes_iron_for_the_ridge.offer: A forge this high lives on what gets carried up to it, and the last iron-train's a week overdue on a road that's been eating trains lately. I've bar-stock for a fortnight, no more. There's raw bog-iron scattered in the crag-scree where old carts spilled it -- climb up and gather what you can. A soft blade on this pass is a dead carter, and I'll not shoe my conscience with that.
whisper_passes_iron_for_the_ridge.progress: The spilled bog-iron lies in the crag-scree above the switchbacks, where the old carts came to grief. Gather what the forge needs.
whisper_passes_iron_for_the_ridge.complete: Good iron, most of it, under the rust. Enough to keep edges hard till the next train comes -- if it comes. You know the strange thing? Every cart that spilled up there was pointed east. Loaded, on the eastbound climb, and something took the drivers and left the iron. Whatever's on this pass doesn't want our steel. It wants our people going east. Take this reaver. Keep it hard.

objectives.text: Gather spilled bog-iron from the scree

whisper_passes_iron_for_the_ridge.note: Every spilled cart on the crag-paths was pointed east, loaded, its driver gone and its iron left. Whatever walks the pass has no use for steel. It has a use for people, and the use is eastward. Ecaterina keeps her edges hard and says nothing of it to the carters.

whisper_passes_the_ones_who_listen.title: The Ones Who Listen
whisper_passes_the_ones_who_listen.summary: Draga's neighbors on the east row have started standing at their windows at the same hour each dusk, heads tilted, facing the eastbound road. She wants someone to keep the vigil with her and see if any of them turn back.
whisper_passes_the_ones_who_listen.offer: You'll think me mad, or worse, wishing it. Three doors down the east row, folk have taken to standing at their windows come dusk -- all of them, same hour, heads leaned the same way, facing the road east. They don't answer when called. Come morning they're themselves again and don't remember. Keep the dusk-watch with me tonight. I need to know if it's the pass doing it, or if I'm the one gone wrong.
whisper_passes_the_ones_who_listen.progress: The east row of houses stands along the eastbound road. Take up the dusk-watch there with Draga and see the standing for yourself.
whisper_passes_the_ones_who_listen.complete: You saw it. You saw them lean, all together, the same slow tilt, like grass finding a wind that isn't blowing. And you saw the one thing I couldn't bear to watch for alone -- not one of them turned back west. They all face east, every dusk, toward the ridges. So it's the pass, and not me. I don't know which I wanted to hear. Thank you for staying. It's a lonelier thing to see it than to be it, I think.

objectives.label: the east row at dusk
objectives.text: Keep the dusk-watch on the east row
Sit the watch through with Draga

whisper_passes_the_ones_who_listen.note: At dusk the east row leans as one toward the ridges, and come morning remembers nothing. Not one of them ever turns back west. Draga keeps the watch still, alone now. She says it is a lonelier thing to see it than to be it. She may be right.

whisper_passes_stalker_hides.title: Hides That Lie Flat
whisper_passes_stalker_hides.summary: Cliff-stalker hide makes the toughest cordage on the pass, and Ecaterina needs a batch for climbing-lines. But she wants a careful hand to gather it -- because lately the hides lie flat in one direction, and she needs to know she's not imagining it.
whisper_passes_stalker_hides.offer: Stalker-hide braids into the best climbing-line on this road -- carters trust it over rope. I need a batch cut clean. But do this careful and look at what you bring me: the last hides came off the crags with the nap all lying one way, every one, like something combed the beasts while they lived. Bring me six good hides. Tell me I dreamed the combing.
whisper_passes_stalker_hides.progress: The stalkers work the switchbacks and the upper crags. Cut six good hides -- clean, and mind how the nap lies.
whisper_passes_stalker_hides.complete: You saw it too, then. The nap lies all one way, every hide, north to south, same as the dust does in the gallery, same as the filings in the old tales. It's not just the pilgrims listening. The beasts are being combed by it. Even the stalkers point the same direction now. I'll braid the lines anyway. A carter still has to cross. Take the skinner -- and cross careful.

objectives.text: Cull stalkers for their hides
Recover a clean skinner from the crags

whisper_passes_stalker_hides.note: Every stalker-hide lies flat one way now, north to south, the way the gallery dust lies, the way the old filings lay. It is not only the pilgrims listening. The beasts are combed by it too. Ecaterina braids her climbing-lines regardless. A carter still has to cross.

whisper_passes_the_deepbed_lantern.title: A Lantern for the Deep Fog
whisper_passes_the_deepbed_lantern.summary: Mira has a buyer in the ridges who pays in Blestem silver for a deepbed lantern -- the kind whose flame reads the fog's true colors. She wants one recovered from a foundered cart, and asks no questions about why the East wants to see what the fog hides.
whisper_passes_the_deepbed_lantern.offer: I've a standing order from a factor over the ridges -- pays in good Blestem silver, weighs it himself, asks for one thing: a deepbed lantern. The kind whose flame changes color by what's in the air. There's one in a foundered cart up the east spur, or was. Fetch it and the silver's split with you. And no, I don't ask why a Blestem man wants to see what our fog's made of. In my trade, the coin that pays best is the coin that asks least.
whisper_passes_the_deepbed_lantern.progress: The foundered cart lies up the east spur, along the eastbound road. Recover the deepbed lantern from it.
whisper_passes_the_deepbed_lantern.complete: There it is -- and look, the flame's already shifting up here, blue at the wick and something worse at the edges. That's why the East wants it. They want to map the color of the pass without setting foot on it. Here's your silver. I'll not pretend this trade sits easy. But a merchant who only sold to easy consciences would've frozen on this pass years ago. Spend it warm.

objectives.text: Recover the deepbed lantern
Deliver the lantern to Mira

whisper_passes_the_deepbed_lantern.note: A Blestem factor pays good silver to map the color of the pass without setting foot on it. Mira asks no questions and takes the coin. The lantern's flame ran blue at the wick and shifting orange at its edges. She sold that knowledge east. She will not sleep easier for the silver.

whisper_passes_the_warm_lane.title: The Warm Lane
whisper_passes_the_warm_lane.summary: A single lane of the eastbound road stays warm underfoot when all the rest is frost, and the fog won't lie in it. Lenuta wants it walked and scanned -- because a warm lane on this cold pass means the ground beneath is awake.
whisper_passes_the_warm_lane.offer: There's a stretch of the east road -- one lane's width, no more -- where the frost won't take and the fog won't lie and the stone's warm through your boots. My grandmother taught me the rhyme every child up here knows: warm is wrong, copper is close, quiet is worst. Well, it's warm on that lane, and the well beside it's gone copper, and it is very, very quiet. Walk it. Read it with the alchemist's sight if you've got it. And come tell me how bad.
whisper_passes_the_warm_lane.progress: The warm lane runs along the eastbound road, past the copper well. Walk it, and read the ground with alchemical sight.
whisper_passes_the_warm_lane.complete: Green, you say -- the sight read green over the lane. A stone waking under us, then. Not sleeping. Not sealed. Waking. My grandmother had one more line to the rhyme she only said once: green is the ground remembering what it's for. I always thought she made that part up. Take the crown -- coldlight, it holds a true flame in fog. You'll want to see clearly on the road ahead. I fear we all will.

objectives.label: the warm lane
objectives.text: Walk and read the warm lane
Report the reading to Lenuta

whisper_passes_the_warm_lane.note: One lane of the east road stays warm where all else is frost, and the sight reads green above it: a stone beneath, waking, not sleeping. Lenuta's grandmother had a line for it she said only once. Warm is wrong. Copper is close. Green is the ground remembering what it is for.

whisper_passes_the_carters_widow.title: The Carter's Widow
whisper_passes_the_carters_widow.summary: A carter died on the switchbacks and his keepsake -- a cord his wife braided him -- was never brought down. Ruxandra promised the widow it would come home. She asks you to recover it where the stalkers took him.
whisper_passes_the_carters_widow.offer: A carter died on the lower switchbacks a fortnight back -- stalkers, in the fog. His widow's below in the ridges, and I sent word, the way I always do, because someone has to. She asked for one thing back. Not the cart, not the coin. A keepsake-cord she braided him, worn at his wrist. It'll be up where he fell, if the stalkers left it. Bring it down. I gave her my word, and my word's the last warm thing I've got to give up here.
whisper_passes_the_carters_widow.progress: The carter fell on the lower switchbacks, where the stalkers den. Search the place he died for the keepsake-cord.
whisper_passes_the_carters_widow.complete: You found it. Frayed, but hers -- I'd know that braid; the widows up here all learn the same one from the same grandmother. I'll carry it down myself come the thaw. A cart, a purse, a season's wage -- the pass takes all of it and gives nothing back. But a braided cord it let me keep, this once. That'll have to be enough. It's more than most get on this road.

objectives.text: Recover the carter's keepsake-cord
Return the cord to Ruxandra

whisper_passes_the_carters_widow.note: The pass took the carter, his cart, his purse, his season's wage. It let his widow keep a braided cord. Ruxandra will carry it down at the thaw. On this road that is counted a great deal returned. She does not say so aloud, in case the pass is listening for a reason to correct the account.

whisper_passes_folk_warding.title: The Old Warding
whisper_passes_folk_warding.summary: Anca's grandmother's warding needs a chalk handprint pressed at the pass's oldest shrine to renew the line that keeps the choir's hum from crossing into the town. Anca is too afraid to go alone. She asks you to walk her there and stand while she presses it.
whisper_passes_folk_warding.offer: The salt-lines hold the small things, but the hum -- the choir's note, when the wind's wrong -- needs the old warding, the handprint at the shrine above the town. My grandmother pressed hers there fifty years back and it's faded to nothing, and the hum's been reaching the near houses since. I'll press a new one. I've the chalk. But I can't go up alone, I can't, not since the east row started leaning. Walk me up. Stand while I do it. That's all.
whisper_passes_folk_warding.progress: The old shrine stands on the ridge above the town. Walk Anca up, and stand watch while she presses the warding.
whisper_passes_folk_warding.complete: It's done. My print beside my grandmother's, and hers beside her mother's before, all the way back to whoever first knew the pass needed warding. Look how many hands. Fifty of them, a hundred. Each one afraid, each one pressing anyway. That's the warding, really -- not the chalk. The line of frightened women who came up here and pressed their hand to the cold stone and refused to stop. I'm one of them now. So are you, near enough. Take the sign. Keep the line going.

objectives.label: the old shrine
objectives.text: Walk Anca to the shrine
Press the warding with Anca

whisper_passes_folk_warding.note: The shrine bears a hundred chalk handprints, mother beside daughter beside grandmother, back to whoever first knew the pass needed warding. Each one was afraid. Each one pressed anyway. Anca calls that the true warding -- the unbroken line of frightened women who refused to stop. The chalk is only chalk. The refusal is the wall.

whisper_passes_the_accord_survey.title: The Accord's Survey
whisper_passes_the_accord_survey.summary: The Accord wants the pass surveyed -- every warm lane, every copper well, every silent camp mapped for a report to the four powers. Otilia hands you the survey, uneasy that measuring the wrongness so precisely feels like doing the stone's own bookkeeping.
whisper_passes_the_accord_survey.offer: There's an Accord man came up the east road -- quiet sort, good boots, a satchel of blank vellum. He wants the pass surveyed. Every warm lane, every coppered well, every camp gone silent, marked and measured for a report to the four thrones. It's the right thing, mapping the sickness. And yet. Every time I mark a warm spot on his vellum I feel like I'm keeping the stone's own ledger for it. Reach the survey points. Read them. Bring the tally back to me, not to him. Let me be the one who decides what the four thrones get told.
whisper_passes_the_accord_survey.progress: The survey wants two readings: the aligned dust at the notch, and the warm lane past the copper well. Reach each, then bring the tally to Otilia -- not the Accord man.
whisper_passes_the_accord_survey.complete: You read them both. Dust aligned at the notch, the lane warm and green past the copper. It's a full picture now -- exactly the picture the stone would draw of itself, if it drew. That's what troubles me. We measure the sickness to fight it, and the measuring makes a map, and a map of every warm place is a map anyone could walk straight to the grave by. I'll give the Accord man half of it. The dangerous half stays in my head. Some surveys shouldn't be complete. This is one.

objectives.label: the aligned notch
objectives.text: Survey the aligned dust at the notch

objectives.label: the warm lane
objectives.text: Survey the warm lane past the copper well
Bring the tally to Otilia

whisper_passes_the_accord_survey.note: The Accord's survey would have drawn a perfect map of the pass's sickness -- which is to say, a perfect map to the grave, warm spot by warm spot. Otilia gave the Accord man half. The dangerous half she keeps unwritten, in her own head. Some surveys, she decided, are better left incomplete. The stone keeps complete ones.

whisper_passes_the_frozen_bell.title: The Frozen Bell
whisper_passes_the_frozen_bell.summary: The pass's old signal-bell rang the fog-lost home for three hundred years, until its rope froze and its ringer went quiet. Zamfira wants it climbed to and rung once more -- so anyone lost on the road tonight can steer by a sound that isn't the wind's new note.
whisper_passes_the_frozen_bell.offer: There's a bell on the high crag -- iron, older than the inn, rung to guide the fog-lost home for three hundred years. The last ringer stopped a season back; found him at his post, they say, not a wound on him, just quiet. Nobody's rung it since. And with the wind singing what it sings now, a soul lost up there has nothing true to steer by. Climb to the bell. Ring it, plain and honest, three times. Give the lost a sound that isn't the pass's.
whisper_passes_the_frozen_bell.progress: The signal-bell hangs on the high crag, above the notch. Climb to it and ring it three times, true.
whisper_passes_the_frozen_bell.complete: Three rings -- I heard them, down here, clear over the wind, the first honest sound off that crag in a season. You'll have steered someone home tonight who'd have walked east otherwise. That's the whole war, really, up here. Not the killing. The ringing. Giving a lost soul one true note to walk toward, against all the false ones the pass is learning to sing. Keep this. It's the last ringer's -- he'd want it rung by someone who understood why.

objectives.label: the signal-bell
objectives.text: Climb to the signal-bell and ring it true

whisper_passes_the_frozen_bell.note: The last ringer died at his post, no wound, just quiet -- the Courier's death, though no one on the pass has the word for it.
