The bell rings again now, three honest notes against the pass's false song. Zamfira calls that the whole war up here: not the killing, the ringing. One true note for the lost to walk toward.

whisper_passes_the_incurious_charm.title: The Charm Against Reading
whisper_passes_the_incurious_charm.summary: Ecaterina forges incurious charms -- worn to blunt the urge to read what shouldn't be read on this pass. She's out of blackglass to face them and needs it cut from the crags, where the stalkers hoard it in their dens.
whisper_passes_the_incurious_charm.offer: I make a charm folk here won't cross the pass without -- an incurious charm, we call it. You wear it and the writing on the stones stops pulling at your eye. Won't stop a determined fool reading, but it makes restraint a little easier, and up here restraint is life. I'm out of the blackglass I face them with. The stalkers hoard the stuff in their dens, glossy black, like they're drawn to it too. Cut me a batch. I'll charm you one for the road.
whisper_passes_the_incurious_charm.progress: The stalkers hoard blackglass in their crag-dens along the upper switchbacks. Cut the beasts and gather the glass from their nests.
whisper_passes_the_incurious_charm.complete: Good black glass, clean-fractured. Strange, isn't it -- the beasts pile it like a magpie piles bright things, and the one thing on this pass that pulls a beast's eye is the same thing that pulls a man's. The writing. The gloss. The urge to look closer. I'll face your charm and speak the words over it. Wear it on the road east. When the stones start pulling at your eye -- and they will -- let the charm remember for you that you don't want to know.

objectives.text: Cull stalkers at their dens
Gather hoarded blackglass

whisper_passes_the_incurious_charm.note: The stalkers hoard blackglass like magpies hoard bright things -- drawn, like men are drawn, to gloss and to the urge to look closer. Ecaterina faces her incurious charms with it. On this pass the beasts and the pilgrims share one weakness, and it is curiosity. She forges against it, blade and charm alike.

whisper_passes_the_dossier.title: A Dossier of Habits
whisper_passes_the_dossier.summary: The Blestem factor over the ridges keeps a dossier on the pass's people -- who leans east at dusk, who rings the bell, who refuses to read. Mira wants it stolen back before the East learns exactly whom the pass can no longer trust to itself.
whisper_passes_the_dossier.offer: That Blestem factor I trade with -- he's been keeping a book. Not of goods. Of people. Who on this pass leans east at dusk, who's stopped sleeping, who still rings the bell and who won't read the stones. A dossier of habits, they call it in the East, and a man's habits are the leash they lead him by. He left it at his east-spur station last crossing. Get it back before he files it in Blestem. Once it's filed, we're all just entries.
whisper_passes_the_dossier.progress: The factor's dossier is at his station on the east spur. Recover it before it's carried into Blestem.
whisper_passes_the_dossier.complete: You got it. And you didn't read it -- I can tell, you'd not meet my eye so easy if you had. Wise. A dossier of habits reads two ways: you learn who's compromised, and you learn you're in it too. We all are. Every name on this pass, ranked by how far gone. I'll burn it. Not because burning helps -- Blestem keeps copies of copies -- but because I'll not be the one who let our habits get filed without a fight. Take the still. You've earned a clear head. Keep it clear.

objectives.text: Recover the dossier of habits
Bring the dossier to Mira

whisper_passes_the_dossier.note: The Blestem factor ranked every soul on the pass by how far the fog had taken them -- a dossier of habits, the leash the East leads men by. Mira burned it, knowing Blestem keeps copies of copies. She burned it anyway. Not because it helped. Because she would not let their habits be filed without a fight.

whisper_passes_the_hessik_method.title: The Hessik Method
whisper_passes_the_hessik_method.summary: The governor's map named a method -- the Hessik Method -- for sealing the listening safely. Zamfira has found the treatise it came from, half-buried at a wayshrine, and wants it recovered before some desperate soul finds it first and starts bricking doors again.
whisper_passes_the_hessik_method.offer: The governor's map named his method -- the Hessik Method, after the man who wrote it. A treatise on sealing the listening 'humanely,' brick and prayer and a sealed room. It's why he walled his people up thinking it mercy. There's a copy at the old wayshrine, half in the mud. I want it -- not to use, saints forbid, but to keep it from the next frightened governor who finds it and thinks a sealed door is the same as a saved soul. Fetch it before someone hopeful does.
whisper_passes_the_hessik_method.progress: The wayshrine sits below the east spur, along the lower road. Search it for the copy of the Hessik Method.
whisper_passes_the_hessik_method.complete: Here it is, the whole wretched method. Listen to how it opens: 'The listening cannot be cured, only contained; and the kindest containment is the sealed room, wherein the afflicted may finish in peace among their own.' Peace. He called the gallery peace. Every word of it reasonable, every word of it a tomb. I'll keep it locked in the pass-book's iron box, next to the twelfth door. Some knowledge you preserve only so no one uses it. Take the method's counter-draught. If ever you're sealed in somewhere, drink it and stay yourself a while longer.

objectives.text: Recover the Hessik Method treatise
Bring the treatise to Zamfira

whisper_passes_the_hessik_method.note: The Hessik Method taught that the listening could not be cured, only sealed, and that the kindest seal was a locked room where the afflicted might finish among their own. Every word reasonable, every word a tomb. Zamfira keeps the treatise locked away -- preserved only so no hopeful, frightened hand ever uses it again.

whisper_passes_the_switchback_toll.title: The Switchback Toll
whisper_passes_the_switchback_toll.summary: The stalkers breed back into the switchbacks faster than the guard can cull them. Lenuta pays a standing bounty to any traveler who thins the pack enough to keep the day's caravans crossing alive.
whisper_passes_the_switchback_toll.offer: The stalkers come back into the switchbacks faster than we can put them down -- cull a den and by next dusk there's another. So I've a standing coin for any road-hand who'll thin them of a morning. Not a war, just a toll. Keep the number down, keep the caravans crossing, keep the widows fewer. Same ask tomorrow, and the day after. This pass doesn't get won. It gets held.
whisper_passes_the_switchback_toll.progress: The stalkers work the switchbacks each morning. Thin them for the day's crossings and come collect the coin.
whisper_passes_the_switchback_toll.complete: That's the morning's number down. Caravans'll cross alive today, most of them. Come back tomorrow -- there'll be more, there's always more. It's a poor sort of work, killing the same pack forever. But a poor sort of holding beats no holding at all. Here's your coin. Keep sharp.

objectives.text: Thin the switchback stalkers

whisper_passes_the_switchback_toll.note: The pass does not get won. It gets held. Lenuta pays the switchback toll every morning to any hand that will thin the stalkers, knowing tomorrow there will be more. A poor sort of holding, she says. It beats no holding at all.

whisper_passes_keeping_the_gallery_quiet.title: Keeping the Gallery Quiet
whisper_passes_keeping_the_gallery_quiet.summary: The opened gallery draws new Aligned from the pilgrim road as fast as the old ones are broken. Zamfira asks that the choir be kept thinned each day, so its hum never again gathers enough voices to reach the town.
whisper_passes_keeping_the_gallery_quiet.offer: We opened the gallery, and opening it was right -- but an open door on the pilgrim road is a door the road keeps filling. New ones drift in and finish and stand and hum, a few every few days. Keep them thinned for me. Not sealed -- never sealed again, I'll die before I brick that door -- just thinned, so the hum never gathers enough voices to carry down to the houses. Do it daily if you're on the road. I'll keep the count, and the count is the whole of my faith now.
whisper_passes_keeping_the_gallery_quiet.progress: New Aligned gather in the open gallery under the notch. Break the shells and keep the hum from gathering.
whisper_passes_keeping_the_gallery_quiet.complete: Thinned, and the hum stays scattered -- I can hear the difference from here, the way you can hear when a choir's missing its center. Thank you. Every day you do this is a day the town sleeps without the note in its walls. It's not a cure. It's a vigil. But this pass was built on vigils, and I mean to keep this one till my hand can't hold the pen to count it.

objectives.text: Keep the gallery choir thinned

whisper_passes_keeping_the_gallery_quiet.note: The open gallery fills as fast as it empties, the road feeding it new listeners. Zamfira keeps the count each day -- not a cure, a vigil. This pass was built on vigils. She means to keep this one until her hand can no longer hold the pen to tally it.

whisper_passes_the_writ_of_unfiling.title: The Writ of Unfiling
whisper_passes_the_writ_of_unfiling.summary: The Accord man filed the survey in Blestem after all -- and with it, the pass's people, ranked and entered. Otilia has one chance: a Writ of Unfiling, carried east to a clerk who owes her, before the entries harden into the stone's own ledger.
whisper_passes_the_writ_of_unfiling.offer: He filed it. The Accord man -- he took the half I gave him and filed it in Blestem, and a Blestem file doesn't stay half. They completed it. Our warm lanes, our coppered wells, and every name on this pass, entered and ranked. I've one throw left. A Writ of Unfiling -- there's a clerk over the ridges owes me a life, and a writ from his own hand can pull a file before it's sealed. Carry it east. Get it to him before the entries harden. Once a thing's filed complete and sealed in Blestem, it's not paperwork anymore. It's arrangement. Ask the pilgrims. Ask the choir. They were all filed complete, once.
whisper_passes_the_writ_of_unfiling.progress: The writ must reach the Blestem-side clerk at the eastern crossing before the file seals. Carry it east; do not read it; deliver it whole.
whisper_passes_the_writ_of_unfiling.complete: It's away, in his hand, unread -- and you carried it sealed, which is the only reason I trusted you with it. He'll pull the file, or he won't; a clerk's debts are worth what the clerk decides. But we tried. We measured the sickness and it turned into a map, and the map got filed and the file nearly became a sentence, and at the last we spent a favor to un-write it. That's the shape of every fight up here. You do the right thing, it becomes a weapon, and then you fight to take the weapon back. Take the writ-copy. You'll want proof you were never entered.

objectives.label: the eastern crossing
objectives.text: Carry the writ to the eastern crossing
Confirm the delivery with Otilia

whisper_passes_the_writ_of_unfiling.note: The survey they made to fight the sickness was filed in Blestem, complete -- every warm lane, every name, ranked and nearly sealed. Otilia spent a life's-worth favor to un-write it. That is the shape of the pass's war: do right, watch it become a weapon, then fight to take the weapon back. The pilgrims were all filed complete once. It is worth remembering how that ended.

whisper_passes_the_last_ringer.title: What the Last Ringer Knew
whisper_passes_the_last_ringer.summary: The last bell-ringer left something at the inn before he climbed to his post and went quiet -- a sealed keepsake, and a request. Ruxandra has kept it a season, afraid to open it. She asks you to be present when she finally does.
whisper_passes_the_last_ringer.offer: Before the last ringer climbed to the bell for the final time, he left this at my bar -- sealed, with a note: 'Open it when the bell rings again, and not before.' Well. The bell rang again, three nights past, thanks to you. So it's time, and I find I can't do it alone. He was kind, that man. Kept the fog-lost safe thirty years and asked nothing. Sit with me while I open what he left. I'm afraid of what a kind man seals up before he goes to die quiet at his post.
whisper_passes_the_last_ringer.progress: Ruxandra keeps the ringer's sealed keepsake at the inn bar. Be present with her when she opens it.
whisper_passes_the_last_ringer.complete: Look. Not a warning. Not a curse. A list -- every soul he steered home in thirty years, name by name, all the way down. Four hundred and eleven names, and a last line in a shakier hand: 'I could hear it coming for me for a season. I rang until I couldn't. Whoever reads this: ring on. The names are the whole of it. Keep the names.' ...He knew. He knew it was taking him and he rang anyway, and he counted every soul he saved so someone would keep the count. Take the token. It's got his name on it. He never wrote his own down in the list. So I will.

objectives.text: Open the ringer's keepsake with Ruxandra

whisper_passes_the_last_ringer.note: The last ringer left a list -- four hundred and eleven souls he steered home in thirty years, counted so someone would keep the count. He heard it coming for him for a season and rang until he couldn't. He never entered his own name. Ruxandra entered it for him. The stone keeps a ledger of the taken. The pass keeps a ledger of the saved. Both are counted. Only one is kind.

whisper_passes_the_shroud_of_the_named.title: The Shroud of the Named
whisper_passes_the_shroud_of_the_named.summary: Draga is stitching a shroud with the name of every neighbor the pass has taken, so that no one on the east row goes into the fog unremembered. She needs thread that holds its dye in the damp, gathered from the deep crag-moss where the choir's hum keeps the weavers away.
whisper_passes_the_shroud_of_the_named.offer: Since the leaning started, I've been stitching -- a shroud, and on it every name the pass has taken off our row. If the fog's going to unmake them, if it's going to file them somewhere cold, then let there be one warm cloth up here that says they were ours first, by name. But my thread won't hold its color in this damp, and the crag-moss that dyes it fast grows deep, where the hum keeps folk away. Gather me a good binding of it. Help me keep the names.
whisper_passes_the_shroud_of_the_named.progress: The dye-moss grows deep in the crags near the gallery, where the hum keeps weavers away. Gather a binding of thread and moss.
whisper_passes_the_shroud_of_the_named.complete: That'll hold now, dark and fast, through any damp. Look at them all -- forty names, and room for more, saints forgive the room. When one of us goes quiet and leans east and doesn't come back to herself, I stitch her in. Not to grieve. To remember she had a name before the fog gave her a number. That's the only fight I've got, a needle and a list. But it's a fight, and I'll not put it down. Take the shroud-cloth; it's blessed by every name on it.

objectives.text: Clear the hum from the dye-moss crags
Gather thread and dye-moss

whisper_passes_the_shroud_of_the_named.note: Draga stitches a shroud with the name of every neighbor the pass has taken, so each is remembered as a name before the fog made them a number. Forty names, and room stitched for more. A needle and a list against a world-machine. She calls it the only fight she has. She will not put it down.

bloodstone_pit_the_last_rim.title: The Last Rim
bloodstone_pit_the_last_rim.summary: The rim-watch keeps a lantern-line no army ordered and no king pays for. Dorica lost two of the string this week to the warm ground below. Walk the last rim with her before she has to walk it alone.
bloodstone_pit_the_last_rim.offer: You came down the grave-road on purpose. Good. Most don't, and the ones who do, I mostly bury. Walk the rim with me -- reach the far post where Sanda used to stand -- and mind the ground stays cool under your boots. Warm ground listens. Cold ground lets you leave.
bloodstone_pit_the_last_rim.progress: The far post sits east along the pit-rim, where the lantern-line breaks. Keep your feet on the cold stone and go see it.
bloodstone_pit_the_last_rim.complete: You reached it and you came back cold. Sanda's post is empty, lantern still lit, boots gone. She wasn't taken. She walked. There's a difference down here, and I hate that I know it. Keep the striker -- light's the only wall we've got.

objectives.label: Sanda's rim-post
objectives.text: Walk the rim to the far post

bloodstone_pit_the_last_rim.note: The far post stood empty, lantern lit, boots gone. Dorica named it plainly: not taken. Walked. She has a word for the ground that does the asking, and she does not say it twice.

bloodstone_pit_cold_lanterns.title: Cold Lanterns
bloodstone_pit_cold_lanterns.summary: Marta sells the one thing the rim cannot do without: lantern-oil that burns steady in warm air. Her deepbed lanterns went out down-slope and the wights walk where the light fails. Fetch her lanterns back from the dark and she will trade you fair.
bloodstone_pit_cold_lanterns.offer: Everything down here is grim except me, and I intend to stay the odd one out. My deepbed lanterns rolled off the low ledge when the ground shivered, and I'll not send my boy after them -- he's the only cheerful thing I've got left. You look like you keep your head. Bring me back three, and I'll not overcharge you. Much.
bloodstone_pit_cold_lanterns.progress: Three deepbed lanterns lie down the low ledge, guttering in the warm dark. Gather them before the oil runs out.
bloodstone_pit_cold_lanterns.complete: Three, and only one cracked -- that's a good day at the rim. Here, take the poultice, on the house, which I never say. A body that walks into the warm dark for a stranger's lanterns ought to walk out again. Come back when you need oil. You will.

objectives.text: Recover Marta's lanterns from the low ledge

bloodstone_pit_cold_lanterns.note: Three lanterns back on Marta's rack, one cracked, all burning. She keeps a cheerful shop at the mouth of the grave because someone has to, and because the alternative is to start counting.

bloodstone_pit_they_kept_working.title: They Kept Working
bloodstone_pit_they_kept_working.summary: The old dig-crews died in the pit and did not stop -- they still swing at the rock, animated by what seeps up from the stone. Ileana forged their tools once. Put down the ones nearest the rim, for the sake of who they were.
bloodstone_pit_they_kept_working.offer: I hammered picks for those crews. Knew their grips, the way a smith does -- this one favored his left, that one wore his handle smooth. They died in the pit and they never set the picks down. Still swinging at the same rock, still on the same shift, six hundred years past their pay. Put down the ones that climbed to the rim. Not for mercy. There's no mercy in it. Just so the rest of us don't have to watch.
bloodstone_pit_they_kept_working.progress: The deep-wights work the rim-face where the old shafts open. Put down eight of them.
bloodstone_pit_they_kept_working.complete: Eight down, and every one still had a pick in its fist when it fell. You noticed how they never once looked at you -- swinging at the rock, not the living. We're not the enemy down here. We're just not on the schedule. Take the reaver. It was a dig-master's, before. He'd want it swung by someone who leaves at the end of the shift.

objectives.text: Put down the working dead at the rim-face

bloodstone_pit_they_kept_working.note: Eight of the working dead laid down, picks still in their fists. They swung at the rock, never at the living. Ileana forged their tools once. She did not watch them fall, and no one asked her to.

bloodstone_pit_count_the_kerbs.title: Count the Kerbs
bloodstone_pit_count_the_kerbs.summary: Voica keeps the rim's grave-rows the old way -- counted, in rows of twelve. Last night a row that read twelve kept now reads eleven, and one kerb has gone warm. Read the changed stone with a cold eye and bring her back the count.
bloodstone_pit_count_the_kerbs.offer: I keep the rows. Twelve to a row, always twelve, that's how the north does its dead -- neat, counted, kept. Only my eleventh row read twelve at dusk and reads eleven at dawn, and one stone's warm to the hand. I'd read it myself, but I'm old and I'm curious, and down here the curious don't stay long. Go to the eleventh row. Read the warm kerb. Then look at your boots and walk away, and tell me only the number.
bloodstone_pit_count_the_kerbs.progress: The eleventh row lies south of the chronicle-tent along the kerb-line. Read the warm kerb, then take only the count back to Voica.
bloodstone_pit_count_the_kerbs.complete: Eleven. You read it and you left it, and you kept your eyes down coming back -- I watched you do it. That's why you're standing here to tell me. Twelve kept, one pending, the warm stone said. Pending. As if a grave could be a thing still owed. Keep the lens. And keep the habit of looking at your boots.

objectives.label: the eleventh kerb-row
objectives.text: Read the warm kerb, then look away
Bring Voica only the count

bloodstone_pit_count_the_kerbs.note: The eleventh row read twelve at dusk and eleven at dawn. The warm kerb read twelve kept, one pending, in shifting orange. Voica wrote down the number and nothing else. Some counts are a mercy left unfinished.

bloodstone_pit_the_sealed_satchel.title: The Sealed Satchel
bloodstone_pit_the_sealed_satchel.summary: A courier came up the grave-road with a sealed satchel and no wound, clean fingernails, and a stillness that was not sleep. Sanda will not open it. Carry it, sealed, up to the chronicler -- and do not read what the pit routed into your hands.
bloodstone_pit_the_sealed_satchel.offer: A runner came up the road last night. No blood, no mark, nails clean as a scrubbed plate -- and gone, the way the courier in the old chamber went. Comprehension, the chroniclers call it. Understood something all the way. His satchel's still sealed. I want it out of my sight and into Voica's keeping, but I can't be the one to carry it, I've a wife at the rim. You carry it. Sealed. Whatever it wants you to read, you look at your boots.
bloodstone_pit_the_sealed_satchel.progress: Carry the sealed satchel up to Voica the chronicler. It will ask to be opened at every rest. Refuse it, and deliver it whole.
bloodstone_pit_the_sealed_satchel.complete: Sealed still. Good. You had it in your hands a full climb and you never once looked -- I can see that you never looked, it's in how you're standing. Voica will lock it in the lead box unread. That courier didn't die of a knife, friend. He died of knowing. You chose not to. That choice is the only wall down here that holds. Take the charm. It quiets the curious itch, a little.

objectives.text: Take the sealed satchel from Sanda

objectives.label: the chronicle-tent
objectives.text: Carry it, unread, to the chronicle-tent
Deliver the satchel sealed to Voica

bloodstone_pit_the_sealed_satchel.note: The courier died with clean fingernails and a sealed satchel, of nothing but understanding. It rode up the grave-road in unreading hands and went into the lead box unopened. The one wall that holds down here is the choice not to look.

bloodstone_pit_forty_seven_years.title: Forty-Seven Years
bloodstone_pit_forty_seven_years.summary: Under the rim lies a queen who ruled forty-seven just years and was buried alive in the ground she tended. Her cedar throne is the one warm true thing in the grave. Go down to the tomb-stone, read what she cut there in her own hand, and stand a moment with the throne.
bloodstone_pit_forty_seven_years.offer: There is a stone below the rim with words worn nearly smooth, cut by a hand that meant them. A queen lies under it. Ruled forty-seven just years, built a cedar throne to grow old in, and her own people put her living in the ground she'd tended and called it a pact. Everything the stone does, it does through what she guards. Go down. Read her line. Stand with the throne a breath -- the cedar still smells, impossibly, of a warmer world. Then come up and tell me you stood there and left. That's the whole errand: to leave.
bloodstone_pit_forty_seven_years.progress: The tomb-stone and the cedar throne lie deep below the rim. Read the worn line, stand a moment with the throne, and climb back out.
bloodstone_pit_forty_seven_years.complete: You read it. '47 years. I gave them 47 just years, and a cedar throne. They gave me a pit.' And you stood with the throne and you came back up. That smell of cedar follows a body a while -- it's the last warm human thing down there, and it's a grief, not a wonder, once you know whose it is. She isn't the villain. She's the wall the villain built her into. Take the shroud. Wear it when you go down again. And you will go down again.

objectives.label: Lilith's tomb-stone
objectives.text: Read the worn line on the tomb-stone

objectives.label: the cedar throne
objectives.text: Stand a breath with the cedar throne

bloodstone_pit_forty_seven_years.note: Below the rim a queen who gave forty-seven just years lies in the pit her own people dug for her. Her line is worn nearly smooth; the cedar still smells of the world she meant to keep. She is not the enemy. She is the wall, and the sadness that holds it up.

bloodstone_pit_transmit_not_receive.title: Transmit, Not Receive
bloodstone_pit_transmit_not_receive.summary: The counter to the stone is six words a dying seer gave the world: a living touch receives, a dead touch transmits. The horrors below are records trying to close. Force a small, present, imperfect truth into eight of them so they cannot balance, and prove the six words hold.
bloodstone_pit_transmit_not_receive.offer: There are six words that matter more than any king's decree: a living touch receives, a dead touch transmits. The stone erases by making you understand it. But a record can be spoiled -- push one small, present, imperfect truth into the thing and it can't finalize. It can't close on a truth that's still happening. The horrors at the deep face are records trying to balance. Go spoil eight of them. Not to kill -- you can't kill a ledger -- to keep it open. Prove the six words. We'll need them proven before the end.
bloodstone_pit_transmit_not_receive.progress: The bloodstone horrors gather at the deep face. Break the closing of eight -- force a present truth into each so it cannot finalize.
bloodstone_pit_transmit_not_receive.complete: Eight kept from closing. You felt it, didn't you -- the moment each one reached to file you, and you handed it something small and true and still happening, and it couldn't. Specificity. Stubbornness. A present-tense truth the perfect record can't swallow. That's the wall, and it's the only one that scales to the stone itself. Take the focus. When the day comes to go all the way down, you'll push one small logged truth into the thing that ate the world. Small. Temporary. Ours.

objectives.text: Keep eight horrors from closing at the deep face

bloodstone_pit_transmit_not_receive.note: Eight records kept from closing, each spoiled by a truth still happening. A living touch receives; a dead touch transmits. Six words, proven at the deep face. The wall against the stone is not power. It is a small, present, imperfect thing, held open.

bloodstone_pit_the_first_debt.title: The First Debt
bloodstone_pit_the_first_debt.summary: The oldest overdue account in the world has climbed up out of the deep -- a shape assembled from every unpaid death in the first ledger. It comes for whoever holds the tablet. Dorica will not let it reach the rim. End it with a present truth, and let the rim keep one more night.
bloodstone_pit_the_first_debt.offer: Something came up the deep shaft that isn't a wight and isn't a horror. It's older. The first account that was ever left unpaid, walking, wearing every death it's owed. It's coming for the tablet, and the tablet's in my keeping now, saints help me. I've watched the rim thirty years. I've never once run. I'm not running tonight either -- but I can't do this one alone, and I won't ask Sanda, she's stood enough posts. Come down with me. Hand it something true. Send it back to pending.
bloodstone_pit_the_first_debt.progress: The First Debt climbs the deep shaft toward the rim. Meet it with Dorica and force it back to pending.
bloodstone_pit_the_first_debt.complete: Back to pending. Not paid -- nothing down here is ever paid -- but open, unclosed, owed forever, which is the only victory the pit allows. You gave it a truth it couldn't swallow and it couldn't finalize you and it went back down the shaft. The rim keeps one more night. That's how we count wins here: one more night, and then we count again. Take the plate. It's turned worse than teeth.

objectives.text: Force the First Debt back to pending

bloodstone_pit_the_first_debt.note: The oldest unpaid account climbed the deep shaft and was sent back to pending -- not settled, only kept open, owed forever. Dorica has watched the rim thirty years and never run. She counts wins in nights survived. The rim kept one more.

bloodstone_pit_yeast_that_died.title: The Yeast That Died
bloodstone_pit_yeast_that_died.summary: Ana keeps the only hearth at the rim, and her bread has come out flat and grey for a month -- the yeast dies in the warm air the way it does when the ground beneath makes up its mind. She wants a jar of live culture from the mercy-cart. Fetch it before her hearth goes cold for good.
bloodstone_pit_yeast_that_died.offer: I bake for everyone who comes off that road, and lately my loaves come out flat and grey as a headstone. It's not my hand -- the yeast dies. Down here the warm ground kills the small living things first, before it gets to us. There's a mercy-cart came in from the Last Hearth with live culture in a sealed jar. I need it before my hearth goes cold, because a cold hearth at the pit-mouth is a thing I won't allow. Bring it, and there's a warm loaf in it, saints willing.
bloodstone_pit_yeast_that_died.progress: The mercy-cart from the Last Hearth stands at the road-mouth. Fetch Ana a sealed jar of live culture.
bloodstone_pit_yeast_that_died.complete: It lives -- look, it's already working the flour, poor stubborn little thing. A loaf tonight, then, and warm, and grey be damned. You know why I fight for a hearth at the mouth of a grave? Because the ground down there wants everything cold and counted and quiet, and every warm loaf is a small no. Sit. Eat. Say no with me a while.

objectives.text: Fetch live culture from the mercy-cart
Bring the culture to Ana's hearth

bloodstone_pit_yeast_that_died.note: The yeast died in the warm air, as it does when the ground below decides. Ana got her culture and baked a warm loaf at the mouth of the grave. She calls each loaf a small no. It is the bravest arithmetic at the rim.

bloodstone_pit_the_ground_warms.title: Where the Ground Warms
bloodstone_pit_the_ground_warms.summary: A new warm patch has opened on the west terrace -- the first symptom, the ground making up its mind. Sanda needs it read before it starts to 'listen.' Reach the warm ground, read it, and get clear before it learns your weight.
bloodstone_pit_the_ground_warms.offer: West terrace has a new warm patch since the frost broke. That's the first sign, the ground warming, before the wells and the yeast and the rest of the ladder. If it's a waking node we chalk it and rope it and never stand on it again. I need it read, and I need it read by someone who scans and steps back, not someone who scans and leans in. The leaners don't come back. Reach it, read it, and step clear before it learns your weight.
bloodstone_pit_the_ground_warms.progress: The new warm patch lies on the west terrace. Reach it, read it, and step clear.
bloodstone_pit_the_ground_warms.complete: Green shading toward orange at the edges. Waking, not woke. We've time to rope it, then -- days, maybe. You read it and you stepped back and you didn't lean in, which is the whole skill down here. There's no bravery in the pit, only the discipline of leaving. Take the pouch. Salt won't stop the ground, but it'll mark where not to stand.

objectives.label: the west-terrace warm patch
objectives.text: Read the new warm patch, then step clear

bloodstone_pit_the_ground_warms.note: A new warm patch opened on the west terrace, green shading toward orange -- the ground waking, not yet woke. Sanda roped it and named the only skill the pit rewards: the discipline of leaving before you understand too much.

bloodstone_pit_a_true_accounting.title: A True Accounting of the Rim
bloodstone_pit_a_true_accounting.summary: Voica is writing the rim's chronicle for whoever comes after -- names, not numbers, of the ones the pit took. She needs three witnesses to fill the gaps her own eyes missed. Speak with the innkeeper, the smith, and the merchant, and carry their small true stories back.
bloodstone_pit_a_true_accounting.offer: The stone keeps a perfect record and it is the coldest thing in the world -- every death an entry, closed and filed. So I keep the other kind. Names. What they favored. The stew Ana made them, the grip Ileana forged them, the trinket Marta let them buy on credit they never lived to clear. Imperfect, warm, unclosable. That's the account that spites the pit. Go to the three of them. Gather three small true things. Bring them to me, and I'll write them where the stone can't file them.
bloodstone_pit_a_true_accounting.progress: Speak with Ana the innkeeper, Ileana the smith, and Marta the merchant. Carry back a small true story from each.
bloodstone_pit_a_true_accounting.complete: Three names, three warm imperfect things, three lives the stone would rather have as numbers. I'll ink them tonight. This is the work, you understand -- not the sword, the sword only buys time. The record that can't be finalized is a life remembered too specifically to close. We don't beat the pit. We just refuse to be its arithmetic. Take the craft-book. Learn the keeping of accounts that spite the cold.

objectives.text: Hear Ana's story of one the pit took
Hear Ileana's story of a grip she forged
Hear Marta's story of a debt uncleared

bloodstone_pit_a_true_accounting.note: Three names set down warm and imperfect, where the perfect cold record cannot file them. Voica keeps the other kind of account. The living are not beaten by the pit while someone remembers them too specifically to close.

bloodstone_pit_stanca_wont_leave.title: Stanca Won't Leave
bloodstone_pit_stanca_wont_leave.summary: Rada's sister Stanca has taken to standing at the terrace edge, head tilted, listening -- the third-to-last rung of the ladder. Rada is terrified. Go to Stanca, talk her back from the edge, and see how far the listening has taken her.
bloodstone_pit_stanca_wont_leave.offer: My sister stands at the terrace edge now. Head tipped, quiet, listening to something that isn't there -- and I know what that means, everyone at the rim knows, it's on the ladder right before the end. She was fine a week ago. She laughed at Marta's jokes. Please -- go to her. Talk to her. Get between her and whatever she's hearing. If she still knows your face, there's time. If she doesn't... don't tell me tonight. Just get her back from the edge.
bloodstone_pit_stanca_wont_leave.progress: Stanca stands listening at the terrace edge. Go to her, speak, and see whether she still knows a living voice.
bloodstone_pit_stanca_wont_leave.complete: She turned. She looked at me and she knew me -- said my name, even, though she couldn't say what she'd been hearing. Partial, then. Listening, but not gone all the way to knowing. We keep her from the edge and we keep her talking and we keep her ours. That's all we can do -- keep the ones we love partial, and never let them finish understanding. Thank you. She'd have finished, if you hadn't come between.

objectives.text: Talk Stanca back from the listening edge
Tell Rada that Stanca still knows a face

bloodstone_pit_stanca_wont_leave.note: Stanca stood at the edge, listening -- the rung before the last. She turned when a living voice called and knew her sister's name. Partial, not finished. At the rim, love is the work of keeping the ones you hold from ever understanding all the way.

bloodstone_pit_the_grave_sweepers_round.title: The Grave-Sweeper's Round
bloodstone_pit_the_grave_sweepers_round.summary: Every dawn the deep-wights climb a little higher, and every dawn someone must walk them back down. Dorica runs the round; today she needs a second spade. Put down the night's climbers before the rim wakes.
bloodstone_pit_the_grave_sweepers_round.offer: Same round as every morning. The working dead climb in the dark -- always a few more than yesterday, never fewer -- and if they reach the rim while folk are sleeping, the folk stop sleeping permanent. I walk it at first light with a spade and a lantern. Today I want a second spade. Six climbers should be waiting by the shaft-mouth. Walk them down. Then coffee, if Ana's yeast held.
bloodstone_pit_the_grave_sweepers_round.progress: The night's climbers wait by the shaft-mouth. Put down six before the rim wakes.
bloodstone_pit_the_grave_sweepers_round.complete: Six, and the rim slept through it, which is the whole point -- they'll never know how close the dark came, and that's the kindness. Same time tomorrow. There's always a tomorrow at the rim, and always six, and the coffee's on me when the yeast holds. Off you go. And thank you. The round's shorter with two spades.

objectives.text: Walk the night's climbers back down

bloodstone_pit_the_grave_sweepers_round.note: Six climbers walked down before the rim woke. Dorica runs the round every dawn -- always a few more, never fewer -- so the sleepers never learn how close the dark came. The kindness is that they will never know.

bloodstone_pit_marks_where_none_stand.title: Marks Where None Should Stand
bloodstone_pit_marks_where_none_stand.summary: The chalk wards on the warm-ground rope-lines fade by nightfall, and folk forget where not to stand. Sanda re-chalks the danger-marks each evening. Refresh the ward-marks along the terrace and keep the sleepwalkers off the listening ground.
bloodstone_pit_marks_where_none_stand.offer: Chalk fades. The wards on the rope-lines, the handprints, the do-not-stand marks -- gone by dusk, and then some tired soul crosses a warm patch in the dark and starts listening and we lose them by dawn. I re-chalk every evening. It's small work and it never ends, which is most work worth doing. Take the ward-chalk. Refresh the marks along the terrace. You'll have kept someone off the ground and never know who.
bloodstone_pit_marks_where_none_stand.progress: Chalk the fading ward-marks along the terrace rope-lines before dusk.
bloodstone_pit_marks_where_none_stand.complete: Marked and clear again -- another night the sleepwalkers stay off the warm ground. You kept someone alive tonight and you'll never meet them, never be thanked, never know their name. That's the honest shape of good work at the rim. Off you go. The chalk fades by dawn, and we do it again. We always do it again.

objectives.text: Refresh the ward-marks along the terrace

bloodstone_pit_marks_where_none_stand.note: The ward-chalk refreshed along the rope-lines, and the sleepwalkers kept off the warm ground one more night. Sanda re-marks it every dusk because chalk fades and good work never ends. Someone lived tonight, unknowing, unthankful, safe.

bloodstone_pit_the_leaning_note.title: The Leaning Note
bloodstone_pit_the_leaning_note.summary: A note came up the road addressed 'to whoever reads this' -- the kind that arrives when the pit wants to be read. Ana kept it sealed and wants it destroyed, not carried into the settlement. Take it to the smith's forge and burn it unread, ash tells no one anything.
bloodstone_pit_the_leaning_note.offer: This came with the last cart, tucked in the oats. No name on it but 'to whoever reads this.' You know what kind of letter arrives addressed to whoever's curious enough. I've kept it face-down in a lead cup since. I don't want it in my inn, I don't want it read, I want it ash. Ileana's forge runs hot enough. Carry it there sealed and burn it, and don't you dare unfold it on the way. Ash tells no one anything. That's the whole trick of the thing.
bloodstone_pit_the_leaning_note.progress: Carry Ana's sealed note to Ileana's forge and burn it unread.
bloodstone_pit_the_leaning_note.complete: Burned, and you never read it -- I asked Ileana to watch you and she said you fed it in folded, face-down, like a person with sense. Good. Whatever it wanted to tell whoever read it, it can tell the ash now. My grandmother had it right about wells and copper, and I've made my own saying for letters: when it's addressed to your curiosity, feed it to the fire. Here. A warm cup, for keeping your eyes down.

objectives.label: Ileana's forge
objectives.text: Carry the sealed note to the forge
Burn the note unread at the forge

bloodstone_pit_the_leaning_note.note: A note came addressed to whoever read it, and Ana fed it to the forge unopened. Ash tells no one anything -- the oldest wall against the pit, and the cheapest. When a letter is addressed to your curiosity, burn it face-down.

bloodstone_pit_the_dig_masters_ledger.title: The Dig-Master's Ledger
bloodstone_pit_the_dig_masters_ledger.summary: A drowned ledger surfaced in the shaft-water -- the old dig-master's tally of every debt the pit was owed. Three parties want it: Marta to burn it, the Accord to file it, the rim-watch to read one name. Recover it from the working dead and decide who holds it.
bloodstone_pit_the_dig_masters_ledger.offer: The shaft flooded and coughed up a book. The old dig-master's ledger -- six hundred years of who owed the pit their bodies, all in a dead hand. It's worth something to somebody, always is. The Accord wants it filed. Voica wants to read one name in it -- her grandmother's, she thinks. And I want it burned before it draws more of the wrong sort. The working dead have it now, down at the flooded face. Fetch it up. Then you decide who I sell your trouble to. Or whether I sell it at all.
bloodstone_pit_the_dig_masters_ledger.progress: The drowned ledger is on the flooded face among the working dead. Recover it, then choose its keeping.
bloodstone_pit_the_dig_masters_ledger.complete: You brought it up dripping and you've a choice on your hands, same as I did. Burn it and the pit loses a claim-book but Voica never reads her grandmother's name. File it with the Accord and it's safe and cold and used against someone in a room you'll never see. Read Voica her one name and the rest stays a loaded thing at the rim. There's no clean pick here. There never is with a ledger. Whatever you chose, I'll not tell it twice. Take your cut.

objectives.text: Recover the drowned ledger from the flooded face
Decide who keeps the ledger

bloodstone_pit_the_dig_masters_ledger.note: The dig-master's drowned ledger came up out of the shaft -- six hundred years of debts owed to the pit. Three parties wanted it and none of them cleanly. However it was decided, a ledger never leaves a room the way it entered. Marta took her cut and told it no more.

bloodstone_pit_the_writ_of_unfiling.title: The Writ of Unfiling
bloodstone_pit_the_writ_of_unfiling.summary: A finalized district near the rim went quiet and cold, every door shut, every name closed. Voica has copied a writ of unfiling -- a present-tense truth that reopens a closed record. Use it on the horrors holding the district shut and reopen what the stone finalized.
bloodstone_pit_the_writ_of_unfiling.offer: There's a stretch of old dig-quarters past the shaft that went finalized -- doors shut, hearths cold, every soul in it closed and filed by the stone. Resolved, the Collector would call it. But resolved things can be unresolved. I've copied a writ -- a present, imperfect, still-happening truth, worded to unfile. The horrors are what hold the district closed; they're the seal on the file. Break the seal with the writ. Reopen the district. Understand what it costs first: unfiling here just moves the closing somewhere else. Mercy is a transfer, not a cancellation.
bloodstone_pit_the_writ_of_unfiling.progress: Bloodstone horrors seal the finalized district. Use the writ of unfiling to reopen it -- break seven seals.
bloodstone_pit_the_writ_of_unfiling.complete: Seven seals broken, and the district's doors are ajar again -- a few faces flickered back, a hearth caught. And you felt the cost, I know you did: somewhere down-slope, something else went cold to pay for it. That's the law of mercy at the rim. It doesn't cancel the debt. It carries it. We reopen a district and the Orange Fog creeps a street closer, and we do it anyway, because a flickering face is worth a street. I think. Most days I think that. Take the writ. You'll need to have held one, before the end.

objectives.text: Break the seals holding the district closed

objectives.label: the finalized district
objectives.text: Reopen the finalized district

bloodstone_pit_the_writ_of_unfiling.note: Seven seals broken, a finalized district reopened, a few faces flickered back to warmth. And down-slope something went cold to pay for it. Mercy at the rim is a transfer, never a cancellation. Voica reopened the doors anyway. Most days she thinks that was right.

bloodstone_pit_wardens_watch.title: The Warden's Long Watch
bloodstone_pit_wardens_watch.summary: Something has been leaving the rim-watch small offerings on the cold stone at night -- coins, a comb, a child's shoe, laid out in a straight even line. Stand the night watch at the offering-stone and see what leaves them, and whether it means the rim well or ill.
bloodstone_pit_wardens_watch.offer: Every few nights now, something's left on the cold stone by the second post. Small things. A coin. A bone comb. Once a child's shoe, and none of us has a child at the rim. Laid out neat, in a straight even line, the way the stolen coins were laid in the old town's tale, if you've heard it. I want to know what leaves them. Stand the watch tonight at the offering-stone. Don't touch what's there. Just watch, and mark whether it comes as a friend or as a lure.
bloodstone_pit_wardens_watch.progress: Reach the offering-stone by the second post and stand the night watch. Watch what comes, and touch nothing.
bloodstone_pit_wardens_watch.complete: You stood it. And you saw -- a wight, one of the working dead, come up out of the dark and set down a coin and go back, gentle as you like, and lay it in the line with the others. Not a lure. A habit. It's still doing its old pay-day, six hundred years dead, leaving its wages on the stone for a family that's dust. That's the worst of it, down here -- not the malice. The habits that outlived their reasons. Take the token. You watched, and you didn't touch. That's the whole of it.

objectives.label: the offering-stone
objectives.text: Stand the night watch, and touch nothing

bloodstone_pit_wardens_watch.note: A working wight came up in the dark and laid a coin on the cold stone, in a straight even line, the way it left its wages six hundred years alive. Not a lure. A habit that outlived its reason. That is the worst of the pit -- not the malice, the loyalty with nowhere left to go.

bloodstone_pit_bind_the_shaft.title: Bind the Broken Shaft
bloodstone_pit_bind_the_shaft.summary: The old west shaft has a cracked frame and the working dead push through it a dozen a night. Ileana has forged new bracing but her back won't take it down. Haul the thread-binding down and shore the frame -- honest work that keeps the dark where it belongs.
bloodstone_pit_bind_the_shaft.offer: Enough grim talk. Here's a job with a hammer in it. The west shaft's frame is cracked and the dead shove through the gap -- a dozen a night, and it doubles when the wind's warm. I've forged binding to shore it. Good binding, proper work, none of your chalk-and-prayer. My back's done, though, and the young ones are on the round. Haul it down, brace the frame, and we've one less hole in the dark. Simple work. I like simple work. There's precious little of it left down here.
bloodstone_pit_bind_the_shaft.progress: Haul the thread-binding to the cracked west shaft and shore the broken frame.
bloodstone_pit_bind_the_shaft.complete: Braced, plumb, and tight -- that's a frame that'll hold a season, and a dozen fewer dead a night at the rim. Feel that? That's the honest ache of work that stayed done. Not everything down here unravels the moment you finish it. Some of it just holds, because you made it hold. Take the pot-hook -- I forged it same as the bracing, to last. Come back when there's more honest iron to swing. There always is.

objectives.text: Haul bracing to the west shaft

objectives.label: the cracked west shaft
objectives.text: Shore the broken frame

bloodstone_pit_bind_the_shaft.note: The west shaft braced plumb and tight, a dozen fewer dead a night. Ileana likes simple work because there is precious little left down here that stays done. This did. Some things hold because a smith made them hold.

bloodstone_pit_the_incurious_draught.title: The Incurious Draught
bloodstone_pit_the_incurious_draught.summary: Marta brews a draught that dulls the curious itch -- the one that makes folk lean toward inscriptions they shouldn't. Her supply of the calming reading-draught ran dry. Collect the reagents from the horrors' leavings so she can keep the rim's curiosity safely bored.
bloodstone_pit_the_incurious_draught.offer: My best seller down here, and I'm ashamed of neither the trade nor the price: a draught that makes you incurious. Dulls the itch that makes a body lean toward a warm inscription and read it all the way to dead. I sell more of it than bread. But my reagent's gone -- the blue reading-draught base, it settles out of the horrors' leavings if you know where to skim. Bring me a few and I'll keep the rim comfortably bored. Bored is alive, down here. Bored is the goal.
bloodstone_pit_the_incurious_draught.progress: Skim the reading-draught base from bloodstone horrors. Bring Marta four to restock the incurious draught.
bloodstone_pit_the_incurious_draught.complete: Four -- that's a month of keeping fools uncurious, myself included. You'd think I'd be out of business, selling boredom at the edge of the most interesting hole in the world. But that's exactly the trade. Everything down here wants to be understood, and understanding is the wound. I sell the cure for wanting to know. Here's your cut, and a bottle for yourself. Drink it before you go near the deep face. Trust a merchant on this one thing.

objectives.text: Skim reading-draught base from the horrors

bloodstone_pit_the_incurious_draught.note: Marta restocked the draught that dulls the curious itch -- her best seller at the mouth of the most interesting hole in the world. Down here, understanding is the wound, and she sells the cure for wanting to know. Bored is alive. Bored is the goal.

bloodstone_pit_the_veil_thins.title: Where the Veil Thins
bloodstone_pit_the_veil_thins.summary: Below the tomb the Veil that holds the sleeping thing thins whenever the buried queen feels emptiness instead of rage. Voica needs the thinning measured. Reach the place where the Veil bleeds violet, and learn how close the sub-terrestrial dark has come to bleeding through.
bloodstone_pit_the_veil_thins.offer: There's a membrane below the tomb -- old sorcery, the Veil, holding back what feeds on patience. It's held six hundred years on one thing: the queen's contempt. Her fury is the wall. When she feels grief instead, it thins, and something with no name leans through. I need the thinning measured before we go all the way down. Reach where it bleeds violet -- that's the sub-terrestrial reading, the dark under the dark. Tell me how thin. And do not, whatever you feel down there, try to comfort what's on the other side.
bloodstone_pit_the_veil_thins.progress: The Veil bleeds violet below the tomb. Reach it and measure how far the dark has come through.
bloodstone_pit_the_veil_thins.complete: Violet, and bright -- brighter than my last reading. Thinner. Every kindness anyone's shown that queen in six hundred years has thinned it a hair, and every cruelty's kept it whole. That's the trap the stone built: to save the world we have to keep her furious, and to free her we have to open the door. There's no version where everyone walks away. I'm sorry you had to read that in violet. Take the veil-cloth. It steadies the sight, and lately the sight needs steadying.

objectives.label: the thinning Veil
objectives.text: Measure the thinning of the Veil

bloodstone_pit_the_veil_thins.note: The Veil below the tomb bleeds violet, and brighter than before -- thinner. Six hundred years of the queen's fury has held the dark back; every mercy shown her thins it. To keep the world safe is to keep her furious. There is no version where everyone walks away.

bloodstone_pit_marks_of_the_border.title: The Marks of the Border
bloodstone_pit_marks_of_the_border.summary: The Accord has sent a marked map showing which rim-quarters they mean to abandon and which to hold -- and the rim-watch's homes fall on the wrong side of the line. Sanda wants the map read and the line understood before the Accord's decision reaches them as an order.
bloodstone_pit_marks_of_the_border.offer: A rider from the Accord left this and didn't stay for questions. A marked map -- their border for the rim, which quarters they'll hold and which they've written off. My house is on the wrong side of their line. So's Ana's inn, and the offering-stone, and half of everything we've bled to keep. I want to understand it before it arrives dressed as an order. You've the eye for the Accord's kind of paper. Read me the line. Tell me plainly which of us they've already filed as loss.
bloodstone_pit_marks_of_the_border.progress: Study the Accord's marked map. Read the abandon-line and learn which quarters they've written off.
bloodstone_pit_marks_of_the_border.complete: So they mean to hold the deep gate and let the rim-village go. Us, the inn, the offering-stone -- loss, acceptable, filed. Cold arithmetic in a warm room a hundred leagues off. Well. They can draw their line. We'll keep standing on the wrong side of it, because someone has to hold the ground they've written off, and we're the someone. Thank you for reading it plain. A hard truth's still better than an order you didn't see coming. Keep the map. Know where they think the world ends.

objectives.text: Recover the Accord's marked map
Read Sanda the abandon-line

bloodstone_pit_marks_of_the_border.note: The Accord's map draws a line: hold the deep gate, write off the rim-village. Sanda's home, Ana's inn, the offering-stone -- filed as acceptable loss in a warm room a hundred leagues away. The rim will keep standing on the wrong side of it. Someone has to hold the ground the mapmakers gave up.

bloodstone_pit_the_boys_first_round.title: The Boy's First Round
bloodstone_pit_the_boys_first_round.summary: Marta's boy is old enough now for the dawn round, and Stanca -- steadier since she was called back from the edge -- means to teach him the way she was taught. She asks you to walk his first round beside him, so his first lesson is that no one walks the pit alone.
bloodstone_pit_the_boys_first_round.offer: Marta's boy turned old enough for the round, which at the rim means old enough to see the dead up close. I'll teach him -- I'm steadier now, since you and Rada pulled me back from the edge, and teaching's how I say thank you to the world for that. But a first round wants three, not two. Walk it with us. Let his first lesson be the true one: nobody walks the pit alone, not the smallest wight-round, not the last descent. Nobody. That's the only rule that's ever kept anyone.
bloodstone_pit_the_boys_first_round.progress: Walk the boy's first dawn round with Stanca. Put down five climbers, and teach the rule by keeping it.
bloodstone_pit_the_boys_first_round.complete: Five down, and the boy's hands only shook a little, and he never once had to face a wight alone -- there was always a shoulder beside his. That's the lesson. Not how to swing. How to not be alone while you swing. He'll teach it to the next one someday, and they to the next, and that's how a rim survives at the mouth of a grave: we hand down company, one round at a time. Thank you for standing the third spot. Here -- the cord's his gift, from all of us.

objectives.text: Walk the boy's first round beside him
Close the round with Stanca and the boy

bloodstone_pit_the_boys_first_round.note: Marta's boy walked his first round and never faced a wight alone. Stanca taught the only rule that keeps anyone: nobody walks the pit alone. It is handed down one round at a time, company against the dark, and it is how a rim survives at the mouth of a grave.

bloodstone_pit_the_ground_says_your_deed.title: The Ground Says Your Deeds
bloodstone_pit_the_ground_says_your_deed.summary: At the deepest ledge the stone chalks a line addressed not to your name but to your deeds -- the buried knife, the walked-home girl, the burned page. Voica cannot go; she is too curious. Go down, read what the stone has written of you, and prove you can read it and leave.
bloodstone_pit_the_ground_says_your_deed.offer: The stone has started writing at the deep ledge. Not runes -- plain words, in chalk, in the flat clerical voice, and they're addressed to you. Not your name. Your deeds. Something you buried, someone you walked home, a page you fed to a fire. It knows you by what you've done, the way it knew the courier, the way it knows the queen. It means to offer you rest. To tell you you've carried enough, and the ground is warm, and you could set it all down. Go and read it. Then do the one thing that beats it. Leave. Come back up and tell me you left.
bloodstone_pit_the_ground_says_your_deed.progress: The stone's chalked address waits at the deep ledge. Read what it has written of your deeds -- then climb back out.
bloodstone_pit_the_ground_says_your_deed.complete: You read it and you climbed out. I can see on you what it offered -- rest, completion, an end to the carrying. It always offers rest; a threat would be hope, and it deals in the other thing. But you set the offer down instead of yourself, and that refusal is the whole war in one small motion. The stone knows your deeds now. Good. Let it. A record of what you've done is only a weapon if you'd rather it were forgotten -- and you wouldn't, would you. Carry the razor back up. You'll take it all the way down, when the day comes.

objectives.label: the deep ledge
objectives.text: Read the stone's address to your deeds

objectives.label: the rim-climb
objectives.text: Climb back out and refuse the rest

bloodstone_pit_the_ground_says_your_deed.note: The stone wrote to the player by their deeds -- the buried knife, the walked-home girl, the burned page -- and offered rest. The offer was refused; the climb was made. A threat would be hope. The stone deals in rest, and the war against it is the small motion of setting the offer down instead of yourself.

bloodstone_pit_the_pause_holds.title: The Pause Holds
bloodstone_pit_the_pause_holds.summary: The rim keeps a small feast on the night the descent-parties gather -- warm bread, the good stew, one lantern per soul who ever walked down. Ana asks you to gather the rim-folk to the hearth so the Pause is marked not with silence but with a warm loud room.
bloodstone_pit_the_pause_holds.offer: The night before a descent, I set the long table. Warm bread -- the yeast held, saints be thanked -- the good stew, and a lantern lit for every soul who ever walked down that grave-road, come back or not. The stone wants us cold and counted and quiet. So we get warm and loud and unaccountable. Go round them all up -- Dorica off her round, Ileana from the forge, all of them -- and bring them to the hearth. The Pause isn't held with silence. It's held with a full room saying no.
bloodstone_pit_the_pause_holds.progress: Gather the rim-folk to Ana's hearth for the descent-eve feast. Speak to each and bring them in.
bloodstone_pit_the_pause_holds.complete: Look at them. All of them, warm and loud and eating my grey-no-longer bread, every lantern lit, every name at the table said out loud where the stone can't file it. That's the Pause, friend. Not a victory -- there's no victory down here, only the holding. The stone paused the world and we pause right back, one warm loud night at a time, and we mean to still be pausing when you come back up from the last descent. You will come back up. Say it with me. Then sit. Eat. The stew's the good one tonight.

objectives.text: Bring Dorica in from her round
Bring Ileana from the forge

objectives.label: Ana's hearth
objectives.text: Gather the rim to the descent-eve feast

bloodstone_pit_the_pause_holds.note: On descent-eve the rim gathered at Ana's hearth -- warm bread, the good stew, a lantern for every soul who walked the grave-road. The stone wants the world cold and counted and quiet. The rim gets warm and loud and unaccountable. The Pause is not held with silence. It is held with a full room saying no.

bloodstone_pit_the_copper_wellstone.title: The Copper in the Wellstone
bloodstone_pit_the_copper_wellstone.summary: The rim's one clean well has started to taste of copper -- the second rung of the ladder, right behind the warming ground. Rada draws water for the whole settlement. Bring her a warding-jar from the deep-face leavings so the well stays drinkable one more season.
bloodstone_pit_the_copper_wellstone.offer: The well's gone copper. Just at the bottom of the bucket, just a taste, but I know that taste -- everyone at a rim knows it, it comes right after the ground warms. If it turns all the way, we haul water from the mercy-cart and the mercy-cart's a week between visits and I've the whole rim to keep watered. There's a warding-jar you can steep from the horrors' leavings that holds the copper back a while. It's not a cure. Nothing here is. But a while is what we trade in. Bring me one.
bloodstone_pit_the_copper_wellstone.progress: Steep a marshlight warding-jar from the deep-face leavings and bring it to Rada's well.
bloodstone_pit_the_copper_wellstone.complete: Sunk it down the shaft and the taste pulled back -- clean, or clean enough, for a season maybe. A season's a fortune, down here. I know what it means, mind. Ground warmed, well went copper, and next it'll be the yeast, and then folk start standing at edges, listening -- I watched it climb that ladder in my own sister. But we hold each rung as long as we can hold it, and this rung's held. Thank you. Drink first, if you like. It's clean today.

objectives.text: Steep a warding-jar from the deep-face leavings
Sink the warding-jar in Rada's well

bloodstone_pit_the_copper_wellstone.note: The rim's clean well went copper -- the second rung of the ladder. A warding-jar held the taste back a season. Rada has watched the ladder climb all the way, in her own sister. The rim holds each rung as long as it can hold it. This rung held.

bloodstone_pit_the_striker_and_the_stone.title: The Striker and the Stone
bloodstone_pit_the_striker_and_the_stone.summary: Ileana has forged an obsidian razor keyed to the stone's own grammar -- a blade that carries a present truth instead of an edge. To finish it she needs blackglass shed by the deep-face horrors. Gather it, and help her make the one weapon the pit respects.
bloodstone_pit_the_striker_and_the_stone.offer: I've made blades my whole life and none of them ever meant anything to the thing down there -- steel's just steel to a record. But I've been listening to Voica, and I think I can forge a different kind of edge. The horrors shed blackglass at the deep face when you spoil their closing -- the stone's own grammar, cooled and cast off. Bring me a good measure and I'll knap it to a razor that carries a truth, not a cut. A blade the pit can't file. Won't be pretty. Won't need to be.
bloodstone_pit_the_striker_and_the_stone.progress: Gather blackglass shed by the deep-face horrors and bring it to Ileana's forge.
bloodstone_pit_the_striker_and_the_stone.complete: Enough, and good and dark -- look how it drinks the lantern. I'll knap it slow, the proper way, no shortcuts, because a rushed edge on this stuff shatters and takes a hand with it. When it's done it'll carry one present truth into whatever it touches and keep that truth from closing. The first honest weapon against the pit, made from the pit's own leavings. There's a grim justice in that I almost like. Take the striker meanwhile. Fire's still the oldest edge we've got.

objectives.text: Gather shed blackglass at the deep face
Bring the blackglass to Ileana

bloodstone_pit_the_striker_and_the_stone.note: Blackglass gathered from the deep-face horrors and given to Ileana's forge -- the stone's own grammar, to be knapped into a blade that carries a truth instead of a cut. The first honest weapon against the pit, made from the pit's own leavings. She almost likes the justice in it.

bloodstone_pit_what_the_horror_wore.title: What the Horror Wore
bloodstone_pit_what_the_horror_wore.summary: The horrors at the deep face wear shapes assembled from unpaid deaths -- and lately one has surfaced wearing a keepsake Voica recognizes. She needs the token recovered from the things below, to learn whose grief the pit has been wearing. The answer is not one she wanted.
bloodstone_pit_what_the_horror_wore.offer: The horrors down there aren't shapeless -- they're assembled, cobbled together out of every death a ledger left unpaid. Rags, bone, a debt so old the body's a rumor. But one of them surfaced wearing a bound keepsake I know. My mother's, I think. Buried with her, forty years back, up in Gravemark. If the pit's wearing her grief now, I need to know how it got here, and I need the keepsake back off the thing that's wearing it. Cull enough of them at the deep face and it'll shed. Bring me what it wore.
bloodstone_pit_what_the_horror_wore.progress: Cull the deep-face horrors until the wearing one sheds. Recover the bound keepsake for Voica.
bloodstone_pit_what_the_horror_wore.complete: It's hers. Forty years in Gravemark ground and here it is at the bottom of a pit six kingdoms south, worn by a thing made of debts. You know what that tells me? The stone doesn't keep its accounts in one place. It moves them. Grief is portable to a machine that files it. I'll bury this again, proper, with a name on the stone this time -- a name is the one thing it can't move, because a name is warm and specific and still ours. Thank you for bringing her back. Twice now, I've buried her. This time I'll say the words out loud.

objectives.text: Cull the deep-face horrors until it sheds
Recover the keepsake the horror wore

bloodstone_pit_what_the_horror_wore.note: A horror at the deep face wore Voica's mother's keepsake, buried forty years north in Gravemark ground. The stone moves its accounts; grief is portable to a machine that files it. Voica buried her mother a second time, with a name cut in the stone -- the one warm thing the pit cannot move.

bloodstone_pit_the_last_descent.title: The Last Descent
bloodstone_pit_the_last_descent.summary: The descent-party is ready and the way down is barred by everything the pit can raise -- working dead, records that will not close, and the first unpaid account itself. Cut the road open to the deepest ledge, so the ones carrying the counter can reach the stone. Not a victory. A door held open.
bloodstone_pit_the_last_descent.offer: This is it. The party's assembled, the counter's proven, the blade's knapped, the queen's read and grieved. All that's left is the road down, and the road down is barred by everything the stone can lift against us -- the working dead thick as flies, the horrors sealing every gate, and the first debt itself waiting at the bottom of the deep shaft. I need the road cut open to the deepest ledge. I'm coming with you -- nobody walks the pit alone, least of all this walk. Clear it. Hold the door. Let the ones with the counter reach the stone. We don't win down there. We hold. Holding's the whole war.
bloodstone_pit_the_last_descent.progress: Cut the descent open to the deepest ledge. Break the working dead and the horrors, end the First Debt at the shaft-bottom, and reach the ledge where the counter must be carried.
bloodstone_pit_the_last_descent.complete: The road's open, the door's held, and you reached the ledge with the counter still in living hands. This isn't the end -- the stone doesn't end, it pauses, and the pause is the most anyone's managed in six hundred years. You cut the way down for the ones who'll push one small true thing into the thing that ate the world. Small. Temporary. Imperfect. Ours. Take the reaver and the draught -- you've earned the good steel and the good drink both. And when you go down that last shaft: come back up. Say it. Nobody walks the pit alone, and nobody walks it forever. Come back up.

objectives.text: Break the working dead barring the descent
Break the horrors sealing the gates
End the First Debt at the shaft-bottom

bloodstone_pit_the_last_descent.note: The descent was cut open to the deepest ledge and the counter reached the stone in living hands. Not a victory -- the stone does not end, it pauses. The rim held the door so the ones carrying one small true thing could push it into the machine that ate the world. Small, temporary, imperfect, and theirs. The Pause holds.

eastern_ridges_grey_teeth_01.title: The Grey Teeth
eastern_ridges_grey_teeth_01.summary: The ridges have always been the last honest ground before the listening city. Neaga wants a soldier's eyes on the high road east, where the fog has taken to pooling deeper than any winter should allow.
eastern_ridges_grey_teeth_01.offer: You're new to the teeth, so hear it once and remember it. The fog here isn't weather -- it pools where it's told to. It's been sitting shoulder-high on the east road three days running, and my sight-lines end at arm's length. Walk the high road for me. Count what you see. Don't let it count you first.
eastern_ridges_grey_teeth_01.progress: The high road climbs east along the ridge-spine, toward the cleft where Blestem's fog begins. Reach the road's shoulder and mark what the fog is hiding.
eastern_ridges_grey_teeth_01.complete: Waist-deep and standing still in no wind. That's not fog, that's a curtain, and something drew it. I've soldiered these teeth eleven years and never seen it reach this far west. Take the striker -- and keep it dry. In this fog, a lit fire is the only thing that tells the truth.

objectives.label: the high road's shoulder
objectives.text: Walk the fogged high road east

eastern_ridges_grey_teeth_01.note: The fog on the east road stands waist-deep in a country with no wind to move it. Neaga has watched these ridges eleven years. She has never once seen the curtain drawn this far from the cleft.

eastern_ridges_grey_teeth_02.title: What the Curtain Hides
eastern_ridges_grey_teeth_02.summary: The fog on the east road does not merely hide the ridge -- it hunts from it. Cliff-Stalkers have learned to move inside the curtain, and Neaga will not send a patrol into ground where she cannot see her own boots.
eastern_ridges_grey_teeth_02.offer: So we agree the fog was drawn on purpose. Fine. Somebody drew it, and somebody's beasts have learned to live in it. Cliff-Stalkers -- they come out of the grey at the throat and go back into it before you've drawn breath. Thin them on the east road. I want the curtain to hold nothing but curtain.
eastern_ridges_grey_teeth_02.progress: The Cliff-Stalkers hunt from inside the fog on the east road. Cut them down where the grey is thickest.
eastern_ridges_grey_teeth_02.complete: Six of them, and every one came out of the fog at the same height off the ground, like they'd measured it. Beasts don't measure. Something's teaching them the trick of the curtain. I'll not say what. Take the pouch -- a salt-line won't stop them, but it reminds the ground there's supposed to be a door.

objectives.text: Thin the Cliff-Stalkers in the fog

eastern_ridges_grey_teeth_02.note: Every Cliff-Stalker struck from the fog at the same height, the way a surveyor keeps a level. Beasts do not measure. Neaga would not say aloud what teaches them the trick.

eastern_ridges_grey_teeth_03.title: The Stone That Woke
eastern_ridges_grey_teeth_03.summary: A basalt outcrop on the north shoulder of the ridge has begun to move. Paraschiva has watched it take three steps in a week -- always east, always toward the cleft -- and she means to put it down before it learns a fourth.
eastern_ridges_grey_teeth_03.offer: There's a black stone on the north shoulder that's grown legs. Don't look at me like that. It's moved three times this week, and every time it faces the cleft after, like a man who's remembered an errand. The old wardens had a word for waking stone. They only ever used it once, and the man who said it went east and didn't come back. Break it while it's still slow.
eastern_ridges_grey_teeth_03.progress: The waking basalt shifts on the north shoulder of the ridge, east toward Blestem's cleft. Bring it down before it walks again.
eastern_ridges_grey_teeth_03.complete: You cracked it open and there were marks inside -- cut clean, angled, never touched by weather because they were never on the outside. Somebody carved the heart of the mountain and let the mountain grow around it. I burned my sketch of them. You should forget you saw them. Here -- the ward's not much, but it means nothing on purpose, and meaning nothing is the whole trick out here.

objectives.text: Bring down the waking basalt

eastern_ridges_grey_teeth_03.note: The waking stone had marks cut in its heart, clean-angled, never weathered because they were never meant to be seen. Paraschiva burned her sketch of them. Whatever was carved into the mountain, the mountain has begun to carry it east.

eastern_ridges_grey_teeth_04.title: The Warden Who Went East
eastern_ridges_grey_teeth_04.summary: Paraschiva's brother walked the cleft road a year ago and never sent word. His last post is out on the far ridge, under a cairn he built himself. She cannot walk out to it. She asks you to, and to tell her the cairn is only a cairn.
eastern_ridges_grey_teeth_04.offer: My brother kept the far post before me. Walked east to check the cleft markers a year gone and never came back down. His cairn's still out there -- he built it himself, superstitious old fool, said a warden should mark his own watch so the ridge knows him. Go and stand at it. If the stones are just stones, come tell me. If they're not... come tell me anyway.
eastern_ridges_grey_teeth_04.progress: The far post cairn sits on the eastern ridge, above where the road drops toward the cleft. Reach it and stand your brother's old watch.
eastern_ridges_grey_teeth_04.complete: You waited a full turn of the glass and nothing came. Good. That's -- that's what I wanted to hear. And the stones lay in a line pointing east, you said. He'd never have built it crooked. He built it straight, toward the cleft, on purpose, the last thing he did. He wasn't lost out there. He was showing me the way he'd gone. Take the lantern. It's the one he left. It still finds the road.

objectives.label: the far post cairn
objectives.text: Stand watch at the far post cairn

eastern_ridges_grey_teeth_04.note: The far post cairn stands straight, its capstones aimed east at the cleft -- the last deliberate act of a warden who walked that way and did not walk back. Paraschiva calls it lost. She knows better. He left her a direction.

eastern_ridges_harpy_wind_01.title: The Old Wind's Hunters
eastern_ridges_harpy_wind_01.summary: Safta keeps the ridge's records, and the oldest of them is the harpies -- megafauna that rode the ridge-thermals long before the kingdoms had names. Their nesting-down is a rite. She would like it counted properly, one more year.
eastern_ridges_harpy_wind_01.offer: Before you go chasing the wrong that's crept into these teeth -- come see something that's only right. The harpies are nesting down for the cold. They rode these thermals when the ridge was young and no man had climbed it, and every autumn they come home to the same crags. My grandmother counted them for the register. Her mother before her. Go up and count for me, and let me write one honest line this year.
eastern_ridges_harpy_wind_01.progress: The harpies roost on the high crags above the ridge road, as they have since before the four kingdoms. Reach the nesting-crags and count the roost for the register.
eastern_ridges_harpy_wind_01.complete: Eighty-one. Down four from my grandmother's best year, but eighty-one is eighty-one, and they came home. Do you know how rare it is out here, to write a number down and have it mean only itself? Take the map -- I marked the good roosts, the honest ones. Not everything in these ridges is a symptom. Hold on to that. You'll want it later.

objectives.label: the nesting-crags
objectives.text: Count the roost on the high crags

eastern_ridges_harpy_wind_01.note: Eighty-one harpies came home to the crags this year, as they have since before the thrones were raised. Safta wrote the number down, and for once the number meant only itself. She told the traveler to remember that. She said they would want it later.

eastern_ridges_harpy_wind_02.title: Come Home Changed
eastern_ridges_harpy_wind_02.summary: The roost Safta counted so proudly has begun to sour. Some of the harpies no longer ride the thermals -- they hang in the updraft facing the cleft, wings still, listening. She needs the changed ones brought down before the change spreads to the true roost.
eastern_ridges_harpy_wind_02.offer: I told you not everything here is a symptom. I was wrong to say it so soon. Some of my harpies have stopped flying. They hang on the wind facing the cleft, wings open, dead still, like they're waiting for a word. The healthy ones won't roost near them anymore. Bring the changed ones down. I'll not have the whole register go strange because I couldn't bear to thin four birds.
eastern_ridges_harpy_wind_02.progress: The changed Ridge-Harpies hang motionless in the updraft, facing the cleft. Bring down the ones that have stopped flying.
eastern_ridges_harpy_wind_02.complete: You said they didn't fight back. Of course they didn't -- they weren't there anymore, not really, just the shape of a harpy holding a listening pose. I've struck a line through them in the register. Four names of birds I knew. The rest still ride the wind, for now. Take the charm -- fold it away and don't read the folk-marks on it. Some wards work best unexamined. So do some people.

objectives.text: Bring down the changed harpies

eastern_ridges_harpy_wind_02.note: Four harpies hung in the updraft facing the cleft, wings open, listening, and did not fight when the traveler came. They were not there to fight. Safta struck a line through them in the register she has kept honest all her life.

eastern_ridges_ledger_road_01.title: A Face on Every Corner
eastern_ridges_ledger_road_01.summary: Maricica trades blackglass up the cleft road to Blestem, and lately every stall she visits already knows her business before she opens her mouth. She wants to know who is reading her -- and asks you to trade blackglass her way while she watches who watches you.
