It rides sealed in the beds, and the ones who guard the night runs aren't teamsters -- they're forge-cult, Ember-Sworn, and they don't sleep. Bring me the manifests off them. I want to see whose name the Forged King is writing at the top of every column.
bloodroad_krunn_second_ledger.progress: The Ember-Sworn escort the night convoys along the upper Bloodroad. Take the sealed manifests from them.
bloodroad_krunn_second_ledger.complete: No name at the top. That's the answer, and it's worse than a name. He's not arming against a king. He's arming for a shape -- one banner, every road pointed the same way. Somebody's teaching a whole war to face one direction. Go careful, friend. I think we just read the first line of it.

objectives.text: Break the night escort
Recover the sealed manifests

bloodroad_krunn_second_ledger.note: The manifests named no enemy. Every column pointed one way, north and inward, like furrows plowed for a single reaping. Lazar filed them where the toll-clerks would never look. A war with no enemy is not a war. It is a rehearsal.

bloodroad_deserters_first_names.title: Names Off the Ledger
bloodroad_deserters_first_names.summary: A man runs from the war-road and is marked 'Gift' in absentia -- his labor owed to the Debt Pit whether his body arrives or not. Radomir's brother is on such a list. He needs the toll-guards distracted long enough to hear where the marked are being hunted.
bloodroad_deserters_first_names.offer: My brother ran. Good sense, that -- but the fort marked him Gift the same hour, and now the dogs go out for anyone the ledger says still owes. I can't ask the guards without being marked myself. Speak to Matei at the toll-gate. Ask about the hunts. Ask like it's idle. I'll be listening from the doorway.
bloodroad_deserters_first_names.progress: Matei keeps the toll-gate. Draw the hunt-routes out of him without letting him mark you for the asking.
bloodroad_deserters_first_names.complete: So they run the marked down the lower bands after dark. And the ledger stays open till the body's found -- alive or Gift, the column closes the same. My brother's a debt now, not a man. I'll get his name bought off it if it takes me everything I own. It might.

objectives.text: Draw the hunt-routes from Matei

objectives.label: the lower bands
objectives.text: Scout where the marked are hunted

bloodroad_deserters_first_names.note: A man who runs is marked Gift the same hour, and the ledger closes on him whether he breathes or not. Radomir learned where they hunt. He did not sleep after.

bloodroad_deserters_currency.title: The Currency Is Other Names
bloodroad_deserters_currency.summary: A name can be bought off the Debt Pit ledger -- but only paid for with another name to take its place. The deserter chief holds the trade. Radomir asks you to carry the offer, knowing what it means to make it.
bloodroad_deserters_currency.offer: There's a way to buy my brother off the ledger. The chief who leads the runners -- he knows the trade. But the price of striking a name off isn't gold. It's a name to write in. Carry this to him. And if you come back and tell me you couldn't do it... I'll understand you the way a decent person understands a decent person. Go.
bloodroad_deserters_currency.progress: The deserter chief keeps his men in the burned toll-station on the middle band. Carry Radomir's offer, and cull the cinder-hounds that scent the marked.
bloodroad_deserters_currency.complete: He took it. Named a man already dead -- says a corpse owes nothing, so let a corpse pay. Clever. Maybe even clean. But the ledger doesn't check if a name still breathes, only that it's written. My brother walks free on a dead man's debt. I should feel lighter than this.

objectives.text: Clear the scent-hounds from the burned station

objectives.label: the burned toll-station
objectives.text: Carry the offer to the deserter chief

bloodroad_deserters_currency.note: A name comes off the ledger only if another goes in. The chief paid with a dead man, and called it mercy. Radomir got his brother back and something he will not name back. The ledger never asks if a name still breathes.

bloodroad_ilion_audit.title: The Auditor Comes North
bloodroad_ilion_audit.summary: Ilion, the executioner-general, audits the road fort by fort in perfect silence, and every skimming toll-sergeant wants to be elsewhere when he arrives. Oprea has skimmed. He needs the audit-stones counted before Ilion reads them -- and Ilion has one question about a grave.
bloodroad_ilion_audit.offer: The auditor's coming up the road, fort by fort, quiet as a closing door. He files everything, that one, and he's never wrong twice. I've -- rounded some tolls in my favor. Count the audit-stones at each post before he does, tell me which don't tally, and I'll fix them clean. And traveler -- he asked after you by a grave you opened once. He didn't say which. He just filed the asking.
bloodroad_ilion_audit.progress: The audit-stones stand at the three toll-posts along the upper road. Count them before Ilion's silence reaches them.
bloodroad_ilion_audit.complete: Two posts short. I'll square them tonight and pray his ledger runs a day behind mine. As for his question about your grave -- he doesn't ask to accuse. He asks because a thing left unresolved itches him like a splinter under the nail. He'll ask again. He always asks again.

objectives.label: the first toll-post
objectives.text: Count the first post's audit-stones

objectives.label: the second toll-post
objectives.text: Count the second post's audit-stones

objectives.label: the third toll-post
objectives.text: Count the third post's audit-stones

bloodroad_ilion_audit.note: Ilion audits fort by fort and never once raises his voice. He asked after a grave the player once opened, then filed the asking under nothing at all. A thing unresolved itches him. He always asks again.

bloodroad_dug_up_stone.title: What the Dogs Faced
bloodroad_dug_up_stone.summary: A war-dog convoy halted on the road, every hound turned to face the ditch, for a count of three. In the ditch a boundary stone sits humming, freshly dug up from below. Pavel keeps the road's chronicle and needs to know what the ground pushed to the surface.
bloodroad_dug_up_stone.offer: I write down what the road does, and last night the road did something I've no word for. A convoy stopped -- the dogs all turned to the ditch, held it three heartbeats, then walked on like nothing. I went and looked. There's a stone down there. Someone dug it up, or it dug itself. It's warm, and it hums, and the dust around it lies in lines. Go and read it for me. Read it -- and then look at your boots and walk away.
bloodroad_dug_up_stone.progress: The humming stone lies in the roadside ditch below the dog-run. Read what surfaced -- and do not stay to understand it.
bloodroad_dug_up_stone.complete: You looked away in time. Good. The ones who don't -- they get a quiet in them after, like a room too big for the words in it. The stone was dug up, not planted. Something under the road is reaching for the air. I'll write it down. Writing it down is the only power I've got, and I'm not certain it's on my side.

objectives.label: the dug-up stone
objectives.text: Read the humming boundary stone
Tell Pavel what surfaced

bloodroad_dug_up_stone.note: The dogs faced the ditch for a count of three, and something under the road was reaching up to meet them. The stone was dug up, not buried. Pavel wrote it down. Writing it down was the only power he had, and he was not sure whose side it was on.

bloodroad_slag_widow.title: The Shift He Still Keeps
bloodroad_slag_widow.summary: Sava's husband died on the road-works and did not stop working. The slag-horrors are dead labor still keeping their shifts, animated by seepage from the north. She cannot bury a man who won't lie down. Lay his shift to rest, and find whose ledger still lists him.
bloodroad_slag_widow.offer: They brought me his boots but not his body, because his body's still out there loading carts. My man died in the cut three weeks back and nobody told his hands. The slag-things on the works -- that's what they are, the dead still on shift. Put him down proper, love. Put them all down. And find out who's still writing his name on the roster, because the dead don't clock themselves in.
bloodroad_slag_widow.progress: The slag-horrors work the road-cut through the night. Lay them to rest, then find the roster that keeps calling the dead to labor.
bloodroad_slag_widow.complete: It wasn't a necromancer. It was a clerk. His name's still on the works-roster because nobody struck it -- and the ground here listens close enough to a written order that it fills the shift itself. He was a debt, and a debt keeps working till it's paid. I'll take his boots home. It's the only part of him the ledger let me keep.

objectives.text: Lay the working dead to rest
Recover the works-roster

bloodroad_slag_widow.note: The dead kept their shifts because no clerk struck their names, and the ground here obeys a written order the way a mule obeys a whip. It was never a necromancer. It was paperwork. Sava took his boots home. The ledger let her keep that much.

bloodroad_forge_water.title: Water for the Long Haul
bloodroad_forge_water.summary: Gavril's road-house is the last clean water before the forge-haze thickens, and the teamsters bless him for it. His cistern's run low with the caravan traffic. Haul spring-water up from the cold seep so a hard road keeps one soft stop.
bloodroad_forge_water.offer: This is the last house on the Bloodroad where the water doesn't taste of the forge, and by the saints I mean to keep it that way. The cistern's low -- too many thirsty caravans, and I won't turn a teamster away dry. There's a cold seep up the goat-track that the haze never found. Fill these from it, would you? A hard road ought to have one honest cup in it.
bloodroad_forge_water.progress: The cold seep hides up the goat-track above the road-house, past where the haze thins. Fill Gavril's jars.
bloodroad_forge_water.complete: Cold and clean and tasting of nothing but stone -- perfect. You should see their faces, the drivers, when the water doesn't bite back. For one cup they forget where they're hauling to. That's worth more than the coin, though here's the coin too. Sit a while. The stew's honest, same as the water.

objectives.text: Fill the jars at the cold seep

bloodroad_forge_water.note: For the length of one clean cup the teamsters forgot the forge they hauled toward. Gavril keeps the last honest water on the Bloodroad, and asks nothing for it but that men remember how water is supposed to taste.

bloodroad_smith_seized.title: The Forge That Wouldn't Ring
bloodroad_smith_seized.summary: Horia mends the caravan gear the war can't wait for, but his good anvil has gone dead -- struck true, it gives no ring, only a flat thud. The metal is listening to something. He needs blackglass off the vent-slopes to re-face it, and a smith who won't ask why steel goes quiet.
bloodroad_smith_seized.offer: A live anvil sings when you hit it -- rings clean, tells you the metal's true. Mine's gone flat. Thud, thud, like striking wet clay, and the steel I draw off it comes out wrong, like it's holding its breath. I need blackglass from the vent-slopes to re-face the horn. Fetch me a good haul. And don't wonder too loud about why an anvil goes deaf. It's catching, that kind of wondering.
bloodroad_smith_seized.progress: Blackglass gathers on the vent-slopes above the forge-cut, where the ember-cult works the fires. Bring Horia enough to re-face his anvil.
bloodroad_smith_seized.complete: That'll do. New face, and it rings again -- listen. Clean as a bell. A smith learns to trust the ring; when it stops, the steel's stopped agreeing to be steel. I don't know what my old anvil was listening to. I know I sleep better with it broken up and buried. Take the blade. It rang the whole time I made it. That still means something to me.

objectives.text: Gather blackglass from the vent-slopes
Drive off the Ember-Sworn at the fires

bloodroad_smith_seized.note: A live anvil rings; a listening one goes flat. Horia's went deaf to the ring and clear to something else. He broke it up and buried it, and slept better, and did not ask what the steel had started agreeing to instead.

bloodroad_cinder_pack.title: The Pack That Runs the Marked
bloodroad_cinder_pack.summary: The cinder-hounds were bred to run down deserters marked Gift, and lately they run whether the fort orders it or not. Matei, who keeps the toll-gate, has stopped sleeping to the sound of them. Thin the pack before it decides a paying traveler smells like a debt.
bloodroad_cinder_pack.offer: We bred the hounds to chase the marked -- Gift-runners, ledger-skips. Fair enough, I told myself, when the fort still held their leashes. But they hunt on their own scent now, and the scent they've taken to lately isn't a runner's. It's just... anyone the road's kept too long. Thin them for me before they can't tell a customer from a debtor. I've a bad feeling I already smell wrong to them.
bloodroad_cinder_pack.progress: The cinder-hound pack ranges the road below the toll-gate after dark. Cut it down before it stops taking orders entirely.
bloodroad_cinder_pack.complete: Quieter tonight. Good. But you saw how they cast about, didn't you -- not hunting a man, hunting a mark, sniffing the road itself for who owes. Nobody set them on that scent. They found it. When the dogs start reading the ledger without being told, that's the ledger reaching past the clerks. Take the pay. Sleep light.

objectives.text: Thin the pack below the toll-gate

bloodroad_cinder_pack.note: The hounds no longer wait for the fort to loose them. They read the road for who owes and run the debt down themselves. Matei watched them cast for a mark that no clerk had written, and understood the ledger had learned to command without a hand.

bloodroad_ember_shrine.title: The Fire That Wasn't Warmth
bloodroad_ember_shrine.summary: The Ember-Sworn tend a roadside shrine-fire that never goes out, and they say it warms the ground for the good of the caravans. But the warmth spreads wrong, and the yeast in Gavril's bread has died. Break up the cult's fire and see what they were really feeding.
bloodroad_ember_shrine.offer: The forge-cult keeps a fire by the third milestone -- says it blesses the caravans, keeps the road from freezing men in the passes. But Gavril's bread won't rise anymore, and the ground three fields around that fire is warm as a fevered brow, and the dust there lies in stripes. That's not blessing. Go put their fire out and dig up what they've been feeding it. I've buried enough this season.
bloodroad_ember_shrine.progress: The Ember-Sworn tend their fire at the third milestone. Scatter them, quench it, and uncover what lies beneath.
bloodroad_ember_shrine.complete: Under the ashes -- a stone, warm and marked, and the fire wasn't warming the road. The road was warming the stone, and the cult was only tending the hearth. Kill the fire and the warmth stays, they say. The ground's made up its mind. Gavril's yeast is dead and won't come back. Small thing to lose. It's how it goes -- the small warm things first.

objectives.text: Scatter the Ember-Sworn and quench the fire
Uncover what the fire was feeding

bloodroad_ember_shrine.note: The cult did not warm the road for the caravans. The road warmed the stone, and the cult only kept the hearth. The fire went out; the warmth stayed. The yeast died first, as the small warm things always do.

bloodroad_chronicle_milestones.title: The Milestones That Moved
bloodroad_chronicle_milestones.summary: Pavel measures the road against the old milestones, and the distances have started disagreeing with his grandfather's survey. Stones that stood a thousand years are a pace off, all leaning the same way. Walk the line and mark which have shifted.
bloodroad_chronicle_milestones.offer: The milestones on this road were set before the forge had a name, and my grandfather surveyed every one. His numbers were gospel. But I paced it last week and the count's wrong -- not much, a stride here, two there, and every stone leaning the same way, like iron filings that found a lodestone. Stones don't walk. Yet the road's a little shorter than it was. Walk the line for me. Mark the ones that moved.
bloodroad_chronicle_milestones.progress: Pavel's grandfather's milestones run the length of the Bloodroad. Walk them and mark which have shifted toward the line.
bloodroad_chronicle_milestones.complete: Nine moved. All leaning north-and-in, toward the forge, toward the Pit. The road's shortening -- not much, a pace a year, but shortening, and everything on it turning to face one point like a congregation. I'll add it to the chronicle. My grandfather measured to know the road. I measure to know it's still where I left it. Lately I'm not sure.

objectives.label: the northern milestones
objectives.text: Mark the milestones that shifted

objectives.label: the southern milestones
objectives.text: Mark the milestones that shifted

bloodroad_chronicle_milestones.note: Nine milestones leaned north-and-in, and the road came up a stride shorter than the grandfather's survey. Stones do not walk, yet the road is shortening, and everything on it turns slowly to face one point. Pavel measures to be sure the road is where he left it. He is no longer sure.

bloodroad_convoy_relief.title: A Cart Gone Off the Line
bloodroad_convoy_relief.summary: A supply cart threw a wheel in the pass and its driver, a boy on his first haul, is stranded with a load the forge is counting on. Lazar can't leave the fort. Recover the cart's iron and get the lad's load tallied before he's marked short.
bloodroad_convoy_relief.offer: Young Tavi threw a wheel in the high pass on his first solo run, poor lad, and if his load comes up short at the count they'll dock him -- or worse, mark him owing. He's a good boy, works hard, laughs easy, the kind the road hasn't taught to stop laughing yet. Fetch his spilled iron back and get it to the counting-post. I'll square his tally so his first haul isn't his last.
bloodroad_convoy_relief.progress: The boy's cart lies broken in the high pass. Recover the spilled bog-iron and carry it to the counting-post before the tally closes.
bloodroad_convoy_relief.complete: Full load, and the tally squared -- Tavi keeps his wage and his good name both. He'll drive this road forty years now, most like, and it'll wear him down slow the way it wears us all. But not today. Today he's a boy who got his cart in on time, laughing about it in the yard. Let him have the laugh. There aren't so many left in him.

objectives.text: Recover the spilled iron from the pass

objectives.label: the counting-post
objectives.text: Deliver the load before the tally closes

bloodroad_convoy_relief.note: The boy got his cart in on time and laughed about it in the yard. The road will wear him down slow, forty years of hauling, the way it wears everyone. But not today. There are not so many laughs left in a man who drives the Bloodroad, and he got to keep this one.

bloodroad_pit_bound_manifest.title: The Column That Reads Collected
bloodroad_pit_bound_manifest.summary: The southbound carts carry the spent -- workers used up on the road, hauled to the Debt Pit to become Gift. Their manifest lists a final column already stamped 'collected' before the carts even reach the rim. Pavel wants proof that a living man rides marked dead.
bloodroad_pit_bound_manifest.offer: Not all the carts go north. The empty-looking ones go south, to the Pit, and they're not empty -- they're carrying the spent, the ones the road used up, off to be worked the last of the way into Gift. Their manifest's got a column stamped before they roll: collected. The word's a thousand years too early, and I've reason to think one of the men under it still breathes. Get me that manifest. Let me prove the ledger buried a living man.
bloodroad_pit_bound_manifest.progress: The southbound Pit-carts assemble at the lower marshalling yard. Recover a manifest and find the man marked dead who still breathes.
bloodroad_pit_bound_manifest.complete: There he is. Third name. Marked collected, and I watched him lift his own head. The ledger decided he was Gift and the carts obey the ledger, not the man. I can't stop the cart. But I can write it down: that on this day, the road called a living man dead, and the road was believed over his own breathing. It's not much. It's what a chronicler has.

objectives.label: the marshalling yard
objectives.text: Reach the southbound Pit-carts
Recover the Pit-bound manifest

bloodroad_pit_bound_manifest.note: The manifest stamped a living man collected, and the carts believed the ledger over his own breathing. Pavel could not stop the cart. He wrote it down instead -- that on this day the road called a breathing man dead, and was believed. It is what a chronicler has.

bloodroad_toll_forgery.title: A Receipt for a Dead Man
bloodroad_toll_forgery.summary: Matei has been passing marked men through the toll on forged receipts -- small mercies, one name at a time. But Ilion's audit is closing in and a forged receipt in the wrong hand is a rope. Retrieve the loose forgeries before the auditor tallies them.
bloodroad_toll_forgery.offer: I've been letting a few of the marked through on paper that doesn't quite exist. Toll-receipts I wrote myself, for men the ledger wanted to keep. Small mercy, one at a time -- but the auditor's coming, and a forged receipt he finds is my neck and theirs. Go collect the ones still loose, out at the way-stations. Bring them to me to burn. I'd rather answer for mercy I can't prove than mercy I left lying around.
bloodroad_toll_forgery.progress: The forged receipts are scattered among the way-stations where Matei passed the marked. Gather them before Ilion's audit finds them.
bloodroad_toll_forgery.complete: All of them? Good. Into the brazier they go. Now there's nothing but the men themselves, walking free somewhere, and no paper to say I helped them. That's the trick of a mercy on this road -- do it, then make sure it can't be read. Ilion files what he can read. What he can't, he only wonders about. I can live with a man wondering.

objectives.text: Gather the loose forged receipts
Bring the forgeries to Matei to burn

bloodroad_toll_forgery.note: Matei burned the receipts so his mercy could not be read. The men walk free somewhere, and no paper says he helped them. On the Bloodroad a kindness survives only if it leaves no record. Ilion files what he can read. What he cannot, he only wonders about.

bloodroad_hounds_provenance.title: The Collar Under the Ash
bloodroad_hounds_provenance.summary: One of the cinder-hounds wore a collar with a name older than the pack -- a war-dog's name from a campaign eighteen years gone. Sava thinks the pack descends from Anara's people's dogs, bred and re-bred by the forge. Recover the old collars and lay the question to rest.
bloodroad_hounds_provenance.offer: I skinned one of the hounds for the fort and found a collar grown into its neck -- old bronze, a name on it, and the name's from before the last war, from the people Valrom put down eighteen years back. These dogs are their dogs' dogs' dogs, bred on and on for the forge. I want the old collars off the pack, love. If a thing's going to be a weapon, it shouldn't have to wear the name of who it used to guard.
bloodroad_hounds_provenance.progress: The cinder-hounds still carry the old collars beneath their ash-fused hides. Recover them from the pack.
bloodroad_hounds_provenance.complete: Six collars, six names, all from a people that isn't anymore. The forge kept their dogs and forgot their dead -- kept the tool, threw out the hand. I'll bury the collars proper, with the names up. Somebody ought to. The dogs don't remember whose they were. That's the mercy in it, maybe. The only one left to go around.

objectives.text: Recover the old collars from the pack
Gather the named collars

bloodroad_hounds_provenance.note: The hounds carry the collars of a people who no longer are, bred on and on for the forge. Sava buried the collars with the names up, because someone ought to. The dogs remember nothing of whose they were. That is the only mercy left to go around.

bloodroad_ilion_grave_question.title: The Auditor's One Question
bloodroad_ilion_grave_question.summary: Ilion has finished the forts and has one loose end he cannot file: a grave the player opened in Act I, and the question of what was in it. He will not accuse. He will only ask, through Oprea, and file the answer -- wherever it leads.
bloodroad_ilion_grave_question.offer: The auditor's done with the forts. Clean, quiet, and he's leaving one thing open, which for him is like leaving a wound unstitched. It's about you. A grave you opened, years and a world ago. He doesn't want you punished -- he wants it resolved, the way a splinter wants pulling. He asked me to bring you to the old survey-cairn at road's end. Just to answer. He files everything, and this one's been open too long.
bloodroad_ilion_grave_question.progress: Ilion waits at the old survey-cairn where the Bloodroad ends and the range begins. Go and answer the one question he has kept.
bloodroad_ilion_grave_question.complete: He heard you out. Wrote nothing down -- first time I've seen it. Just closed the little book and looked north a while. 'Filed,' he said, which from him is almost gentle. Whatever you told him, it fit a shape he'd been holding. He knows something about that grave he's not saying, and now it's connected to you in his ledger. On the Bloodroad, being in Ilion's ledger is the same as being on the road itself. You don't get off. You just find out where it goes.

objectives.label: the old survey-cairn
objectives.text: Answer Ilion at the survey-cairn

bloodroad_ilion_grave_question.note: Ilion asked his one question, heard the answer, and wrote nothing down -- the first time anyone had seen it. He looked north a while and said only, 'Filed.' Whatever the answer was, it fit a shape he had been holding. To be in Ilion's ledger is to be on the road. You do not get off. You find out where it goes.

bloodroad_last_honest_meal.title: The Stew That Asks Nothing
bloodroad_last_honest_meal.summary: Gavril refuses to serve Gift-grown grain in his road-house -- the only table on the Bloodroad where a man can eat without eating the dead. His clean stock runs thin. Gather honest game and mountain roots so his kitchen stays the one meal that asks nothing of you.
bloodroad_last_honest_meal.offer: Everything grows out of the Gift down here -- red soil, red harvest, and every loaf is a body if you ask where the grain slept. I don't ask, and I don't serve it. My stew's made of what I can shoot and dig myself, honest meat and honest root, and it's the one meal on this road that doesn't cost a stranger his grave. Stock's low, though. Bring me game and mountain-root and I'll keep the one clean table lit.
bloodroad_last_honest_meal.progress: Honest game runs the upper slopes and mountain-root grows past the vent-line. Bring Gavril enough to keep the clean kitchen going.
bloodroad_last_honest_meal.complete: Now that's a stew a man can eat and still sleep. No Gift in it, no debt in it, nothing that used to have a name. Just meat and root and salt. You'd be surprised how many drivers weep a little over it, quiet, into the bowl -- first honest thing they've swallowed since the north. Everything good's temporary, friend. That's what makes it good. Eat while it's hot.

objectives.text: Take honest game on the upper slopes
Dig mountain-root past the vent-line

bloodroad_last_honest_meal.note: Gavril keeps the one table on the Bloodroad where a meal is not a body. Drivers weep into the bowl, quiet, over the first honest thing they have swallowed since the north. Everything good is temporary. That is what makes it good. He serves it while it is hot.

bloodroad_slag_foreman.title: Whose Order the Dead Obey
bloodroad_slag_foreman.summary: Striking her husband's name did not stop the slag-horrors -- the whole night-shift still labors, called by a standing order no clerk will rescind because the forge needs the work done. Sava wants the order itself found and broken, cost be what it may.
bloodroad_slag_foreman.offer: I struck his name and he lay down, thank the saints. But the rest of them didn't. There's a standing order over the whole night-cut -- 'the works proceed' -- and no living clerk will cancel it, because the war wants the road widened and the dead work cheaper than the living. Find that order. Break it, if breaking's possible. I can't watch a whole shift of the used-up keep loading carts because a war won't say stop.
bloodroad_slag_foreman.progress: The standing works-order is held at the foreman's post above the night-cut. Break it, and lay to rest the shift it commands.
bloodroad_slag_foreman.complete: Broken. And they stopped -- all of them, mid-stride, and just... sank. Like men allowed at last to be tired. The foreman won't thank you. The war won't. The road'll be a week behind and somebody'll answer for it, and it might be me. Worth it. A dead man deserves to be done working. Somebody on this road had to decide that was worth a week. I'm glad it was us.

objectives.text: Lay the night-shift to rest
Break the standing works-order

bloodroad_slag_foreman.note: The order read 'the works proceed,' and the dead obeyed it because the war would not say stop. Sava broke it, and a whole shift of the used-up sank mid-stride, like men allowed at last to be tired. The road ran a week behind. Somebody had to decide that a dead man deserves to be done. She was glad it was them.

bloodroad_convoy_daily.title: The Day's Loading
bloodroad_convoy_daily.summary: The convoys never stop, and neither does the counting. Lazar pays a day-rate to any pair of hands willing to load the northbound carts and note whose seal rides on each -- honest work, if you don't read the manifests too closely.
bloodroad_convoy_daily.offer: Convoy's in and the fort wants it loaded by dusk. Day-work, if you want it -- haul the iron, note the seals, keep your count against mine. Don't read the manifests. Just count the carts and the crates and tell me the tally. Honest wage for honest lifting, and on this road honest is rarer than the wage. Come back at dusk with your number.
bloodroad_convoy_daily.progress: The northbound carts marshal at the fort yard through the day. Load them, count the seals, and bring Lazar the tally.
bloodroad_convoy_daily.complete: Tallies match. Good hands. Here's the day-rate -- and my thanks for not reading what you weren't asked to read. That's the whole art of surviving Krunn: count the crates, not the cost. Same time tomorrow, if the road's still where we left it.

objectives.text: Load the northbound carts

objectives.label: the fort yard
objectives.text: Bring the tally to Lazar at dusk

bloodroad_convoy_daily.note: Count the crates, not the cost -- the whole art of surviving Krunn. The carts roll north loaded and tallied, and Lazar pays honest for hands that do not read. On the Bloodroad, honest is rarer than the wage.

bloodroad_hound_bounty_daily.title: The Fort's Standing Bounty
bloodroad_hound_bounty_daily.summary: The fort keeps a standing bounty on the cinder-hounds that slip their handlers and range the road. Matei pays per pelt, no questions, and asks only that you don't count how many of the pack there always somehow are.
bloodroad_hound_bounty_daily.offer: Standing bounty, friend: cinder-hound pelts, coin apiece, brought to the toll-gate. They breed faster than we cull, or the forge makes more when we're not looking -- either way there's never fewer. Take what you can off the road and I'll pay per pelt. Don't ask where the fresh ones come from. I stopped asking. It's better for the sleeping.
bloodroad_hound_bounty_daily.progress: The cinder-hounds range the road below the toll-gate. Take pelts and bring them to Matei for the bounty.
bloodroad_hound_bounty_daily.complete: Good pelts, good count, coin's yours. And there'll be as many tomorrow -- there always are. A bounty you can collect forever isn't a bounty, it's a wage, and a wage means the work's meant to go on. I try not to think about what keeps making the work. Come back tomorrow. The work will.

objectives.text: Take cinder-hound pelts for the bounty

bloodroad_hound_bounty_daily.note: A bounty you can collect forever is not a bounty. It is a wage, and a wage means the work is meant to go on. Matei stopped asking what keeps making the hounds. It is better for the sleeping. The work comes back tomorrow. It always does.

bloodroad_pilgrim_south.title: The Ones Who Walk Toward the Heat
bloodroad_pilgrim_south.summary: Entranced pilgrims drift south along the Bloodroad because the forge-heat feels, to a listening mind, like an answer. The Ember-Sworn let them walk on into the vents. Radomir wants them turned back before the road delivers them where they think they want to go.
bloodroad_pilgrim_south.offer: There's folk on the road walking south who've no business here -- eyes gone soft, no packs, no purpose but the warmth ahead. The heat feels like answers to them, poor things, and the forge-cult doesn't stop them. Lets them walk right on into the vents. I can't decide if that's murder or mercy and I've quit trying. Turn back the ones you can. The cult won't like it. Do it anyway.
bloodroad_pilgrim_south.progress: The entranced drift south past the milestones toward the vents. Turn back those you can, and drive off the Ember-Sworn who wave them on.
bloodroad_pilgrim_south.complete: Some turned back. Blinked, like waking, asked where they were and why -- then set off north again, homeward, before the road could re-catch them. Some wouldn't turn. Walked on past my hands toward the heat, and the cult smiled and let them. I couldn't save those. I'm not sure they were mine to save. The road took them where they were already going. That's the horror of it -- they wanted to.

objectives.text: Drive off the Ember-Sworn shepherds

objectives.label: the vent-approach
objectives.text: Turn back the pilgrims before the vents

bloodroad_pilgrim_south.note: The entranced walk south because the forge-heat feels like an answer, and the cult waves them on into the vents. Radomir turned back the ones he could. The rest walked on toward the heat, smiling, because they wanted to. That is the horror of it. They wanted to.

bloodroad_smith_quiet_steel.title: Steel That Won't Take a Name
bloodroad_smith_quiet_steel.summary: The war-blades coming off the forge-cult's fires won't hold an inscription -- a maker's mark, a name, a ward all slide off the metal as if the steel refuses to be told what it is. Horia wants raw ingots tested before the fort ships blades that answer only to one thing.
bloodroad_smith_quiet_steel.offer: New blades came up from the cult-fires for me to finish, and I can't mark them. Chased a maker's stamp into one and the metal just -- shrugged it off, smooth again by morning. Steel's supposed to keep what you cut into it. This won't take a name. Won't take a ward. It's saving itself for one word, and I don't want to know the word. Fetch me raw ingots off the fires. Let me see how deep the quiet goes.
bloodroad_smith_quiet_steel.progress: The forge-cult's raw ingots cool at the fires past the vent-line. Bring Horia enough to test, and clear the Ember-Sworn tending them.
bloodroad_smith_quiet_steel.complete: Deep as the core, the quiet. I struck every ward I know into these and by the third dawn they were bare again, patient, waiting. Steel that refuses every name but one is a steel being kept for something. I'll not finish these blades. Let the fort call me lazy. A smith who arms a thing he can't name into obedience is a fool, and I've been a lot of things, but I've always known what my own work answered to.

objectives.text: Clear the Ember-Sworn from the fires
Recover the quiet ingots to test

bloodroad_smith_quiet_steel.note: The steel shrugged off every name and every ward, patient, bare by dawn, saving itself for one word Horia would not learn. He refused to finish the blades. Steel that answers only to one thing is being kept for it. He had always known what his own work answered to, and meant to keep it that way.

bloodroad_chronicle_finale.title: A True Accounting of the Bloodroad
bloodroad_chronicle_finale.summary: Pavel means to close his chronicle of the road and send it where it can do some good -- to the Accord, before the range swallows the last honest witness. The stone under the road answers him as he writes. He needs the chronicle carried past the vents to the caravan that climbs into Basaltfang.
bloodroad_chronicle_finale.offer: I've written it all -- the shortening road, the dug-up stones, the dead on shift, the living marked collected. It's the truest account of this road there'll ever be, and it's no good to anyone in my drawer. Carry it up to the last convoy before it climbs into the range. Get it to the Accord. And traveler -- as I wrote the final page, the ground under my chair went warm, and the words on the paper lay themselves in lines I didn't rule. It's read what I wrote. It let me finish. That frightens me more than if it hadn't.
bloodroad_chronicle_finale.progress: The last convoy before the range marshals at the high pass. Carry Pavel's chronicle to it, past the vents and the cult that guard the climb.
bloodroad_chronicle_finale.complete: It's away, then. North to the Accord, if the road lets it arrive -- and the road let me finish, which means either it doesn't fear the truth or it's already spent the truth for something. I've thought on which, and I've decided not to decide. A chronicler writes it down. He doesn't get to know if it helped. That's the last honest thing I have to teach you: do the true thing, send it on, and let the not-knowing be somebody else's. Go safe. The road's a stride shorter than when you came.

objectives.text: Clear the climb of the Ember-Sworn

objectives.label: the high pass convoy
objectives.text: Carry the chronicle to the last convoy

bloodroad_chronicle_finale.note: The chronicle went north to the Accord, if the road let it arrive. The ground warmed as Pavel wrote the last page and laid his words in lines he had not ruled -- it read what he wrote, and let him finish, which frightened him more than silence would have. A chronicler writes the true thing, sends it on, and does not get to know if it helped. The road is a stride shorter than it was.

bloodroad_widows_relief_daily.title: Boots for the Marshalling Yard
bloodroad_widows_relief_daily.summary: Sava collects the boots of the road-dead so their families have something to bury when the bodies become Gift. The slag-horrors wear them still. Each day she asks for a few pairs back, and a few names carried home.
bloodroad_widows_relief_daily.offer: The Pit keeps the bodies, but I keep the boots -- it's what I can give a family when the ground eats the rest. The working dead still wear theirs out on the cut. Bring me back a few pairs, love, and the names if the leather's stamped. I'll see each one goes home. A pair of boots isn't a man. But it's a thing a widow can hold, and I know the worth of that better than most.
bloodroad_widows_relief_daily.progress: The slag-horrors wear the road-dead's boots on the night-cut. Recover a few pairs and bring them to Sava.
bloodroad_widows_relief_daily.complete: Three pairs, three names. I'll walk them home myself, or send them with a driver I trust. It's a small thing, boots. But when the ground takes everything else, small is what's left to give, and I've made my peace with giving small. Come tomorrow. There's always more boots than there ought to be, and always a family waiting to hold one.

objectives.text: Recover the road-dead's boots
Gather boots with names still stamped

bloodroad_widows_relief_daily.note: The Pit keeps the bodies; Sava keeps the boots, because it is what a widow can hold when the ground eats the rest. Three pairs, three names, walked home each day. Boots are not a man. But small is what is left to give, and she has made her peace with giving small.

bloodroad_toll_sergeant_skim.title: The Sergeant's Second Set of Weights
bloodroad_toll_sergeant_skim.summary: A rival toll-sergeant keeps two sets of weights -- one for the ledger, one for his purse -- and means to lay his skim at Oprea's gate before Ilion's audit lands. Oprea needs the false weights recovered and the sergeant's hired Ember-Sworn scattered before the frame sets.
bloodroad_toll_sergeant_skim.offer: There's a sergeant two forts down who weighs every convoy twice -- honest weights for the ledger, light weights for his own pocket -- and now he's fixing to leave the shortfall at my gate when the auditor comes. I'll not swing for another man's greed. Find his false weights, bring them here, and thin the forge-cult muscle he's hired to keep folk from looking. Let the shortfall stay where it was earned.
bloodroad_toll_sergeant_skim.progress: The false weights are hidden at the rival sergeant's post down the road, guarded by hired Ember-Sworn. Recover them and scatter the guard.
bloodroad_toll_sergeant_skim.complete: There they are -- lead cored, filed light, a thief's whole confession in a handful of brass. Now the shortfall points at him, not me, and Ilion's ledger will land where it belongs. That's not justice, mind. That's just the frame pointing the right way for once. On this road you don't get justice. You get the frame turned, if you're quick and you're lucky, and today we were both.

objectives.text: Scatter the sergeant's hired guard
Recover the false weights

bloodroad_toll_sergeant_skim.note: The sergeant kept honest weights for the ledger and light ones for his purse, and meant to leave the shortfall at Oprea's gate. Oprea turned the frame the other way. It was not justice. On the Bloodroad you do not get justice. You get the frame turned, if you are quick and lucky, and this once he was both.

bloodroad_lost_convoy.title: The Convoy That Never Came In
bloodroad_lost_convoy.summary: A whole convoy failed to reach Gavril's road-house -- twelve carts, thirty drivers, and one girl who always stopped for his stew. He fears the worst but hopes for a wheel thrown, a wrong turn. Find where they stopped, and bring back word or bring back what's left.
bloodroad_lost_convoy.offer: Twelve carts due three nights back and not one rolled in. Thirty drivers, and among them a girl -- Doina, she always stopped for a second bowl and never let me refuse the coin. Maybe they threw wheels. Maybe they camped. Tell me they camped. Go out the south fork where they'd have come, and find them. If it's bad, come tell me gentle. If it's not, tell me quick.
bloodroad_lost_convoy.progress: The missing convoy's route runs the south fork below the vents. Find where the twelve carts stopped.
bloodroad_lost_convoy.complete: All twelve, drawn up in a ring off the road -- neat, orderly, like they'd stopped to rest. No wounds. No struggle. The stew still in the pots, cold. And every one of them sitting up, facing south, listening, the way the entranced do. Doina among them. I can't call it a killing. There's no killer. The road just... asked them to stop, and they were tired, and they stopped. I'll set a bowl out for her anyway. It's what we do here. Set a place, and hope.

objectives.label: the south fork camp
objectives.text: Find where the convoy stopped
Lay to rest what the camp became

bloodroad_lost_convoy.note: Twelve carts drawn in a neat ring, no wounds, the stew gone cold in the pots, and every driver sitting up facing south and listening. The road asked them to stop, and they were tired, and they stopped. Gavril set a bowl out for Doina anyway. It is what they do on the Bloodroad. Set a place, and hope.

blestem_forty_pace_welcome.title: The Forty-Pace Welcome
blestem_forty_pace_welcome.summary: The keeper of Blestem's only warm door has watched strangers arrive here for thirty years, and has learned that the ones who last are the ones who learn the streets before the streets learn them. Walk the maze she names, and be seen doing it.
blestem_forty_pace_welcome.offer: You've the look of someone who reads faces. Good. This city reads back. First thing they teach a Blestem child, before letters: never walk in a straight line, never finish a sentence a wall could carry. Go stand in the Lower Market, then the mouth of the Riddler's Quarter -- let the corners take your measure. Come tell me you felt it.
blestem_forty_pace_welcome.progress: The Lower Market is downhill, where the lamps cluster. The Riddler's Quarter is the district that will not hold still on any map. Stand in both.
blestem_forty_pace_welcome.complete: You felt it, then. That itch on the back of the neck that doesn't leave the whole time you're here. Everyone gets a file the day they arrive, dear. Mine's six inches thick and I've never left the inn. Bread's fresh. Eat while it's warm -- warm's the only honest thing in this town.

objectives.label: the Lower Market
objectives.text: Stand in the Lower Market

objectives.label: the Riddler's Quarter mouth
objectives.text: Stand at the Riddler's Quarter

blestem_forty_pace_welcome.note: Aglaia gave the newcomer bread and a warning worth more than bread. In Blestem you arrive already known, and the only choice left is how much you add to the file.

blestem_ledger_reads_forty_four.title: The Ledger Reads Forty-Four
blestem_ledger_reads_forty_four.summary: A generation ago a whole ward of informers was reconciled in a single night, twelve at a time, until the ledger read forty-four -- then the maze itself was rebricked so no street ran to them. The chronicler keeps the count no one else will. She wants it read aloud where the wall stands, by someone the city has not yet silenced.
blestem_ledger_reads_forty_four.offer: I keep the books this city would rather I burned. There was a ward once -- informers who started informing on the informers. They were reconciled in a night, twelve at a time, four turns of the ledger, until it read forty-four and the wall went up over the fourth turn. The address was erased. I have the number. Go to the rebricked ward and say it. Numbers un-said become numbers un-happened, and I will not have that.
blestem_ledger_reads_forty_four.progress: The rebricked wall stands where the maze folds back on itself, north of the Black Spire's shadow. Go, and say forty-four aloud.
blestem_ledger_reads_forty_four.complete: Forty-four. You said it where the mortar could hear. That's not much. But this city buries people twice -- once in the wall, once in the forgetting -- and you just dug the second grave back open. Keep the writ. It unfiles one name. Use it on someone who deserves to be a person again.

objectives.label: the rebricked ward
objectives.text: Reach the wall of the Silenced Forty
Clear the perches above the sealed ward

blestem_ledger_reads_forty_four.note: The ward is still bricked shut, and it always will be. But a stranger stood at its wall and said the number the city spent a generation erasing. Varvara wrote that down too.

blestem_secrets_by_weight.title: Secrets Sold by Weight
blestem_secrets_by_weight.summary: In the Lower Market coin is the soft currency and a good secret is the hard one. A stall-keeper who deals in dossiers has a rival undercutting her prices with stolen files -- and wants the stolen goods recovered before her own name ends up in one.
blestem_secrets_by_weight.offer: You want to know how Blestem works? Watch a market where nobody sells grain. I trade dossiers -- habits, debts, who visits whom after dark. Priced, escrowed, honest as this place gets. But someone's been lifting my files and selling them cheap two stalls down. Bring me back three of my dossiers before my competitor learns what's in the one about her. Then we're square.
blestem_secrets_by_weight.progress: The lifted dossiers move through the Lower Market's back stalls. Recover three before they're read by the wrong ear.
blestem_secrets_by_weight.complete: All three, seals mostly intact. Good. You didn't read them -- I can tell, your face is too calm. Smart. In this market the one who reads is the one who owes. Here's a fair copy of the trade, and a lens my grandmother ground. It shows you the marks on a page that other pages leave. Don't use it on anything that shows orange.

objectives.text: Recover the lifted dossiers

blestem_secrets_by_weight.note: Three dossiers back on Despina's shelf, priced and escrowed once more. She never said whose habits they held. In the Lower Market, that is a kindness.

blestem_where_cazimir_stands.title: Where the Voice Stands
blestem_where_cazimir_stands.summary: A minor court dignitary has spent forty years petitioning a spymaster who speaks only from the stone of the walls, never from a face. He is old now, and wants -- once, before he dies -- to know which wall Cazimir was behind. He is asking the wrong question, and half-knows it.
blestem_where_cazimir_stands.offer: Forty years I've brought petitions to this court, and forty years the answer has come out of the wall. Never a face. You learn to bow to masonry. I'm old, traveler. Before they file me for good I want to stand in the room where he answers and know which stone it was. Ask the guards who post the Spire's inner halls -- Ilinca keeps the watch-roster, Smaranda walks the night side. One of them has stood where I want to stand.
blestem_where_cazimir_stands.progress: Guard Ilinca keeps the Spire's watch-roster; Guard Smaranda walks its night side. Ask them both which wall the voice comes from.
blestem_where_cazimir_stands.complete: They told you the same thing, didn't they. That the voice comes from wherever you're not looking, and that the ones who found the wall were never seen to leave the finding. ...Forty years I wanted to know. You've given me the mercy of knowing I shouldn't. That's worth more than the answer. Take this. A man my age has no use for a warding, and every use for a witness.

objectives.text: Ask Guard Ilinca about the voice in the stone
Ask Guard Smaranda about the voice in the stone

blestem_where_cazimir_stands.note: Dragan stopped asking which wall Cazimir stands behind. He is the rare Blestem petitioner who lived because he chose, at the end, not to know.

blestem_ears_in_the_mortar.title: Ears in the Mortar
blestem_ears_in_the_mortar.summary: The smith who repoints the city's old walls has found something she was never meant to find: the walls that listen are not a figure of speech. There are ears mortared into them. She wants proof pulled from the stone before she is made part of a wall herself for having looked.
blestem_ears_in_the_mortar.offer: I mortar the maze back together where the frost cracks it. Honest trade. But last week I opened a seam near the old ward and found -- I'll say it plain, because plain's all I have -- an ear. Set in the mortar. Cured. Listening. There are more. Bring me three, pulled clean, and a rubbing of the marks cut behind them. I have to know if I've been patching a wall or feeding one.
blestem_ears_in_the_mortar.progress: The listening seams run through the maze's oldest courses. Pull three set ears from the mortar and take a rubbing of what's cut behind them.
blestem_ears_in_the_mortar.complete: Three. Cured proper, the way you'd cure hide. And the marks behind them -- I don't read, but I know deliberate when I see it, and that's deliberate. Someone builds this city to hear. I'll mortar no wall in Blestem again without listening back. Take the razor. Blackglass holds an edge the old walls respect.

objectives.text: Pull the set ears from the mortar
Break the wardens of the listening seam

blestem_ears_in_the_mortar.note: The walls of Blestem listen, and it is not a saying. Casandra has three proofs of it, cured and kept in a box she does not open near lamplight. She patches nothing near the old ward now.

blestem_managed_blankness.title: Managed Blankness
blestem_managed_blankness.summary: The city apothecary compounds a draught the operatives use to keep their faces still under questioning -- nothing on the face, nothing in the hands. Her supply of the binding agent has been cut off, and the operatives who go without it start, quietly, to feel things again. She needs it restocked before one of them slips.
blestem_managed_blankness.offer: You know how a spy keeps a knife at her throat and shows nothing? A little of my work, and a lifetime of practice. I compound the binder that holds a face blank -- managed blankness, they call it in the Quarter, like it's a virtue and not a leash. My binding agent's run dry. The ones who go without it start feeling for the doomed again. That gets them killed, and worse, it gets them honest. Fetch me a soothing knot and a measure of counteragent from the market stores.
blestem_managed_blankness.progress: The market stores keep the soothing knots and the counteragent of quiet. Bring one of each to Profira's counter.
blestem_managed_blankness.complete: Both. Good. I'll have them blank again by the third bell. ...Between us? The best of them, Cazimir's own, she slips exactly once per crisis. Two words. 'Poor bastards.' Every time. She's trained a lifetime not to grieve and fails, quietly, always. That failure's the last human thing in this whole listening city. I keep her blank because he pays me to. I hate that I'm good at it. Take a knot for yourself. You'll want your own face someday.

objectives.text: Fetch a soothing knot
Fetch a measure of counteragent

blestem_managed_blankness.note: Profira keeps the operatives' faces blank because the spymaster pays her to. She told a stranger about the two words the best of them cannot stop saying. It was the closest thing to grief the city allowed her.

blestem_i_only_wanted_to_know.title: I Only Wanted to Know What It Said
blestem_i_only_wanted_to_know.summary: In a roofless Church of Transcub, someone scratched a confession into the altar in a shaking hand -- and the stone beneath it has gone warm. A woman who prays there for a brother who read too much begs a stranger to see the altar, and to understand nothing.
blestem_i_only_wanted_to_know.offer: My brother prayed to Transcub -- the old god, the one who punished cruelty a body does to itself. Nobody prays there now; the roofs are gone and the god's not answering. He went to read the marks under the altar. Said he only wanted to know what they said. He came back and he was -- quieter. Emptier. Then not back at all. Go to the church. Look at what he scratched into the altar before he understood it. Look. Don't read the rest.
blestem_i_only_wanted_to_know.progress: The Church of Transcub stands roofless past the market's edge, ivy where the beams were. Reach the altar, and take only the warmth as your proof.
blestem_i_only_wanted_to_know.complete: 'I only wanted to know what it said.' That's what he cut into the stone. In his hand, shaking. And the stone was warm -- you felt it. Warm as a held hand, and that's the wrongest thing there is. He understood the rest, traveler. That's what took him. The god of Transcub punished cruelty turned inward. My brother's curiosity was the cruelest thing he ever did to himself. Take this. It keeps a mind incurious. Wear it in that church. Wear it everywhere here.

objectives.label: the Church of Transcub altar
objectives.text: Reach the warm altar
Drive off the perched listeners

blestem_i_only_wanted_to_know.note: The altar-stone is still warm, and the confession is still cut in a shaking hand. Zenobia's brother learned what the marks said. In Blestem, comprehension is the one debt that collects the reader entire.

blestem_the_stall_left_open.title: The Stall Left Open
blestem_the_stall_left_open.summary: A market woman finds a rival's stall fully stocked, its coin-box open and full -- the one thing no one in Blestem would ever leave. In the flour spilled across the counter, the grains have settled into faint parallel lines. She needs someone to look, and to see what the alignment means.
blestem_the_stall_left_open.offer: Old Nedelcu never left his stall unwatched a day in sixty years. This morning it's standing open -- goods out, coin-box up and full to the lip. In Blestem you'd sooner leave your own child than a full coin-box. He's just gone. And the flour he spilled across the counter, it's -- lined. The grains lie in rows. Parallel. Like something combed them. You've the lens for marks. Go see, and tell me I'm imagining the lines.
blestem_the_stall_left_open.progress: Nedelcu's stall stands open in the Lower Market, coin-box full. Reach it and read the alignment in the spilled flour.
blestem_the_stall_left_open.complete: You saw them too. Parallel. Even as fenceposts, in spilled flour nobody touched. That's not a draft and it's not a hand. Nedelcu didn't run and he wasn't robbed -- the coin proves it. He was collected, and the ground kept a record of the collecting. I'm closing my stall. Coin's no good to the taken. Here -- a border-map I marked with the safe streets. There aren't many left.

objectives.label: the abandoned stall
objectives.text: Reach the open stall and read the flour

blestem_the_stall_left_open.note: A full coin-box, left open in the one city that would never leave it, and dust lined in rows across the counter. Nedelcu was not robbed. He was scheduled. Catrina counts her candles and closes early now.

blestem_the_spire_bleeds_orange.title: The Spire Bleeds Orange
blestem_the_spire_bleeds_orange.summary: The Black Spire watches the city with no windows because it does not watch with eyes. Under alchemical sight its base bleeds a faint shifting orange -- the color of a signal writing itself into stone. The watch-captain, who has served the Spire her whole career, needs a stranger to confirm what she cannot let herself have seen.
blestem_the_spire_bleeds_orange.offer: I've stood the Spire's watch thirty years. A windowless tower that sees everything -- I made my peace with the wrong of it young. Then last month, on the alchemist's glass, its base showed a color I've no name my superiors would forgive. Shifting. Orange. Not lamp, not lichen -- moving, like it was writing itself into the stone. I can't be the one who reports it and I can't be the one who didn't. You're no one's asset. Go to the Spire's foot. Look through the lens. Tell me the color.
blestem_the_spire_bleeds_orange.progress: The Black Spire stands windowless at the city's heart. Reach its base and read the color bleeding from the stone. Whatever perches on it will not want you close.
blestem_the_spire_bleeds_orange.complete: Orange. Shifting. You said it before I could stop you, and now we've both got it in our files. The Spire doesn't watch with eyes because it's stopped needing to -- it listens, and the listening is turning to voice. Cazimir thinks he's collecting the stone under his own tower. He isn't. It's collecting his city, brick by listening brick. Take the veil. It thins what you'd otherwise be seen through. I've said too much. So have you. We were both always going to.

objectives.label: the base of the Black Spire
objectives.text: Read the color at the Spire's foot
Clear the Spire's perched watchers

blestem_the_spire_bleeds_orange.note: The Black Spire's base bleeds shifting orange -- the villain's own color, self-writing into the stone at the heart of the listening city. Marioara reported it to no one. She and a stranger simply both carry it now, which is exactly how Blestem was built to work.

blestem_priced_into_indecency.title: Priced In
blestem_priced_into_indecency.summary: A decent man is being slowly turned into an informer -- not by threat, but by a planted debt he can only pay one way. He does not want out of the debt. He wants someone to know he tried not to sink, so that when his file is read a hundred years from now, one line says he resisted.
blestem_priced_into_indecency.offer: I'm not asking you to save me. Nobody's saved here; you're just priced. There's a debt against my name I never took on -- Blestem plants them, you know, the way a farmer plants seed. I can pay it in coin I don't have, or in names, and they've made very sure I have names. In a month I'll be someone's ear. I've made my peace. All I want -- go to the market clerk, buy the record of my debt, and hold it. So there's proof, somewhere, that I looked for another road before I took the only one they left.
blestem_priced_into_indecency.progress: The debt-record sits in the Lower Market's clerking stalls. Buy Mircea's planted debt and keep it, so his trying is witnessed.
blestem_priced_into_indecency.complete: You bought it. It's real, then -- I wasn't imagining the seed they planted in me. Keep it. When they file me for good, that record says I tried. It's a small thing. But a hundred years on, some clerk reads my file and sees one line where a man said no before he said yes. That's the only clean thing I get to own. Take the craft-note. It's how the ledgers are forged. You'll need to know their tricks to survive their market.

objectives.text: Buy Mircea's planted debt-record
Confirm the debt with the market clerk

blestem_priced_into_indecency.note: Mircea will be an informer within the month. He did not fight the debt -- there is no fighting it. He only asked that his resistance be witnessed and kept, one clean line in a file that will outlive him. It was kept.

blestem_the_walled.title: The Walled
blestem_the_walled.summary: Blestem's cruelest punishment is to entomb a person alive in a listening-wall until they become part of its hearing. A guard has heard one of the walled still breathing in a seam he was told was empty forty years ago. He cannot free them -- the mortar is the sentence. He wants only for someone to stand where they can hear it, so a second living person knows the walled are still there.
blestem_the_walled.offer: There's a punishment here we don't name to outsiders. The walled. You're mortared into a listening-course, alive, until you stop being a person and start being part of what the wall hears. I posted a dead seam for years -- empty, they said, sealed forty winters. Last night I heard breathing in it. Slow. Patient. Still going. I can't break them out; the wall is the sentence, and breaking it makes me the next brick. Go stand at the seam. Hear it. So it's not just me carrying that someone's still in there.
blestem_the_walled.progress: The sealed seam runs cold and low behind the old ward. Reach it, stand close, and hear what still breathes in the wall.
blestem_the_walled.complete: You heard it. Breathing. Forty years and still breathing. ...I don't sleep well knowing, but I sleep worse alone with it. Now two of us carry that someone's in there. The gargoyles nest thick on that course -- they always roost where the wall's still hearing, like they know it's alive. Take the shroud. It's woven for the named. The walled had names once. This one still does, somewhere under the mortar.

objectives.label: the sealed seam
objectives.text: Hear what breathes in the wall
Clear the gargoyles roosting on the live course

blestem_the_walled.note: Someone is walled into a listening-seam behind the old ward, breathing still after forty winters. Vasile cannot free them; the mortar is the sentence. He asked only that a second living soul know they endure. Now two people carry it. In Blestem, that is a rescue.

blestem_the_disorientation_lift.title: The Stairs That Reorder
blestem_the_disorientation_lift.summary: The Riddler's Quarter is built as a disorientation engine -- a lift of stairs and corridors that reorders itself so no map stays true and no assassin arrives twice the same way. A guard posted at its edge has noticed the reordering has stopped being random. It is herding people to one particular door, and she wants to know which door, and whose.
blestem_the_disorientation_lift.offer: You've been in the Quarter. You noticed you couldn't have walked out the way you came -- nobody can. The stairs reorder. It's Cazimir's masterpiece: petitioners arrive already lost, already having told him something by the route they were forced to choose. Here's what's got me: the reordering's gone deliberate. Everyone's being steered to one door now. Same door. I want to know which. Walk the lift, let it herd you, and reach the door it wants you at. Then get out, if it lets you.
blestem_the_disorientation_lift.progress: Enter the Riddler's Quarter and let the reordering stairs steer you. Reach the door they herd everyone toward.
blestem_the_disorientation_lift.complete: You reached it. And it's the door under the Spire, isn't it -- the one nobody's assigned to guard because nobody's supposed to know it's there. The machine's not confusing people anymore. It's collecting them. Funneling. Cazimir built a maze to protect his secrets, and something's turned it into a throat that swallows toward the center. Take the lens-work. It reads the marks the stairs leave when they move. Somebody should be reading them who isn't him.

objectives.label: the door the lift herds toward
objectives.text: Reach the herded door in the Riddler's Quarter
Break the golems set in the shifting halls

blestem_the_disorientation_lift.note: The Riddler's Quarter no longer confuses. It herds -- every path bending toward one door beneath the Spire. Cazimir built a maze to guard his secrets. Ilinca watched it become a throat, swallowing inward, and could not say toward what.

blestem_blackglass_under_duress.title: Blackglass Under Duress
blestem_blackglass_under_duress.summary: Blestem imports its blackglass from the forge-city of Sangeroasa, and imports it under duress -- the trade is a leash the werewolves pull. A shipment has gone missing between the two cities, and the smith needs the raw glass recovered before the forges use the loss as a lever in the next negotiation.
blestem_blackglass_under_duress.offer: Everything I temper needs blackglass, and every ingot of it comes up from Sangeroasa -- those furnace-dogs who think with their hammers. We trade for it under duress; they know we can't make it and never let us forget. A shipment's gone missing on the road in, and if the forges decide we lost it on purpose, that's a lever they'll pull for a year. Find the raw glass -- the golems that guard the eastern passes have a habit of hoarding what falls off a cart. Bring it back before the negotiation.
blestem_blackglass_under_duress.progress: The basalt golems of the passes hoard what spills from the blackglass carts. Recover four ingots' worth before the forges make a lever of the loss.
blestem_blackglass_under_duress.complete: Blackglass, all of it, dug out of a golem's cairn. Good. Now the forges have nothing to hold over us this season, which means Blestem holds something over them -- their surprise that we found it. That's how this works. Every recovered thing is a lost lever. You've a forge-city's frustration to your name now, and they keep files too. Take the striker. Veinflint. It'll light a fire blackglass can't smother.

objectives.text: Break the golems hoarding the shipment
Recover the blackglass ingots

blestem_blackglass_under_duress.note: The blackglass came back, and with it a season's worth of leverage the forges will not enjoy losing. In Blestem every recovered thing is a lost lever for someone else. The smith counts the trade a victory, and knows exactly what it cost her in Sangeroasa's ledgers.

blestem_the_unmeasured_liability.title: The Unmeasured Liability
blestem_the_unmeasured_liability.summary: Cazimir's finest operative is also his one unmeasured liability -- her tenderness for the doomed. A dignitary who fears what her exposure would trigger wants proof of the flaw buried, not surfaced, and hires a stranger to intercept a dossier before it reaches the wrong desk. The right choice is not the paid one.
blestem_the_unmeasured_liability.offer: I'll speak plainly, which in Blestem is itself a risk. Cazimir's best operative has a flaw the whole apparatus depends on no one indexing: she grieves. For the dead, the doomed, the poor bastards. If someone files that -- proves the spymaster's sharpest instrument feels -- it's a lever against her, and levers against her are levers against the peace she keeps. A dossier's moving toward a desk that mustn't get it. Take it off the road. Bring it to me, and it never existed. That's the job.
blestem_the_unmeasured_liability.progress: The dossier on the operative's tenderness moves through the market's escrow stalls toward the wrong desk. Intercept it, and decide who it reaches.
blestem_the_unmeasured_liability.complete: You brought it to me. Good. It'll be unmade tonight, and her flaw stays her secret -- which is to say, her only human thing stays hers. You could have sold it three ways for more coin. You didn't. In this city that's either foolishness or a strategy so long I can't see the end of it. Either way I'll remember it, and my memory is a currency. Take the token. It keeps one name off the ledgers. Even here, a person is allowed to keep one.

objectives.text: Intercept the dossier on the operative
Deliver the dossier to Dragan, unread

blestem_the_unmeasured_liability.note: The dossier proving the operative grieves was unmade before it reached the desk that wanted it. Her tenderness stays her secret, which is to say her one uncollected thing stays hers. Dragan filed the favor in a memory that is itself a currency in Blestem.

blestem_never_a_straight_line.title: Never Speak in a Straight Line
blestem_never_a_straight_line.summary: A newcomer's child cannot yet do the forty-pace instinct -- speaks plainly, walks in straight lines, says whole sentences a wall could carry. The innkeeper, fond of the family, wants a few small warding tokens the market children wear, so the little one can learn to be careful the gentle way, through a game, before the city teaches it the hard way.
blestem_never_a_straight_line.offer: There's a family new to the maze -- come up from the border to trade, sweet people, and their girl breaks my heart. She walks straight lines. Finishes her sentences. Says exactly what she means, right out loud, where any wall can keep it. Here that's not innocence, it's exposure. The market children wear little wardings and learn carefulness as a game -- hopscotch and hand-signs and never the whole word. Fetch me three of the tokens. Let her learn gentle. This city will teach her hard soon enough.
blestem_never_a_straight_line.progress: The market children's warding tokens are sold at the honest stalls -- the ones that still deal in coin. Bring three back to the inn.
blestem_never_a_straight_line.complete: Three. Look at them, bless the little clay things. I'll give them to her tonight and tell her they're for a game where you never say the last word out loud, and you always turn a corner before a secret. She'll think it's play. By the time she learns it wasn't, she'll be safe -- careful in her bones. It's the only childhood Blestem gives. Take a hex-sign for your own door, dear. Warm doors are the only ones worth warding.

objectives.text: Gather the children's warding tokens

blestem_never_a_straight_line.note: A border child too honest for Blestem will learn carefulness as a game of corners and unsaid last words, and never know it was survival. Aglaia gave her the gentlest version of the only childhood the maze allows. It was, in this city, an act of love.

blestem_the_transcub_audit.title: The God Who Audits
blestem_the_transcub_audit.summary: Transcub was the god who punished cruelty a person does to themselves -- self-harm, self-deceit, the lie you tell yourself. A people who weaponized the truth about others abandoned him, because they had nothing to say to a god who audited the truth about themselves. The chronicler wants his last liturgy recovered from the roofless churches before it is forgotten entirely, and half-suspects why the city fears it.
blestem_the_transcub_audit.offer: The Strigoi worshipped Transcub once -- the god who punished the cruelty you do to yourself. The lie you tell your own heart. His churches stand roofless now, and do you know why we left him? Not disbelief. Fear. A people who made a science of others' secrets could not bear one god still auditing their own. His last liturgy is scattered in the ruined naves, and I'll not have it lost. Gather the pages. I want to read what a city can't stand to be told about itself.
blestem_the_transcub_audit.progress: The liturgy of Transcub lies scattered through the roofless churches past the market's edge. Gather the pages the city abandoned.
blestem_the_transcub_audit.complete: Here it is, in a dead god's own hand. Listen -- 'The wound you deny is the only one that festers unseen.' No wonder they fled him. This whole city is a wound denied, mortared over and called architecture. And the stone -- the thing under the Spire -- it feeds on exactly what Transcub warned of: the self-harm of wanting to know too much. The old god saw the shape of our ending six centuries early and we roofless-ed him for it. Take the counteragent. Quiet's the only prayer that still works here.

objectives.label: the ruined naves
objectives.text: Search the roofless churches for the liturgy
Gather the scattered liturgy pages

blestem_the_transcub_audit.note: Transcub punished the cruelty a soul does to itself, and Blestem abandoned him because it could not bear the audit. His last liturgy warned of a hunger fed by wanting to know too much -- six centuries before the stone proved him right. Varvara keeps the pages now. The city still will not read them.

blestem_the_route_you_chose.title: The Route You Chose
blestem_the_route_you_chose.summary: In Blestem, the way you walk tells the watchers what you are before you speak. A night-guard has been ordered to log the routes petitioners take through the maze -- because the route reveals the fear. She has begun to suspect the logging serves something beneath the Spire, not the court, and wants a stranger to walk a false route to test where the record goes.
blestem_the_route_you_chose.offer: My orders are to log how petitioners walk. Sounds like nothing. It isn't. The route you choose through the maze tells us what you're afraid of, who you trust, which corners you already know -- a confession you make with your feet before you open your mouth. Lately my logs go somewhere I can't follow. Not the court. Lower. Toward the Spire. Walk me a false route -- a liar's path -- and let me log it. Then we'll see where a lie ends up filed, and by whom.
blestem_the_route_you_chose.progress: Walk a deliberately false route through the maze so Smaranda can log it, then reach the filing-point beneath the Spire to see where the record travels.
blestem_the_route_you_chose.complete: Your false route filed itself under the Spire within the hour. I followed the copy. It didn't stop at any desk I could name -- it went into the stone, the way the voice comes out of the stone. We don't log for Cazimir anymore, traveler. We log for whatever he thinks he's collecting. Every step every petitioner takes is being written into the thing under the tower. Take the shroud. Walk crooked. And for the love of the roofless god, never walk the same way twice.

objectives.label: the liar's path marker
objectives.text: Walk the false route for the log

objectives.label: the Spire filing-point
objectives.text: See where the record is filed

blestem_the_route_you_chose.note: Every step every petitioner takes through the maze is logged, and the logs no longer stop at any desk -- they flow into the stone beneath the Spire. Smaranda logs for the thing Cazimir believes he is collecting. She walks crooked now, and never the same way twice.

blestem_a_debt_you_cant_pay.title: A Debt You Can't Pay
blestem_a_debt_you_cant_pay.summary: In Blestem, premature knowledge is a debt you can't pay -- learn a thing before you can afford to know it, and you owe. A dealer has come into a dossier so valuable it has made her a target, and she wants it escrowed by a stranger who owes nothing yet, until she can survive owning it.
blestem_a_debt_you_cant_pay.offer: There's a rule here they teach with the forty-pace instinct: premature knowledge is a debt you can't pay. I've come into a file I can't afford to be holding -- what's in it makes me a target the moment anyone knows I have it. I need it out of my hands and into someone the ledgers don't have a line for yet. You. Carry it to my escrow-man three streets over and leave it there. Don't read it -- reading it makes you owe what I owe. Just place it, and come tell me it's done. Then I can survive having ever touched it.
blestem_a_debt_you_cant_pay.progress: Carry Despina's dangerous dossier to the escrow-man's drop and leave it, sealed, then return to her. Reading it makes the debt yours.
blestem_a_debt_you_cant_pay.complete: Placed. And your face is still calm, so you didn't read it -- you don't owe. Good. You've no idea what you just chose not to know, and that ignorance is the most valuable thing you own in this city. I can survive holding it now, because it's not in my hands, it's in the escrow-man's, and he's owed favors by people even the Spire steps around. Take the charm. It keeps a mind incurious. In Blestem that's not a weakness. It's a lung.

objectives.label: the escrow-man's drop
objectives.text: Place the sealed dossier at escrow, unread
Tell Despina the file is placed

blestem_a_debt_you_cant_pay.note: Despina survived owning a file too dangerous to hold by placing it in an escrow the Spire steps around. A stranger carried it unread, and so owes nothing -- which in Blestem is the richest thing a person can be. Curiosity here is a debt that collects the reader entire.

blestem_the_listeners_without_faces.title: The Listeners Without Faces
blestem_the_listeners_without_faces.summary: Some informers have been embedded so long they have stopped having faces of their own -- the managed blankness curing into permanence, feature by feature. The apothecary who once kept them blank now watches them empty entirely, and needs a draught of remembrance carried to the last one who still has a name, before he loses it too.
blestem_the_listeners_without_faces.offer: I told you I keep the operatives' faces blank. Here's the part I didn't say. Do it long enough and the blankness cures. Sets. They stop having faces of their own -- become just listeners, ears with a body attached, no self left to interrogate. It's the perfection of the craft and it's the worst thing I've made. There's one left who still knows his own name. Barely. I've compounded a draught that might hold the last of him. Carry it to where he stands his silent watch. Let him remember he was a man, one more hour.
blestem_the_listeners_without_faces.progress: The last named listener stands his silent watch in the maze's deep courses. Reach him and give him the draught of remembrance.
blestem_the_listeners_without_faces.complete: You reached him. And he took it, and for a moment -- you saw it -- there was a face there. A person, surfacing. He said his own name. Then the blankness took it back, feature by feature, the way tide takes a footprint. That's what this city makes of loyalty, traveler: it wears the loyal down to ears. I can't stop it. I can only give them an hour of remembering they were more. Take the soothing knot. Keep your own face. It's the one thing here worth more than any secret.

objectives.label: the last listener's watch
objectives.text: Reach the last named listener
Clear the wardens of the deep course

blestem_the_listeners_without_faces.note: The oldest informers have worn down to ears -- managed blankness cured to permanence, no face, no self left to question.
