Profira gave the last named one an hour of remembering he was a man. Then the tide of the craft took it back. This is what Blestem makes of loyalty.

blestem_gargoyle_watch.title: The Roosts of the Spire
blestem_gargoyle_watch.summary: The spire-gargoyles roost thickest where the walls hear best, and lately they have grown bold, dropping onto petitioners in the market lanes. The watch-captain sets a standing bounty on thinning them -- honest, systemic work in a city that offers little of it.
blestem_gargoyle_watch.offer: The gargoyles are nesting low again. They roost where the walls hear best -- always have, like they know which stone's alive -- and now they're bold enough to drop on folk in the lanes. I can't spare wardens for vermin duty; I need them on the Spire watch. Thin the roosts for me. There's a standing bounty, paid clean in coin, no file, no favor owed. Rare thing in Blestem, a clean coin. Take it while it's offered.
blestem_gargoyle_watch.progress: The spire-gargoyles roost above the market lanes and the listening courses. Thin them where they've grown bold.
blestem_gargoyle_watch.complete: Roosts thinned, lanes clear another day. Clean coin, as promised -- no strings, which I know reads as suspicious here, and fair enough. Some work's just work. Come back tomorrow; they'll have nested again by the roosts that hear best. They always do. Something about the live walls draws them. I've stopped wondering what.

objectives.text: Thin the gargoyle roosts over the lanes

blestem_gargoyle_watch.note: The gargoyles nest where the walls hear best, and are thinned each day, and return each night to the same live courses. Marioara has stopped wondering what draws them. In Blestem, the questions you stop asking are the ones keeping you alive.

blestem_the_market_reads_you.title: The Market Reads You Back
blestem_the_market_reads_you.summary: Every stall-keeper in the Lower Market is someone's ear, and the fresh rumors need sorting from the planted ones each day. A market woman pays a stranger -- someone with no file yet to bias them -- to gather the day's talk so she can tell the true whispers from the seeded ones.
blestem_the_market_reads_you.offer: The market runs on rumor, and half of it's planted -- seeded by the Quarter to see who repeats what. I need the day's talk gathered by someone with no file yet, so I can sort the true whispers from the bait. Walk the stalls, buy three loose dossiers -- the cheap habit-files everyone trades -- and bring them. I'll read which are real by which contradict each other. Coin for your trouble, and you learn how the city thinks. That's worth more, though you won't believe me yet.
blestem_the_market_reads_you.progress: The cheap habit-dossiers trade at every Lower Market stall. Gather three of the day's loose files for Zenobia to sort.
blestem_the_market_reads_you.complete: Three files, and two of them tell the same lie -- which means it's planted, and now I know which stall's carrying the Quarter's water today. See? The market reads you, but if you're patient it reads back. Here's your coin. Come tomorrow; the whispers turn over daily, and the ones who stop listening are the ones who stop mattering, and the ones who stop mattering get filed.

objectives.text: Gather the day's loose habit-files

blestem_the_market_reads_you.note: The Lower Market's rumors turn over daily, half of them planted to see who repeats them. Zenobia sorts the true from the seeded by their contradictions. In Blestem even gossip is a proving-ground, and the ones who stop listening stop mattering, and the ones who stop mattering are filed.

blestem_the_thick_file.title: A File Thick Enough
blestem_the_thick_file.summary: In Blestem a person who is trusted is only a person whose file is thick enough that betrayal has been priced in. A dignitary, near the end of his usefulness, wants his own file made so complete that the city can predict him entirely -- and thus, paradoxically, leave him alone. He needs a stranger to fetch the last pieces of his own dossier and hand them to the clerks who keep it.
blestem_the_thick_file.offer: You'll think this mad. I want to make my own file thicker. Hear me out -- in Blestem you're only trusted when your file is so complete that your betrayal's already been priced in. A man fully known is a man they stop watching, because there's nothing left to learn. I'm old. I want to be boring to them. Fetch the last loose records of me from the market -- my debts, my habits, my one small vice -- and take them to the Spire clerks. Let me be finished. Fully filed. Finally dull.
blestem_the_thick_file.progress: The loose records of Dragan's own life are scattered through the market's escrow. Gather them and deliver them to the Spire clerks, completing his file.
blestem_the_thick_file.complete: Delivered. My file's complete now -- every debt, every habit, the small vice I'll not name to you. Fully known. And do you feel it? They've already stopped watching me. There's nothing left to collect. I've bought my peace by surrendering the last of my privacy, which is the only currency this city ever let me keep. It's a strange freedom, being wholly known and therefore wholly ignored. Take the token. Keep one thing of yourself unfiled. Unlike me, you're still young enough to afford it.

objectives.text: Gather the last records of Dragan's own life
Complete Dragan's file with the Spire clerks

blestem_the_thick_file.note: Dragan bought his peace by making his own file complete -- fully known, and therefore no longer worth watching. He surrendered the last of his privacy, the only currency Blestem let him keep, to become finally dull. It is the strangest freedom the city offers, and the last one he could afford.

blestem_the_catapult_cable.title: The Catapult-Cable Feat
blestem_the_catapult_cable.summary: The Riddler's Quarter still tells of a teenage operative who once crossed a chasm on a catapult-cable in a single act of impossible nerve, and made her name in a night. A market woman who loves the story wants the old cable-anchor found and its plate recovered, so the one legend the grim city keeps does not rust away unmarked.
blestem_the_catapult_cable.offer: There's one story this whole grim city tells like it's proud -- and it lets itself be proud so rarely. Years back, a girl, barely grown, crossed the chasm at the Quarter's edge on a single catapult-cable. One act of impossible nerve. Made her name in a night; she's Cazimir's own now, they say. The old anchor's still bolted in the rock, rusting. Nobody's marked it. Find it -- the golems have overrun that ledge -- and bring me the anchor-plate. I want one brave thing in Blestem kept shining.
blestem_the_catapult_cable.progress: The old catapult-cable anchor is bolted into the chasm ledge at the Riddler's Quarter's edge, where the golems roam. Recover its plate.
blestem_the_catapult_cable.complete: The anchor-plate. Look at it -- rusted near through, and still holding, like her nerve. I'll polish it and hang it in the taproom, and when someone asks I'll tell the story straight, no dread on the end of it for once. She crossed on a cable, a child, and lived, and became someone. In a city that files everything, that's the one record I'll keep for the joy of it. Take the striker. Light of it, that's all. We're allowed a little light.

objectives.label: the chasm ledge anchor
objectives.text: Reach the old catapult-cable anchor
Clear the golems from the ledge

blestem_the_catapult_cable.note: A girl once crossed the chasm on a single catapult-cable and made her name in a night. Catrina recovered the rusted anchor-plate and hung it in the taproom, and tells the story straight, with no dread on the end. In a city that files everything, it is the one record kept for the joy of it.

blestem_the_files_on_fielderine.title: The Files on the Queen He Cannot Read
blestem_the_files_on_fielderine.summary: The spymaster keeps more files on the poor human queen of Angel Wings than on any other leader, and understands her least -- a quiet strategist gives a collector nothing to collect. A guard tasked with couriering those useless files wants a stranger to carry the newest one, because everyone who carries them starts, slowly, to admire what cannot be filed.
blestem_the_files_on_fielderine.offer: Cazimir has more dossiers on the western queen than on any three other leaders -- and understands her less than any of them. It maddens him. A quiet strategist gives a collector nothing to collect; she governs by giving up power he'd kill to hold, and he can't index it, can't price it, can't believe it. I courier the files. They keep getting thicker and telling him less. Carry the newest to the archive-drop for me. And watch yourself -- everyone who carries them starts to admire her. It's contagious, being unfileable.
blestem_the_files_on_fielderine.progress: Carry the newest useless file on the western queen to the archive-drop beneath the Spire, then return to Ilinca.
blestem_the_files_on_fielderine.complete: Dropped. Another dossier that'll tell him nothing. You've the look now -- you admire her too, don't you. Everyone does, once they carry the proof of how completely she's outwitted the finest spymaster on the continent simply by wanting nothing he can take. He collects architecture, he says. She built a throne out of what she refused to hold. He'll never understand it, and that's her whole defense. Take the charm. Learn from her: the surest way to be uncollectible is to want less than they can steal.

objectives.text: Take up the queen's useless file

objectives.label: the archive-drop beneath the Spire
objectives.text: Courier the file to the archive-drop

blestem_the_files_on_fielderine.note: The spymaster keeps thick files on the western queen and understands her least -- she governs by refusing the power he covets, and cannot be priced. Everyone who carries the files starts to admire her. Ilinca has learned the lesson the hard way: the surest way to be uncollectible is to want less than they can steal.

blestem_thread_anomaly_warning.title: Early Warning from the North
blestem_thread_anomaly_warning.summary: Blestem understands better than anyone what sleeps under the North, and trades the Council of Six for early warning of thread-anomalies -- terrified the vigil-keepers are right about what is coming. A message from the North has arrived, and its carrier fell silent on the road. A guard needs the sealed warning recovered and read by the right people, unlike the last one.
blestem_thread_anomaly_warning.offer: We trade the North for one thing money can't buy: early warning. When their threads twitch wrong, they tell us, and we're the only city that listens, because we're the only city that believes them about what's under Black Night. A warning came south this week. The carrier went quiet three streets from the drop -- and I mean quiet the wrong way, the stall-left-open way. The seal's still on the message; whatever took him didn't want it, it wanted him. Recover it. Get it to the clerks who can act. The North doesn't warn twice.
blestem_thread_anomaly_warning.progress: The sealed thread-warning lies where its carrier fell silent, near the Spire's shadow. Recover it and clear whatever took him.
blestem_thread_anomaly_warning.complete: Recovered, and the seal's whole -- so the thing that took the carrier wasn't after the message. It was after a listener. The warning itself says the North's threads are waking faster than the old count allows. We'll act on it -- quietly, the way we do everything. But I'll tell you what chills me: the carrier was collected within paces of the drop, as if something wanted the warning delivered slow and short a witness. Take the shroud. The North's right about what's coming. We've always known. We just kept it in a file.

objectives.label: where the carrier fell silent
objectives.text: Recover the sealed thread-warning
Clear what took the carrier

blestem_thread_anomaly_warning.note: The North warned the one city that believes it: the threads wake faster than the old count allows. The warning arrived whole, but its carrier was collected within paces of the drop -- as if something wanted the news delivered slow, and a witness short. Blestem will act, quietly. It always knew. It kept the knowing in a file.

blestem_the_collector_collected.title: The Word Already Belongs to Something Else
blestem_the_collector_collected.summary: The spymaster believes anything that exists can be acquired, indexed, and held -- and covets the buried world-machine as the ultimate asset. The chronicler has assembled the proof that he has it precisely backwards: the stone does not get acquired, it acquires, and a collector who wants everything is the exact shape of door it was built to travel through. She needs the dossier carried to the one place it might do good.
blestem_the_collector_collected.offer: I've built a dossier no one in this city dares hold, and I'm too old to carry it far. It proves what our spymaster refuses to see: he thinks he's collecting the stone under his own Spire. He has it backwards. The stone doesn't get acquired -- it acquires. A man who wants to own everything is exactly the shape of door the thing was built to walk through. Curiosity dressed as prudence. His whole genius is the vector. Carry this proof to the safe-drop past the market. If it ever reaches the right ear, maybe someone stops him being the door.
blestem_the_collector_collected.progress: Carry Varvara's dossier -- the proof the collector is the collected -- to the safe-drop past the Lower Market. Do not read it; understanding it is the very trap it warns of.
blestem_the_collector_collected.complete: Placed. And you didn't read it -- good, because the cruelest joke in the whole dossier is that understanding it too well is the exact door it warns about. He collects architecture, he says. He does not know the word 'collect' already belongs to something older, and that it has been reading him back this whole time, indexing his ambition, filing his every acquisition toward the day it walks out through him into the world. He thinks he's the collector. He'll learn otherwise the moment it's too late to unlearn. Take the shroud. Keep one thing you never try to know.

objectives.text: Take up Varvara's sealed proof

objectives.label: the safe-drop past the market
objectives.text: Carry the proof to the safe-drop, unread

blestem_the_collector_collected.note: The spymaster covets the stone as the ultimate asset, and has it exactly backwards: the collector is the collected. A man who wants everything is the door the Underlanguage was built to travel through -- curiosity dressed as prudence. Varvara's proof is placed where it might do good. It will not reach him in time. It was never going to.

sangeroasa_forge_rest_hammers.title: The Count of Three
sangeroasa_forge_rest_hammers.summary: The hammers of Sangeroasa have struck as one since before the walls, and lately they stop together for a count of three, then start again. Fane wants a sober head to stand near the killing floor and hear it once.
sangeroasa_forge_rest_hammers.offer: You feel it in your teeth before you crest the ridge -- a thousand hammers as one heart, and it has beat that way longer than any smith's grandfather can name. Only now it stops. All of them, for a count of three, and starts again like nothing. Go stand by the low channels and hear it, will you. Tell me a man can go deaf and imagine such a thing.
sangeroasa_forge_rest_hammers.progress: The hammers stop near the blood channels, below the pit road. Stand where you can hear the whole city at once, and wait for the silence.
sangeroasa_forge_rest_hammers.complete: Three. You counted three, same as me. No smith drops his stroke for three counts on his own -- and every one of them, together, and none will meet your eye about it. Take the ward. My grandmother pressed one just like it into my swaddling. She said the quiet was worse than the fire, and she fed six winters of strangers, so she knew quiet.

objectives.label: the blood channels
objectives.text: Stand where the whole city rings at once

sangeroasa_forge_rest_hammers.note: The hammers stopped for a count of three, and every smith kept his eyes on the iron. Nobody ordered the silence. Something did. Fane still counts, some nights, and hopes to be wrong.

sangeroasa_gift_child_shoe.title: What the Furrow Keeps
sangeroasa_gift_child_shoe.summary: The red rows of the Gift give the best grain in the south, and Radu will not walk his own field since he turned a child's shoe up in the good soil. He asks a stranger to bring in the harvest he can no longer face.
sangeroasa_gift_child_shoe.offer: The Gift feeds us. Reddest soil in Draconia, and it gives back tenfold -- ask no farmer here what it's made of, we love it too well to know. But last plow-turn I brought up a little shoe. A child's. Clean laces. And the wheat above that row is the finest I ever grew. I can't walk down there. Cut the near rows for me. I'll not tell you which one.
sangeroasa_gift_child_shoe.progress: The near rows of the Gift lie south of the vent road, red as an open wound. Bring in the grain Radu cannot face.
sangeroasa_gift_child_shoe.complete: Bless you. That's a season's bread cut and I never had to see the row. The old ones say the Gift is the war-dead giving back, generous as guilt. We eat it and we thank the soil and we do not ask. Take the coins and the poultice -- and if you found aught small down there, don't tell me. I've one shoe too many already.

objectives.text: Cut the red-row grain of the Gift

sangeroasa_gift_child_shoe.note: Radu got his bread and kept his eyes shut. The Gift is the war-dead, plowed under and grown back sweet. The south worships the harvest and has forgotten, on purpose, what it is thanking.

sangeroasa_pit_ledger_rim.title: The Ledger at the Rim
sangeroasa_pit_ledger_rim.summary: A ledger is nailed to the rim of the Debt Pit, every name owing labor-days, the dead marked with one word. Ozun the chronicler wants it copied before the pit-bosses can take it down, and wants to know why the word is a thousand years too early.
sangeroasa_pit_ledger_rim.offer: I keep the city's record, for what a record is worth in a place that eats its own. There's a ledger nailed to the Pit rim -- names, labor-days owed, and for the ones the Pit took, a single word in the last column. Collected. That word belongs to a colder age than ours, and it is written here already, in a smith's hand. Reach the rim. Read it. Come back before you understand it. That last is not a courtesy.
sangeroasa_pit_ledger_rim.progress: The ledger hangs on the north rim of the Debt Pit, where the vents breathe hottest. Read it, and turn your eyes away before the columns pull them.
sangeroasa_pit_ledger_rim.complete: Collected. In our own century, in a working hand. You read it and came back with your wits, which is more than the last man I sent. The word means a debt closed and a life closed with it, filed like grain in a barn. Somewhere a colder ledger is learning our alphabet. Take the striker -- light your own fires. Don't read anything that's already been read to you.

objectives.label: the Debt Pit rim
objectives.text: Read the ledger nailed to the rim

sangeroasa_pit_ledger_rim.note: The dead of the Pit are marked 'collected' -- a word from an age not yet come, written in a living hand. Ozun copied it and burned the copy. Some words are a debt to have read at all.

sangeroasa_pit_labor_days.title: Labor-Days Owed
sangeroasa_pit_labor_days.summary: One name on the rim-ledger owes a debt Ozun knows to be paid: a man who died three winters back and is still marked as working. The chronicler asks the truth of it -- and the pit gives up its answer in the form of what the dead now do.
sangeroasa_pit_labor_days.offer: One name on that ledger I know by heart. Marku, a channel-man, drowned in slag three winters gone -- I wrote his own grave-kerb. The rim-ledger has him working still, labor-days accruing. A dead man does not owe hours. Go down toward the killing floors and see what walks where he walked. Put down whatever is paying his debt in his place. Then I'll believe the Pit keeps books past the grave.
sangeroasa_pit_labor_days.progress: The slag-driven dead work the killing floors below the rim, animated by the north's cold seepage. Lay down the ones that shamble Marku's channel.
sangeroasa_pit_labor_days.complete: Forge-thralls. Men who died at the iron and never learned to stop -- the north's rot creeps this far south now, and the Pit is glad of the free labor. Marku's owing hours because Marku's still swinging, dead as he is. The ledger isn't wrong. It never is. That's the horror of it. Take the edge, and take my thanks, small as it weighs against that.

objectives.text: Lay down the dead still paying their debt

sangeroasa_pit_labor_days.note: The dead of Sangeroasa keep working, and the ledger keeps counting their hours. The Pit does not lie. A closed life is only a life whose column is full. Ozun stopped writing grave-kerbs after that.

sangeroasa_dagger_rumor.title: The King's Bad Hand
sangeroasa_dagger_rumor.summary: Valrom the Forged King wants Draconia under one banner, and those closest to him have watched his speech drift lately into a flatter, colder cadence. Ileana of the court needs a quiet outsider to gauge the truth by asking those who serve him.
sangeroasa_dagger_rumor.offer: I sit close enough to the Forged King to hear him change. Valrom carries a dagger, a bloody old thing he took as a trophy, and never sets it down -- and lately, mid-sentence, his king's fire goes out and something level speaks in its place. Words like a clerk reading a bill. Unification, he says, one banner, one hand. Ask the captain and the apothecary what they've marked in him. Court eyes see only what flatters. I want a stranger's.
sangeroasa_dagger_rumor.progress: Costel the captain drills the king's guard; Ana the apothecary tends the king's sleep. Both have watched Valrom close. Ask them what changes in him.
sangeroasa_dagger_rumor.complete: So. The captain hears the cadence flatten when the dagger's near his hand, and the apothecary can't dose a sleep that isn't his own body's. A king can want a united realm honestly. But a want that speaks like a ledger isn't his want any longer -- it's something using his mouth for a pen. Keep this between the three of us. And take the dossier; know his habits, in case knowing keeps you alive.

objectives.text: Ask Captain Costel what changes in the King
Ask Apothecary Ana about the King's sleep

sangeroasa_dagger_rumor.note: Valrom's ambition speaks, more often now, in a cadence that is not his -- flat, clerical, near the dagger's edge. A king can want unity. Something wanted it through him. Ileana marks the days his voice is his own; the list grows shorter.

sangeroasa_arsenal_quota.title: The Arsenal Never Rests
sangeroasa_arsenal_quota.summary: Sangeroasa is the continent's arsenal, and Gherghe is a blade behind quota with a brazier-imp problem: the little furnace-devils are stealing his tempering coals. Clear them so an honest smith can make his count.
sangeroasa_arsenal_quota.offer: I'll tell you plain, I love this work. Steel that comes out true, a haft that fits a hand -- there's honesty in it, whatever the city does with the blades after. But I'm behind quota, and it's the brazier-imps. Little soot-devils nesting in my slack-tub, thieving my good coals to warm their own hides. Clear a dozen off my floor and I'll temper you something worth carrying.
sangeroasa_arsenal_quota.progress: Brazier-imps swarm the working forges along the smith-row. Thin them so an honest man can meet his count.
sangeroasa_arsenal_quota.complete: Ha! Look at my tub -- coals where coals should be, not a devil in sight. That's a quota met and a floor a man can walk without singeing his boots. Here -- I tempered this the slow way, full heat, no shortcuts. It'll outlast the war it's meant for, and saints willing, so will you. Come back if they nest again; they always do.

objectives.text: Clear brazier-imps from the smith-row

sangeroasa_arsenal_quota.note: Gherghe met his quota and loved the making, and did not let himself think long on the using. The arsenal never rests. A blade is honest; the ledger it feeds is not. He tempers on, dark to dark, and keeps his door warm.

sangeroasa_gift_grave_row.title: A Place at the Table
sangeroasa_gift_grave_row.summary: Anton lost his brother to the Debt Pit and has no body to bury, only a name in the rim-ledger. He asks a stranger to gather the good red earth of the Gift, that he might raise a grave for a man the Pit will not release.
sangeroasa_gift_grave_row.offer: My brother went into the Pit for a debt neither of us could count, and the Pit does not give back what it takes -- no body, no bone, just a line in a ledger nailed where I can't reach. But a man deserves a grave. Fill me a jar of the Gift's own soil, the reddest row, and I'll raise him a marker. The dead of the war made that ground. Let one more join them proper, with a name cut in stone.
sangeroasa_gift_grave_row.progress: The reddest earth of the Gift lies in the deep rows past the vent road. Gather good soil to raise a grave the Pit denied.
sangeroasa_gift_grave_row.complete: Red as he'd have wanted -- he always said the Gift was the honestest soil in Draconia, being made of honest men. Now he's in it, or a jar of it stands for him, and there's a kerb with his name and the one thing he did: he pulled two children from a collapsing channel and went in himself. That's all a grave needs. Not perfect. True. Take his old striker. He'd want it lit.

objectives.label: the deep red rows
objectives.text: Gather soil from the Gift for a grave

sangeroasa_gift_grave_row.note: The Pit takes bodies and leaves ledger-lines. Anton raised his brother a grave of Gift-soil -- war-dead earth for a war-dead man -- and cut on it the one true thing he did. Specificity is the only praise the south can afford.

sangeroasa_ilion_files.title: Nothing Left Unresolved
sangeroasa_ilion_files.summary: Ilion, the King's silent tracker-general, files every unresolved thing in the city -- and a page has gone missing from his tally. Manea, a guard who fears what a discrepancy means to such a man, wants it found before Ilion comes looking himself.
sangeroasa_ilion_files.offer: You've not met Ilion. Pray you don't. The King's headsman, they call him, but that's the least of him -- he files. Every loose end in Sangeroasa, every debt half-paid, every man who slipped a rope, catalogued in that silent head. And a page of his tally is gone from the guard-post. A discrepancy. To Ilion a discrepancy is a wound. Find where it blew, before he decides one of us pocketed it. Ask the merchant and the other post -- papers move where coin moves.
sangeroasa_ilion_files.progress: Iosif the merchant handles the city's paper; Nica keeps the far guard-post. One of them will have seen where Ilion's lost page went.
sangeroasa_ilion_files.complete: The merchant had it -- blew into his stall, he swears, and he was too frightened to hand it back to a man who files corpses. It's a name, that's all. A channel-worker marked 'unresolved,' no date of settling. One open account, in a city that closes everything. Ilion will want it closed. I'll slip it back and let him think it never left. Take the forgery-craft; a false receipt has saved better men than us.

objectives.text: Ask Merchant Iosif about the lost page
Ask Guard Nica about the missing tally

sangeroasa_ilion_files.note: Ilion files what will not resolve, and a resolution he cannot reach itches like a splinter in the mind of the world's most patient man. The lost page named one open account. Manea closed it with a lie. In Sangeroasa, a lie that closes a ledger is a mercy.

sangeroasa_collector_early.title: The Man Who Was Already Dead
sangeroasa_collector_early.summary: A figure walks the pit road at dusk, collecting on debts with paperwork instead of a blade, and the smiths swear he is legally dead and knows it. Nica wants the thing off her patrol before it collects a living name.
sangeroasa_collector_early.offer: There's a shape works the pit road after the third bell. Long coat, a satchel of papers, and he collects debts -- not with a knife, with a signature. The channel-men say he's dead. Not walking-dead, thrall-dead. Dead on the books, and still filing. He carries solutions, he says, not weapons. He carried one to old Sava and Sava sat down in the red soil and did not get up, no wound on him, his account settled. Put him down before he settles mine.
sangeroasa_collector_early.progress: The Debt-Collector walks the pit road after the third bell, filing lives closed. End it before it reaches a living name.
sangeroasa_collector_early.complete: It went down like a man and got up like a filing, twice, before it stopped -- and even then it looked almost grateful, like I'd resolved something for it. He was dead already, and knew, and kept collecting anyway, because a debt is a debt whether the collector breathes. That's a thing coming to this world a thousand years too soon. Take the counteragent. If it comes for you, don't sign anything.

objectives.text: Put down the Debt-Collector on the pit road

sangeroasa_collector_early.note: A dead man collected debts with paperwork, and the collected simply sat down and were settled -- no wound, account closed. Nica ended him twice. He seemed grateful. The Debt-System is a thousand years off, and it has already learned to walk south.

sangeroasa_forge_camaraderie.title: Slack-Tub and Small Beer
sangeroasa_forge_camaraderie.summary: Forge-Rest comes but rarely, the one day the hammers are meant to stop by choice, and Fane's taproom needs good ale hauled up from the deep cellar before the smiths come off the floor thirsty and loud.
sangeroasa_forge_camaraderie.offer: Forge-Rest tomorrow -- the one blessed day the hammers stop because we choose it, not because something else does. My smiths come off the floor with a season's soot in their throats and a thirst to match. My good barrels sit in the deep cellar, past the vent-warmth where the ale keeps cool. Roll four up for me. It's honest work for an honest night, and there's precious few of those down here.
sangeroasa_forge_camaraderie.progress: The good barrels rest in the deep cellar past the vent-warmth, below the taproom. Haul the ale up before the smiths come thirsty.
sangeroasa_forge_camaraderie.complete: Now that is a Forge-Rest. Taproom full, hammers quiet by our own say-so, Gherghe singing something filthy and Ana laughing at it -- warmth we made ourselves, not the kind that leaks up from under. It won't last. Nothing good does; that's what makes it good. But tonight it's ours. Sit, drink, take the knot -- my mother tied them for luck, and this one's for you.

objectives.text: Haul good ale from the deep cellar

sangeroasa_forge_camaraderie.note: On Forge-Rest the hammers of Sangeroasa fall silent because the smiths choose it, and the taproom is loud and warm and briefly free of the world below. Fane knows warmth is temporary. He pours it anyway. That is the whole of his courage.

sangeroasa_killing_floor_drain.title: The Channels Run Wrong
sangeroasa_killing_floor_drain.summary: The killing floors drain by an old engineered channel, and the channel has begun to run against its own fall, uphill, warm. Captain Costel needs the forge-thralls fouling the works cleared so his men can read what the water is doing.
sangeroasa_killing_floor_drain.offer: Every drop of blood off the killing floors runs downhill through the channels -- engineered by better men than us, cut to fall true. Now it doesn't fall true. It creeps up the grade, warm, patient, toward the pit and not away. My men can't clear the works to read it because the thralls have fouled the sluices, dead hands still doing dead labor. Break them off the channel. Then maybe we'll see why water's forgotten which way is down.
sangeroasa_killing_floor_drain.progress: Forge-thralls clog the sluices of the killing-floor channels. Break them clear so the works can be read.
sangeroasa_killing_floor_drain.complete: Cleared, and the water still runs the wrong way. Uphill. Warm. Toward the pit. I've soldiered forty years and I know when a thing's being called, and that blood is being called somewhere it shouldn't want to go. I'll not put that in a report -- Ilion files reports, and I'd not have him file this. Take the plate. It's turned worse than a channel-thrall's grip, I promise you.

objectives.text: Break the thralls fouling the sluices

sangeroasa_killing_floor_drain.note: The blood-channels of Sangeroasa have begun to run uphill, warm, toward the Pit -- as if the water too were being called to settle an account. Costel cleared the thralls and filed no report. Some things are safer unresolved, even to a man who hates loose ends.

sangeroasa_apothecary_sleep.title: A Dose for the Dreamless
sangeroasa_apothecary_sleep.summary: Ana's patients have stopped dreaming -- the channel-workers sleep like the settled, flat and still, and wake unrested. She needs blackglass ground fine for a counter-draught, and it only comes from the pit-side glassworks.
sangeroasa_apothecary_sleep.offer: I keep this city's sleep, such as it is. Lately my channel-folk lie down and go still as filed paper -- no dreaming, no turning, they sleep like the dead do their accounts. And they wake more tired than they lay. A dreamless sleep isn't rest; it's a small rehearsal of being finalized. I can cut a draught against it, but I need blackglass ground fine, and the good glass is worked by the pit where I'll not go. Bring me a measure.
sangeroasa_apothecary_sleep.progress: Worked blackglass comes from the pit-side glassworks, hot and dangerous. Bring a measure for Ana's counter-draught.
sangeroasa_apothecary_sleep.complete: Fine as flour, this -- it'll ground the draught proper. A dreamless sleep is the body practicing for a closed ledger, and I'll not let my people rehearse that if a bitter cup can teach them to dream again. Small work. But smallness is the point; the world's saved a dose at a time, or not at all. Take the counteragent -- and if you ever sleep and don't dream, come to me before you sleep so again.

objectives.text: Gather worked blackglass from the pit-side works

sangeroasa_apothecary_sleep.note: The channel-folk had stopped dreaming, sleeping flat as settled accounts. Ana cut them a draught to teach them to dream again -- a small mercy against a large arrangement. A dreamless sleep, she said, is a body rehearsing to be finalized.

sangeroasa_blackglass_run.title: Glass for the Ridge Cities
sangeroasa_blackglass_run.summary: Blackglass is Sangeroasa's finest export, worked volcanic glass the ridge cities pay well for, and Iosif needs a strong back to gather a shipment while the imps are thick in the cooling sheds.
sangeroasa_blackglass_run.offer: Blackglass -- worked from our own vents, cooled just so, sharp enough to shave a shadow. The ridge cities can't get enough, and a trade in glass is a trade I can look at square, unlike some this city runs. Trouble is the cooling sheds are thick with brazier-imps warming themselves on my racks and cracking my good panes. Gather me a shipment's worth off the sound racks. Coin in it, and my honest thanks, which I don't spend often.
sangeroasa_blackglass_run.progress: Sound blackglass sits on the cooling-shed racks the imps haven't cracked. Gather a shipment for the ridge cities.
sangeroasa_blackglass_run.complete: A clean shipment, not a cracked pane in it -- that's a fair week's trade and no man harmed to make it, which in Sangeroasa is nearly a holiday. The ridge folk will pay in good silver and ask no questions, and neither will I. Here's your cut and a razor of the same glass -- keeps its edge past the point a steel one would give up. Safe roads. Come back; I always need a back that doesn't flinch at heat.

objectives.text: Gather sound blackglass from the cooling sheds

sangeroasa_blackglass_run.note: Blackglass is the one trade in Sangeroasa a man can look at square: glass for silver, no debt, no pit. Iosif runs it honestly and does not think about the vents that make it, or the city that surrounds the vents. A clean trade is its own small defiance.

sangeroasa_thrall_recognition.title: He Had a Name Once
sangeroasa_thrall_recognition.summary: Marta's husband died in the channels and now works them still as a forge-thrall, and she cannot bear that he swings iron he no longer knows. She asks a stranger to lay the thralls down -- and to leave his keepsake where he fell.
sangeroasa_thrall_recognition.offer: They tell you the thralls are just dead labor, no man left in them. I know better. My Grigore's down there, dead two winters, and still he swings, still he stokes, dead hands doing the work that killed him. He had a name. He had a laugh you could hear over the hammers. Lay them down -- lay him down -- so he's not made to pay in death what he couldn't in life. And leave this cord where he falls. He gave it me at our binding. Let him have it back.
sangeroasa_thrall_recognition.progress: The forge-thralls labor the deep channels where Grigore fell. Lay them down, and leave his keepsake cord where his work ends.
sangeroasa_thrall_recognition.complete: It's done, then. He's down, and the cord's with him, and maybe -- maybe somewhere the swinging's stopped for good. The Pit will mark his hours settled and his ledger'll balance and nobody in this city will call that a horror, because here it's just Tuesday. But I'll call it a horror. Someone has to. Take the token. It kept his name warm when the world tried to make him a number. Let it keep yours.

objectives.text: Lay down the laboring dead of the deep channels

objectives.label: where Grigore fell
objectives.text: Leave the keepsake cord where he fell

sangeroasa_thrall_recognition.note: Grigore died in the channels and worked them still, until Marta sent a stranger to lay him down with his binding-cord in his dead hand. The Pit balanced his ledger and called it order. Marta called it a horror. In Sangeroasa, that naming is the whole of grief's rebellion.

sangeroasa_gorescream_temper.title: Iron for a King's Axe
sangeroasa_gorescream_temper.summary: The King's own war-axe needs retempering, and Gherghe has been ordered to do it -- but the good bog-iron for the work is hauled up the pit road guarded by things that shouldn't be. He needs it fetched by someone the pit-bosses won't question.
sangeroasa_gorescream_temper.offer: They've handed me the King's axe to retemper. Gorescream itself, the blade he unifies the realm with, notch by bloody notch. It's an honor I'd sooner refuse -- but you don't refuse Valrom, or the flat thing that speaks through him lately. I need good bog-iron for the temper, hauled from the pit-road cache, and the road's thick with imps and worse. Fetch me the iron. What I make of it is on my conscience; the fetching's clean enough.
sangeroasa_gorescream_temper.progress: Good bog-iron sits in the pit-road cache past the brazier-imp nests. Fetch it for the King's temper.
sangeroasa_gorescream_temper.complete: Enough iron for the temper, and honest metal at that, whatever unhonest use it's bent to. I'll retemper the King's axe because a man does his work, but I'll tell you true -- I've watched him grip it and go somewhere behind his own eyes, and I'd not sharpen the thing that's steering him if I saw a way clear. There isn't one. Not for a smith. Take the striker. Light better fires than I'm asked to.

objectives.text: Fetch bog-iron from the pit-road cache

sangeroasa_gorescream_temper.note: Gherghe retempered the King's axe because a smith does his work, and hated the hand it would arm -- a hand no longer wholly the King's. He saw no way clear. A smith mends iron; he cannot mend a will the stone is slowly filing down to a nib.

sangeroasa_dust_lines.title: The Way the Soot Falls
sangeroasa_dust_lines.summary: Sangeroasa's soot has begun to settle in fine parallel lines on every still surface, all pointed the same way -- toward the Pit. Ozun, who has read the symptom-ladder in older records, needs a witness to confirm the alignment before he dares name it.
sangeroasa_dust_lines.offer: I've read the old accounts of the border towns -- how a live thing under the ground announces itself. Ground warms, wells go copper, yeast dies, dust aligns, folk start to listen. We're a hot city; the warm ground and warm wells hide the first signs like a lie hides in a crowd. But dust doesn't lie. Go to the still shrine off the smith-row and look how the soot has settled -- fine, parallel, all one way. Confirm it points where I fear. I need a second pair of eyes before I write a thing I can't unwrite.
sangeroasa_dust_lines.progress: The still shrine off the smith-row gathers undisturbed soot. Read which way the fine parallel lines are pointed.
sangeroasa_dust_lines.complete: Toward the Pit. Every line, arrow-straight, toward the Pit. In a cool town they'd have seen it a year ago; here the heat let it hide, and that's precisely why the thing chose to wake beneath us. Fourth rung of the ladder, and the fifth is folk listening, and I have heard men on the killing floor go still and tilt their heads at nothing. I'll write it now. Someone must. Take the lens -- see the small wrong things before they're large ones.

objectives.label: the still shrine
objectives.text: Read the way the soot has aligned

sangeroasa_dust_lines.note: The soot of Sangeroasa settles now in parallel lines, all pointed at the Pit -- the fourth rung of a ladder Ozun learned from border chronicles. The heat had hidden the earlier signs. That, he understood, was why the thing woke here. Dust does not lie. It only points.

sangeroasa_imp_hoard.title: Devils in the Slack-Tub
sangeroasa_imp_hoard.summary: Brazier-imps have hoarded a nest of stolen forge-goods in the old works, and Dorica -- a guard with a soft spot for the smiths they rob -- wants the little thieves cleared and the workmen's tools recovered.
sangeroasa_imp_hoard.offer: You'd laugh if it weren't a nuisance. The brazier-imps -- soot-devils, thumb-high, mean as a cracked anvil -- have built a proper hoard in the old works. Not gold; they've no use for it. Tools. Good tongs, temper-strikers, half a smith's living, all thieved and piled to warm their nest. The smiths can't make quota without them and Ilion doesn't care why a quota's missed. Clear the devils and let me give the poor men their tongs back. It's the closest to a good deed this posting allows.
sangeroasa_imp_hoard.progress: The brazier-imps hoard stolen tools in the old works past the smith-row. Clear them so the smiths' tools can be returned.
sangeroasa_imp_hoard.complete: Cleared, and look at the pile -- half the smith-row's missing tongs, warm from being sat on by devils. I'll have every one back to its owner by the third bell, and there'll be quota met and no man dragged before Ilion for a theft that wasn't his. A small justice. This city runs so short on those I hoard them like the imps hoard tongs. Take the striker -- one of theirs, recovered. It's earned a better hand.

objectives.text: Clear the imp hoard in the old works

sangeroasa_imp_hoard.note: Dorica cleared the imps and returned the stolen tools, sparing honest smiths from Ilion's ledger for thefts they never made. A small justice, hoarded against a city that runs short on them. She counts such days the way a miser counts coin -- rarely, and in private.

sangeroasa_gift_harvest_song.title: The Song They Don't Remember
sangeroasa_gift_harvest_song.summary: There is a harvest song the Gift-farmers sing every reaping, and none of them know its words are a soldier's lament from the Great War. Radu wants the old chronicler asked, and the truth of the tune settled -- or left buried.
sangeroasa_gift_harvest_song.offer: We sing at the reaping. Always have -- my father sang it, his before him, a tune that goes back past anyone's grandmother. And last month a traveler wept to hear it, said it was a muster-song from the Great War, a lament for the dead we plow the Gift with. We sing our own dead's dirge over the grain they became and we don't know it. Ask Ozun -- he keeps the old accounts. I need to know if the traveler was right. Then I'll decide if I ever sing it again.
sangeroasa_gift_harvest_song.progress: Ozun the chronicler keeps what old accounts survive. Ask him the truth of the Gift-farmers' harvest song.
sangeroasa_gift_harvest_song.complete: He was right. Ozun found the muster-roll -- the song's a lament the war-dead sang marching to the field that became our Gift. We've been keening for the very men we eat, generation on generation, and calling it a harvest tune. ...I'll sing it still. Radu Lupei will sing it knowing, now, which is more than my father did. Better a dirge sung true than a joy sung ignorant. Take the ward. My grandmother said the honest dead protect the honest living. I choose to believe her.

objectives.text: Ask Ozun the truth of the harvest song

sangeroasa_gift_harvest_song.note: The Gift-farmers' harvest song is a Great War lament, a dirge for the dead who became the soil. Radu learned it, and chose to sing on -- knowing now. Better a dirge sung true, he said, than a joy sung ignorant. The south feeds on its history and, once, chose to remember the meal.

sangeroasa_collector_receipt.title: Solutions, Not Weapons
sangeroasa_collector_receipt.summary: The dead Debt-Collector left receipts on the bodies it settled, and Ileana of the court wants one recovered and read -- to learn the language of a system not yet born, and to warn the Accord what is coming south.
sangeroasa_collector_receipt.offer: The thing that collects on the pit road leaves paper. On each one it settles -- no wound, just a signature -- there's a receipt tucked in the coat. A receipt for a life. I want one, and I want it read, because the words on it are the grammar of a colder age, and the Accord should hear what dialect death is learning to speak this far south. It's a foul errand. Reach the pit road where it works, take a receipt off the last it settled, and bring it me sealed. Do not read it yourself. The reading is what closes the account.
sangeroasa_collector_receipt.progress: The Debt-Collector's receipts lie on the bodies it settles, along the pit road. Recover one -- sealed -- and do not read it.
sangeroasa_collector_receipt.complete: Sealed, good. You didn't read it -- I can tell; you still have the look of a person with an open account, which is to say, alive. 'Debt in full. Account closed. Rest.' That is what it says, in a clerk's kind flat hand. Not a threat. An offer of rest, phrased as bookkeeping. That is the voice the whole world will speak in, a thousand years on, and it is speaking it here first. The Accord will hear of it. Take the writ -- it unfiles what the coat would file.

objectives.label: the pit road
objectives.text: Recover a sealed receipt from the settled dead

sangeroasa_collector_receipt.note: The Debt-Collector settles lives and leaves receipts -- 'account closed, rest' in a flat kind hand. Ileana read one and sent word to the Accord: death is learning a new dialect in the south, clerical and gentle, and it is not a threat. Threats are hope. This offers only rest.

sangeroasa_vent_ward.title: Wards Against the Warm Ground
sangeroasa_vent_ward.summary: The ground near the vents has grown warmer than even Sangeroasa's ground should be, and folk who sleep on it stop dreaming. Ana wants folk-wards set at the hot places, and lichen gathered to bind them.
sangeroasa_vent_ward.offer: Our ground runs warm always -- volcanic, honest heat. But there are patches now warmer than the vents can answer for, and the folk who bed near them wake settled-eyed and dreamless. I'd ward the hot places the old way -- a hex-sign, a chalk hand, a knot of blessed lichen -- but I need the lichen that grows only where the deep warmth reaches, and gathering it means kneeling on the very ground I mean to ward. Bring me a good measure. I'll bind the signs myself.
sangeroasa_vent_ward.progress: The blessed lichen grows where the deep warmth reaches, near the too-warm patches. Gather it for Ana's wards.
sangeroasa_vent_ward.complete: Enough to ward three hot patches, maybe four. I know a hex-sign won't stop what's warming the ground -- you can't chalk a door shut against something that lives under the floor. But it'll give a sleeper an honest night, and an honest night is a thing worth a ward even when the ward's half faith. Take the sign. My mother made these; I make them still; my daughter will, if the world lets her. That handing-down is the only magic I trust.

objectives.text: Gather blessed lichen from the warm ground

sangeroasa_vent_ward.note: The ground by the vents runs warmer than volcanic ground should, and those who sleep on it dream no more. Ana warded the hot places with chalk and lichen and half-faith, knowing a hex-sign cannot shut a door in the floor. It buys honest nights. She counts that enough, because it must be.

sangeroasa_ilion_hunt.title: The Tracker's Errand
sangeroasa_ilion_hunt.summary: Ilion the tracker-general has marked a slag-thrall pack as 'unresolved' and, unable to file the dead, has quietly asked the captain to see them ended. Costel passes the grim work to a stranger, and wonders what it means when even Ilion wants a thing to simply stop.
sangeroasa_ilion_hunt.offer: Ilion doesn't ask for help. He files, he tracks, he ends things himself and enters them closed. So when he leaves a mark on my duty-slate -- a thrall pack in the deep channels, tagged 'unresolved' -- it means something's beyond even him. Dead men he can't kill twice and can't file, because you can't close an account that keeps swinging iron. He wants them stopped. I want to know what stops a thing that can't die -- and I'd rather you learned it than me. End the pack. Bring me the truth of how they finally lie down.
sangeroasa_ilion_hunt.progress: Ilion's marked thrall pack labors the deep channels, unresolved even to the tracker-general. End them, and mark how the dead finally stop.
sangeroasa_ilion_hunt.complete: So they stop when you break the iron from their hands -- take away the labor and the animating purpose has nothing to grip, and they fold like a settled account. Ilion will want that noted, and I'll note it, and I'll not think too long about a man who files corpses being unable to file these. When even Ilion wants a thing to simply end, the world's tilted somewhere it won't tilt back. Take the reaver. It ended what he couldn't. That's no small blade.

objectives.text: End Ilion's unresolved thrall pack

sangeroasa_ilion_hunt.note: Ilion files everything unresolved, and could not file the laboring dead -- an account that will not close because it will not stop. He marked them for ending and looked away. When the man who catalogues corpses wants a thing to simply stop, Costel understood, the world has tilted somewhere it will not tilt back.

sangeroasa_forge_rest_toll.title: The Toll on the Ridge Road
sangeroasa_forge_rest_toll.summary: The ridge road out of Sangeroasa charges a toll that changes by the day and by who's collecting, and Iosif needs a discreet hand to settle the crossing so his blackglass caravans keep moving. Different faces, different prices, every dawn.
sangeroasa_forge_rest_toll.offer: The ridge road's the only clean way my glass leaves the city, and the toll on it is a living thing -- different collector each dawn, different price, and half of them answer to Ilion and the other half to nobody honest. I need it settled, quiet, so my caravan rolls before the heat of the day cracks the panes. Go clear the crossing of whoever's fouling it today and see my toll-post minded. Come back tomorrow; it'll want doing again. This city never lets a thing stay settled.
sangeroasa_forge_rest_toll.progress: The ridge-road crossing wants clearing today, whoever's fouling it. Settle it so the glass caravan rolls.
sangeroasa_forge_rest_toll.complete: Rolled clean, panes whole, and the toll paid to the right hand for once. It'll be a different hand tomorrow and a different price and I'll ask you again, because in Sangeroasa nothing stays closed but a grave -- and lately not even those. Here's your cut. A forged receipt, if you want to skip the post next dawn; I'll not tell Ilion, and Ilion, praise the vents, cannot yet read my mind.

objectives.text: Clear the ridge-road crossing

sangeroasa_forge_rest_toll.note: The ridge-road toll is never the same twice, and Iosif's glass moves only when it's settled that dawn. In a city that closes people and calls it order, a toll that will not stay closed is almost a comfort -- proof that something, at least, still refuses to be finalized.

sangeroasa_pit_daily_labor.title: Hours Against the Pit
sangeroasa_pit_daily_labor.summary: The Pit claims a fresh tally of labor each day, and each day the thralls born of yesterday's dead must be put down before they foul the living shifts. Manea posts the grim standing order to whoever will take it.
sangeroasa_pit_daily_labor.offer: The Pit takes its hours daily, and daily the ones it took come back to work -- dead men on the morning shift, swinging beside the living, and the living can't bear to strike their own. So the striking falls to strangers. It's a standing order, and I hate that it stands. Clear yesterday's dead off today's floor. Come back tomorrow; there'll be more. There are always more. The Pit keeps a ledger and the ledger's never in the black.
sangeroasa_pit_daily_labor.progress: Yesterday's dead labor today's floor as fresh thralls. Clear them before they foul the living shift.
sangeroasa_pit_daily_labor.complete: Cleared for a day. Tomorrow the Pit posts a fresh tally and I post this order again, and a stranger swings where a son can't. I keep a count of the days I've had to give this order. It's a long count. I keep it because someone should know how many mornings the dead reported for work in Sangeroasa, even if the someone is only me. Take your pay. Rest while the floor's quiet. It won't be long.

objectives.text: Clear the dead off today's shift

sangeroasa_pit_daily_labor.note: Each dawn the Pit's dead report for labor, and each dawn a stranger clears them so the living needn't strike their own. Manea keeps a count of the mornings this order has stood. It is a long count. He keeps it so that someone, at least, knows the tally the Pit will never publish.

sangeroasa_smith_apprentice.title: The Boy at the Bellows
sangeroasa_smith_apprentice.summary: A smith's apprentice has been working double shifts to buy his mother out of a pit-debt, and Fane -- who feeds the boy on the sly -- wants a good meal's worth of supplies gathered so the child eats before he burns out.
sangeroasa_smith_apprentice.offer: There's a boy works Gherghe's bellows, twelve if he's a day, pulling double shifts to buy his mother off the Pit's ledger before her debt takes her down it. He's got a good heart and a bad cough and he doesn't eat enough, so I feed him when he'll let me -- pride, that one, pride like his mother. Gather me the makings of a proper supper from the pit-side stores and I'll see he eats a real meal for once, and rests an hour, and stays a boy a little longer.
sangeroasa_smith_apprentice.progress: The makings of a proper supper wait in the pit-side stores. Gather them so the apprentice eats and rests.
sangeroasa_smith_apprentice.complete: Look at him go at that stew -- first hot meal in a week, and he's pretending he's not near tears over it, pride, like I said. He'll pull his shifts and buy his mother free or die trying, and I can't fix the Pit or the debt or the world under our feet. But I can fill a boy's belly and let him be a boy for one supper. That's a hearth's whole job, some nights. Take the poultice -- for that cough, or your own. Kindness keeps, if you let it.

objectives.text: Gather supper-makings from the pit-side stores

sangeroasa_smith_apprentice.note: A boy works double shifts to buy his mother off the Pit's ledger, and Fane feeds him on the sly and lets him rest an hour. He cannot fix the Pit, or the debt, or the ground. He can fill a belly. That, some nights, is a hearth's whole job -- and the boy, briefly, is only a boy.

sangeroasa_thin_veil.title: Where the Heat Wears Thin
sangeroasa_thin_veil.summary: Ozun has traced the symptom-ladder to its source: a place past the Pit where the veil between the world and the thing beneath is thinnest, and the Debt-Collectors are thickest. He needs the way cleared and the site witnessed before the Accord's answer can even begin.
sangeroasa_thin_veil.offer: I've read the dust, heard the listeners, watched the water climb. It all points to one place -- past the Pit's north rim, where the heat wears thin and the ground is thinnest over whatever writes itself below. The dead collectors gather there, drawn like soot-lines to a lodestone, and no one who reaches it comes back able to speak of it plain. But it must be witnessed. The Accord cannot answer a thing no living eye has confirmed. Cut through the collectors. Reach the thin place. Look -- once -- and turn away. Then come and tell me, in plain words, while you still have them.
sangeroasa_thin_veil.progress: The veil wears thinnest past the Pit's north rim, where the Debt-Collectors gather. Cut through, witness the thin place, and turn away before comprehension takes hold.
sangeroasa_thin_veil.complete: You came back with plain words. Good. Most don't. The ground there reads shifting orange under a true alchemist's sight -- the signal, writing itself, the colour of the thing that will one day be the Debt-System entire. It is awake beneath Sangeroasa, and the heat that made us the arsenal is the very cloak that let it wake unseen. I'll send this to the Accord tonight. It changes nothing today. It may change everything in time. Take the reaver -- and take my thanks, though thanks weighs light against what you looked at.

objectives.text: Cut through the collectors at the thin place

objectives.label: the thin place
objectives.text: Witness the thin place and turn away

sangeroasa_thin_veil.note: Past the Pit's north rim the ground reads shifting orange -- the signal writing itself, the seed of a colder age. The heat that made Sangeroasa the arsenal is the cloak that let the thing wake unseen. Ozun sent word to the Accord. It changes nothing today. It is the beginning of an answer, and beginnings are all the south can afford.

sangeroasa_ledger_burn.title: Ash Tells No One Anything
sangeroasa_ledger_burn.summary: The rim-ledger of the Pit has begun to write new names on its own, in a hand no clerk owns, and Ileana means to burn it before it can finalize a living soul. She needs the imps guarding it cleared and the deed witnessed, knowing full well it will only be renailed by dawn.
sangeroasa_ledger_burn.offer: The ledger at the Pit rim writes itself now. New names in a hand no clerk in this city owns -- names of the living, appearing overnight, labor-days already accruing. I mean to burn it. Ash tells no one anything, and a name unread is a life uncollected, at least until they nail up a fresh sheet. Which they will, by dawn. But a day's reprieve for the names on today's page is a day, and days are lives, one at a time. Clear the imps that nest on the rim and stand witness while I put the thing to the fire. Then we'll both pretend it stays burned.
sangeroasa_ledger_burn.progress: Brazier-imps nest on the Pit rim, guarding the self-writing ledger. Clear them so Ileana can burn the sheet.
sangeroasa_ledger_burn.complete: Burned. The names on today's page are ash, and the living they belonged to have a day they wouldn't have had. Tomorrow there'll be a fresh sheet in that same impossible hand and we'll burn that too, and the day after, and we'll lose in the end -- you don't out-write a thing that writes itself. But we bought these names a day. In Sangeroasa that's a victory, and I've stopped being ashamed to call it one. Take the writ. Unfile what you can. Burn what you can't.

objectives.text: Clear the imps guarding the self-writing ledger

objectives.label: the Pit rim
objectives.text: Stand witness as the ledger burns

sangeroasa_ledger_burn.note: The Pit's ledger writes the living into it now, in a hand no clerk owns, and Ileana burns each fresh sheet knowing another will be nailed by dawn. She buys the named a single day and calls it victory. You do not out-write a thing that writes itself. But a day is a life, one at a time, and she has stopped being ashamed to fight for one.

the_gift_thin_red_soil.title: The Soil Gone Thin
the_gift_thin_red_soil.summary: Mira's south row grew a chair from a walking-stick within living memory. This spring the red came up pale and the seed sat dumb in it. She wants a second pair of eyes on the furrow before she says the word she is afraid to say.
the_gift_thin_red_soil.offer: My grandmother planted a broom-handle in that row for a joke and we ate off the table it grew. Now look at it -- pink where it should run dark, and the barley just sitting there, sulking. Walk the south furrows with me before I have to go asking the overseers for the fresh red. You know what fresh red is. Everyone here knows and nobody says.
the_gift_thin_red_soil.progress: The pale rows lie at the southern skirt of the Gift, past the last dark furrow. Reach them and read the soil for yourself.
the_gift_thin_red_soil.complete: Pale as a scar, all of it. You felt how it's gone cold, too -- warm ground's the good ground here, that's the joke of the place. This row's asking for feeding and I know what it eats. I'll not decide today. Take a coin for the walk. Some walks you take so you're not the only one who saw.

objectives.label: the pale south furrows
objectives.text: Walk the pale south rows with Mira

the_gift_thin_red_soil.note: The red came up pale, and the good ground here is the warm ground -- a joke nobody in the Gift finds funny twice. Mira did not send for the overseers. Not that day.

the_gift_fresh_red.title: Fresh Red
the_gift_fresh_red.summary: The row will take no seed until it is fed, and in the Gift a field is fed with the dead. Mira asks the player to broker fresh red from the Debt Pit overseers -- to save one harvest by carrying a body to a furrow, the way this land has fed itself for six hundred years.
the_gift_fresh_red.offer: I said I'd not decide, and the row decided for me -- three days and the seed still sulks. So. The overseers hold the ones the Pit is done with, and they'll trade fresh red to a farmer who asks plain. Carry me a load and turn the furrow with it, and the barley comes. I know what I'm asking. My whole family has always known and eaten anyway. Say yes or say no, but say it to my face.
the_gift_fresh_red.progress: The Debt Pit overseers keep the fresh red at the pit rim, east of the killing floors. Speak to the overseer, then turn it into Mira's furrow.
the_gift_fresh_red.complete: It's turned in. The row's already warming under my hand -- warm's the good sign, warm's the harvest. And I'll not sleep tonight, and I'll eat the bread come autumn, and I'll thank the Gift for it out loud in the market like my mother did. That's the whole of the south in one loaf. Take the skinner. It was a Pit man's. He's in the furrow now.

objectives.text: Ask the overseer for fresh red at the Pit rim

objectives.label: Mira's fed furrow
objectives.text: Turn the fresh red into Mira's row

the_gift_fresh_red.note: The furrow took the fresh red and went warm under Mira's hand, and the barley will come. Every meal in the south is a body. There is no ethical harvest -- only the one you can carry, and the sleep it costs.

the_gift_childs_shoe.title: A Small Thing in the Furrow
the_gift_childs_shoe.summary: A child's shoe has come up in the abundant row where the barley grows best -- pressed into the red, small and whole. Ecaterina keeps the Gift's chronicle and will not write a lie into it. She asks the player to stand at the row and read what the ground offers, and decide what the record says.
the_gift_childs_shoe.offer: I keep the book of this place, and the book has a rule: it counts what is, not what we can bear. A shoe came up in the best row -- a child's, the buckle still bright -- and the harvest above it is the finest in the Gift. Abundance and the atrocity sharing one furrow. Go and stand at that row and look, truly look. Then come tell me. I will write down whatever you say. I would rather it were true.
the_gift_childs_shoe.progress: The abundant row lies at the heart of the Gift's dark soil. Reach it, and stand at the shoe.
the_gift_childs_shoe.complete: You saw it, then. The shoe and the barley from the same handful of red. I'll write it plain: the harvest was good, and this is what good costs here. No man now living planted that child, and the row does not care. Take the shroud. In the south we wrap the named so the ground gets a body and the mother gets a grave. It is the only mercy we can afford, and we cannot always afford it.

objectives.label: the abundant row
objectives.text: Stand at the row where the shoe came up
Tell Ecaterina what the furrow held

the_gift_childs_shoe.note: A child's shoe and the finest barley in the Gift came up from the same red handful. Ecaterina wrote it down as it was. The book counts what is. Somebody has to.

the_gift_the_reap.title: The Reaping-Voice
the_gift_the_reap.summary: The Mourners of the Gift -- old women who once wept the harvest home so the dead would rest quiet under the barley -- have started singing the same three notes in unison, at the same hour, in a voice too flat to be grief. Ecaterina fears the rite has stopped being theirs. She asks the player to end the ones the ground now speaks through.
the_gift_the_reap.offer: There have always been Mourners here. Widows, mostly, who walk the rows at reaping and weep for the corpses we plant, so the harvest is grief and not just greed. It was a kindness. Now they sing three notes together, all of them, at the hour the hammers go quiet -- and it is not weeping, it is keeping time. Something borrows their mouths. Go and quiet the ones past saving. Then come home and I will pretend it was mercy.
the_gift_the_reap.progress: The changed Mourners walk the deep rows of the Gift after the hammers still. Quiet the ones the ground now sings through.
the_gift_the_reap.complete: Quieted. And you heard it -- three notes, flat as a ledger line, the same the hammers keep when they stop for their count of three. The Gift always ate the body. Now it wants the voice. I have written that down and I wish I had not. Take the counteragent. Drink it if the notes follow you home. They followed me.

objectives.text: Quiet the Mourners the ground sings through

the_gift_the_reap.note: The Mourners kept the three notes the still hammers keep. The Gift long ate the body; now it borrows the voice. Ecaterina named it in the chronicle and asked no one to read that page.

the_gift_market_bread.title: Bread for the Market
the_gift_market_bread.summary: Reaping-day fills Sanda's taproom, and a full room wants dark barley loaves that only the Gift's flour makes right. She sends the player to gather threshed grain from the day's harvest so the bread is on the boards before the forge-crews come off shift.
the_gift_market_bread.offer: Reaping-day and the whole shift comes through my door hungry as the Pit. There's no bread like Gift bread -- comes out dark and sweet and nobody here asks why, we're raised not to. Fetch me threshed grain off the floor, good and clean, four sacks' worth, and I'll have loaves up before the hammers quit. A warm loaf and a warm bowl for you, and you'll have earned both.
the_gift_market_bread.progress: The threshing floor runs along the Gift's north edge, where the barley comes in. Gather four sacks of clean grain.
the_gift_market_bread.complete: Now that's flour. The boards will groan with loaves and the crews will eat till they're loud, and for one evening this place is just a warm room that smells of bread. That's worth doing. That's worth doing even here. Sit -- eat one hot. You don't have to think about the field to be glad of the loaf. Nobody in the Gift does. That's the trick of us.

objectives.text: Gather threshed Gift grain

the_gift_market_bread.note: The taproom was warm and loud and full of dark sweet bread, and for one evening no one in the Gift asked what the flour was grown in. Everything good here is temporary. That is what makes it good.

the_gift_forge_crew_meal.title: A Meal Off the Floor
the_gift_forge_crew_meal.summary: The forge-crews come off the killing floors with a hunger that honest work earns, and Sanda's stew wants more than barley in it. She asks the player to bring good cuts from the beasts that den in the vents, so the pack that keeps pace can eat like it kept pace.
the_gift_forge_crew_meal.offer: My crews break their backs and they'll not eat thin for it, not on my watch. Brazier-imps have denned in the warm vents south of the works -- vicious little cinders, but there's good rendering fat on them and it takes the barley from grey to grand. Bring me tallow off eight and there's a bowl with your name spooned into it. A pack that keeps pace eats. That's the one fairness we've got.
the_gift_forge_crew_meal.progress: The brazier-imps den in the warm vents south of the forge-works. Render tallow from eight.
the_gift_forge_crew_meal.complete: That'll do the pot proud. Sit the crews down and watch them go quiet with eating -- happiest sound in the south, a room too full to talk. They keep the pace and I keep the pot, and it's cruel here in a hundred ways but this bowl isn't one of them. Eat. You kept pace today too. Take the flint-striker; a cook's gift, and it never sulks in the wet.

objectives.text: Render tallow from the vent brazier-imps

the_gift_forge_crew_meal.note: The crews ate till the room went quiet, which is the happiest sound the south makes. A pack that keeps pace eats. It is the only fairness the Gift owns, and Sanda ladles it out like it were plenty.

the_gift_pit_ledger.title: The Rim Ledger
the_gift_pit_ledger.summary: A ledger is nailed to the Debt Pit's rim, names against labor-days owed, and Rada trades in what it says before it says it. She wants the player to read down it and bring her the tally -- so she can price a man's remaining days before the overseers do.
the_gift_pit_ledger.offer: Everything in the south is a debt with a body attached, and I make my living knowing whose is nearly due. There's a ledger nailed at the Pit rim -- names, labor-days owed, and a last column that reads plain for the ones who've paid in full. Walk it for me, top to bottom, and bring back the tally. A man near the bottom of that list is a man whose tools come cheap by month's end. It's ugly. It's also true, and true is what I sell.
the_gift_pit_ledger.progress: The rim ledger hangs at the Debt Pit's edge, past the blood-channels. Read it down and bring Rada the tally.
the_gift_pit_ledger.complete: Good. Now I know who's spent before they do. That last column -- the one that just reads the one word for the dead -- somebody wrote that word a thousand years too early for it to mean what it's going to mean. I don't like how it's written. Same flat hand, every line, and the ink's warm to the touch. Here's your cut. Spend it before it's owed to somebody.

objectives.label: the Debt Pit rim
objectives.text: Read down the ledger nailed at the Pit rim
Bring Rada the ledger tally

the_gift_pit_ledger.note: The rim ledger's last column, for the dead, read collected -- a word a thousand years too early, in a flat warm hand. Rada priced the living off it. In the south, the paperwork is the harvest.

the_gift_overseers_cut.title: The Overseer's Cut
the_gift_overseers_cut.summary: An overseer skims fresh red off the Pit's dead before the farmers get their share, selling the strongest corpses on the side. Rada has the proof and three buyers for it. She sets the player to gather the skimmer's forged receipts -- then to decide who the leverage lands on.
the_gift_overseers_cut.offer: An overseer's been cutting the fat off the top -- keeping the strongest dead for private sale and stamping the loss as 'worked to spending.' I've a dossier half-built. Get me his forged toll-receipts off the collection posts and I've got him by the collar. Then there's a choice, and it's a real one: I sell it to Valrom's files and the man goes in his own Pit, or I sell it back to him and he owes us fresh red for life. Get the paper. We'll argue the rest.
the_gift_overseers_cut.progress: The skimmer's forged receipts are pinned at the collection posts around the Pit. Gather six.
the_gift_overseers_cut.complete: Six, and every one a forgery -- see the seal, pressed twice where a nervous hand double-checked itself. Now I own an overseer, and owning one is worth more than jailing one. He'll send fresh red to the thin rows for a year to keep this quiet. Everybody's fed, nobody's hanged, and one bad man works off a debt he'll never finish. That's a southern mercy. Take your cut and don't examine it.

objectives.text: Gather the skimmer's forged receipts

the_gift_overseers_cut.note: Rada owns an overseer now, which in the south is worth more than hanging one. The thin rows will be fed on a bad man's leverage. A southern mercy: nobody hanged, everybody eating, no one clean.

the_gift_plow_broke.title: A Plow Against the Warm Ground
the_gift_plow_broke.summary: Plowshares snap in the Gift where the ground runs unnaturally warm -- the good ground, the fertile ground, the ground you should not stand on long. Voica needs blackglass off the vents to edge shares that will bite it. She sends the player to the glass fields.
the_gift_plow_broke.offer: A plow's an honest tool and this ground breaks it. Where the red's richest it runs warm -- warm as a sleeping body -- and warm ground eats iron edges in a season. I edge my shares in blackglass now, worked volcanic, the only thing that keeps a bite in that soil. Fetch me raw glass off the vent fields. And mind you don't stand still on the warm rows. Folk who stand still on the warm ground get a look on them. You'll know it when you see it.
the_gift_plow_broke.progress: The blackglass beds lie among the active vents at the Gift's southern edge. Gather six raw pieces -- and keep moving on the warm rows.
the_gift_plow_broke.complete: Good glass, clean fracture, it'll take an edge that bites the warm soil for a full reaping. You kept moving out there? Good. My cousin didn't. Stood on a warm row to rest his back one noon and by supper he was tilting his head at the furrow like it was talking low. He plows fine now. He just... listens while he does it. Take the striker-glass razor. Sharp, and it never went warm on me. That matters here.

objectives.text: Gather raw blackglass from the vent beds

the_gift_plow_broke.note: The good ground of the Gift runs warm, and warm is the ground you must not stand on. Voica edges her shares in blackglass to bite it. Her cousin plows it fine now. He just listens while he does.

the_gift_share_temper.title: Temper for the Reaping Steel
the_gift_share_temper.summary: Reaping-week is coming and every scythe in the Gift wants an edge. Voica's forge runs day and night; she needs bog-iron hauled from the low channels to keep the temper true. Honest heavy work, and the kind that ends with a whole quarter's blades hung ready.
the_gift_share_temper.offer: Reaping-week and every farmer wants a scythe that'll take the barley in one draw. I'm at the anvil dark to dark and short on iron -- the good bog-iron off the low channels, not the slag they push at me from the Pit. Haul me a load. It's back-work and no glory, but there's a fair few blades in it, and there's a satisfaction, hanging a wall of ready steel before the harvest. Simple thing to be proud of. We don't get many of those.
the_gift_share_temper.progress: Bog-iron settles in the low blood-channels below the works. Haul six loads to Voica's forge.
the_gift_share_temper.complete: That's a week of scythes right there, hung and waiting. Look at them -- a whole wall of good steel and every one going out to cut bread from the ground come morning. I made that. In a place like this you take your pride where it's clean, and a hung wall of honest blades is as clean as the south gets. Take the emberfold edge. First off the week's temper. It's a good one; I'd not give you a poor one.

objectives.text: Haul bog-iron to Voica's forge

the_gift_share_temper.note: Voica hung a wall of ready scythes before reaping-week, and took her pride where it was clean. A hung wall of honest blades is as clean as the south gets. She knows what they cut, and cuts anyway.

the_gift_thin_rows_daily.title: Tending the Thin Rows
the_gift_thin_rows_daily.summary: The thin rows keep going pale faster than one farmer can feed them, and the imps come up out of the warm vents to root at the seed. Each reaping-dawn Mira asks for the vent-brood cleared before they take the little the pale soil holds.
the_gift_thin_rows_daily.offer: Same ask, most mornings, and I'm sorry for it: the vent-brood's rooting my thin rows again, cinders after the seed. They smell the warm soil coming through and they come up hungry. Clear a nestful for me before the light's full? It's never finished. Nothing here is ever finished. But cleared today is fed today, and fed today is a loaf we'll have. Come back tomorrow. There's always a tomorrow's worth in the Gift.
the_gift_thin_rows_daily.progress: The vent-brood roots the thin rows at the Gift's southern skirt. Clear a nestful before full light.
the_gift_thin_rows_daily.complete: Cleared. The seed's got a day's peace and I've got a day's hope, and that's the size of it here -- you don't win the field, you win the morning. Take the coin and my thanks. Come back at dawn; the vents don't rest and neither, more's the pity, do I. But we're fed today. In the Gift you say that like a prayer, because it is one.

objectives.text: Clear the vent-brood from the thin rows

the_gift_thin_rows_daily.note: You do not win the field in the Gift. You win the morning. Mira wins it again, and again, and calls being fed today a prayer -- because in the south that is exactly what it is.

the_gift_rim_watch_daily.title: The Rim Watch
the_gift_rim_watch_daily.summary: The Debt Pit rim wants walking each shift -- the changed Mourners drift up from the deep rows toward the works, and the ledgers get pilfered by those trying to unwrite a name. Ruxandra pays a standing bounty for a rim kept quiet.
the_gift_rim_watch_daily.offer: Rim needs a set of eyes each shift and I've not enough of my own. The Mourners come drifting up from the deep rows humming their three flat notes, and now and then some poor soul tries to prise a name off the ledger before the Pit collects it -- as if unwriting it changed the body's mind. Walk the rim, quiet what needs it. Standing pay for a standing worry. It's never done. The Pit doesn't take days off, so neither does the worry.
the_gift_rim_watch_daily.progress: The changed Mourners drift up along the Debt Pit rim each shift. Quiet a set and hold the ledgers.
the_gift_rim_watch_daily.complete: Rim's quiet again. For a shift. You unwrite a name off that ledger and the body still walks its labor-days -- I've watched folk learn that the hard way, weeping over ink like it was a grave. Take the pay. Come back next shift; the humming starts again by the hammers' count, regular as a debt coming due. In the south, everything comes due. That's the whole religion.

objectives.text: Quiet the Mourners drifting up the rim
