Something directs the flights, moving live signal across the city under everyone's noses. The map of where the drakes have flown is a map of where the fog means to grow.

greyhollow_side_the_ferry_does_not_wait.title: The Ferry Does Not Wait
greyhollow_side_the_ferry_does_not_wait.summary: A grey ferry runs from Greyhollow's water-gate deeper into the drowned city, and it does not wait, and it does not run on a schedule anyone has ever caught the pattern of. Mircea's watch-partner boarded it last week to chase a fleeing forger and has not come back. He asks you to stand the ferry landing and learn its hours -- and to look for a face.
greyhollow_side_the_ferry_does_not_wait.offer: There's a ferry off the water-gate, grey wood, grey ferryman, running deeper into the drowned quarter where our writ doesn't reach. It keeps no schedule I've cracked in six years of watching -- comes when it comes, leaves when it leaves, waits for no one. My partner Ostrov boarded it last week chasing a forger and I've not seen him since, and the ferryman only shrugs when I ask, if a shrug is a thing a man that grey can be said to do. Stand the landing for me. Learn its hours if it has any. And watch the deck when it returns -- watch for Ostrov's face, and don't tell me if it's changed.
greyhollow_side_the_ferry_does_not_wait.progress: Stand the ferry landing at the water-gate, learn the grey ferry's hours, and watch the returning deck for Ostrov.
greyhollow_side_the_ferry_does_not_wait.complete: You watched it come and go the whole night and there's no pattern, you say -- it runs to no clock but its own, deeper in, where the fog's so thick the gaslight drowns in it. And Ostrov wasn't on the deck. Not changed, not unchanged. Just not there. Which means he's still in the drowned quarter, or he's water now, and I've learned this year not to ask the ferryman which. I'll go in after him one day, when I've the nerve. The ferry doesn't wait -- but it does return, and a thing that returns can be boarded. Take my thanks, and if you go in before I find my nerve, look for him. Grey coat. Kind face. He owed everyone and paid it anyway.

objectives.label: the grey ferry landing
objectives.text: Stand the ferry landing and watch the returning deck

greyhollow_side_the_ferry_does_not_wait.note: A grey ferry runs deeper into the drowned quarter, to no clock but its own, where the gaslight drowns in the fog. Mircea's partner boarded it and did not return -- not changed, not unchanged, just not there. The ferry waits for no one. But it does return, and a thing that returns can be boarded.

basaltfang_hunt_name_earned.title: A Name from the Fang
basaltfang_hunt_name_earned.summary: The varcolaci packs on the high fang name their outsiders by what the range sees them do. Tudor keeps the hunt-camp fire and the old courtesy: run the pack-rite, and be named. It is the honestest thing in the South.
basaltfang_hunt_name_earned.offer: Down in Blestem a stranger's worth a file. Up here it's worth a name, and the name's earned in blood off the ridge -- the pack sees what you are, and calls it. Vursa's runners hold the low scree. Take a hunt off them, clean. Then the fang'll have something to howl about you. After the ledger cities, I know how that sounds. Kindly. It sounds kindly.
basaltfang_hunt_name_earned.progress: The runners work the low scree under the haze. Take your hunt clean, the way the pack watches for.
basaltfang_hunt_name_earned.complete: There. Heard it go up the relay -- one ridge to the next, your name in it. That's yours now, earned honest, no file, no toll. ...Odd, though. It came back off the far fang sounding a hair different than it went out. Wind, most like. The range does that. Take the cord -- you're named on it now, whatever the wind decides that means.

objectives.text: Take your hunt off the low scree
Bring your kill-tally to Tudor at the fire

basaltfang_hunt_name_earned.note: The fang gave a name, freely, for honest work -- a thing no city in the South would ever do. It came back down the pack-relay one ridge later meaning something almost else. Wind, Tudor said. He did not say it twice.

basaltfang_hunt_name_answered.title: The Name Comes Back
basaltfang_hunt_name_answered.summary: Cornel reads the vents for the pack -- omens in the haze, weather in the smoke. Your hunt-name went down the relay and came back changed, and the change followed the same rhyme the vents have started keeping. He wants you at the live stone to hear it yourself.
basaltfang_hunt_name_answered.offer: I read smoke for a living, and lately the smoke reads back. Your name went out of Tudor's mouth one thing and came home off the south fang another -- and the shift in it kept time with the vents. Three notes, near enough, converging. Go down to the leaning stone past the diggings, the warm one, and stand where I say. Don't speak the name there. Just listen for who's saying it now.
basaltfang_hunt_name_answered.progress: The live stone leans past the blackglass diggings, above the one warm patch of ground. Reach it, and listen. Say nothing.
basaltfang_hunt_name_answered.complete: You heard it. Your name, in the drift off the vent, and not in your voice or mine. It's being kept somewhere now -- the way a shaman keeps an omen, only this omen keeps you. I burned my reading of it unread. Ash tells no one anything. But the notes are a hair closer than they were at dawn, and that's not weather. Take the charm. Wear it when the smoke starts rhyming.

objectives.text: Hear Cornel's reading of the vents

objectives.label: the leaning stone above the warm ground
objectives.text: Stand at the live stone and listen

basaltfang_hunt_name_answered.note: The name came back off the fang carried on the vent-drift, in no living mouth. Something under the range has begun to keep it. Cornel burned his omen unread. The three notes hold -- a hair closer than they were.

basaltfang_hunt_name_spent.title: What a Name Is Worth
basaltfang_hunt_name_spent.summary: A hunt-name can be kept or spent -- traded down to the pack-mother for a place in the last hunt before the convoys go down to Sangeroasa. Tudor lays the choice out plain. He does not tell you the range has already decided which it prefers.
basaltfang_hunt_name_spent.offer: Here's the range's oldest bargain, and I'll not dress it. Keep your name and stay a guest of the fang, welcome and apart. Or spend it -- speak it once to the pack-mother's runners and hunt as one of them, in the pack, for the convoy's last climb down. Spent, it's gone; you'll be one of Vursa's, no longer your own. Folk think the honest choice is the free one. Up here the honest choice is just the one nobody hid from you.
basaltfang_hunt_name_spent.progress: Vursa's pack forms on the high fang for the convoy's last climb. Spend the name among the runners, or keep it -- either way, clear the road down.
basaltfang_hunt_name_spent.complete: It's done, and the convoy's away down to the forge-city. Whichever you chose, the range logged it -- I can hear that now, thanks to you and Cornel both. A kept name, a spent name; the fang wanted the hunt run either way, and it got its hunt. That's the thing about honest bargains in the South. They're still bargains. Take the skinner. You'll want an edge that doesn't ask questions.

objectives.text: Clear the convoy's climb, named or unnamed
Tell Tudor which the name became

basaltfang_hunt_name_spent.note: Kept or spent, the name served the hunt the range wanted run. The convoy went down to the forge unmolested. Both roads pleased something under the fang equally -- which is how you know it was never your choice to lose.

basaltfang_obsidian_alpha.title: The Glass-Backed Alpha
basaltfang_obsidian_alpha.summary: Danciu is the one human trapper fool enough to den on the high fang, and he lies about the size of every animal he has ever seen -- except the obsidian wolves, which he describes with religious accuracy. He wants the glass-pelted alpha off his trapline before it teaches the others.
basaltfang_obsidian_alpha.offer: Now, most of what I tell you is a lie -- ask anyone, ask the wolves -- but this one's true as tax: there's an alpha up on my line with a pelt like broken glass, and it's clever. Clever enough to spring my sets and leave the bait, which is a personal insult. The pack runs the scree past the diggings. Put the glass-backed one down before the pups get ideas. I'll make it worth a warm night.
basaltfang_obsidian_alpha.progress: The obsidian pack runs the scree past the blackglass diggings. The alpha's the one that shines wrong. End it.
basaltfang_obsidian_alpha.complete: Ha! Look at that hide -- black glass, every hair, catches the vent-light like a mirror that's ashamed of itself. Told you that part true, didn't I. I'll knap the claws for luck and lie about the rest for years. Here, a proper coin and a razor Cremene owed me. You keep the fang honest better than I do, and that's saying nothing, because I don't.

objectives.text: Put down the glass-backed alpha

basaltfang_obsidian_alpha.note: The alpha's pelt was every bit the black glass Danciu swore it was -- the one true thing in a man who lies for warmth. He'll tell the tale wrong by winter. The truth was in the hide, and he kept that much.

basaltfang_obsidian_trophy_wall.title: Danciu's Wall of Honest Lies
basaltfang_obsidian_trophy_wall.summary: Every trophy on Danciu's cabin wall has a taller story than the animal deserved. He wants one wall that is true -- pelts enough from the obsidian pack that the glass will speak for itself, and he will finally have to shut up.
basaltfang_obsidian_trophy_wall.offer: I've a wall of trophies and not a true word among 'em. Bear the size of a barn -- was a cub. Wolf that talked -- didn't. I'm getting old up here and I'd like one honest wall before the fang has me. Bring me pelts off the glass-pack, a good stack, and I'll hang 'em plain, no story. Let the glass do the lying. It's better at it than I am, and it never has to warm up to it.
basaltfang_obsidian_trophy_wall.progress: Take glass-pelts off the obsidian pack on the scree -- enough for a wall that needs no story.
basaltfang_obsidian_trophy_wall.complete: There. A wall of black glass and not one lie nailed to it. Feels strange -- like standing in church sober. The vent-light comes through 'em and lays these little lines on the floor, all one way, tidy as a fence. Huh. Didn't do that in the old cabin. ...Ah, never mind an old fool. Take the striker. You gave me an honest wall. That's more than I've given anyone.

objectives.text: Gather glass-pelts for the honest wall

basaltfang_obsidian_trophy_wall.note: Danciu got his one true wall. The vent-light through the black glass throws thin lines across his floor, all lying the same way, tidy as fence-posts. He called himself an old fool and looked away. He is not a fool. He is only usually wrong about which thing to fear.

basaltfang_trappers_last_lie.title: The One He Got Right
basaltfang_trappers_last_lie.summary: Danciu has stopped coming down to the post. Aurel the road-warden liked the old liar and fears the range finally called a bluff it does not have. Go up the trapline; find out which of the trapper's tales was the true one.
basaltfang_trappers_last_lie.offer: The old liar's not come down in four days. That's nothing, for him -- he'll den in a snowbank to finish a bad joke. But he left his fire cold, and Danciu never leaves a fire. He used to say the fang would take him mid-sentence, laughing. Everyone laughed back. Go up his line. If he's just drunk and horizontal, cuff him for me. If he's not -- bring down whatever the range left, so I can bury the one true thing about him.
basaltfang_trappers_last_lie.progress: Danciu's trapline climbs the high scree above his cold cabin. Find the trapper, or what the range left of him.
basaltfang_trappers_last_lie.complete: Cold camp, cold fire, and his boots still by it, laced. He always said the fang would take him laughing, and everyone thought that was another lie. Turns out it was the one he got right. No wound on him, Cornel says -- clean hands, still smiling. That's not the range. That's the other thing. Hang his glass-claws on the post. He'd hate a solemn word, so I'll not say one. Take the cord. He'd want you warm.

objectives.label: Danciu's cold camp on the high scree
objectives.text: Reach the trapper's cold camp
Bring word down to Aurel at the post

basaltfang_trappers_last_lie.note: Danciu died smiling, no wound on him, clean hands -- the death the Courier died, a range away and years apart. He always swore the fang would take him laughing. It was the one lie that wasn't. Aurel hung the glass-claws on the post and said no solemn word.

basaltfang_knappers_flawed_cores.title: Because They Tried
basaltfang_knappers_flawed_cores.summary: Ursu knaps blackglass the old Border way -- flint before glass, patience before edge. He keeps his failed cores on a shelf, one for every apprentice the fang took from him. He needs fresh vent-glass, and he needs someone who will not laugh at the shelf.
basaltfang_knappers_flawed_cores.offer: Blackglass takes an edge once -- it never needed a second, and that's a lesson before it's a stone. My cores are running out and the good seams are up past the claim-guards, who think the whole range is theirs to bill. Dig me clean glass from the high diggings. And when you bring it -- see the shelf? Every flawed core there was somebody's first try before the fang had 'em. I keep 'em because they tried. Don't knock the shelf.
basaltfang_knappers_flawed_cores.progress: The good blackglass seam sits high past the claim-guards. Knap clean glass and bring it down to Ursu's bench.
basaltfang_knappers_flawed_cores.complete: Clean glass -- see how it wants to split true? That's the range being honest for once. I'll set the best of it aside and shelve the failures with the rest. Someday somebody knaps me one that doesn't crack, and I'll not know what to do with my hands. Here -- an edge off the last good core. Takes its edge once, mind. So did the folk on that shelf. Once was enough to matter.

objectives.text: Knap clean glass from the high seam
Bring the glass to Ursu's bench

basaltfang_knappers_flawed_cores.note: Ursu keeps a shelf of cracked cores, one for each apprentice the fang took, because they tried. He shelved another failure and did not say whose. The good glass takes its edge once. So, he says, did they. Once was enough to matter to him.

basaltfang_obsidian_heart_seam.title: The Heart of the Black Seam
basaltfang_obsidian_heart_seam.summary: Deep in the diggings sleeps a vent-core of glass so dark it drinks the forge-light -- an obsidian heart, the knappers call it. Ursu wants it before Sangeroasa's armorers hear it exists. What such a stone is for, he will only say once it is on his bench.
basaltfang_obsidian_heart_seam.offer: There's a heart-core down the deep seam -- glass so black it eats the light off my coals, drinks it and gives nothing back. A focus-stone, if you knew the working, and I do. But the forge-city's armorers will strip this whole range for one, and they've longer arms than me. Fetch it up before word climbs the road. What it's for -- I'll tell you at the bench, and not on the wind, because the wind up here carries.
basaltfang_obsidian_heart_seam.progress: The heart-core sleeps in the deep blackglass seam past the diggings. Cut it free and bring it down before word climbs the road.
basaltfang_obsidian_heart_seam.complete: There it is -- feel it drink the light off your hand? A focus, for a working the Hessik folk would kill to shelve. I'll not send it up to Valrom's armorers to point at someone. It'll stay on my bench, dark and useless and mine, and that's the safest a thing like this ever gets. Take its lesser twin -- an honest focus, no teeth in it. And keep the deep seam to yourself. Some seams pay better left unspoken.

objectives.label: the deep blackglass seam
objectives.text: Cut the heart-core from the deep seam
Deliver the heart-core to Ursu's bench

basaltfang_obsidian_heart_seam.note: The heart-core drinks light and gives nothing back. Ursu kept it off Sangeroasa's benches and dark on his own -- the safest a thing like that ever gets. He would not name aloud the working it serves. The wind up here carries.

basaltfang_claim_guard_toll.title: The Claim That Bills the Range
basaltfang_claim_guard_toll.summary: Someone has staked a paper claim on the whole high seam and posted armed guards to bill every knapper who cuts a stone. Vintila the factor smells a forgery -- no throne charters basalt. Thin the claim-guards so honest cutters can work, and see whose seal the toll-writ carries.
basaltfang_claim_guard_toll.offer: There's a claim posted on the high diggings -- guards, tolls, the whole insult -- and I've factored this range twenty years and never heard of it. No throne bothers to charter black rock. Somebody wrote themselves a right and hired arms to enforce it. Thin the guards enough that a knapper can cut without paying a stranger, and bring me a toll-writ off one of them. I want to know whose seal is bold enough to bill a mountain.
basaltfang_claim_guard_toll.progress: The claim-guards hold the high seam and bill every cutter. Thin them, and lift a toll-writ to read the seal.
basaltfang_claim_guard_toll.complete: Let me see the seal -- ah. Not a throne's. A Blestem clerk's mark, the kind you can buy dry off a Riddler's stall. So a paper man in the ledger-city sold himself a mountain and hired blades to make the lie stand up. That's the whole South in a writ, isn't it. The guards'll thin faster than the paper. Take your cut -- honest coin, off a dishonest claim I'll be filing to burn.

objectives.text: Thin the claim-guards on the high seam
Lift a toll-writ to read its seal

basaltfang_claim_guard_toll.note: The claim was a forgery -- a Blestem clerk's bought seal billing a mountain that answers to no throne. Paper wrote itself a right and hired blades to make it true. Vintila will file to burn it. The blades thinned faster than the paper ever will.

basaltfang_convoy_last_climb.title: The Last Climb Down
basaltfang_convoy_last_climb.summary: The war-caravans that climb from the Bloodroad must cross the fang before the drop to Sangeroasa, and the packs no longer let them pass free. Vintila needs the road cleared for the last convoy of the season -- and needs someone to count what the wagons carry.
basaltfang_convoy_last_climb.offer: The season's last convoy is up from the Bloodroad and stalled on my fang -- war-freight for the forge-city, and the packs won't wave it through the way they used to. Clear the road down. And while you're counting heads on the wolves, count the wagons too, quiet. Valrom's arming against somebody in a quantity that only makes sense if the somebody is everybody. I like to know what climbs my mountain before it kills its way past me.
basaltfang_convoy_last_climb.progress: The stalled convoy holds on the fang road. Clear the drop to Sangeroasa, and count the war-wagons as they roll.
basaltfang_convoy_last_climb.complete: Road's clear and the wagons are away down the drop. I counted -- twice, then wished I hadn't. That's not a war's worth of steel. That's every war's worth, all at once, pointed nowhere and everywhere. A king arms like that when a king's stopped being a king and started being a thing that just wants forges fed. ...Listen to me. Take your pay. Forget my arithmetic. The range would rather you didn't do sums up here.

objectives.text: Clear the convoy's drop to the forge-city
Bring Vintila the wagon-count

basaltfang_convoy_last_climb.note: The last convoy went down to the forge -- and its count made no sense as a war, only as a hunger. Valrom arms against everyone at once, which is what a king does when the king is a thing wanting forges fed. Vintila took his pay and asked to forget his own arithmetic.

basaltfang_vent_omen_reading.title: The Vents Read Back
basaltfang_vent_omen_reading.summary: Cornel reads weather in the vent-smoke for the packs, an honest old craft. Lately the smoke answers questions he did not ask, and its answers have begun to rhyme. He wants a witness at the vents before he dares call it more than weather.
basaltfang_vent_omen_reading.offer: I call the weather off the vents -- always have, my mother did, hers before. Smoke leans east, hunt eats; smoke sits low, den down. Craft, not magic. But last three readings, the smoke told me things I hadn't asked -- and told 'em in a kind of time, like a tune with two notes reaching for a third. I need eyes that aren't mine at the vents. Stand where the smoke gathers. Tell me if you hear it counting. Then tell me I'm an old fool who's read too much haze.
basaltfang_vent_omen_reading.progress: The smoke gathers thick at the low vents under the fang. Stand in the reading-place, and hear whether it counts.
basaltfang_vent_omen_reading.complete: You heard it count. Then I'm not the fool, and I'd give a finger to be. Two notes reaching for a third, in vent-smoke, on a range that never had a note in it before the convoys started climbing. My mother read weather. I'm reading something that reads me. I'll keep calling it haze to the packs -- they've enough to hunt. Take the still. If the smoke ever runs orange, don't stand where you're standing now.

objectives.label: the low vents where the smoke gathers
objectives.text: Stand in the vent-reading and listen
Tell Cornel what the smoke counted

basaltfang_vent_omen_reading.note: The vent-smoke has learned to count -- two notes reaching for a third, on a range that had no note before the convoys came. Cornel will call it haze to spare the packs. He reads weather no longer. Now the weather reads him.

basaltfang_shaman_warning_south.title: A Warning Down the Wind
basaltfang_shaman_warning_south.summary: The Ashvents wardens south of here read the same vents Cornel does. If the smoke has learned to count on the fang, it will learn faster where the vents run hottest. Cornel wants his omen carried down toward the warden's post before the heat there hides it entirely.
basaltfang_shaman_warning_south.offer: Whatever's teaching the smoke to count starts small on my cold fang and gets loud where the vents run open -- and there's no hotter vents than the Ashvents, south past the forge-city. Their wardens read smoke like I do. If it's rhyming here it's shouting there, and the heat down there hides the tell. Carry my reading to the road-warden's post so it can go south with the convoys. Sealed. Don't open it. What I wrote isn't for a stranger's eyes -- it's barely for mine.
basaltfang_shaman_warning_south.progress: Carry Cornel's sealed reading to the road-warden's post so it can travel south to the Ashvents. Leave it sealed.
basaltfang_shaman_warning_south.complete: You brought it, and you brought it sealed. Good -- there's them that read a thing like that and start keeping it, and I've lost apprentices to worse. It'll go south with the freight now, warden to warden, till it reaches folk who read vents that could drown my whole reading in honest heat. Maybe it warns them. Maybe it just tells the smoke it's being watched, and the smoke likes that. Either way I've done the old duty. Take the charm, and thank you for not being curious.

objectives.label: the road-warden's post
objectives.text: Carry Cornel's sealed reading to the warden's post
Tell Cornel the warning is away south

basaltfang_shaman_warning_south.note: Cornel's omen went south sealed, warden to warden, toward the Ashvents where the vents run hot enough to drown any reading in honest heat. It may warn them. It may only tell the smoke it is watched -- which the smoke, he fears, would like.

basaltfang_war_dead_shells.title: What Still Wears Three Rusts
basaltfang_war_dead_shells.summary: The high fang holds Great-War dead in its scree, and the digging for blackglass has woken them. They walk the ridge at night in three armies' rust, killing no one, keeping some old order. Zaharia wants them laid down before a knapper meets one alone.
basaltfang_war_dead_shells.offer: We dug too near the old dead cutting glass, and now they walk the east scree -- war-dead, the big fight, near fifty year gone. Strange thing: they don't hunt. They form up. Three different rusts on 'em, three armies that killed each other once, and now they stand shoulder to shoulder like the war ended in a treaty nobody told the living about. It's not right and it frightens the cutters. Lay them back down. Gently as steel gets.
basaltfang_war_dead_shells.progress: The war-dead form up on the east scree after dark, three armies' rust among them. Lay them back down.
basaltfang_war_dead_shells.complete: Down again, poor old soldiers. Did you mark it -- how they stood? Enemies, all three armies, shoulder to shoulder, facing the same way, dressed rank. Fifty years they hated each other and death sorted them into one tidy line pointed south, toward the forge-city, like they'd been called to a muster. The Gift's dead do that too, they say. Somebody's still keeping the roster. Take the blade. Don't dig near the old dead again.

objectives.text: Lay the war-dead back down on the east scree

basaltfang_war_dead_shells.note: The blackglass digging woke the Great-War dead -- three armies that killed each other, now standing dressed and ranked, facing south toward the forge, as if called to a muster. Zaharia laid them down. Someone underground is still keeping the roster.

basaltfang_murder_on_the_scree.title: The Cutter Who Read the Stone
basaltfang_murder_on_the_scree.summary: A knapper lies dead on the low scree with no wound and a charcoal rubbing sealed in his fist -- he copied a carving off a fresh-cut stone and did not survive the reading. Zaharia needs the scene walked, and the rubbing brought down without a soul looking at it.
basaltfang_murder_on_the_scree.offer: Found a cutter on the low scree this morning. No wound. No robbery -- his glass and coin still on him. Just dead, sitting up, with a rubbing balled in his fist -- charcoal on paper, angular marks he took off a stone that oughtn't have marks. I've buried enough this season to know the signs, and this is a new one and a worse one. Walk the place. Bring me the rubbing. Face down, in your pouch, and don't you dare unroll it. I'll not lose a second man to a piece of paper.
basaltfang_murder_on_the_scree.progress: The cutter lies on the low scree by the fresh-cut stone. Walk the scene and bring down the rubbing -- face down, unread.
basaltfang_murder_on_the_scree.complete: You kept it rolled. Good man. Clean fingers, no wound, and a copy of a carving in his fist -- he read the stone, that's what killed him, understanding it, same as they say killed the Courier north years back. I'll seal the rubbing in lead and bury it deeper than the man. Curiosity's the whole disease up here, and the cure is looking at your boots. Take the ward. Wear it past any stone you didn't watch get carved.

objectives.label: the murder-scene on the low scree
objectives.text: Walk the scene by the fresh-cut stone
Recover the sealed rubbing, unread

basaltfang_murder_on_the_scree.note: The cutter died of comprehension -- no wound, clean hands, a copied carving in his fist, the Courier's death worn a range and years apart. Zaharia sealed the rubbing in lead deeper than he buried the man. The disease up here is curiosity. The cure is your own boots.

basaltfang_standing_farmer.title: The Man Who Faced South
basaltfang_standing_farmer.summary: A drover from the low farms has stood on the mid-fang for two days, facing south toward the vents, not eating, not answering. Emilian ran messages past him twice and cannot stand to a third time. He wants the man moved -- or wants to know why he cannot be.
basaltfang_standing_farmer.offer: There's a man standing up on the mid-fang. Just standing -- drover from the low farms, I know his face. Two days now, turned south toward the vents, won't eat, won't answer, won't fall over the way a body ought after two days. I ran the message-path past him twice and both times my feet wanted to stop and stand beside him, and that's the part I can't sleep on. Go up. Try and turn him home. If he won't turn -- see which way he's really facing.
basaltfang_standing_farmer.progress: The drover stands on the mid-fang, turned south. Reach him, try to turn him home, and mark what he faces.
basaltfang_standing_farmer.complete: Wouldn't turn, would he. I felt it too, the pull to stand -- like the ground's warm just there and asking you to rest. He's not sick and he's not dead and he's not choosing it, any more than iron chooses the lodestone. He faces the vents, south, dead on the hot ones. They're letting the entranced walk into the vents down in the Ashvents, I hear, calling it mercy. Up here we've not decided what to call it. Take the charm and don't stand still on the fang.

objectives.label: the standing drover on the mid-fang
objectives.text: Reach the drover and try to turn him home
Tell Emilian which way the man truly faced

basaltfang_standing_farmer.note: The drover would not turn -- stood two days facing the hot vents south, drawn like iron to a lodestone, not sick, not dead, not choosing. The Ashvents let such folk walk into the heat and call it mercy. On the fang, they have not yet decided what to call it.

basaltfang_pack_runner_debt.title: A Runner's Kept Word
basaltfang_pack_runner_debt.summary: Emilian runs messages between the hunt-camp and the low post, and he made a promise to a dying old runner: keep the relay honest, never a word changed. The scree between is thick with packs now. He needs an escort so the word he carries stays the word he was given.
basaltfang_pack_runner_debt.offer: I run the word between Tudor's fire and the low post, and I keep it true -- every syllable, the way old Barbu taught me before the fang had him. 'A runner who edits is a runner who lies,' he'd say, and box my ear. The scree's gone thick with the glass-pack and I can't run it and fight it both. Walk the low relay-line with me. Keep the wolves off, so the word I carry down is the word I got up. It matters. It's the last honest thing on this range I'm sure of.
basaltfang_pack_runner_debt.progress: Run the low relay-line with Emilian, keeping the scree-packs off, so the word stays unchanged.
basaltfang_pack_runner_debt.complete: Made it, word intact, not a syllable slipped. Barbu'd have boxed my ear anyway, on principle. That's a good day up here -- a message that means at the bottom what it meant at the top. Rare, lately. Even the pack-relay's started handing names back wrong, and if the wolves can't keep a word straight, what hope've I got. ...Ah, listen to me gloom. Take a coin and my thanks. You kept my word honest, and that's worth more than the coin.

objectives.label: the low relay-post
objectives.text: Run the relay-line to the low post
Deliver the unchanged word to Emilian

basaltfang_pack_runner_debt.note: Emilian kept old Barbu's rule -- the word meant the same at the bottom of the run as the top, a rare thing now. Even the pack-relay has begun handing names back wrong. If the wolves cannot keep a word straight, he wonders what hope a man has.

basaltfang_cairn_wardens.title: The Cairns That Were Counted
basaltfang_cairn_wardens.summary: Three cairns stand on the mid-fang for the wardens the range took before Zaharia's watch. He keeps them as human graves are kept -- named, missed, tended. Something has been unstacking them stone by stone in the night. He needs the mid-fang cleared so he can rebuild them right.
basaltfang_cairn_wardens.offer: Three cairns up the mid-fang, for wardens the range had before me. I keep 'em the old way -- a name to each, and a thing they did, not praise, just the true small thing. And somebody's been taking them apart in the dark, stone by stone, tidy, no violence in it, like a man reading a wall down to its footing. The packs won't touch a cairn. So it's not the packs. Clear the ridge so I can restack them, and put a name back on each. The dead here get missed. That's the whole difference between us and the forge-city.
basaltfang_cairn_wardens.progress: Clear the mid-fang of what walks it, so Zaharia can restack the three unmade cairns.
basaltfang_cairn_wardens.complete: Restacked, all three, names recut. Thank you. Down in the Gift they bury in counted rows -- twelve to a line, a ledger in the dirt. Up here we bury one man at a time and miss him by name. That's the last wealth we've got. Somebody's unstacking our graves to make them count instead of matter, and I'll rebuild them every night they do it. Take the plate. A warden ought to have good steel between him and the range.

objectives.text: Clear the mid-fang around the unmade cairns

objectives.label: the three cairns on the mid-fang
objectives.text: Return to the cairns so Zaharia can rebuild

basaltfang_cairn_wardens.note: Something unstacks the fang's cairns in the night, stone by patient stone, to make counted graves of missed ones. Zaharia recut each name and swore to rebuild them nightly. To miss a man by name is the last wealth the range has. The Gift buries in rows of twelve.

basaltfang_hunt_camp_meat.title: Meat for the High Fire
basaltfang_hunt_camp_meat.summary: The hunt-camp fire feeds every pack-runner and stranded convoy hand who climbs the fang, and Tudor will not let a body go up the range hungry. The low scree runs thick with game gone strange. He needs a stack of clean meat, whatever the range has made of it.
basaltfang_hunt_camp_meat.offer: My fire feeds whoever climbs to it -- pack-runner, lost drover, forge-city hand who took a wrong pass. Nobody goes up the fang from my hearth with an empty belly; that's the one law I keep and the packs respect it. But the game's gone odd, like everything, and I need a stack of clean meat off the scree. Bring me good haunch and I'll fill you a bowl so hot it forgets it's the South. Warmth's a thing you have to make up here. So we make it.
basaltfang_hunt_camp_meat.progress: Take clean haunch off the low scree for the hunt-camp fire. A good stack fills the pot.
basaltfang_hunt_camp_meat.complete: Now that's a stack. The pot'll be full and the fire loud tonight, and for a few hours nobody up here listens for anything on the wind -- they just eat, and grumble, and are warm. That's the whole trick of a hearth in a bad country: you make one small warm hour and you don't ask it to last. Here -- bowl's yours, and a coin, and a seat by the fire as long as you want it. Everything good is temporary. That's what makes it good.

objectives.text: Bring clean haunch to the hunt-camp fire

basaltfang_hunt_camp_meat.note: Tudor's fire fed the fang for a night -- pack-runners and lost drovers grumbling, warm, not listening to the wind. A hearth in a bad country makes one small warm hour and does not ask it to last. Everything good up here is temporary, and that, he says, is what makes it good.

basaltfang_prospector_hut.title: The Prospector's Empty Hut
basaltfang_prospector_hut.summary: A blackglass prospector paid Vintila a season's factor-fee in advance, then stopped sending ore. His hut stands open and unlooted on the high fang -- which, Vintila notes, is worse than robbed. He wants the place walked and the ledger of what the man was really cutting recovered.
basaltfang_prospector_hut.offer: A prospector took my factoring, paid a season up front -- generous, which should've warned me -- and then the ore stopped and the letters stopped. His hut's still up the high fang, door open, glass on the shelves, coin in the box. Open and unlooted. In this country that's worse than burned; a burned house had a reason. Walk it. Bring me his cutting-ledger. I want to know what a man cuts that pays a season ahead and then walks off leaving the money.
basaltfang_prospector_hut.progress: The prospector's hut stands open on the high fang. Walk it, and recover his cutting-ledger.
basaltfang_prospector_hut.complete: His ledger, good. Let me -- huh. Ordinary cuts for weeks, honest tallies, and then one entry: 'found a stone already carved. copied the marks to keep. they're very fine.' And nothing after. He didn't run and he wasn't robbed. He copied a carving to keep, and then he had no more use for coin or glass or doors. Same sickness as the scree-cutter. I'll burn the ledger's last page and factor no more prospectors this season. Take your fee off his advance. He's not coming for it.

objectives.label: the prospector's open hut
objectives.text: Walk the open hut on the high fang
Recover the prospector's cutting-ledger

basaltfang_prospector_hut.note: The prospector's hut stood open and unlooted -- worse than robbed. His ledger ended on one line: he copied a carving to keep, and after that had no use for coin, glass, or doors. The same sickness as the scree. Vintila burned the last page and will factor no more prospectors this season.

basaltfang_dolmen_watch.title: The Watch at the Leaning Dolmen
basaltfang_dolmen_watch.summary: The old dolmen on the far fang has kept its silence since before the packs had names. Cornel needs a still watch kept over it through the dark hours -- not to fight, only to witness whether the vent-smoke reaches it, and what the stone does when it comes.
basaltfang_dolmen_watch.offer: There's a dolmen on the far fang, three uprights and a capstone, standing since before anything up here had a name to howl. It's never done a thing. But the vent-smoke's begun to drift toward it of a night, against the wind, and I'll not believe that till a pair of eyes I trust has stood the watch. Go out at dark. Don't fight, don't speak, don't touch the stone. Just stand the watch and see if the smoke comes -- and if the dolmen answers it. Then come tell me it didn't.
basaltfang_dolmen_watch.progress: Keep the dark watch at the far dolmen. Do not touch the stone. See whether the smoke comes to it.
basaltfang_dolmen_watch.complete: The smoke came to it, then. Against the wind, and the capstone -- warmed, you say, on a night that froze the scree. It's not doing anything yet. That's the worst part; the stones that end up loud all start by warming quiet, in that order Marta's granddaughter's granddaughter recites -- ground warms, wells copper, yeast dies, dust lines up, folk listen. We're on the first rung out here. I'll watch the rest with you if you'll have the company. Take the still. Some watches are better kept two.

objectives.label: the leaning dolmen on the far fang
objectives.text: Keep the dark watch at the dolmen
Tell Cornel what the dolmen did

basaltfang_dolmen_watch.note: The vent-smoke drifts against the wind to the far dolmen now, and the capstone warms on nights that freeze the scree. The first rung of the old ladder -- ground warms, wells copper, yeast dies, dust lines up, folk listen. The dolmen is only warm yet. Cornel will keep the rest of the watch.

basaltfang_quench_oil_run.title: Oil for the Old Way
basaltfang_quench_oil_run.summary: Ursu quenches his blades in vent-oil drawn from the low fissures, the old southern way -- and the good oil sits where the packs den. A standing errand: keep his quench-trough filled so the fang's edges stay honest.
basaltfang_quench_oil_run.offer: Steel's no good to anybody till it's quenched, and I quench in vent-oil the way my master did -- drawn low, off the honest fissures, not the hot ones. Trouble is the good oil pools where the wolves like to lie. It's a daily bother and I'm too old to fight for my own trough. Fetch me a run of clean oil when you're passing. Keep my quench full and the fang keeps its edges. That's a fair trade in a country that mostly isn't.
basaltfang_quench_oil_run.progress: Draw clean vent-oil from the low fissures and fill Ursu's quench-trough.
basaltfang_quench_oil_run.complete: That'll quench a good few blades. Hear that hiss? That's the range being useful for once, which it hates. Same time tomorrow, if the packs allow and you're inclined. An honest trough and an honest edge -- small things, but they're mine, and up here you keep what small things you can. Take your coin. And mind the fissures at dusk; the oil's clean but the company isn't.

objectives.text: Draw clean vent-oil for the quench-trough

basaltfang_quench_oil_run.note: Ursu's quench-trough is full again, the fang's edges honest for another day. Small useful things, kept against a range that hates being useful. He means to fill it tomorrow, if the packs allow.

basaltfang_relay_sweep.title: The Relay-Line Sweep
basaltfang_relay_sweep.summary: The road-warden's relay must stay walkable or the fang goes deaf between camp and post. A standing duty: sweep the scree-packs off the relay-line each day so the runners can carry their words unchanged.
basaltfang_relay_sweep.offer: A range this size, the only thing holding it together is the relay -- runners carrying word camp to post, post to convoy, day on day. Let the scree-packs thicken and the fang goes deaf, and a deaf range is a dead one, in order, quiet. So I've a standing ask: sweep the line clear when you walk it. Thin the wolves off the relay so the words keep moving. It's not glory. It's just the thing that has to be done again tomorrow, like most things worth doing.
basaltfang_relay_sweep.progress: Sweep the scree-packs off the road-warden's relay-line to keep the runners moving.
basaltfang_relay_sweep.complete: Line's walkable again, and the runners are moving. It'll thicken back by morning -- it always does -- and I'll ask you again, and it'll matter again. That's the shape of it up here: no ending, just a thing kept up or let fall. I'd rather keep it up. Take your pay. A range stays alive one swept line at a time, and today you swept it.

objectives.text: Sweep the packs off the relay-line

basaltfang_relay_sweep.note: The relay-line runs walkable for another day, the fang's words still moving. It thickens back by morning; the duty has no ending, only a keeping-up. A range stays alive one swept line at a time.

basaltfang_the_kept_roster.title: The Roster Nobody Signed
basaltfang_the_kept_roster.summary: The war-dead muster nightly now, dressed and ranked, and a slab of black basalt among them carries a list of names cut fresh -- the fang's dead and the fang's living, mixed, some marked. Zaharia needs it broken before the range finishes reading it aloud.
basaltfang_the_kept_roster.offer: The war-dead won't stay down, and now there's a stone stood up among them -- flat basalt, cut with names, fresh. I know some of those names. My cairn-wardens are on it. So's the scree-cutter that died reading. So's a runner still drawing breath at the low post. It's a roster, and it's being kept by no hand I can find, and the ones marked 'kept' are the dead and the ones marked nothing are the living -- yet. Break the muster. Throw the slab down the deep fissure. I'll not have the range calling a roll of my people.
basaltfang_the_kept_roster.progress: The war-dead muster on the east scree around the name-cut slab. Break the muster and cast the roster into the deep fissure.
basaltfang_the_kept_roster.complete: Down the fissure with it, and the muster scattered. But I read it first -- couldn't not, and that's the trap, isn't it. My name was on it. Marked nothing. Not 'kept.' Not yet. Like a debt with the date left open, waiting. The forge-city nails a ledger at its Pit and marks the dead 'collected' a thousand years early. Now the fang's grown its own. I'll keep rebuilding cairns and breaking rosters till one of them wins. Take the ward. Both of us are on a list now.

objectives.text: Break the nightly muster on the east scree

objectives.label: the deep fissure past the muster-ground
objectives.text: Cast the name-slab into the deep fissure

basaltfang_the_kept_roster.note: The fang has grown its own roster -- the dead marked 'kept,' the living marked nothing, yet -- a debt with the date left open. Zaharia cast it down the fissure and read his own name first, unmarked, waiting. He will break rosters and rebuild cairns till one of them wins.

basaltfang_obsidian_heart_hunt.title: The Wolf That Ate the Light
basaltfang_obsidian_heart_hunt.summary: Danciu swears there is a wolf on the far fang whose pelt does not shine like the others' -- it drinks the vent-light, black as the heart-core Ursu cut. He wants it taken and its hide brought down, so he can learn whether the range is breeding its glass into the living now.
basaltfang_obsidian_heart_hunt.offer: One more, and this one's true even for me. There's a runner in the glass-pack that doesn't catch the light -- eats it, black as a hole, black as that heart-stone Ursu keeps hid on his bench. The others shine. This one drinks. I want its hide, and I want you to be the one takes it, because you believe me now and nobody else up here will. If the range is putting the deep-glass into the living wolves... I don't know what that means. But somebody honest ought to hold the proof.
basaltfang_obsidian_heart_hunt.progress: The light-drinking runner denns with the glass-pack on the far fang. Take it, and bring Danciu its hide.
basaltfang_obsidian_heart_hunt.complete: There it is -- feel the hide? Doesn't shine. Drinks. Same as the heart-core, cold and hungry for light. So the range is breeding its deep-glass up into things that run and hunt and den. That's new. That's the mountain reaching for legs. I'll hang this one plain, no story -- the truth's frightening enough without me improving it. Take the focus off it; Ursu'll socket it honest. And thank you for believing an old liar the once it counted.

objectives.text: Take the light-drinking runner on the far fang
Bring Danciu the black hide

basaltfang_obsidian_heart_hunt.note: The light-drinking wolf's hide swallowed the vent-glow like the heart-core itself -- the deep-glass bred up into the living now, the mountain reaching for legs that run and hunt. Danciu hung it plain, no story. The truth was frightening enough without improving. He was right, the once it counted.

basaltfang_farewell_to_the_fang.title: The Road Bends South
basaltfang_farewell_to_the_fang.summary: The season's freight is down and the fang empties toward Sangeroasa. Vintila settles his books, and asks one last errand: carry his tally and his private warning down toward the forge-city gate, and see for yourself the country the road has been feeding all along.
basaltfang_farewell_to_the_fang.offer: Freight's away, season's turning, and I'm settling books before the fang freezes shut. One last run, if you'll take it -- carry my season-tally down toward the forge-city gate, where the road drops into Sangeroasa proper. And carry this, folded inside it: a word of my own, for a factor down there I half-trust. Not sealed by the packs, not read by the wind -- read by you, if you must, though I'd take it kindly if you didn't. See the forge that all this steel's been climbing toward. Then you'll understand the fang.
basaltfang_farewell_to_the_fang.progress: Carry Vintila's tally and folded warning down toward the Sangeroasa gate, then look on the forge-city the road feeds.
basaltfang_farewell_to_the_fang.complete: Down and looked-on, then. So now you've seen it -- the forge-row and the Debt Pit and the ledger nailed at its rim, the dead marked 'collected' before they're cold. Everything that climbed my fang was feeding that. The steel, the war-dead standing to muster, the roster on the slab -- one hunger, one command-path, all pointing down that drop. The fang's just the last honest ridge before it. Take your pay, and a good map-border I marked myself. You'll want to know exactly where the honest country ends.

objectives.text: Take Vintila's tally and folded warning

objectives.label: the drop toward the Sangeroasa gate
objectives.text: See the forge-city the road feeds

basaltfang_farewell_to_the_fang.note: The fang is the last honest ridge before the forge that eats -- and everything it carried, steel and war-dead and the roster on the slab, was one hunger pointed down one drop into Sangeroasa. Vintila marked the map where the honest country ends. Below it, the road bends south, and does not bend back.

ashvents_warm_water.title: The Water That Runs Warm
ashvents_warm_water.summary: Otilia keeps the only inn where the vent-warm river is a comfort and not a curse. Fetch her clean draw-water from the high spring before the trade-carts come down from the Gift.
ashvents_warm_water.offer: Everybody down here bathes in warm water and calls it luxury, and I let them, bless them. But you can't brew tea in river that tastes of the deep. There's a spring up the vent-road that still runs cold and honest. Bring me two jars before the Gift carts arrive and I'll owe you the good chair by the fire.
ashvents_warm_water.progress: The cold spring sits up the vent-road, past where the steam thins. Two jars, and mind the loose basalt.
ashvents_warm_water.complete: Cold and sweet, and not a whiff of copper in it. You'd think that a small thing. Down here it's the whole difference between a home and a place people stop to die warm. Sit. The chair's yours tonight, and the tea's on the house.

objectives.label: the cold high spring
objectives.text: Draw water from the cold spring

ashvents_warm_water.note: Two jars of cold water, up where the steam thins. A small warm inn in a country that runs its rivers hot. Otilia knows exactly what she is keeping, and how long it keeps.

ashvents_forge_hunger.title: The Forge Is Hungry
ashvents_forge_hunger.summary: Zamfira's vent-forge never wants for heat, only for good iron. Cinder-hounds den in the old slag-drifts where the best scrap lies. Clear a few so her runners can dig.
ashvents_forge_hunger.offer: You want to know the joke of Ashvents? A smith with all the fire in the world and nothing worth burning. My iron's in the slag-drifts, good war-steel the Gift carts spill, and the cinder-hounds have made a kennel of it. Thin the pack so my girls can dig, and I'll hammer you something that remembers its edge.
ashvents_forge_hunger.progress: The cinder-hounds den in the slag-drifts below the vent-road. Break the pack — six should send the rest off to easier meat.
ashvents_forge_hunger.complete: Heard the digging start before you were back through the door. That's the sound of a season's blades, that is. Here — took me the afternoon, but it'll hold a line and hold it warm. A smith with fire and no iron is just a woman staring into a pretty light. You gave me back the work.

objectives.text: Break the cinder-hound pack in the drifts

ashvents_forge_hunger.note: Six hounds down, and the slag-drifts open again. Zamfira has all the fire in the world. What she wanted was the iron, and the work, and a reason to swing the hammer that was not the Pit's.

ashvents_green_lie.title: The Green Lie
ashvents_green_lie.summary: Ioana keeps the vent-country's records, and its oldest problem: under alchemical sight, everything here reads thermal-green. The heat of the ground hides whatever else might be waking in it. Walk the vent-lines and read them for her.
ashvents_green_lie.offer: You've the look of someone who's carried a reading-still. Good. I need eyes I can trust, because mine have gone useless here. In every other country the alchemist's washes tell you plain — blue for the dead things, violet for the deep, orange for the worst. Here the ground runs green from the vents, and green swallows everything. If something were writing itself into this country, we could not see it under all this warmth. Walk the three vent-lines. Tell me what the still shows. Tell me if it is only green.
ashvents_green_lie.progress: Three vent-lines cross the field: the near seep, the middle boil, and the far crack under the black ridge. Read each with the still.
ashvents_green_lie.complete: Green, green, and green. Just as I feared and just as I hoped. You understand the trouble now — this whole country is a hand held over a candle so you can't see if the candle's lit. I've kept these records forty years and I have never once been able to swear this ground was clean. Only that I couldn't prove it wasn't. Take the still. You read truer than I do. And come back — there's a fourth line I've never dared read alone.

objectives.label: the near seep
objectives.text: Read the near seep with the still

objectives.label: the middle boil
objectives.text: Read the middle boil

objectives.label: the far crack under the ridge
objectives.text: Read the far crack under the ridge

ashvents_green_lie.note: Three vent-lines, and every one read green. The warmth of the ground drowns every other color a still can show. Ioana has spent forty years unable to prove her country clean. That is the vent-country's whole curse: not that a stone might be waking here, but that here of all places you would never know.

ashvents_fourth_line.title: The Fourth Line
ashvents_fourth_line.summary: There is a fourth vent-line Ioana has never dared read: the deep crack under the black ridge, where the ember-sworn keep a fire lit day and night. Clear the fire-keepers, then read what the still shows where the heat is thickest.
ashvents_fourth_line.offer: The fourth line runs under the black ridge, and it is the hottest ground in the country. The Ember-Sworn keep a fire on it, night and day, and they will tell you they keep it against the cold. There is no cold here. They keep it so no one can ever bring a still close enough to read the crack beneath. If you want the truth of this ground, you'll have to put out their fire first. I'm too old and too fond of breathing. You are neither. Go and read the hottest place in Ashvents, and pray it reads green like the rest.
ashvents_fourth_line.progress: The Ember-Sworn tend their fire on the fourth line, under the black ridge. Break the fire-keepers, then read the crack.
ashvents_fourth_line.complete: You're pale. I've been pale for forty years. Say it. ...Not green. For one breath, when their fire guttered, the crack read shifting orange — the color of a thing writing itself into the ground. Then the vents flared and buried it under warmth again. That is why they keep the fire. Not to hide the crack from us. To keep the crack warm enough that even the crack forgets what it is. Say nothing to the smith. Say nothing to the guard. Take this. And know that you are now one of the very few who has looked at the worst thing in the south and lived to keep the record.

objectives.text: Break the fire-keepers of the fourth line

objectives.label: the crack under the black ridge
objectives.text: Read the crack when the fire gutters

ashvents_fourth_line.note: For one guttering breath the fourth line read shifting orange, and then the warmth took it back. The Ember-Sworn do not keep their fire against the cold. They keep the ground warm so it cannot cool enough to know what it has become. Ioana filed the reading under no name at all. Some records are safer nameless.

ashvents_dead_shift.title: The Shift That Doesn't End
ashvents_dead_shift.summary: The vent-works were worked to death and closed a generation ago, but the ash-wraiths of the dead crews still walk the lines they were spent on, still carrying, still tallying. Draga wants them put to rest before a new crew is sent down.
ashvents_dead_shift.offer: They're sending a fresh crew to reopen the north works next week. Living men, with names. Trouble is the old crew never left. You'll see them on the vent-lines at dusk — ash-wraiths, the smiths call them, but they're not wraiths so much as men who died mid-shift and were never told to stop. They carry ore that isn't there to a forge that's cold. I can't send the living down to work beside that. Put the old crew down. Let them off the shift at last.
ashvents_dead_shift.progress: The old crew walks the north works at dusk, ash-wraiths still carrying their loads. Lay eight of them to rest before the new crew comes.
ashvents_dead_shift.complete: Eight down, and the lines are quiet for the first time in my service. You want to know the thing I can't shake? They didn't fight you. They just stopped, and looked at their empty hands like they'd been waiting a long while for someone to notice the ore was gone. Being spent for the forge was the only ending most of them were promised. You gave them a different one. Take this — I've no better way to thank a person for finishing what a whole country started and left half-done.

objectives.text: Lay the old crew to rest on the north lines

ashvents_dead_shift.note: Eight ash-wraiths laid down, and not one of them fought. They died mid-shift and were never told to stop; they carried ore that was not there to a forge gone cold. Draga freed them the only way the vent-country frees anyone. The new crew starts Monday. They have names. For now.

ashvents_ledger_marked.title: A Name Freshly Marked
ashvents_ledger_marked.summary: A debt-ledger from the Pit was nailed at the vent-road crossing, its newest line marked 'collected.' Anca recognizes the name: a carter she sold blackglass to yesterday, alive and whole. Carry the ledger-leaf back and let her show you what the mark means here.
ashvents_ledger_marked.offer: Someone nailed a leaf off the Debt Pit's ledger to the crossing-post last night. That's not unusual — they do it to remind us who's behind. What's unusual is the newest line. It's marked collected, in fresh ink, and the name is Emilian the carter. I sold him a case of blackglass yesterday. Shook his hand. He owed the Pit, everyone owes the Pit, but he was alive, do you understand me? Bring me the leaf before someone reads it who shouldn't. I need to know if the ink is telling me a thing that's happened or a thing that's been decided.
ashvents_ledger_marked.progress: The ledger-leaf is nailed to the crossing-post at the vent-road junction. Take it down and bring it back to Anca whole — do not read further than you must.
ashvents_ledger_marked.complete: ...It's his hand on the receipt and his name on the ledger, and one of them is right. I sent a girl to his house an hour ago. He's fine. Eating supper. Complaining about his knee. And the Pit has him marked collected in ink that was wet this morning. The ledger isn't recording that he died. It's scheduling it. Someone up the road decided, and the ink dried before the man did. Keep the leaf. Keep it face-down. And if you ever hear that Emilian the carter passed peaceful in his sleep — you remember it was written first.

objectives.label: the crossing-post
objectives.text: Take down the nailed ledger-leaf
Bring the leaf back to Anca

ashvents_ledger_marked.note: The carter is alive, eating supper, complaining of his knee. The Pit has him marked collected in this morning's ink. The ledger did not record a death. It scheduled one, and let the man keep breathing until the entry came due. Anca kept the leaf face-down. Some debts are paid before you know you owe them.

ashvents_ember_sworn.title: What the Fire Is For
ashvents_ember_sworn.summary: The Ember-Sworn have taken to tending vent-fires all along the west field, and the newest of them was Floarea's own cousin a month ago. She wants to know what they're feeding, and whether her cousin can still be talked to. Break their fires and speak with one before he burns.
ashvents_ember_sworn.offer: My cousin Petru joined the Ember-Sworn at the last dark. Sweet boy, useless with a spade, and now he stands a vent-fire on the west field and won't come home. They say they keep the fires to honor the warm ground that feeds us. But I've watched them, and they don't feed the fires so much as keep them from going out — like a fire going out here would let something get cold that mustn't. Scatter the ones on the west field. And if Petru's still Petru under the ash, get one word of sense out of him for me.
ashvents_ember_sworn.progress: The Ember-Sworn tend fires across the west field. Scatter them, and speak with one who still has words left.
ashvents_ember_sworn.complete: You spoke to one. And he said — 'we don't keep it warm for us.' That's all. That's all any of them will say. Not warm for us. Then who, Petru? Who mustn't get cold? He looked at the ground when he said it, not at the fire. Like the thing that mustn't cool is under our feet and always was. I'm not asking you back to the west field. I'll grieve him the honest way, the way you grieve someone still breathing. Take the ward. I don't want it anymore. I've decided I'd rather not know when something's near.

objectives.text: Scatter the Ember-Sworn on the west field
Get one word of sense from a fire-keeper

ashvents_ember_sworn.note: We don't keep it warm for us. The Ember-Sworn tend their fires with their eyes on the ground, not the flame. Floarea's cousin is under the ash now, and she has decided to grieve him as the living are grieved. She gave away her ward. She would rather not know when the cold thing comes near.

ashvents_slag_scrap.title: Good Steel Gone Begging
ashvents_slag_scrap.summary: The Gift carts spill blackglass and war-steel scrap along the vent-road, and it's honest salvage if you get it before the ash-wraiths drag it back down the works. Gather Zamfira a load.
ashvents_slag_scrap.offer: Here's a thing they won't teach you up in the clean kingdoms: half the finest steel on the continent ends up as spillage on the Ashvents road, dropped off a Gift cart, kicked into the ditch, forgotten. Free iron for a woman with the nerve to bend down for it. Only trouble is the old crew — the ash-wraiths — they gather it up nights and carry it back down the dead works, tidy as you please. Beat them to a good load and it's yours to sell me.
ashvents_slag_scrap.progress: Blackglass and war-steel scrap lie along the vent-road ditches. Gather ten pieces before the old crew tidies them away.
ashvents_slag_scrap.complete: Now that is a load. Blackglass enough to edge a dozen skinners and war-steel that took a real forge to make. Funny old country, isn't it — richest scrap-ground on the continent, and the only ones who ever collect it proper are the dead. You beat them to it. Here's your cut, and a striker I made off the last good billet. Strike true; it'll light a fire anywhere. Anywhere but here, where the fires light themselves.

objectives.text: Gather spilled blackglass and steel scrap

ashvents_slag_scrap.note: The richest scrap-ground on the continent, and the only ones who collect it properly are the dead. Zamfira got her load and made her joke. The joke, like most jokes in the south, is exactly true and no one laughs long.

ashvents_carter_supper.title: The Carter Eats His Supper
ashvents_carter_supper.summary: Anca cannot un-know what she read. She wants to warn Emilian the carter without telling him the terrible how of it. Carry a plain, kind message up to his house on the ridge road, and see for yourself whether the ledger has come due.
ashvents_carter_supper.offer: I can't file a warning against a ledger. There's no office for it, no clerk who'd take it. But I can do the small human thing: send word to Emilian to stay home a while, off the roads, near his own fire. Not because I can save him — I don't think anyone can un-write that ink — but because a man ought to get to eat a few more suppers in his own chair without knowing he's an entry someone's already closed. Carry it to his house on the ridge road. Say it's about the blackglass account. Let him believe it's about the blackglass account.
ashvents_carter_supper.progress: Emilian's house sits on the ridge road, above the vent-field. Carry Anca's message and see how the carter fares.
ashvents_carter_supper.complete: He's well, then. Good. Ate his supper, showed you the knee, sent his thanks about the account. Let him have that. Every supper he eats in that chair is a day the ledger's wrong, and I'll take the ledger being wrong for as long as the world will let me. You did a kind thing, carrying a lie that let a marked man rest easy. That's most of what kindness is, down here. Here — for the walk, and for the not-telling.

objectives.label: Emilian's house on the ridge road
objectives.text: Carry the message to the carter's house

ashvents_carter_supper.note: Emilian ate his supper and showed off his bad knee and thanked her for the note about the account. He does not know he is a closed entry. Anca will take the ledger being wrong for as long as the world allows. Kindness in the vent-country is mostly the careful not-telling.

ashvents_hound_bane.title: The Kennel in the Drifts
ashvents_hound_bane.summary: The cinder-hounds have grown bold enough to den near the vent-road itself, and they've dragged off a night-carter's mule and most of the night-carter. Draga wants the pack broken and the alpha's warm hide brought back as proof for the road-wardens.
ashvents_hound_bane.offer: Cinder-hounds took Ignat the night-carter on the low road two evenings back. Mule and man both, and the mule they left. That tells you the hounds down here have got a taste that isn't hunger — they picked the man over the meat. I want the pack broken and I want the big one's hide to nail up at the crossing, so the wardens upriver believe me when I say the low road's shut after dark. Hides here run warm long after the beast is cold. That's how they'll know it's true.
ashvents_hound_bane.progress: The cinder-hound pack dens near the low road. Break seven, and bring back the alpha's warm hide.
ashvents_hound_bane.complete: Warm still. Feel it — hours dead and the hide's like a loaf just out. That's the vent-country in a nutshell: even our monsters can't cool down. I'll nail it at the crossing and shut the low road proper. Ignat had a daughter over in the Gift. Somebody ought to ride and tell her, and it won't be pretty, but she'll want the mule at least. It came home. Take this for the trouble. You earned the warm work.

objectives.text: Break the pack near the low road
Take the alpha's warm hide

ashvents_hound_bane.note: The hounds picked the man over the mule — a taste that is not hunger. Draga nailed the warm hide at the crossing so the upriver wardens would believe the low road was shut. Even the country's monsters cannot cool down. Ignat's daughter got the mule back. It came home; he did not.

ashvents_ash_in_the_bread.title: Ash in the Bread
ashvents_ash_in_the_bread.summary: Otilia's starter has stopped rising, and it's not the heat — the heat never bothered it before. She's heard the same complaint from every hearth on the vent-road. Gather the failed starters and take them to the chronicler, who keeps a record of when yeast dies.
ashvents_ash_in_the_bread.offer: Twenty years my starter's risen in this warm kitchen and never complained. This week it lies flat as a coin and smells of nothing. And it's not just mine — every baking-woman on the road says the same, all at once, like the whole country's yeast agreed to quit together. I want you to gather the dead starters up and down the road and carry them to Ioana. She keeps a book of when the yeast dies. I didn't used to think that was a thing worth a book. I've changed my mind.
ashvents_ash_in_the_bread.progress: Failed starters sit in crocks at hearths along the vent-road. Gather them and carry them to Ioana the chronicler.
ashvents_ash_in_the_bread.complete: Ioana took one look and dated it in her book without a word — same page, she said, as the wells going copper and the dust lying straight. She's got a whole ladder of little wrongnesses and the yeast's just the next rung. I asked her the next rung after that and she wouldn't say. Bake with dead yeast and you get flat grey bread. We've all been eating it a week. Tastes like the border villages, folk say. I've never been north. Now I don't want to go.

objectives.text: Gather failed starters from the hearths
Carry the dead starters to Ioana

ashvents_ash_in_the_bread.note: Every starter on the road died in the same week, as if the yeast agreed to quit together. Ioana dated it on the same page as copper wells and straight-lying dust — the next rung on a ladder of small wrongnesses she keeps a book of. She would not say what rung comes after. The bread is flat and grey now, and tastes, they say, like the north.

ashvents_dust_lines.title: The Dust Lies Straight
ashvents_dust_lines.summary: The vent-ash has begun to settle in fine parallel lines across the dead works, all pointing the same way. Ioana wants the alignment read at three points to know what the lines point toward. It is not the wind.
ashvents_dust_lines.offer: There's a thing the ash does now that ash should not. It settles in lines — fine, parallel, straight as a ruled page — across the floor of the dead works. I told myself it was the vent-draft, and then I marked the draft, and the draft goes one way and the lines lie another. They all point the same direction, and I have not had the nerve to stand where they point. Read the alignment for me at the three tally-stones. Tell me the bearing. I'll do the arithmetic I'm afraid of once I have your numbers.
ashvents_dust_lines.progress: The ash lies in lines at the three tally-stones of the dead works. Read the alignment at each.
ashvents_dust_lines.complete: Three bearings, and they converge. Of course they do. Everything here converges, if you're honest enough to draw the line — and the point they meet is the fourth vent-line, under the black ridge, where the Ember-Sworn keep their fire. The dust is pointing at the thing the fire hides. Dust doesn't point. Dust settles. Unless the ground beneath it is arranging it, grain by grain, into an arrow it hopes no one's still curious enough to follow. I followed it. I wish I hadn't. Take the lens. See straighter than I can bear to.

objectives.label: the first tally-stone
objectives.text: Read the ash-lines at the first tally-stone

objectives.label: the second tally-stone
objectives.text: Read the ash-lines at the second tally-stone

objectives.label: the third tally-stone
objectives.text: Read the ash-lines at the third tally-stone

ashvents_dust_lines.note: Three bearings, and they converge on the fourth vent-line under the black ridge — the ground the Ember-Sworn keep warm. The dust does not point; dust settles. Unless something beneath is arranging it grain by grain into an arrow, in the patient hope that no one is still curious enough to walk where it aims. Ioana walked it, and wished she had not.

ashvents_pit_wages.title: Wages of the Pit
ashvents_pit_wages.summary: Maricica's man works the Debt Pit down the road, and his wages come as a tally against his debt, never as coin. She's owed a widow's portion for her dead father and wants it collected before the ledger 'matures' it away. Clear the ash-wraiths off the counting-shed so a living clerk can be reached.
ashvents_pit_wages.offer: You'll not have met a country where you can work forty years and end deeper in debt than you started, but here we are, and here it is. My man toils the Pit and his wages come as a mark scratched off what he owes, never a coin in the hand. Now they've a portion owed me for my dead father, and I need to reach a living clerk to claim it before the ledger 'matures' — before it decides the debt outlived the daughter. The counting-shed's overrun with the old crew. Clear them. Let me get to a man who still has a pulse to argue with.
ashvents_pit_wages.progress: Ash-wraiths crowd the Pit's counting-shed. Clear six so Maricica can reach a living clerk.
ashvents_pit_wages.complete: Got to the clerk. He was decent enough — for a man whose job is telling widows the arithmetic went against them. Says my father's portion 'transferred to the estate,' and the estate's in arrears, so it cancels out. Forty years of a life, cancelled out, tidy as a sum. But I argued it in person, to a face, and he wrote 'disputed' in the margin. That's a word the ledger has to keep. It's not much. Down here a word in the margin is a monument. Take this. You bought me the arguing, and the arguing's all we've got.

objectives.text: Clear the old crew from the counting-shed

ashvents_pit_wages.note: Forty years of a life, cancelled out tidy as a sum — the portion transferred to an estate in arrears. But Maricica argued it to a living face, and the clerk wrote 'disputed' in the margin, a word the ledger must keep. Down here a word in the margin is a monument. It is not much. It is what there is.

ashvents_last_smith.title: The Last Honest Billet
ashvents_last_smith.summary: Zamfira wants to forge one blade the old way — quenched in cold water, not the warm river, the way steel was hardened before the vents claimed everything. But the cold spring is guarded now by an ash-wraith foreman who won't let the water be taken. Win it back.
ashvents_last_smith.offer: I'll tell you what I miss. Cold-quenched steel. You drop a hot blade in cold water and it screams and sets hard and true, and there's nothing warm about the making of it — it's the one honest cold thing left in this country. The only cold water is the high spring, and the old works' foreman walks it now, an ash-wraith that won't let a living soul dip a bucket. Put him down. Let me quench one blade the honest way before I forget there was ever a way that wasn't warm.
ashvents_last_smith.progress: The ash-wraith foreman guards the cold spring above the works. Put him down so Zamfira can quench cold.
ashvents_last_smith.complete: Heard it scream in the cold water from all the way down here, and I nearly wept, and I'm not a weeping woman. One honest cold-set blade. It's yours — no, take it, I made it to be carried, not hung on my wall like a memory. The foreman's gone. He kept that spring cold-guarded forty years past his death, like keeping the living from cold water was the last order anyone ever gave him. Maybe it was. Maybe down here the last order you get is the one you can't stop obeying.

objectives.text: Put down the foreman at the cold spring

objectives.label: the cold spring
objectives.text: Return so Zamfira can quench cold

ashvents_last_smith.note: One blade, quenched cold and honest, screaming in the only cold water left in the country. The dead foreman had guarded the spring forty years past his own death, keeping the living from cold water as if it were the last order he was ever given. Perhaps it was. Down here the last order is the one you cannot stop obeying.

ashvents_night_reading.title: A Reading After Dark
ashvents_night_reading.summary: Ioana believes the vents cool a little in the deep of the night, just enough that a still might catch the true color of the ground before the day's heat returns. She wants a watch kept over the fourth line until the cold hour, and one honest reading taken. It is the most dangerous vigil in the south.
ashvents_night_reading.offer: There's an hour before dawn when the vents draw thin and the ground cools — not cold, but cooler, cooler than the fire the Ember-Sworn keep. In that hour, and only then, a still might read the fourth line true, before the day warms the lie back over it. I need someone to keep the watch until the cold hour and take the reading. I will not pretend it is safe. The ground does not want to be read, and the closer you stand to understanding it, the more it will offer you rest. Do not accept the rest. Take the reading, and walk away while you still want to.
ashvents_night_reading.progress: Hold the watch over the fourth line until the cold hour before dawn, then take the true reading.
ashvents_night_reading.complete: You held it. And you walked back, which is the part I feared. Tell me the color. ...Orange. Steady orange, for the whole cold hour, no guttering, no vent to hide it. The ground here is writing itself, and has been, and the heat was only ever the blanket over the cradle. I have the reading now, in my own book, dated and sworn. It changes nothing and it is everything — because now the Accord cannot say the south was never warned. You gave me the one thing this country has never had: proof. Rest, if you can. I know now you can, and won't. Good. Stay that way.

objectives.label: the fourth line at the cold hour
objectives.text: Hold the watch, then read the fourth line true

ashvents_night_reading.note: Steady orange, the whole cold hour, no vent left to hide it. The ground here is writing itself, and the heat was only ever the blanket over the cradle. Ioana has the reading now, dated and sworn, and the Accord can no longer claim the south went unwarned. It changes nothing. It is everything. The ground offered rest, all through the watch. Rest was declined.

ashvents_warm_dead.title: The Warm Dead
ashvents_warm_dead.summary: Bodies buried in the vent-ground don't cool, and lately they don't stay buried — they rise warm, still, and turned to face the black ridge, like the risen dead of the far north. Floarea wants the grave-plot put right and the graves that emptied recorded.
ashvents_warm_dead.offer: We bury warm here — the ground never lets a body cool, so the grave-diggers wear gloves, and nobody remarks on it. What we're remarking on now is the graves that don't stay shut. Three opened this week in the road-plot, and the ones that walked out came up warm and quiet and turned toward the black ridge, every one, like they were told which way to face. Put them back down. And count the empty graves for me — I want to know if it's spreading, and by how much a week.
ashvents_warm_dead.progress: The risen warm dead walk the road-plot, turned toward the ridge. Lay five to rest and count the emptied graves.
ashvents_warm_dead.complete: Five put down, and — how many empty? Seven. Seven graves open in a plot of forty. Last week it was three. It doubles, near enough, and I can do the arithmetic same as Ioana can, I just wish I couldn't. They all faced the ridge. The dead don't have opinions about direction. Something gave them one. I'll salt the plot and pray salt still means anything this far south. Take the pouch — there's salt in it, and a little of whatever hope goes into salt. You'll want both where you're headed.

objectives.text: Lay the risen warm dead to rest

objectives.label: the road-plot
objectives.text: Count the emptied graves

ashvents_warm_dead.note: Seven graves open in a plot of forty, and last week it was three; the number doubles, near enough. The warm dead rise turned toward the black ridge, every one, and the dead have no opinions about direction — something gave them one. Floarea salted the plot and prayed salt still meant something this far south. It is a long way south.

ashvents_incurious_man.title: The Incurious Man
ashvents_incurious_man.summary: Neaga's brother copied down an inscription from the fourth line 'to study it,' and now he sits and stares and won't stop reciting it under his breath. She's heard the only cure is to stop being curious. Bring her a charm the Ember-Sworn wear — the thing that lets them tend the fire and not be undone by it.
ashvents_incurious_man.offer: My brother's a clever man, and that's what's killing him. He copied a mark off the black ridge — the fourth line, where you're not to go — and said he'd puzzle it out over the winter. He's stopped eating. He recites it, quiet, over and over, and his eyes are going somewhere. Folk say the Ember-Sworn can stand near the worst of it because they carry a charm that makes them not want to understand. An incurious charm. Get me one. If I can make my brother stop wanting to know, maybe he stops knowing, and maybe he comes back.
ashvents_incurious_man.progress: The Ember-Sworn carry incurious charms. Take one from the fire-keepers and bring it to Neaga.
ashvents_incurious_man.complete: You got one. It's an ugly little thing, isn't it — dull, no shine, nothing to draw the eye. That's the point of it, I suppose. I hung it round his neck and he stopped reciting mid-word. Just stopped. Looked at me like he'd woken from a doze. He doesn't remember the mark. Doesn't remember copying it.
