I've got my brother back and I've got a brother who used to be clever and isn't, and I don't know which of us paid for that. Take his thanks anyway. He'd give it if he knew to.

objectives.text: Take an incurious charm from the fire-keepers
Bring the charm to Neaga

ashvents_incurious_man.note: The charm is dull and ugly, nothing to draw the eye — that is the whole point of it. Neaga hung it on her clever brother and he stopped reciting the mark mid-word, and forgot he had ever copied it. She has him back. She has a brother who used to be clever and is not. She does not know which of them paid for that.

ashvents_gift_border.title: Where the Red Soil Ends
ashvents_gift_border.summary: The Gift's red fertile bands are creeping down into the vent-country, and where they touch, the harvest comes up rich and the ash comes up wrong. Anca wants the new border marked and a sample of the encroaching soil, to know whether to buy the land cheap or flee it.
ashvents_gift_border.offer: Business question, and don't look at me like that — someone down here has to keep the coin moving or we all starve honest. The Gift's red soil is creeping south into the vent-fields. Where it reaches, the ground goes fertile as a curse, and every farmer within a mile wants to buy in before the price climbs. I want to know exactly where the red line is this season, and I want a jar of the soil off the edge of it. Then I'll know whether I'm buying good farmland or a graveyard that grows wheat.
ashvents_gift_border.progress: The Gift's red soil creeps south. Mark the new border at the field's edge and take a jar of the encroaching soil.
ashvents_gift_border.complete: There's the line, and there's the jar, and — look at it. Red as a wound and warm to the hand, and full of things that aren't stones. You know what the Gift is grown from, up in Sangeroasa. War-dead, plowed under, a whole generation for a good harvest. And it's coming here now, one field a year, growing itself south. I won't buy it. Not because it's haunted — everything's haunted — but because I can't set a price on it that I could sleep beside. Some ground you leave to the ones already in it. Take your cut. I'll trade in warm water and honest scrap and let the red soil go.

objectives.label: the edge of the red soil
objectives.text: Mark the new border of the Gift
Take a jar of the encroaching soil

ashvents_gift_border.note: Red as a wound and warm to the hand, full of things that are not stones. The Gift is grown from a generation of war-dead plowed under, and it is coming south one field a year, growing itself toward Ashvents. Anca could not set a price on it she could sleep beside. Some ground you leave to the ones already in it.

ashvents_wardens_watch.title: The Wardens' Long Watch
ashvents_wardens_watch.summary: The vent-road wardens keep a standing bounty on the old crews and the hounds, because the dead here never stop coming and the wardens are too few. Take a turn on the watch and thin whatever walks the lines.
ashvents_wardens_watch.offer: Standing offer, and it's always open, because the work's never done. The old crews rise, the hounds den, the fire-keepers spread, and there's four of us and forty miles of vent-road. Take a turn on the watch. Thin whatever's walking the lines tonight — I'm not fussy which, they're all the same problem wearing different ash. Come back and I'll pay you out of the road-fund like I pay the rest of the wardens. It's honest coin for endless work. That's the only kind of work we have.
ashvents_wardens_watch.progress: Whatever walks the vent-lines tonight — hounds, old crew, fire-keepers. Thin eight and report back for your pay.
ashvents_wardens_watch.complete: Eight, and the road's a shade quieter till dawn, which is all quiet ever is here — a shade, till dawn. Here's your cut from the fund. Same offer stands tomorrow, and the day after, because tomorrow it'll be eight more. That's not despair, mind. That's just the arithmetic of holding a road the ground keeps trying to take back. We hold it a day at a time. You held it today.

objectives.text: Thin whatever walks the vent-lines

ashvents_wardens_watch.note: Eight down, and the road a shade quieter till dawn — which is all quiet ever is here. The wardens hold forty miles of vent-road four hands strong, a day at a time, against a ground that rises again each night. It is not despair. It is arithmetic. Today the arithmetic held.

ashvents_stillfire_daily.title: Feeding the Cold Reading
ashvents_stillfire_daily.summary: Ioana runs her still on grave-lichen and blue reagent, and both burn fast in this heat. She keeps a standing request for fresh culture scraped from threaded ground, so the record of the vent-country's colors never goes dark. Bring her a day's supply.
ashvents_stillfire_daily.offer: A still is only as honest as its reagents, and mine cook off in this warmth faster than any other alchemist's on the continent. Blue base from grave-lichen, mostly, scraped where the ground's gone threaded — and there's no innocent place to scrape it from, I'll not pretend there is. But if the still goes dark, the record goes dark, and then no one's watching the colors at all. So it's a standing ask: bring me a day's culture, and the reading goes on. Small poison, kept up, against a larger one. That's most of my trade.
ashvents_stillfire_daily.progress: Grave-lichen culture from threaded ground keeps the still lit. Bring Ioana a day's supply.
ashvents_stillfire_daily.complete: That'll keep the still honest another day, and the record with it. You look at me sideways for scraping reagent off somebody's disaster — good, keep looking sideways, it's the right way to look at it. There's no clean supply and no clean reading and no clean anything down here. There's only whether you keep watching or let the colors go dark. I keep watching. It costs. Here's your share of what watching pays, which isn't much, because watching never does.

objectives.text: Bring grave-lichen culture for the still

ashvents_stillfire_daily.note: The still cooks off its reagents faster in this heat than anywhere on the continent, and there is no innocent ground to scrape the culture from. Ioana keeps watching anyway — small poison, kept up, against a larger one. There is no clean supply and no clean reading. There is only whether you keep watching, or let the colors go dark.

ashvents_child_shoe.title: A Small Shoe in the Furrow
ashvents_child_shoe.summary: A child's shoe turned up in the red soil at the field's edge, where the Gift creeps into the vent-country, and no child in the village is missing a shoe. Maricica cannot let it lie. Recover it, and lay it where the lost are laid.
ashvents_child_shoe.offer: Ruja who works the red bands found a child's shoe in her furrow this morning, small, old, good leather. She asked every mother on the road. Not one is missing a shoe, nor a child. So it came up out of the Gift with the harvest, the way things do down here, and it belonged to someone a long way back and a long way under. I can't leave it in the dirt to be plowed again. Fetch it here. We'll lay it where we lay the lost, and say the words, even not knowing the name. Especially not knowing the name.
ashvents_child_shoe.progress: The child's shoe lies in the red furrow at the field's edge. Recover it and bring it to Maricica.
ashvents_child_shoe.complete: Thank you. It's so small. We laid it in the lost-plot under a flat stone, and Ioana carved 'ONE OF THE GIFT, KEPT' on it, because that's all any of us can honestly swear — that whoever it was is kept now, and named by us if by no one else. The Gift feeds the whole south on ground like this. We eat the harvest and we don't ask. Today we asked, once, over one small shoe. It's not enough. It's what we had. Go on. And thank you for the carrying.

objectives.label: Ruja's red furrow
objectives.text: Recover the child's shoe
Bring the shoe to Maricica

ashvents_child_shoe.note: A child's shoe, small and old and good, come up out of the Gift with the harvest — and no living child missing one. They laid it under a flat stone carved ONE OF THE GIFT, KEPT, and said the words over a name no one knew. The south eats the harvest and does not ask. Today, once, over one small shoe, they asked.

ashvents_convoy_debt.title: The Convoy's Ledger
ashvents_convoy_debt.summary: A Bloodroad arsenal convoy stalled at the vent-crossing, its warden-escort dead of no wound, its cargo-ledger left open. Anca wants the ledger read and its route copied before the Pit sends collectors — leverage worth more than the blackglass it carries.
ashvents_convoy_debt.offer: A war-convoy's been sitting at the crossing three days, oxen dead in the traces, escort dead in their boots and not a mark on any of them — you know that kind of dead by now. The cargo's still sealed, but the ledger's lying open on the driver's seat, and it lists every toll-fort and every debt-mark from here to Sangeroasa. That ledger's worth more than the steel. Copy the route and the marks for me before the Pit sends collectors to close the convoy's account. I'll not touch the cargo. Only the paper. The paper's where the power is, down here.
ashvents_convoy_debt.progress: The stalled convoy sits at the vent-crossing. Reach it and copy the open ledger — clear whatever guards it.
ashvents_convoy_debt.complete: That's the route, and that's the marks, and — look here, at the bottom. The convoy's own account was marked collected the day it left the Bloodroad. Three days before the escort died. They were sent out already closed. Somebody up the ledger knew this convoy would never arrive, and dispatched it anyway, to carry steel to a war that's really a filing convention. I've the copy now. It's worth a fortune and it's worth nothing, because there's no one to sell a truth like that to who'd survive buying it. Take your cut. I'll keep the paper somewhere cold.

objectives.text: Clear the dead escort from the convoy

objectives.label: the stalled convoy
objectives.text: Copy the convoy's open ledger

ashvents_convoy_debt.note: The convoy's own account was marked collected the day it left the Bloodroad, three days before its escort died of no wound. It was dispatched already closed — sent to carry steel to a war that is really a filing convention. Anca has the copy, worth a fortune and worth nothing: no buyer of a truth like that survives the buying. She keeps the paper somewhere cold.

ashvents_smiths_apprentice.title: The Apprentice Who Wouldn't Sweat
ashvents_smiths_apprentice.summary: Zamfira's new apprentice works the vent-forge all day and never sweats, never tires, never complains — and never was hired. Zamfira half-remembers taking him on and can't place the day. Speak to the boy, and find out from the guard whether anyone remembers him arriving.
ashvents_smiths_apprentice.offer: I've an apprentice I can't account for. Good worker — too good. Stands the forge from dark to dark and doesn't sweat, in this heat, doesn't drink, doesn't grumble, and every smith's boy grumbles, it's half the trade. I keep meaning to ask when I hired him and the meaning slides off. Talk to him for me. See if he's a boy or a — see if he's a boy. And ask Draga at the gate whether the watch ever logged him coming into the country. Somebody arrives, somebody's logged. Unless they didn't arrive.
ashvents_smiths_apprentice.progress: Speak with the apprentice at the forge, then ask Draga whether the watch logged him entering the country.
ashvents_smiths_apprentice.complete: Draga's got no record of him. No one walked that boy through the gate. And when you spoke to him — he answered fine, polite even, but you couldn't hold his face in your mind after, could you. I can't either. I've decided to keep him on. There, I've said it. He does honest work and asks for nothing and I'm short-handed and old, and if the price of a good apprentice down here is not looking too hard at where he came from — well. That's the price of most things down here. Take this. And don't tell me what you think he is. I've decided not to know.

objectives.text: Speak with the apprentice at the forge
Ask Draga if the watch logged him

ashvents_smiths_apprentice.note: No one walked the boy through the gate; the watch has no record of him arriving. He works dark to dark, never sweats, and no one can hold his face in memory. Zamfira has decided to keep him on — short-handed, old, and unwilling to look too hard at where a good apprentice comes from. That is the price of most things this far south.

ashvents_last_letter.title: The Letter That Reads Itself
ashvents_last_letter.summary: A guest died in Otilia's inn of no wound, a sealed letter still in his coat — and the writing on the facing blank page is slowly filling in on its own. Otilia wants it carried to the chronicler and never, ever opened. Deliver it sealed.
ashvents_last_letter.offer: A traveler took my corner room three nights back and this morning he's dead in the bed, peaceful, no wound, hands folded, and I'll be plain — I've seen that death before, we all have now. In his coat's a letter, still sealed, addressed to no one. And here's the thing that's got me shaking: the blank page facing the writing is filling in. By itself. A line more each hour, in the same hand, though the man's cold and the wax unbroken. Carry it to Ioana sealed. Do not open it. Do not read the page it's writing. Promise me, and take it, and go quick before it finishes.
ashvents_last_letter.progress: Carry the sealed self-writing letter to Ioana the chronicler. Do not open it. Deliver it sealed.
ashvents_last_letter.complete: You kept it shut. Bless you, you kept it shut. Ioana took it with tongs, tongs, like it was hot, and it was — warm, she said, warmer than the room. She's put it in a lead box and sent word up the Accord line. She says the letter isn't a message the man was carrying. She says it's a message the man was for. The writing's the point; he was only the paper it needed to reach us on. And it's still filling in, in the box, in the dark, patient as anything. I've had that corner room shut. Some rooms you just shut. Take this, and my thanks, and don't lodge in corner rooms, wherever you wander.

objectives.label: the inn's corner room
objectives.text: Recover the sealed letter
Deliver the sealed letter to Ioana

ashvents_last_letter.note: The traveler died of no wound with a sealed letter in his coat, and the blank page facing the writing fills in by itself, a line an hour, in the same hand. Ioana says the letter was never a message the man carried; it was a message he was for — the writing is the point, the man only the paper it reached us on. It fills in still, in a lead box, in the dark, patient as anything.

ashvents_forge_rest.title: The Day the Vents Draw Breath
ashvents_forge_rest.summary: Once a year the deep vents fall quiet for a day and the whole vent-country holds a hearth-feast in the cool. Neaga is short on hands to gather feast-fuel and warm-country wine before the vents wake. Help her lay the tables.
ashvents_forge_rest.offer: One day a year the deep vents go still — nobody knows why, they just do, like the ground itself stops to catch its breath — and the whole road holds a feast in the blessed cool of it. It's the only cold day we get, and we don't waste it grieving. I need hands, though, and mine are two and the tables are many. Gather feast-fuel off the drifts and a few jars of the warm-country wine before the vents wake and the heat comes back. Come to the feast after. You've earned a cool evening if anyone has.
ashvents_forge_rest.progress: Gather warm-country wine before the vents wake. Then help Neaga lay the feast tables.
ashvents_forge_rest.complete: Look at it — every table full, every soul on the road in one place, and cool air, actual cool air, on our faces for one whole evening. The children have never felt cold and they're laughing at it like it's a marvel, which it is, down here. Tomorrow the vents wake and it's warm forever again and the ledger and the fires and all of it. But tonight we ate in the cool and nobody listened for anything. Here — a jar to keep. Everything good is temporary. That's what makes it good.

objectives.text: Gather warm-country wine for the feast
Help Neaga lay the feast tables

ashvents_forge_rest.note: One cold day a year, when the deep vents fall still, the whole road feasts in the blessed cool and does not waste it grieving. The children have never felt cold and laugh at it like a marvel. Tomorrow the vents wake and it is warm forever again. Tonight they ate in the cool and no one listened for anything. Everything good is temporary. That is what makes it good.

ashvents_wraith_captain.title: The Captain Still Counting
ashvents_wraith_captain.summary: The dead works had a shift-captain who tallied every crew in and out, and his ash-wraith still stands the tally-post, counting crews that no longer exist and refusing to reach zero. Floarea wants him laid down and his tally-slate brought back, so the true count of the dead can finally be recorded.
ashvents_wraith_captain.offer: There's a wraith at the old tally-post that troubles me more than all the rest. He was the shift-captain, and in life his whole trade was counting men in at dawn and out at dusk, and making the numbers match. He's still at it. Still tallying. But the crews are dust and the numbers never match anymore, and he won't stop, he can't stop, he'll stand there counting to a total that walked off the ledger a generation back. Put him down. Bring me his slate. Somebody ought to write the true number at last — how many the works ate — and let the count be finished for him.
ashvents_wraith_captain.progress: The shift-captain's wraith stands the old tally-post, counting crews that are dust. Lay him down and take his slate.
ashvents_wraith_captain.complete: There's the slate, and there — you see it — the last living tally, the day the works closed, and then column after column he's scratched since, in the dark, trying to make a dead crew add up. It never will. That was the horror of his afterlife, that the sum wouldn't close, forever. I've written the true number under it now: how many the works spent, all told. Four hundred and eleven. The count is finished. He can stop. I hope stopping is a mercy where he's gone, and not just a different kind of dark. Take this. You closed a sum that had no right to stay open.

objectives.text: Lay the shift-captain's wraith down
Take the captain's tally-slate

ashvents_wraith_captain.note: The shift-captain's wraith stood the tally-post a generation, scratching column after column in the dark, trying to make a dead crew add up. The sum would never close; that was his whole afterlife. Floarea wrote the true number beneath it — four hundred and eleven, all the works ever spent — and closed the count for him. She hopes stopping is a mercy where he has gone, and not just a different dark.

lichenreach_the_wall_that_answered.title: The Wall That Answered
lichenreach_the_wall_that_answered.summary: Lichenreach was cut into the maze-city's oldest wall, where the strigoi once entombed those who wanted too badly to know. Petru has recorded that a walled-up alcove has begun to answer questions. He would like it to stop.
lichenreach_the_wall_that_answered.offer: I keep the records here, and the records keep me honest, so hear me out with a clerk's patience. The old punishment-wall at the deep bed has an alcove bricked shut before my grandfather's grandfather sat where I sit. Three nights running, a woman drawing water put a question to it, idle, the way you'd talk to a cat. Three nights running it answered her. I do not want to know how. I want you to go down, and stand where she stood, and tell me what you hear -- and then not repeat it.
lichenreach_the_wall_that_answered.progress: The bricked alcove is set into the listening-wall at the deep bed, low in the lichen-galleries.
lichenreach_the_wall_that_answered.complete: You look like a man who kept his mouth shut. Good. She has not come to draw water since, and the alcove is quiet again -- for the plain reason that it got the answer it was waiting for. Whatever she asked, it has now been said aloud in the world. That is how they wake. Not with a key. With a curiosity.

objectives.label: the walled alcove
objectives.text: Stand where the woman stood
Carry what you heard back to Petru

lichenreach_the_wall_that_answered.note: The alcove is quiet now, having gotten what it waited three nights to hear. A question, once answered aloud, cannot be un-answered. Petru filed the record and then, for the first time in his keeping, filed it wrong on purpose.

lichenreach_the_sealed_reading.title: The Sealed Reading
lichenreach_the_sealed_reading.summary: A courier out of Blestem died on the deep-bed path with no wound on him, a sealed reading-still still cradled in his arms. Lupu wants it carried to the merchant and logged before anyone thinks to open it -- least of all its carrier.
lichenreach_the_sealed_reading.offer: We found him sat against the wall like a man resting, hands folded round a sealed still, and not a mark on him. Clean nails. The seal not broken. I have seen enough of these to know the carrying is what kills, not the road. Take it up to Grigore, get it logged and locked, and do not -- I will say it once and plainly -- do not read what it distils. The last man who carried it was strong as you and he is sitting against a wall.
lichenreach_the_sealed_reading.progress: Grigore keeps the merchant's ledger in the upper market. Deliver the sealed still there, unopened.
lichenreach_the_sealed_reading.complete: Logged, locked, and the seal never yours to break. You feel that? Lighter. That is the weight of a thing you chose not to know. The dead courier carried it because someone told him it was only glass and orange light. It was that. It was also the reason he is dead. You gave it back to the ledger instead of to your own eye. Few do.

objectives.label: the courier's resting-place
objectives.text: Recover the sealed reading-still
Deliver the still to Grigore, unopened

lichenreach_the_sealed_reading.note: The still is logged and locked and the seal is whole. Grigore did not thank you; in Blestem, a thing not opened is a debt you chose not to take on. The courier is buried facing the wall, which is how they bury the ones who carried too well.

lichenreach_a_dossier_of_habits.title: A Dossier of Habits
lichenreach_a_dossier_of_habits.summary: The merchant's most saleable asset is other people's routines, filed and priced. He wants a fresh dossier compiled on the man drawing lichen at the deep bed -- and cannot say why the file's first page is already written in his own hand.
lichenreach_a_dossier_of_habits.offer: Coin is the soft currency here, friend; the hard one is habit. Where a man goes, when, and what he cannot help doing -- that sells. There is a scraper working the deep-bed lichen who never once looks up from the stone. I want him filed. Watch him, learn his pattern, and gather what he leaves. Odd thing -- the file already has a first page. My hand. My words. I do not remember setting them down. Finish it anyway. A dossier half-written is worse than none.
lichenreach_a_dossier_of_habits.progress: The lichen-scraper works the deep bed below the upper market. Learn his pattern; gather what he sheds.
lichenreach_a_dossier_of_habits.complete: Read it back to me. ...The pattern is a route, and the route is the shape of the deep bed exactly, wall for wall, as if he were copying it by walking it. And the first page -- my page -- describes the last page before either of us had watched him. I wrote the ending first and did not know. Take your pay. I am going to burn this file and I am going to remember that I burned it, because the alternative is that someone wanted it kept.

objectives.text: Clear the lurkers shadowing the scraper
Gather what the scraper leaves behind
Read the finished dossier back to Grigore

lichenreach_a_dossier_of_habits.note: The file ended exactly as its first page foretold, in Grigore's own unremembered hand. He burned it and made a point of remembering the burning. Somewhere a thing that files people had a page it wanted written, and found a merchant to hold the pen.

lichenreach_the_wet_stone_light.title: The Wet-Stone Light
lichenreach_the_wet_stone_light.summary: The blue lichen that lights Lichenreach without flame is failing along the deep galleries. Bogdan needs a living culture pulled from the last bright stones -- before the town goes dark and the dark goes quiet.
lichenreach_the_wet_stone_light.offer: We do not burn lamps down here, and we are proud of it. The wet-stone lichen gives its own cold light, blue as a drowned man's lip, and it has since before the wall had a name. Now it dies back gallery by gallery, and where it dies the crawlers thicken. Cut me a living culture off the last bright stones so I can seed the taproom afresh. Mind the crawlers. And mind this -- the lichen dies in the same order every time, north wall to south, neat as a man reading a page.
lichenreach_the_wet_stone_light.progress: The last bright lichen clings to the deep galleries. Pull a living culture; the cave-crawlers gather where it fails.
lichenreach_the_wet_stone_light.complete: A living culture, still glowing. Good lad. I will seed the taproom and we will have our cold light a while longer. ...Neat as a page, you said. North to south. I did not tell you that. You saw it too, then. The lichen is not dying, is what I am starting to think. It is being turned off. Here -- drink on the house, and do not think about who reads by which light.

objectives.text: Thin the crawlers in the dark galleries
Cut living cultures from the bright stones

lichenreach_the_wet_stone_light.note: The taproom glows blue again. The galleries do not. The lichen died north wall to south, in order, the way a hand moves down a page -- and Bogdan has taken to leaving one candle lit, for the first time in his family's long keeping of the light.

lichenreach_rows_of_twelve.title: Rows of Twelve
lichenreach_rows_of_twelve.summary: In a dead-end alley of the lower galleries, boot-prints have appeared in the lichen-dust: twelve of them, all facing the wall, none facing back out. Sorin wants a watcher to hold the alley mouth and count what walks in.
lichenreach_rows_of_twelve.offer: I have walked the galleries thirty years and I have never once been afraid of a dead end. There is one now I will not go down alone. Boot-prints in the dust -- twelve, counted twelve, all of them toed to the wall and not one turned back toward the mouth. Nobody comes out of that alley because nobody in it wants to. Stand at the mouth for me. Count what goes in. Do not go in with it.
lichenreach_rows_of_twelve.progress: The blind alley opens off the lower galleries. Hold the mouth; count what walks the rows.
lichenreach_rows_of_twelve.complete: Thirteen now, you say. It was twelve. ...I know the woman by the print -- narrow heel, mended sole. She drew water at the deep bed and asked the wall a question. So that is where they go, the ones who learn too well. Not taken. They walk in. And they face the wall because the wall is the only thing left that will still talk to them. Keep the ward. I am bricking that alley Tuesday.

objectives.label: the blind alley mouth
objectives.text: Hold the alley mouth and count
Report the count to Sorin

lichenreach_rows_of_twelve.note: Thirteen prints now, all toed to the wall. One is a woman with a mended sole who once asked the deep bed a question. Sorin bricked the alley on Tuesday. The count, he does not doubt, will go on rising behind the brick.

lichenreach_the_ones_who_listened.title: The Ones Who Listened
lichenreach_the_ones_who_listened.summary: Some informants in Lichenreach have listened so long they have stopped having faces of their own. One has wandered into the deep galleries and joined the lurkers. Petru wants him named before he is only a lurker.
lichenreach_the_ones_who_listened.offer: There is a trade here older than the light: you sell what you hear, and to hear well you learn to be no one. Do it long enough and it takes. A man named Andrei -- was named Andrei -- has listened himself so thin he wandered off among the lurkers and cannot say why he shouldn't be there. I have his name written down, which is more than he has now. Go down, thin the ones he runs with, and bring me a keepsake of his so I can put the name back on the man. Some of us should be filed as people, not as ears.
lichenreach_the_ones_who_listened.progress: Andrei runs with the lurkers in the deep galleries. Thin them; recover a keepsake to restore his name.
lichenreach_the_ones_who_listened.complete: His mother's cord. He kept it against everything the trade did to him -- one thing that was his and not something he'd overheard. I will tie the name to the keepsake and the keepsake to the man and we will see if any of it holds. It is a poor kind of saving, giving a hollow man back a knot of thread. It is the kind we can afford. Poor bastards, all of them, listening the faces off themselves for a winter's bread.

objectives.text: Thin the lurkers Andrei runs with
Recover Andrei's mother's cord
Bring the cord to Petru

lichenreach_the_ones_who_listened.note: Petru tied Andrei's name to his mother's cord and set it in the man's hand. Whether it held, no record says. He wrote the name in full, twice, and underlined it -- a chronicler's small war against the trade that files people down to ears.

lichenreach_the_quiet_god.title: The Quiet God
lichenreach_the_quiet_god.summary: The ivy-eaten Transcub church punished cruelty to the self -- a creed the strigoi found quaint until the stone began teaching people to hurt themselves. Iancu tends the last altar and wants a warding for it, and one honest witness.
lichenreach_the_quiet_god.offer: Old Transcub punished the cruelty a man does to himself. Nobody prays there now -- self-mercy is quaint, they say, in a city that files you. But somebody has been scratching at the altar in the small hours. A shaking hand, deeper every night. It reads: I only wanted to know what it said. And the stone under it is warm. I am too old to sit with it. Take a folk-warding down, set it on the altar, and stay long enough to see whose hand does the cutting. Then tell me it isn't the god's answer, arriving late.
lichenreach_the_quiet_god.progress: The last Transcub altar stands in the ivy churches at the gallery's edge. Ward it; witness the hand.
lichenreach_the_quiet_god.complete: Their own hand. Of course it is their own hand. That is the whole of the horror -- the stone does not need to reach for you. It teaches you to reach for yourself, and calls it curiosity. The old god knew this shape of cruelty before the stone had a name for it. Set the warding; it will not save them, but it names the wound. That is what churches are for, when they cannot cure.

objectives.label: the Transcub altar
objectives.text: Ward the last altar and witness
Set wardings on the marked stones
Tell Iancu whose hand it was

lichenreach_the_quiet_god.note: The hand at the altar was its own owner's, every night. The old god punished exactly this cruelty, and arrives too late to punish anyone but the stone. Iancu keeps the warding lit. It cannot cure the wound. It can, at least, call it by its right name.

lichenreach_edge_of_black_glass.title: An Edge of Black Glass
lichenreach_edge_of_black_glass.summary: Blackglass comes up from the south under duress, and Stelian is short a batch. The crawlers of the deep galleries carry shards in their gullets, if you have the stomach to cut them out.
lichenreach_edge_of_black_glass.offer: Sangeroasa sends us blackglass when it feels like being paid, which is never, so I make do. The cave-crawlers swallow the shards where the old volcanic vein bleeds through the wall -- keeps their gut hot, I'd wager, not that I've asked one. Bring me a satchel of it cut fresh and I will knap you a razor sharper than anything the south deigns to trade us. Ugly work, cutting glass out of a gullet. Honest work, though. Everything down here is honest. It has to be. Nobody can see far enough to lie.
lichenreach_edge_of_black_glass.progress: Cave-crawlers along the old volcanic seam carry blackglass in their gullets. Cut a satchel's worth.
lichenreach_edge_of_black_glass.complete: Look at that -- blacker than the galleries and twice as mean. I'll have your razor by morning. Funny trade, this: the south won't sell us glass, so we butcher the things that ate it. There's a lesson in there about Blestem, but I've a forge to mind and you've a razor to earn. Take it. It'll hold an edge longer than most friendships down here.

objectives.text: Cut blackglass from the crawlers' gullets
Gather a satchel of shards

lichenreach_edge_of_black_glass.note: Stelian knapped the razor by morning, blacker than the galleries. The south will not sell Lichenreach glass, so Lichenreach butchers what swallowed it -- an economy of grudges, edged and cold, and mostly honest for want of the light to lie by.

lichenreach_the_marked_border.title: The Marked Border
lichenreach_the_marked_border.summary: A map came into Grigore's hands with the deep-bed galleries marked in a border of small aligned strokes -- not decoration, he suspects, but a reading. He wants it walked and checked against the ground before he sells it.
lichenreach_the_marked_border.offer: A map is only worth what its errors cost the buyer, so I check mine on foot before I price them. This one troubles me. The border is a fret of little strokes, all leaning the same way, and I have seen dust lie like that -- aligned, parallel, pointing. Somebody drew the deep bed and then drew what the dust does around it. Walk the marked line for me. Tell me the strokes match the ground. If they do, I am selling a map of where the stone is awake, and I will want a great deal of money for it.
lichenreach_the_marked_border.progress: The marked line follows the deep-bed border. Walk it; check the strokes against the ground.
lichenreach_the_marked_border.complete: They match. Stroke for stroke, the dust leans where the map says it leans. So it is not a map of the galleries at all -- it is a map of the listening, and the galleries are only the frame. I will sell it, and I will charge the earth, and I will not sleep well. Here, for the walking. You have earned a lens worth more than the map. Read the world with it, and remember what reading cost the last man to do it here.

objectives.label: the marked border
objectives.text: Walk the map's marked line
Match the strokes against the dust
Confirm the reading to Grigore

lichenreach_the_marked_border.note: The strokes matched the dust, lean for lean: a map not of the galleries but of the listening they frame. Grigore priced it at a fortune and slept badly. Some maps are worth most to whoever never buys them, and never learns what the border truly bounds.

lichenreach_the_bread_that_wont_rise.title: The Bread That Won't Rise
lichenreach_the_bread_that_wont_rise.summary: Bogdan's starter has died -- the bread comes out flat and grey -- and every baker knows what a dead starter means these days. He wants a fresh culture and, mostly, wants to be told he's being foolish.
lichenreach_the_bread_that_wont_rise.offer: My starter's gone dead in the crock and the bread comes out flat as a paving stone. Now, a dead starter is just a dead starter -- but you'll have heard the talk, same as me, about what it means when the yeast quits in a whole town at once. It's the one starter. It's my crock. It's nothing. Fetch me a live culture off the bright lichen -- the good yeast lives in the wet of it -- and tell me I'm a superstitious old fool while you're at it. I'll stand you a loaf that actually rises.
lichenreach_the_bread_that_wont_rise.progress: Live baking-yeast lives in the wet of the bright lichen, deep in the galleries. Bring Bogdan a culture.
lichenreach_the_bread_that_wont_rise.complete: It rises! Look at it climb the crock -- that's a proper loaf, that is. See, one dead starter, nothing more, and here's an old fool proved foolish. ...You'll have noticed I didn't say a whole town. Just mine. Let's leave it at just mine. Sit, eat, the first slice is yours. Everything good's temporary, so you'd best enjoy the warm bread while the crock's still climbing.

objectives.text: Gather live yeast from the bright lichen
Bring the culture to Bogdan

lichenreach_the_bread_that_wont_rise.note: The bread rises again in Bogdan's crock, and he made a point of saying it was only ever his one starter. Only his. Just the one. He set a warm slice out for the walker and did not, aloud, count the other crocks that had gone grey that week.

lichenreach_the_patrol_that_didnt_climb.title: The Patrol That Didn't Climb
lichenreach_the_patrol_that_didnt_climb.summary: A three-man patrol went down into the deep bed at dusk and never climbed back out. Lupu cannot spare the men to look and cannot sleep for not looking. Find them, or find what's left, and bring back their tokens.
lichenreach_the_patrol_that_didnt_climb.offer: Three went down at the last bell -- good men, careful men, men who counted the galleries the way I taught them. They did not come up. No shout, no sign, no blood on the steps. I have walked that stair a thousand times and I have never once heard it go quiet the way it went quiet that night. I cannot send more after them; that is how a whole watch vanishes, one rescue at a time. You are not mine to lose. Go down. Bring me their tokens, and the truth, in whichever order you find them.
lichenreach_the_patrol_that_didnt_climb.progress: The lost patrol went down the deep-bed stair at dusk. Recover their kept-name tokens.
lichenreach_the_patrol_that_didnt_climb.complete: Three tokens. So. No wounds on any of them, you say, and their lamps still full of oil -- they did not run out of light, then, only out of the wanting to climb. That is the worst way I know to lose a man, and I have known the ways. They sat down in the dark because the dark, at last, had something to say to them, and they wanted to hear the end of it. Keep the striker. Light matters more than it did yesterday.

objectives.text: Cut through to the deep bed
Recover the patrol's tokens
Bring the tokens to Lupu

lichenreach_the_patrol_that_didnt_climb.note: Three tokens, three men, no wounds and no spent oil. They sat down in the dark not for lack of light but for want of the will to climb, the dark having found, at last, something to say to them. Lupu keeps their names on the wall and a lamp under each. The lamps he checks twice a night now.

lichenreach_the_well_beneath_the_wall.title: The Well Beneath the Wall
lichenreach_the_well_beneath_the_wall.summary: The deep-bed cistern that waters half the galleries has turned copper on the tongue. Nedelcu wants it read before the town drinks itself into whatever the taste is a symptom of.
lichenreach_the_well_beneath_the_wall.offer: The cistern under the wall waters half of us, and three days back it went copper on the tongue -- you know the taste, everyone knows the taste now, and nobody says the word for it. My wife says it's the old iron in the stone. Maybe. But the iron's been in the stone six hundred years and the water only turned this week. Go down and read it proper, with a clear eye, and tell me if it's the iron or if it's the other thing. And bring up a jar so we've proof, whichever it is. I'd rather have a bad answer than keep guessing at the ladle.
lichenreach_the_well_beneath_the_wall.progress: The deep-bed cistern feeds the lower galleries. Read the coppered water; bring up a jar.
lichenreach_the_well_beneath_the_wall.complete: Copper, and not from the iron -- you're sure. Then we don't drink from it, and we tell the others, and we haul from the far well till my arms fall off, and that's that. Better a long haul than a short guess. You did right to bring the jar. A man can argue with a story. He can't argue with a jar of water gone the color of old blood. Take the poultice -- you'll have breathed enough of that gallery to want it.

objectives.label: the deep-bed cistern
objectives.text: Read the coppered cistern
Jar a sample of the water
Bring Nedelcu the truth and the jar

lichenreach_the_well_beneath_the_wall.note: The cistern ran copper, and not from the iron. Nedelcu's people haul from the far well now, arms aching, and count the days. He keeps the jar on a shelf where the children can see it -- proof of a thing you cannot argue with, gone the color of old blood beneath the listening wall.

lichenreach_the_record_that_shortens.title: The Record That Shortens
lichenreach_the_record_that_shortens.summary: One entry in Petru's oldest ledger grows shorter every time he copies it. He wants an honest hand to copy it once, exactly, and confirm the words are truly going missing and not merely his failing eyes.
lichenreach_the_record_that_shortens.offer: A chronicler's whole faith is that a thing set down stays set down. I have an entry that will not. Every fair copy I make comes out shorter than the last -- a word gone here, a clause there, never the same, always fewer. Either my eyes are going or the record is being read down to nothing by something that keeps the copies for itself. Copy it once, in your own steady hand, word for word. Then we compare. I need to know it is the ledger and not the man. God help me, I am hoping it is the man.
lichenreach_the_record_that_shortens.progress: The shortening entry lies in Petru's oldest ledger. Make one exact fair copy for comparison.
lichenreach_the_record_that_shortens.complete: Your copy is shorter than mine, and mine this morning was shorter than mine last night. It is not the man, then. It is the record. Something reads it and keeps what it reads, and the words do not come back. There is a stone at the Stonepath does the same, they say -- the only inscription in Draconia getting shorter. I did not think it had reached this far east. I am going to copy it every night now, to the last word, so that the last word at least is witnessed leaving. Take the method. You may need to know what unreading looks like.

objectives.text: Make an exact fair copy of the entry
Compare copies with Petru

lichenreach_the_record_that_shortens.note: The entry shortens still, copy by copy, a word read down to nothing each night by something that keeps what it takes. Petru copies it after dark now, to the last surviving word, so that its leaving is at least witnessed. It is the only inscription he has ever kept that is trying to become silence.

lichenreach_a_sign_for_the_lintel.title: A Sign for the Lintel
lichenreach_a_sign_for_the_lintel.summary: Stelian forges hearth-hex signs for doors, folk-magic his own trade half-mocks, and demand has quietly tripled. He needs iron cut from the crawlers' seam to make more, faster than he can smelt it.
lichenreach_a_sign_for_the_lintel.offer: I'll be honest, since honesty's cheap down here: I don't half believe in the signs I hammer. A hex on the lintel to keep the wrong things out -- my grandmother's craft, and mostly nonsense. Except every household in Lichenreach has quietly asked for one this month, and a smith doesn't argue with a full order-book. I'm short of iron. The crawlers carry bog-iron in them, swallowed off the old seam. Bring me a load and I'll hammer signs till my arm gives, believing or not. Folk want something over the door. Who am I to say the door doesn't need it.
lichenreach_a_sign_for_the_lintel.progress: The cave-crawlers carry bog-iron off the old seam. Bring Stelian a load to forge warding signs.
lichenreach_a_sign_for_the_lintel.complete: That'll do. Enough for a dozen lintels, maybe more. I still don't rightly believe in them -- but I've noticed I hang one on my own door, and I've noticed I'm not the only unbeliever who does. There's a kind of faith that's just doing the sensible thing while insisting it's nonsense. Take a sign yourself. Over your door, or in your pack, I don't care which. Just... have it. Grandmother wasn't a fool, and neither, lately, am I.

objectives.text: Cut bog-iron from the crawler seam
Gather iron for the warding signs
Bring the iron to Stelian

lichenreach_a_sign_for_the_lintel.note: Stelian hammered a dozen hearth-hex signs and hung one on his own door, an unbeliever like the rest, doing the sensible thing while calling it nonsense. Every lintel in Lichenreach wears one now. Grandmother's craft, unbelieved and unremoved -- the exact shape of a town that has started, quietly, to be afraid.

lichenreach_where_the_wall_goes_thin.title: Where the Wall Goes Thin
lichenreach_where_the_wall_goes_thin.summary: Iancu swears there's a stretch of the deep wall gone thin -- not cracked, thin, like a page held to a candle -- and that on the wrong nights you can nearly see through it. He wants it found and veiled before anyone reads what's behind.
lichenreach_where_the_wall_goes_thin.offer: You'll think me touched, and that's fine, I've been called worse by better. There's a length of the deep wall gone thin. Not broke -- thin. Like holding a page up to a flame, where the ink on the far side starts to swim up through. On a bad night you can near enough see through it, and what's on the far side is looking back, patient as anything. I want it found and I want it veiled before some fool presses his eye to it and reads what oughtn't be read. Take the veil-cloth down. Hang it where the wall goes thin. Don't look long.
lichenreach_where_the_wall_goes_thin.progress: The thin stretch lies deep along the listening wall. Find it; hang the veil before it is read.
lichenreach_where_the_wall_goes_thin.complete: You veiled it, and you didn't look long -- I can tell, your eyes are still your own. Good man. It's not a hole, that place, and it's not a door. It's a wall worn thin from the far side, by something pressing its reading against it, night on night, the way water thins a stone. The veil won't stop it thinning. It'll just stop us seeing, and being seen, which is the most a body can ask down here. Keep the charm. It quiets the wanting-to-look. You'll want it quiet.

objectives.label: the thin wall
objectives.text: Find where the wall goes thin
Hang the veil over the thin wall
Tell Iancu the wall is veiled

lichenreach_where_the_wall_goes_thin.note: The thin wall is veiled, worn from the far side by a reading pressed against it night on night, the way water thins a stone. The cloth stops no thinning; it only stops the seeing, and the being seen. In Lichenreach that is reckoned mercy enough, and Iancu asks for no more.

lichenreach_the_blue_draught.title: The Blue Draught
lichenreach_the_blue_draught.summary: A reading-draught that grants the alchemical sight is the deep bed's most dangerous trade good -- it lets you see the signal's color. Grigore wants ingredients gathered and one honest field-test run before he stocks it.
lichenreach_the_blue_draught.offer: Every buyer wants the sight -- blue for the necromantic, violet for the deep, green for a waking stone, and the last color, which I do not name in the shop. A draught grants it for an hour. I can distil it from the bright lichen and a leech's copper, but I will not stock a thing I have not tested on a steady head. Gather me the makings, drink the draught at the deep bed, and tell me what colors the galleries read. Then tell me you regret nothing. Half my trade is selling men their own curiosity back to them at a markup. I would rather not sell them their deaths.
lichenreach_the_blue_draught.progress: Gather the draught's makings; drink at the deep bed and read the galleries' colors for Grigore.
lichenreach_the_blue_draught.complete: Blue in the old graves, violet down the deepest bed -- and the last color, the one I do not name, running thin along the thin wall like a thread pulled taut. You saw it and you came back and your eyes still track true. Good. Then it works, and it is exactly as dangerous as I feared, and I will price it accordingly. Keep this vial. Use it once, badly, in a moment you will remember choosing. That is all the sight is ever good for -- one honest look you cannot take back.

objectives.text: Harvest copper from the deep-bed lurkers
Distil and drink the reading-draught
Report the colors to Grigore

lichenreach_the_blue_draught.note: Blue in the graves, violet in the deep, and along the thin wall the color Grigore will not name, run out thin as a taut thread. He priced the draught for what it costs a man and stocked it anyway. The sight is good for one honest look you cannot take back. He sells it knowing exactly that.

lichenreach_the_writ_of_unfiling.title: The Writ of Unfiling
lichenreach_the_writ_of_unfiling.summary: In Blestem a person can be erased on paper -- unfiled, made never-to-have-been. A woman condemned to the listening-wall may yet be saved if her file is pulled first. Sorin has the writ; he needs it carried past the lurkers to the deep bed and back, unread.
lichenreach_the_writ_of_unfiling.offer: Here is a Blestem cruelty you'll not have met: they don't kill you, they unfile you. Strike the record and you never were, and once you never were, they can wall you up and there's no one to have gone missing. There's a woman due for the wall on a false entry. I hold a writ of unfiling that runs the other way -- pull her record before they can strike it, and she stays real, and real people can't be quietly bricked. Carry it to the deep bed and back. Don't read it. A writ read by the wrong eye files the reader instead. I've seen it. Just carry.
lichenreach_the_writ_of_unfiling.progress: Carry the writ of unfiling to the deep-bed registry and back to Sorin -- unread.
lichenreach_the_writ_of_unfiling.complete: Back, and the seal unbroken, and your name still your own -- I checked, don't mind me. She's filed as living now, in ink they can't strike without three signatures they'll never gather in time. She's real. Real's the whole of it, down here. They can do a great deal to a person, but they cannot brick up someone the record insists is still walking about. You carried it and you didn't read it. In Blestem that's not honesty. That's heroism. Take the writ-work. You've the hands for it.

objectives.text: Clear the lurkers from the registry path
Carry the writ to the deep-bed registry
Return the sealed writ to Sorin

lichenreach_the_writ_of_unfiling.note: The woman is filed as living, in ink no one can strike in time, and so cannot be quietly bricked into the wall. In Lichenreach that is a rescue: not a life saved, a record kept. Sorin filed the deed under nothing, so no one would come asking who kept the writ moving.

lichenreach_a_thing_that_was_hers.title: A Thing That Was Hers
lichenreach_a_thing_that_was_hers.summary: Nedelcu's sister walked into the deep bed a month ago and came back a listener with no memory of her own name. He wants a keepsake of her old life recovered from where she left it, on the chance it calls her back.
lichenreach_a_thing_that_was_hers.offer: My sister went down to draw water a month gone and came up someone else -- looks at me like a stranger, answers to no name, sits and listens to the wall for hours. The chronicler says she's a listener now, hollowed. But she left a keepsake down there, her wedding cord, the one thing she'd never willingly set down. If a hollow woman can be called back to herself, it'll be by the one thing she chose to keep. Find it. Bring it up. I have to try. You'd try, for a sister. Tell me you'd try.
lichenreach_a_thing_that_was_hers.progress: The sister's keepsake lies where she left it in the deep bed. Recover it for Nedelcu.
lichenreach_a_thing_that_was_hers.complete: That's it. That's her cord. ...I put it in her hand and for a breath -- one breath -- she looked at it like she knew it. Then the wall said something only she could hear and she was gone again, listening. But there was a breath. A whole breath where she was my sister. I'll take a breath. It's more than yesterday had. Whatever's in that wall, it doesn't get all of her while I've got one thing that was hers to put back in her hand. Take the cord-work. And thank you. For trying.

objectives.text: Clear the listeners from her old path
Recover the sister's wedding cord
Bring the keepsake to Nedelcu

lichenreach_a_thing_that_was_hers.note: For one breath, holding her wedding cord, the sister knew her brother's face. Then the wall spoke and she was a listener again. Nedelcu keeps the cord in her hand each night. One breath is more than yesterday held. He is not a man who will be argued out of trying.

lichenreach_daily_tend_the_cold_light.title: Tending the Cold Light
lichenreach_daily_tend_the_cold_light.summary: The taproom lichen wants daily feeding to keep its glow, and the crawlers must be kept back from the near galleries. Bogdan trades a warm meal for an honest morning's tending.
lichenreach_daily_tend_the_cold_light.offer: Cold light's a living thing, and living things want feeding. The taproom lichen'll dim by evening if it's not tended come morning, and the crawlers creep the near galleries every night looking for the dark I won't give them. Do me the morning round -- thin the near crawlers, cut a fresh culture, feed the glow. Nothing grand, just the daily keeping of a small warm light in a place that'd rather be dark. Do it and there's a hot bowl and a dry seat by the brightest wall in Lichenreach. That's the wage down here. It's a better one than it sounds.
lichenreach_daily_tend_the_cold_light.progress: Do the morning round: thin the near crawlers and cut a fresh lichen culture for the taproom.
lichenreach_daily_tend_the_cold_light.complete: Bright as a spring nobody down here's ever seen. That'll hold the room till nightfall. Sit -- bowl's hot, seat's dry, and the wall behind you glows blue and honest. A day's a small thing to keep lit. Do enough small things and a body's got a life. Come back tomorrow; the light won't tend itself, worse luck, and I've grown fond of the company.

objectives.text: Thin the near-gallery crawlers
Cut fresh cultures for the glow

lichenreach_daily_tend_the_cold_light.note: The cold light holds another day, fed and tended, blue and honest against the dark that would rather have the room. Bogdan keeps the brightest wall in Lichenreach and a hot bowl beneath it. A day is a small thing to keep lit. He keeps it, and keeps it, and keeps it.

lichenreach_daily_keep_the_bed_clear.title: Keeping the Bed Clear
lichenreach_daily_keep_the_bed_clear.summary: The lurkers reclaim the deep-bed path every night, and every morning the watch must cut it open again so water-drawers can pass. Lupu pays a fair coin for a fair morning's work.
lichenreach_daily_keep_the_bed_clear.offer: It's the least heroic work in Draconia and it never once ends. Lurkers take back the deep-bed path every night -- don't ask me from where, they're just there again by the first bell -- and folk need that path to draw water, so every morning somebody cuts it open again. Today it's you, if you've the coin-need and the patience. Thin the lurkers, clear the bed, let the water-drawers through. Tomorrow it'll want doing again. That's not despair talking, mind. That's just the job. The path stays open because somebody keeps opening it. Might as well be a coin in it for you.
lichenreach_daily_keep_the_bed_clear.progress: Cut the deep-bed path clear of lurkers so the water-drawers can pass.
lichenreach_daily_keep_the_bed_clear.complete: Clear again, and the drawers are already lining up with their jars -- ungrateful lot, but that's water for you. Here's your coin, honest for honest. The path'll close tonight and want cutting tomorrow, world without end. There's men who'd call that pointless. I call it Tuesday. A thing worth doing's usually a thing worth doing again. Off you go. Same time, if the coin suits.

objectives.text: Cut the deep-bed path clear

lichenreach_daily_keep_the_bed_clear.note: The deep-bed path is open again, as it was yesterday and will not be tomorrow. The lurkers take it back each night from somewhere Lupu has stopped asking about. The path stays open because somebody keeps opening it. Today that was work worth a coin. Tomorrow it will be again.

lichenreach_the_accords_quiet_ask.title: The Accord's Quiet Ask
lichenreach_the_accords_quiet_ask.summary: A listening-network runner has left word with Petru: the Accord wants Lichenreach's deep bed read and reported, quietly, before Blestem's spymaster prices the same knowledge first. Petru would rather the Accord owe him than Cazimir own him.
lichenreach_the_accords_quiet_ask.offer: A runner came, the kind that leaves no name, and left an ask dressed as a courtesy. The Accord wants to know what's waking in our deep bed -- read it, report it, quietly, before Cazimir's people file it and sell it to the highest fear. I'm a chronicler, not a spy, but I know which master I'd rather be indebted to, and it isn't the one who owns the walls. Go down. Gather what an honest report needs -- what's failing, what's waking, what's listening. Bring it to me. I'll send it where it does the least harm. In Blestem that passes for loyalty.
lichenreach_the_accords_quiet_ask.progress: Gather what an honest report needs from the deep bed: what fails, what wakes, what listens.
lichenreach_the_accords_quiet_ask.complete: That's a report I can send. Lichen failing north to south, the thin wall thinning, the cistern gone copper, the alcove that answers -- all of it, plainly, the way a chronicle should read. The Accord will have it before Cazimir prices it, and Lichenreach will owe the peace instead of the spider. It's not a clean thing I've done. It's a cleaner thing than the alternative. Take the still and the dossier both -- you gathered them, they're yours. And keep our little disloyalty between us.

objectives.text: Read the deep bed's waking signal
Compile the honest report
Bring the report to Petru

lichenreach_the_accords_quiet_ask.note: Petru sent his honest report to the Accord before Blestem's spymaster could price the same truths and sell them to the highest fear. It was not a clean deed. It was a cleaner one than the alternative. Lichenreach owes the peace now, not the spider -- a distinction that, in the maze-city, is the whole of virtue.

lichenreach_the_coppered_leeches.title: The Coppered Leeches
lichenreach_the_coppered_leeches.summary: The lurkers of the deep bed have grown fat on coppered water and carry the metal in them -- reclaimable, if you don't mind the smell. Stelian pays for a load to alloy into his blackglass edges.
lichenreach_the_coppered_leeches.offer: Here's a thrifty horror for you: the lurkers down the deep bed have been drinking the coppered water, the bad water, and it's built up in them like scale in a kettle. Cut one open and there's good reclaimable copper in the muck of it. Bring me a load and I'll alloy it into the blackglass -- makes a wicked edge, copper and glass, though the smell's a crime against God. Waste not, is the smith's creed, even when what you're not-wasting is a bad well's worth of poison strained through a monster's gut. Especially then, maybe. Nothing's clean down here. Might as well be sharp.
lichenreach_the_coppered_leeches.progress: The deep-bed lurkers carry reclaimable copper from the bad water. Cut a load for Stelian.
lichenreach_the_coppered_leeches.complete: Reeks like the inside of the world, but it's good copper. Into the crucible it goes, and out comes an edge that'll part hair. Strange trade, alloying a blade out of the same bad water that's poisoning the wells -- but that's Lichenreach, isn't it. We can't fix the well, so we sharpen ourselves on what it ruined. Take the edge. It's honest steel made of dishonest water. That's about the best any of us manage down here.

objectives.text: Cut copper from the coppered lurkers
Reclaim copper for the crucible

lichenreach_the_coppered_leeches.note: Stelian alloyed a wicked edge from copper strained through the lurkers' guts -- the same poison that fouls the wells, turned to honest steel. Lichenreach cannot mend the water. It sharpens itself on what the water ruined. That, Stelian allows, is about the best any of them manage.

lichenreach_a_toll_that_wasnt_paid.title: A Toll That Wasn't Paid
lichenreach_a_toll_that_wasnt_paid.summary: A caravan owes a toll it swears it paid, and the only proof is a receipt no one can find. Grigore knows a forger's work when he sees the gap -- and would rather sell the missing receipt than see an honest carter unfiled for it.
lichenreach_a_toll_that_wasnt_paid.offer: A toll's gone unpaid, or so the ledger says, and the carter swears on his mother he paid it and lost the receipt. Now, normally I'd let the ledger win -- the ledger always wins, that's Blestem -- but I've a soft spot I can't afford and won't discuss. There's a forgery-hand in the deep galleries sells receipts that pass any clerk's eye. Fetch me one, clean work, and I'll slip it into the carter's file before the toll-men strike his name. It's a lie. It's a lie that keeps a decent man real. Down here, that's the only kind of truth going cheap.
lichenreach_a_toll_that_wasnt_paid.progress: The forger works the deep galleries. Fetch a clean toll-receipt to save the carter's file.
lichenreach_a_toll_that_wasnt_paid.complete: Flawless. No clerk alive would catch it, and I've an eye for what clerks catch. Into the file it goes, and the carter's toll reads paid, and paid it stays. He never knew he was an entry away from being unfiled -- from being nobody who'd owed nobody nothing, ever, forever. A forged receipt saved a real man. Weigh that how you like. I've stopped weighing. Take the forger's work for your trouble. You may meet a file of your own someday, and want to know how they're beaten.

objectives.text: Reach the forger's deep workshop
Obtain a flawless forged receipt
Bring the receipt to Grigore

lichenreach_a_toll_that_wasnt_paid.note: A forged receipt slipped into a file kept an honest carter from being unfiled -- kept him a real man who paid a real toll. Grigore has stopped weighing such things. In Blestem the only truth going cheap is the lie that keeps a decent person on the books. He buys it whenever he can.

lichenreach_the_handprint_ward.title: The Handprint Ward
lichenreach_the_handprint_ward.summary: The children of Lichenreach press chalk handprints on the gallery walls -- a game, a ward, a small warm defiance of the listening dark. Iancu wants fresh chalk-wards set where the little ones won't go, to keep the whole run of wall theirs.
lichenreach_the_handprint_ward.offer: The little ones have a game -- press a chalk hand on the wall, and the wall's yours, and nothing that listens can have it. Nonsense, of course. Beautiful nonsense. But there's a stretch of gallery gone too dark for children, and they won't go, and so the wall there belongs to the other thing. I'd like it back for them. Take chalk-wards down and press the handprints yourself where small hands won't reach. Give the children their whole wall. It's a daft errand for a grown fighter. Do it anyway. Some walls should belong to somebody warm.
lichenreach_the_handprint_ward.progress: The dark stretch of gallery wants chalk handprint-wards set where children won't go.
lichenreach_the_handprint_ward.complete: You did it -- I can see the prints from here, a whole run of little chalk hands and your big daft ones among them, and the wall's ours again, the children's, top to bottom. Silly. I know it's silly. But the children came and looked and stood a bit taller, and the dark's got a stretch less of wall to itself tonight. That's not nothing. That's a hand pressed against the dark that says: not this, not here, not the little ones. Keep a ward. Press it somewhere that needs to stay warm.

objectives.label: the dark stretch
objectives.text: Reach the wall too dark for children
Press chalk-wards along the wall
Tell Iancu the wall is theirs again

lichenreach_the_handprint_ward.note: A whole run of chalk handprints now, small ones and one grown fighter's daft large ones, and the dark holds a stretch less of gallery for it. It is beautiful nonsense: a warm hand pressed against the cold that says not this, not here, not the little ones. In Lichenreach, that is what a ward is.

lichenreach_the_obsidian_heart.title: The Obsidian Heart
lichenreach_the_obsidian_heart.summary: Deep in the galleries, where the volcanic seam meets the listening wall, grows a heart of blackglass the crawlers guard jealously. Stelian has an order for it he does not like -- and needs it cut before a worse buyer comes.
lichenreach_the_obsidian_heart.offer: There's a thing grows where the old volcanic vein pushes into the listening wall -- a heart of blackglass, cooled slow, dense as grief, and the crawlers ring it like they're guarding their own. I've an order for it. Won't say from whom; the coin came in a listening-wall's echo and I didn't like the sound of it. But if I don't cut it, a worse hand will, and better an honest smith holds a dangerous thing than lets it drift to whoever whispered for it. Go down, clear the crawlers, cut me the heart. Then we'll decide together where a thing like that should not go.
lichenreach_the_obsidian_heart.progress: The blackglass heart grows where the seam meets the wall, ringed by crawlers. Cut it for Stelian.
lichenreach_the_obsidian_heart.complete: Heavier than it's any right to be, and cold in a way glass shouldn't manage. So this is what the wall whispered a price for. Well -- it's in an honest smith's hands now, not the whisperer's, and there it'll stay till I've thought of somewhere it can do no harm. Maybe there's no such place. Maybe holding it is the harm, just held slower. But I'd rather carry that than hand it to the echo that asked. Take the focus I've made you. Keep it turned toward things worth watching. And thank you. For not asking who ordered it.

objectives.text: Clear the crawlers ringing the heart
Cut the blackglass heart from the seam
Bring the heart to Stelian

lichenreach_the_obsidian_heart.note: The blackglass heart rests in Stelian's hands, not the whisperer's who paid for it in a listening-wall's echo. He has nowhere safe to put it, and knows there may be no such place -- that holding it is only the harm carried slower. Better his hands than the echo's. He does not sleep as he did.

lichenreach_the_counteragent_of_quiet.title: The Counteragent of Quiet
lichenreach_the_counteragent_of_quiet.summary: The Transcub creed held that a quiet given freely could answer the pull to know. Nedelcu wants the old counteragent brewed from deep-bed makings -- a drink that dulls the wanting-to-listen -- for a town learning it needs one.
lichenreach_the_counteragent_of_quiet.offer: My grandmother made a drink she called the counteragent of quiet. Old Transcub recipe. You took it when the wanting-to-know got loud in you -- when you'd catch yourself leaning toward a wall to hear what it might say. It doesn't cure. It just turns the wanting down, like banking a fire, so a body can sleep instead of listen. Half of Lichenreach could use it now and won't admit why. Gather me the makings from the deep bed. I'll brew a batch for anyone who asks, and never write down who asked. Some mercies keep best off the ledger.
lichenreach_the_counteragent_of_quiet.progress: Gather the counteragent's deep-bed makings for Nedelcu to brew against the wanting-to-listen.
lichenreach_the_counteragent_of_quiet.complete: A whole batch, and already there's folk come quiet to the door for it, coats up, eyes down, not saying what for. I don't ask. I ladle it out and I don't write the names. It won't save a one of them if the wall gets loud enough -- I've no illusions, my grandmother had none either. But it banks the fire. It buys a night's sleep against the pull. In a town the dark is learning to talk to, a night's honest sleep is a thing worth brewing for. Take a dose yourself. You've leaned toward a wall or two this week. Don't argue. I saw.

objectives.text: Gather makings from the deep-bed lurkers
Brew the counteragent of quiet
Bring the batch to Nedelcu

lichenreach_the_counteragent_of_quiet.note: They come quiet to Nedelcu's door for the counteragent, coats up, eyes down, and he ladles it out and writes no names -- some mercies keep best off the ledger. It cures nothing. It banks the fire and buys one night's sleep against the pull, in a town the dark is learning to talk to.

drowned_quarter_warm_tablet_01.title: The Tablet That Came In Warm
drowned_quarter_warm_tablet_01.summary: A debt-tablet washed up on Nedelcu's pier this morning, hairline-cracked and still faintly warm. He wants it out of his water before whatever the tide brought it here for comes looking. Walk the cracked pier and see the thing yourself.
drowned_quarter_warm_tablet_01.offer: You'll think me a fool, but the tide brought something in warm this morning, and nothing comes off that black water warm. A debt-tablet, cracked across like an egg -- somebody's whole life, not quite closed, out with the salt. I'll not touch it. Go to the cracked pier and look at it for me, and tell me it's just a stone.
drowned_quarter_warm_tablet_01.progress: The tablet lies where it grounded, on the cracked pier off the north stair. Go and look. Do not pick it up unless you've made your peace with picking things up.
drowned_quarter_warm_tablet_01.complete: Still warm, you say. Cracked but not closed. Aye -- that's the shape of a thing that got away from the ledger and knows it. Keep it if you dare; I'd sooner keep a live coal in my coat. There's a broker down here trades in tablets like it. He's dead, mind. Doesn't slow him.

objectives.label: the cracked pier
objectives.text: Look at the tablet on the cracked pier

drowned_quarter_warm_tablet_01.note: A tablet came in warm off water that keeps nothing warm. Cracked, not closed -- a life the ledger began to settle and could not finish. Nedelcu wanted it gone. The tide had wanted it here.

drowned_quarter_warm_tablet_02.title: A Broker Who Stopped Arguing
drowned_quarter_warm_tablet_02.summary: Nedelcu names the dead broker who reads tablets for a price: he keeps to the ledger-office in the sunk district, closed on the rolls and open for business. Carry the cracked tablet to Vlaicu, who copies for the annex, and learn what a life half-settled is worth.
drowned_quarter_warm_tablet_02.offer: The broker keeps books that should have shut and haven't -- same as him, same as this tablet you're carrying. If anyone can read what's cracked into it without the reading killing you, it's a man already past being killed. Take it to Vlaicu at the ledger-office first; the annex copies everything, and I'd have an honest hand see it before a dead one does.
drowned_quarter_warm_tablet_02.progress: Vlaicu the clerk keeps the ledger-office copy-desk in the sunk district. Bring him the cracked tablet. Let him do the reading; you've read enough for one crossing.
drowned_quarter_warm_tablet_02.complete: So. A name that owes a debt it can't pay because the debtor's already dead -- a discrepancy, Vlaicu calls it, like it's a smudge and not a person. The annex will want it filed. The broker will want it lost. And you'll want, I think, to know which of them is right. I never do.

objectives.text: Carry the cracked tablet to Vlaicu at the ledger-office

drowned_quarter_warm_tablet_02.note: Vlaicu read the tablet the way a clerk reads a debt: without flinching. A life half-settled, filed under discrepancy. The annex would close it. The broker would lose it. Both, Nedelcu said quietly, are the same knife held at different ends.

drowned_quarter_warm_tablet_03.title: Un-Filing
drowned_quarter_warm_tablet_03.summary: The annex would finalize the cracked tablet; the district around it would close with it, doors and faces and all. Vlaicu shows the player the un-filing -- and its price. The finalized rise where a tablet is un-stamped. Break them, and reopen a district that was already, quietly, shut.
drowned_quarter_warm_tablet_03.offer: I have stamped this line, and un-stamped it, and stamped it again -- my own hand refusing my own verdict, all afternoon. That is what un-filing costs: the debt does not cancel, it moves. Break the tablet's hold and this drowned district reopens -- doors, faces, the lot. But the finalized will rise where the seal comes off. They always do. Go to the sunk ledger-office and let them.
drowned_quarter_warm_tablet_03.progress: The finalized rise in the sunk ledger-office as the seal comes off. Put them down. This is not killing; it is refusing to let the ledger have the last word.
drowned_quarter_warm_tablet_03.complete: It opened. A whole district un-shut in an afternoon, faces flickering back like lamps relit -- and the debt gone nowhere, only elsewhere, onto some warm family who never read a tablet in their lives. Mercy is a transfer, traveler, not a cancellation. Take the writ. It's the only stamp I ever made that I'd make again.

objectives.text: Put down the finalized as the seal comes off

objectives.label: the sunk ledger-office
objectives.text: Un-file the tablet at the sunk ledger-office

drowned_quarter_warm_tablet_03.note: A district reopened; doors that had been shut a hundred years let light back in. And the debt only moved -- landed warm on someone downstream who never asked for it. Vlaicu made the stamp anyway. He said a mercy you can't afford is still a mercy. He did not say to whom.

drowned_quarter_ledger_office_01.title: Books That Should Have Closed
drowned_quarter_ledger_office_01.summary: Sorin keeps the district's histories and cannot make the numbers lie down: accounts stamped collected that keep transacting anyway. He asks the player to clear the clerks that still work the sunk annex, so he can read the ledgers without a dead hand turning the page.
drowned_quarter_ledger_office_01.offer: I keep the histories here, which in this quarter means I keep company with a great many things that ought to be past tense and aren't. There's an annex full of clerks that were collected -- closed on the rolls, filed, done -- and they are still at their desks, still stamping. I cannot read a page they keep turning. Clear the desks for me. Gently, if a spade can be gentle.
drowned_quarter_ledger_office_01.progress: The drowned clerks work the sunk annex desks off the east canal. Put them down so the ledgers hold still. Six should quiet the room.
drowned_quarter_ledger_office_01.complete: Quiet now. Look at this line -- collected, and yet the account moved coin last Tuesday. A closed thing that keeps its habits. In the border villages we had a word for the dead who wouldn't lie still. Here they don't even have the decency to be haunted. They just keep working. That is the drowned quarter entire.

objectives.text: Still the clerks at the sunk annex desks

drowned_quarter_ledger_office_01.note: Sorin read his pages at last. Every closed account still spending, still stamping, still keeping the small habits of the living. Resolved things, the saying goes down here, don't know they're meant to lie down.

drowned_quarter_ledger_office_02.title: The Line That Won't Stay Stamped
drowned_quarter_ledger_office_02.summary: One account in the annex is stamped collected in violet, and un-stamped, and stamped again -- the ink smeared where a dead hand kept refusing the verdict. Sorin wants the ledger itself carried up to daylight before the refusing hand finishes its argument. Find it in the drowned records.
drowned_quarter_ledger_office_02.offer: There is one line down there, near the bottom of a violet page, that has been stamped and un-stamped so many times the ink's gone to smear. A dead clerk arguing with his own death, entry by entry, and losing, and starting again. I want that ledger in my hands and out of that room. Go to the drowned records under the east canal and bring it up where I can watch it in honest light.
drowned_quarter_ledger_office_02.progress: The ledger sits in the drowned records under the east canal, where the violet stamping never quite dries. Bring it up. Try not to read the smeared line too long.
drowned_quarter_ledger_office_02.complete: Stamped, un-stamped, stamped. A man too dead to win the argument and too stubborn to stop having it. I'll keep the book in the light where I can see it change its mind. You look pale, traveler. Reading that line does that. Curiosity is the door here -- and this quarter never did learn to keep a door shut.

objectives.label: the drowned records
objectives.text: Recover the smeared ledger from the drowned records
Bring up the stamped, un-stamped ledger

drowned_quarter_ledger_office_02.note: One line, stamped and un-stamped past legibility -- a dead man refusing his own verdict, and losing, forever. Sorin keeps the book in daylight now, and watches it argue. Some deaths, he says, are just very slow letters of complaint.

drowned_quarter_canal_deposits_01.title: What the Canals Are For
drowned_quarter_canal_deposits_01.summary: Grigore walks the canal patrol and has stopped calling the shapes in the water what they are. The brine-shades gather thick where the current is slow -- the accreted dead of a city that uses its canals as both circulatory and disposal. Thin them before they crowd the low stair entire.
drowned_quarter_canal_deposits_01.offer: You want to know what the canals are for? Two things. They carry the city's water, and they carry the city's people, once the city's done with them. Bodies are deposits here. And where the water goes slow, the deposits... gather. Get thick. Learn to move. Come with me to the low stair and thin the shades before they think to climb it.
drowned_quarter_canal_deposits_01.progress: The brine-shades gather at the low stair where the canal current slacks. Thin them -- eight should break the crowd before it climbs.
drowned_quarter_canal_deposits_01.complete: Better. For a night. They'll gather again -- slow water always finds its dead. I've walked this beat eleven years and I've stopped flinching, which is the part that worries me. A man ought to flinch at the dead in the water. When he doesn't, he's halfway to being a deposit himself.

objectives.text: Thin the brine-shades at the low stair

drowned_quarter_canal_deposits_01.note: The canals carry the city's water and, when the city is done, the city's people. The shades gather where the current slacks. Grigore has stopped flinching. He named that, quietly, as the worst thing the beat had done to him.

drowned_quarter_canal_deposits_02.title: The Deposit Slip
drowned_quarter_canal_deposits_02.summary: Lupu found a name he knew among the deposit rolls -- a canal-side widow, listed as delivered to the water three days before she was seen alive at market. Recover the shade that carries her ledger-scrip and learn whether the paperwork or the woman is the lie.
drowned_quarter_canal_deposits_02.offer: I'll tell you the thing that keeps me up. There's a name on the deposit rolls -- a widow off the fish-market -- marked delivered to the canal on a Tuesday. I saw her buy bread the Friday after. So either the water gave her back, or the paperwork put her in before she went. And down here, brother, the paperwork is never the thing that's wrong. Find the shade that carries her scrip. I have to know which of my eyes to stop trusting.
drowned_quarter_canal_deposits_02.progress: The scrip-bearing shade drifts the market canal where the widow was listed. Put it down and take the slip it carries.
drowned_quarter_canal_deposits_02.complete: Delivered Tuesday. Bought bread Friday. And the slip's in your hand now, stamped and true and three days early. The paperwork was right, Lupu. She was the discrepancy -- collected, and still shopping, till something noticed and corrected the error. I'd rather the water had just taken her. At least the water doesn't keep records.
