dead_timber_barge_manifest.note: Six of the barge's eight stood grey in the deep grove, facing the water; two, Mira chose not to seek. The annex took the cargo, but her log says 'collected en route' -- a splinter left for some tidy auditor a century on, and it will be true.

dead_timber_guards_desertion.title: The Post That Emptied
dead_timber_guards_desertion.summary: Rada's watch-partner walked off his post into the groves three nights running and won't say why, only that something out there is 'calling the tired ones in.' She wants the calling thing found and ended before he answers it for good.
dead_timber_guards_desertion.offer: My partner's walking off post. Three nights now -- I find him gone, and come dawn he's back at the rail, grey-faced, saying he doesn't remember, only that something's 'calling the tired ones in.' He IS tired. We all are; it's the only weather here. Whatever's out there wants the empty ones, and grief's made him empty. Find it. End it. I've had two partners in this town and I'd like to keep the one I've got.
dead_timber_guards_desertion.progress: Hunt the deep groves for whatever is calling the tired ones in. End it before Rada's partner answers.
dead_timber_guards_desertion.complete: You put down a whole stand of the revenants at the grove's heart, where the ground was warm -- warm, in this cold -- and my partner slept the night through for the first time in a week. The calling's quieter. Not gone. It doesn't want the strong; it wants the worn-down, and this town wears everyone down eventually. I keep him talking now. Angry, even. Anger's loud enough to drown a whisper. Take the cord. Tie it to something you'd hate to lose.

objectives.text: End what calls the tired ones in

dead_timber_guards_desertion.note: The calling wanted the empty ones, and grief had emptied Rada's partner. She put down the revenants at the warm-grounded grove-heart and now keeps him angry, because anger is loud enough to drown a whisper. The calling is quieter. It is not gone.

dead_timber_inn_that_listens.title: The Hour the Common Room Went Quiet
dead_timber_inn_that_listens.summary: Every night at the same hour, Marta's whole common room stops talking at once and tilts its head toward the deep water -- then carries on as if nothing happened. She wants someone sober to sit the room and mark exactly where they all turn to look.
dead_timber_inn_that_listens.offer: You'll think me mad, but sit my common room tonight and watch the hour past midnight. The whole room -- drinkers, dicers, my own girl -- goes quiet at once, and turns, all of them, toward the water, and holds a breath, and then picks up the song like it never dropped. Nobody remembers doing it. I've run inns forty years and I know when a room's listening to something that isn't in it. Sit. Watch. Tell me which way they lean.
dead_timber_inn_that_listens.progress: Take a seat in Marta's common room through the quiet hour past midnight and mark which way the drinkers turn.
dead_timber_inn_that_listens.complete: You saw it, then. Same hour, same lean -- toward the deep water, every last head, my girl's included, and her not two feet from me. Then the song came back and nobody the wiser. I keep a warm room, traveler. It's my whole trade and my whole pride. But you can keep a room warm and still not own what it listens to at midnight. Here's a bed on the house, and a lock on the door that won't help, but you'll feel better for turning it.

objectives.label: the inn's common room
objectives.text: Sit the common room through the quiet hour

dead_timber_inn_that_listens.note: At the same hour the whole common room went quiet and leaned toward the deep water, the innkeeper's own daughter among them, and none remembered. Marta keeps a warm room; she cannot own what it listens to at midnight. The lock on the door will not help. She turns it anyway.

dead_timber_leaning_cedar.title: Under the Leaning Cedar
dead_timber_leaning_cedar.summary: The great leaning cedar over the burial plot is the oldest thing standing in Dead Timber, and Stanca has found an inscription on its trunk that is getting shorter. She wants it watched and read -- carefully -- to learn what a fading inscription means.
dead_timber_leaning_cedar.offer: The leaning cedar's older than the drowning -- older than the port, older than the annex, the one thing here that was alive before it all went grey. There are marks cut in its trunk, low, mossed over. And I've measured them across a fortnight and they're getting shorter. Everything else in Draconia, the marks spread. This one shrinks. I daren't read it close alone. Stand under it, read what's there under the lantern, and tell me the color. Only the color. Not the words.
dead_timber_leaning_cedar.progress: Reach the leaning cedar over the burial plot and read the fading marks under honest light -- the color only, not the words.
dead_timber_leaning_cedar.complete: Green. The trunk read green -- thermal, a stone about to wake, except it's a tree, and it's not waking, it's warming, slow, from within. A shrinking inscription and a warming trunk. There's only one such mark in all the old tales: a place where someone, once, transmitted instead of read. Pushed something in instead of taking something out. I don't understand it. But I've copied the shape of it, small, in the margin of my will. It felt like the right place for a thing that gets shorter.

objectives.label: the leaning cedar
objectives.text: Read the fading marks on the leaning cedar

dead_timber_leaning_cedar.note: The cedar read green -- warming from within, its inscription shrinking where all others spread. In the old tales such a mark means someone once transmitted instead of read. Stanca copied its shape into the margin of her will, the right place for a thing that gets shorter.

dead_timber_annex_audit.title: The Auditor's Coming
dead_timber_annex_audit.summary: An Archive auditor is due to reconcile the annex's books, and every pending line Lenuta shielded -- the cracked tablet, the objected names, the barge crew -- is about to be found. She needs the deep-grove nodes disrupted so the discrepancies read as clerical error, not resistance.
dead_timber_annex_audit.offer: An auditor comes on the next tide. They'll walk my books line by line, and every mercy I've hidden -- the warm tablet marked pending, the names Stanca fought for, the barge crew 'collected en route' -- reads as sabotage to a clean eye. I need cover. Out in the deep grove there are anchor-stumps humming the count; break enough of them and the whole district's records go noisy, unreliable. Then my little lies look like rot in the system, not defiance in the clerk. Go make me some honest chaos.
dead_timber_annex_audit.progress: Break the anchor-stumps in the deep grove so the district's records read as damaged, hiding Lenuta's mercies.
dead_timber_annex_audit.complete: It's done -- the count's gone ragged across the whole ward, and the auditor will find noise where I left fingerprints. They'll blame the damp, the rot, the sheer age of the place. Nobody gets audited for a haunted forest. I've bought a year, maybe two, for a dozen people who'd otherwise be receipts by now. Don't mistake it for a victory. I didn't cancel a single debt. I just... smudged the ink, and hoped the smudge lasts longer than they do. Take the writ. It unfiles one name. Choose it when it matters.

objectives.label: the deep-grove anchor-stumps
objectives.text: Reach the humming anchor-stumps
Break the anchor-stumps' guardians

dead_timber_annex_audit.note: The count went ragged across the ward, and the auditor will blame the damp -- nobody audits a haunted forest. Lenuta bought a dozen people a year, maybe two. She cancelled no debt; she smudged the ink and hoped the smudge outlasts them. It is not a victory. She said so first.

dead_timber_first_edition.title: A First Edition of Nobody
dead_timber_first_edition.summary: A crate of 'first-edition draughts' has surfaced in the salvage -- reading-elixirs so potent one dose lets a person read a debt-tablet whole. Sanda won't sell them and won't destroy them alone. She wants them carried to Otilia, who knows how to drown a thing so it stays drowned.
dead_timber_first_edition.offer: Salvage turned up a crate I wish it hadn't. First-edition draughts -- the real thing, the potent brew, one swallow and you could read a tablet cover to cover. Understand a whole finalized life. There are men on this coast would kill for a case, and be dead of their own prize inside the hour. I'll not sell death dressed as knowledge. Carry them up to Otilia at the watch-post. She knows the tides. She knows how to sink a thing where even salvage won't find it.
dead_timber_first_edition.progress: Carry the crate of first-edition draughts to Otilia at the watch-post to be sunk beyond salvage. Do not open it.
dead_timber_first_edition.complete: Sealed the whole way -- I watched you not open it, and that's a rarer discipline than you'd think in a town of the curious. Otilia weighted the crate and put it off the deep shelf where the tide runs under the tide. Gone. You could've made a fortune, and you'd have made it into a corpse. There's coin in restraint, out here, though it never looks like coin. Here -- honest trade for an honest refusal. And a jar of light, because you'll want it going home.

objectives.label: the watch-post
objectives.text: Carry the crate to Otilia at the watch-post
Give Otilia the crate to sink, unopened

dead_timber_first_edition.note: The crate went off the deep shelf sealed, where the tide runs under the tide. A case of first-edition draughts would have made a fortune, and made it into a corpse. There is coin in restraint out here, though it never looks like coin. Sanda paid it in honest trade.

dead_timber_stag_the_last.title: The Last Green Thing
dead_timber_stag_the_last.summary: One hollow stag in all the dead groves still carries a living green in its eyes -- the last, the herders say, to have browsed the forest before it drowned. Ruxandra wants it left alone, and everything that hunts it driven off, so one green thing might outlast the grey.
dead_timber_stag_the_last.offer: There's a stag out past the far grove -- the old herders swear it's the last that fed on the forest when the forest was green, a thousand years gone, kept walking somehow by whatever keeps anything walking here. Its eyes are still green. Everything else in this wood is grey to the marrow. Hunters have started after it for the strangeness. Drive them off -- the crawlers, the risen, anything with teeth for it. Let one green thing outlast the grey. Just one. I'm past asking for more.
dead_timber_stag_the_last.progress: Drive off everything hunting the last green-eyed stag in the far grove. Let one green thing survive.
dead_timber_stag_the_last.complete: You cleared its ground and it's still out there, grey-coated and green-eyed, cropping moss off a drowned world. It won't last forever. Nothing does; that's not the tragedy, that's just the weather. The tragedy would be it ending because we couldn't leave one thing alone. My boy asked why I bothered. I told him: because everything good is temporary, and temporary is the only kind of good there is. He didn't understand. He will.

objectives.text: Drive the crawlers off the green-eyed stag's ground
Cull the grey stags crowding out the last green one

dead_timber_stag_the_last.note: The last green-eyed stag still crops moss off a drowned world, its ground cleared. It will not last -- but the tragedy would be it ending because no one could leave one thing alone. Everything good is temporary, Ruxandra told her son, and temporary is the only kind of good there is.

dead_timber_deadheart_near.title: The Organ in the Fog
dead_timber_deadheart_near.summary: Otilia has traced the orange transmission to its source at the drowned quarter's heart -- and understood it. Something down there wears a matured signal-stone as an organ, and it has been broadcasting the count that finalizes the district. She means to see it once, plainly, and needs the way cleared.
dead_timber_deadheart_near.offer: I've followed the orange to its root, and I've stood close enough to read what it is, and I'm going to tell you plainly because you've earned plain: at the heart of the drowned quarter something walks wearing the signal on its chest, an organ where a heart should be, broadcasting the count that closes this whole ward one soul at a time. I mean to see it. Once. Then I'll decide whether to report it or bury the report with me. Cut me the road. Everything between here and it wants me not to arrive.
dead_timber_deadheart_near.progress: Clear the road to the drowned quarter's heart so Otilia can look upon the source of the orange signal.
dead_timber_deadheart_near.complete: I saw it. I'll not describe it -- describing is halfway to understanding, and understanding is how the Courier died. It didn't attack. It doesn't need to; it finalizes, it doesn't fight. It looked at me the way a ledger looks at a name it hasn't reached yet. Patient. It has all the time there is. I'm not reporting this to the annex -- they'd send collectors, and collectors are what it eats. I'll watch it. Alone. Somebody has to, and I'm already half grey. Take the crown. Coldlight sees what warm light flatters over. You'll need to see clearly, where you're going. This town isn't the end of the road.

objectives.label: the drowned quarter's heart
objectives.text: Clear the road to the quarter's heart
Cut down the finalized standing guard

dead_timber_deadheart_near.note: At the drowned quarter's heart something walks wearing the signal as an organ, finalizing the ward one soul at a time. It did not fight; it looked at Otilia the way a ledger looks at a name it hasn't reached. She will watch it alone, being already half grey. She did not describe it. Describing is halfway to understanding.

dead_timber_daily_pilings.title: Daily: The Low-Water Cut
dead_timber_daily_pilings.summary: The tide goes out, the crawlers come up into the good stumps, and Mira's cutters won't work until the mud's cleared. It happens every low water. It will happen tomorrow. Clear the day's crawlers so the wharf keeps eating.
dead_timber_daily_pilings.offer: Low water again, crawlers in the stumps again, cutters on the quay with their arms folded again. It's the one reliable thing in Dead Timber: the tide goes out and the trouble comes up. Clear me the day's worth off the working stumps. I'd say it'll be the last time, but neither of us is that gullible. Same coin as yesterday. Same crawlers as tomorrow.
dead_timber_daily_pilings.progress: Clear the day's timber-crawlers off the working stumps at low water so the cutters can work.
dead_timber_daily_pilings.complete: Cleared, and the cutters have unfolded their arms and remembered they're paid to work. Till the next tide, anyway. There's a grim comfort in a trouble you can count on, friend. The annex's kind of trouble you never see coming. This kind comes up with the water, twice a day, dependable as grief. Here's your coin. Mind the mud on your way out.

objectives.text: Clear the day's crawlers off the working stumps

dead_timber_daily_pilings.note: The cutters unfolded their arms and went back to work, till the next tide. There is a grim comfort in a trouble you can count on. The annex's kind you never see coming; this kind comes up with the water, dependable as grief.

dead_timber_daily_salt_watch.title: Daily: Salt and Watch
dead_timber_daily_salt_watch.summary: The salt lines round the graves scuff away with every fog, and the risen test them nightly. Ecaterina relays the salt each morning; she needs the night's risen put down so the dead she loves stay dead and hers.
dead_timber_daily_salt_watch.offer: Fog every night, and every night it scuffs the salt thin and something out there tests the lines. I relay them each morning on my knees -- it's my whole religion now, salt and kneeling. But I can't put down what rises while I'm laying it. Walk the plot for me. Put down the night's risen. Let me salt in peace, and let my mother stay my mother one more day.
dead_timber_daily_salt_watch.progress: Put down the night's risen at the burial plot so Ecaterina can relay the salt lines in peace.
dead_timber_daily_salt_watch.complete: Down again, and I've salted round every stone afresh, hers the twice-over as always. Thank you. It's the same work tomorrow, and the day after, until it's me under the salt and someone kneeling for my line. That's not despair, mind. That's just the shape of caring for the dead in a place that would rather file them. See you at dawn. There's always dawn work, here.

objectives.text: Put down the night's risen at the burial plot

dead_timber_daily_salt_watch.note: The night's risen are down and the salt relaid, the mother's line twice-over. It is the same work tomorrow and after, until it is Ecaterina under the salt and another kneeling for her line. Not despair -- just the shape of caring for the dead where they would rather be filed.

dead_timber_hungry_grove.title: The Grove That Fed
dead_timber_hungry_grove.summary: A stand of hollow stags has grown fat and slow feeding on something in the west grove, and Voica has found what: a mass grave the tide uncovered, salvagers the annex never logged. She wants the grove cleared and the ground marked before the annex covers it again.
dead_timber_hungry_grove.offer: The stags in the west grove are fat -- fat, in a dead wood, which makes no sense until you see what they're feeding on. The tide pulled the mud back off a pit. Bodies. Salvagers, by the boots, layered deep, and not one of them in any annex roll I've seen. Somebody's been dumping the collected where the count won't show. Clear the grove -- the stags, the crawlers gorging down there -- so I can mark the ground before the next tide hides it and the annex swears it never was.
dead_timber_hungry_grove.progress: Clear the west grove of the gorging beasts so Voica can mark the unlogged grave before the tide hides it.
dead_timber_hungry_grove.complete: Cleared, and I've driven stakes at the four corners and cut the count into a stake-head: fifty-one that I could tell apart, likely more below. Fifty-one people the annex fed to the mud and left off the books, because a collected soul with no grave is a debt closed with no cost. I can't unbury them. I can make them count. One warden's tally against a whole clean ledger. It's not much. It's a stake in the ground, which is more than they gave these fifty-one.

objectives.text: Clear the gorging stags from the west grove
Clear the crawlers gorging in the pit

dead_timber_hungry_grove.note: The tide uncovered fifty-one unlogged dead, salvagers the annex fed to the mud and left off the books -- a debt closed with no cost. Voica staked the corners and cut the tally into a stake-head. She could not unbury them. She made them count. It is a stake in the ground, which is more than they got.

morven_reach_locked_door_1.title: The Locked Door
morven_reach_locked_door_1.summary: The Morven keep a door in the reach that has stayed shut for a thousand years, and the marsh has begun to read orange around it. The watcher who guards it wants a stranger's eyes, because his own have learned to look away.
morven_reach_locked_door_1.offer: You came up the causeway, which means the reach let you. Good. I keep a door that has not opened since the Archive was still kind, and lately the water at its sill runs the wrong color. Walk the black-glass verge with the reading-still and tell me what the ground is doing. I have watched it too long to see it.
morven_reach_locked_door_1.progress: The reading-still shows a live stone as shifting orange. Walk the verge where the causeway meets the sunk wall and read the sill of the locked door.
morven_reach_locked_door_1.complete: Orange. You are sure. So am I, and I have been sure for a season and told no one, because telling is filing and filing is what we do here. The door is not opening from outside. It is being read from within. Keep the lens. You will want to see what asks to be let out.

objectives.label: the sunk wall verge
objectives.text: Read the verge at the locked door
Bring the reading to Clerk Profira

morven_reach_locked_door_1.note: The door has not opened in a thousand years. The watcher swore its sill ran orange, then swore he had said nothing. Both were true. In the reach, a thing can be known and unfiled at once.

morven_reach_poor_bastards_2.title: Poor Bastards
morven_reach_poor_bastards_2.summary: A district of the reach has gone quiet in the neat way the finalized go quiet. The Morven clerk wants their debt-anchors read before the Wolfarin lenders come to collect what is already, in the ledger's word, closed.
morven_reach_poor_bastards_2.offer: Two words I am not supposed to feel, and I feel them every day: poor bastards. A whole row of houses stopped talking last fortnight. Not dead. Filed. The lenders will be along to collect on paper what is already closed in fact, and I would like, once, to know a name before it is only an entry. Read the anchors. Bring me one that still cracks.
morven_reach_poor_bastards_2.progress: The finalized houses lie along the drowned row past the toll-post. Read their debt-anchors and recover one that is hairline-cracked, not yet closed.
morven_reach_poor_bastards_2.complete: Still warm. Hairline. Not quite settled. There is a person under this stone and the ledger has not finished with them, which means the ledger can be argued with. That is the whole reason our order still bothers to breathe. Keep the writ-blank. A thing that can be filed can be un-filed.

objectives.label: the drowned row
objectives.text: Reach the finalized district
Recover an anchor still cracked

morven_reach_poor_bastards_2.note: The clerk read a name off a warm stone and said it aloud once, quietly, against every rule of her house. It is the oldest work the Morven do. It has never once been enough. She keeps doing it.

morven_reach_the_reading_3.title: What the Water Reads Back
morven_reach_the_reading_3.summary: The locked door's sill answers the alchemical sight now, and the answer is addressed. The watcher asks you to stand the last vigil at the door and hear what a thousand-year silence has decided to say to the one who has been reading it.
morven_reach_the_reading_3.offer: You read the sill and it began reading you. That is how it works — attention is a door swung both ways. The still shows orange steady now, not flickering. It has stopped asking whether anyone is there. Stand the vigil at the door tonight and do not answer what it says. Whatever it names you, it will be a deed of yours, and it will be true. That is the trap. Truth is the bait here.
morven_reach_the_reading_3.progress: Hold the watch at the locked door after dark. Do not speak. Read the sill until the reading finishes, and come away.
morven_reach_the_reading_3.complete: It knew what you carried in. It always knows the deeds and never the name — and it offered, in that flat clerk's patience, to take the weight and file you somewhere warm and closed. You looked at your boots. You walked out. That refusal is the only currency the reach still mints. Keep the veil. You will need to not be read again.

objectives.label: the locked door
objectives.text: Stand the vigil at the locked door
Silence what sings from the sill

morven_reach_the_reading_3.note: The door did not open. It spoke instead — deeds, never a name, and an offer of rest phrased as bookkeeping. The refusal was logged. In the reach, even saying no is an entry. The difference is whose.

morven_reach_managed_blankness.title: Managed Blankness
morven_reach_managed_blankness.summary: Every Morven learns to keep a face that gives the walls nothing. The clerk needs a dossier of a lender's habits assembled — not to strike him, but to know him well enough to be safe from him.
morven_reach_managed_blankness.offer: The first thing we teach is the face — the blank one, the one the listening-walls learn nothing from. The second thing is patience. There is a Wolfarin man working the piers who reads faces for a living, and I would like to read his first. Watch where he eats, where he waits, whom he does not look at. Bring me his habits. Architecture, not assumptions.
morven_reach_managed_blankness.progress: Follow the lender's routine along the piers and the toll-post. Note his habits and assemble the dossier.
morven_reach_managed_blankness.complete: There. Now he is a shape I understand and not a threat I fear, which is the only safety this house ever sells. He takes his tea where a dead man used to. He waits for no one, and looks at no children, which tells me he was taught to. We are not so different, his order and mine. We just file people to different ends.

objectives.label: the lender's pier-round
objectives.text: Trail the lender's routine
Assemble the dossier of habits

morven_reach_managed_blankness.note: The dossier is thin and exact. It says nothing the lender would deny and everything he would rather not be known. The clerk filed it where the walls could not hear, which is a corner she has kept empty for years.

morven_reach_the_chalk_hand.title: The Chalk Hand
morven_reach_the_chalk_hand.summary: On a canal wall a child has pressed a chalk handprint, the old warding-mark from a kinder age, and a Morven has drawn a surveillance-ring around it. A washerwoman wants to know if the ring protects it or catalogues it before the rain takes both.
morven_reach_the_chalk_hand.offer: There's a little chalk hand on the north canal wall — the old kind, the ward my grandmother's grandmother pressed for children. Someone from the House drew one of their careful rings around it. I can't tell if they're keeping it safe or keeping it on file. It'll rain by dark and I need to know which before it's gone. Go and look, and tell me true.
morven_reach_the_chalk_hand.progress: The chalk handprint is on the north canal wall, low, where a child could reach. Read the mark and the ring the Morven drew around it.
morven_reach_the_chalk_hand.complete: Protecting it. You're sure. ...Good. Someone in that cold house looked at a child's hand and could not file it, only fence it round and leave it be. That's not much. But it's the most anyone's spared a warm thing in this reach in years. Take the ward-sign. Press it somewhere a child walks.

objectives.label: the north canal wall
objectives.text: Find the chalk handprint

morven_reach_the_chalk_hand.note: The ring around the little hand was drawn to protect, not to file. The Morven who drew it does not know that themselves. In the reach, tenderness survives only when it can pass for surveillance.

morven_reach_deposits_in_the_canal.title: Deposits
morven_reach_deposits_in_the_canal.summary: The canals of the reach are a disposal as much as a road; the things that sink do not always stay sunk. Something is nesting among the deposits and dragging the tally off, and the dockmaster wants it thinned before the count stops adding up.
morven_reach_deposits_in_the_canal.offer: In this reach the canal is where the ledger keeps what it's done with. Bodies are deposits, we call them — polite, aren't we. Lately something lives down among them and moves them, and a moved deposit is an entry that won't reconcile. I keep an honest tally on a dishonest water. Cull the lurkers before my count and the canal's count disagree, because when they disagree, someone comes to settle it.
morven_reach_deposits_in_the_canal.progress: The canal-lurkers nest among the sunk deposits below the grey piers. Thin them before the tally drifts.
morven_reach_deposits_in_the_canal.complete: The count holds. For now. You'll have noticed the ones you pulled up had swallowed coins, tokens, a name-tablet or two — the canal's little economy, feeding on what the ledger threw away. Even the drowned turn a profit here. Take your due off the top. I'll not enter where it came from.

objectives.text: Cull the canal-lurkers among the deposits

morven_reach_deposits_in_the_canal.note: The lurkers fed on what the ledger discarded, coins and name-tablets both. The dockmaster balanced her tally and asked nothing. A canal that eats the finalized is still, she said, cheaper than a lender.

morven_reach_the_ledger_drake.title: The Thing That Guards the Books
morven_reach_the_ledger_drake.summary: A ledger-drake has coiled itself around the reach's debt-annex, and it does not hoard gold — it hoards accounts, and it kills to keep a balance from being questioned. A Morven guard needs it broken to reach the books that should have closed.
morven_reach_the_ledger_drake.offer: There's a drake in the annex, and it guards no treasure you'd recognize. It guards the books. The stone-record grew a keeper — a thing that reads a settled account as its own body and takes it hard when you argue. We have files in there that closed on people who did nothing but owe. I want them read. First we get past the drake. I've lost two who tried it politely. Don't be polite.
morven_reach_the_ledger_drake.progress: The ledger-drake coils in the debt-annex past the sunk hall. Break it and clear the way to the closed books.
morven_reach_the_ledger_drake.complete: It bled ink, or something the color of it. Under it, the annex — shelf on shelf of lives closed for the crime of owing. My grandmother is in here somewhere, filed, and I mean to read her back into being a person and not a balance. You did the hard part. The rest is paperwork, which in this house is the crueler half. Take the reaver. It's honest steel in a place with none.

objectives.text: Break the ledger-drake in the annex

morven_reach_the_ledger_drake.note: The drake grew from a closed account and defended it like a body. Beneath its coils, the reach's cruelest shelf: the finalized, filed for owing. The guard began reading names. Reading, here, is the slow undoing of murder.

morven_reach_the_sirens_count.title: The Siren's Count
morven_reach_the_sirens_count.summary: A Morven siren sings the three converging notes across the marsh at dusk, and the incurious walk out to it and do not walk back. A guard wants the singing stopped before the reach loses another who only meant to listen a moment.
morven_reach_the_sirens_count.offer: You'll hear her at dusk — three notes, always the same three, closer together each night than the last. The reach calls her a siren. She's a medium, is what she is: something borrows her throat to sing the countdown, and the folk who've stopped being curious walk out to hear it clearer and don't come home. I've a cousin out there in the reeds this minute, listening. End the song. I'll go and see if he still knows me.
morven_reach_the_sirens_count.progress: The siren sings from the reed-drowned shrine at the marsh's heart. Silence her before dusk takes another listener.
morven_reach_the_sirens_count.complete: Quiet. Real quiet, the kind that used to be ordinary. My cousin turned around when the notes stopped and knew my face for a breath — then asked why he was standing in cold water at night. He doesn't remember walking out. Small mercy: he'll remember nothing. Take the counteragent. Some nights the song gets into your own head, and it helps to have it.

objectives.text: Silence the singing in the reeds
See if the listener knows his own kin

morven_reach_the_sirens_count.note: The three notes came a little closer together than the night before. The guard silenced the throat that carried them and got her cousin back short one evening. The song will find another mouth. It always has. The notes keep converging.

morven_reach_stamped_unstamped.title: Stamped, Un-stamped, Stamped
morven_reach_stamped_unstamped.summary: In the records office one account near the bottom of a page is stamped collected, then un-stamped, then stamped again, the violet ink smeared where a dead hand kept refusing the verdict. The clerk wants to know whose hand, and whether it still refuses.
morven_reach_stamped_unstamped.offer: There is a line in the finalized ledger that will not settle. Stamped collected. Then the stamp scratched out. Then stamped again — and the ink's smeared, like someone kept reaching across the page to un-write their own ending. A dead someone. I want the anchor-stone that line points to, and I want to know if the hand is still arguing. Bring me the ledger it came from, whole.
morven_reach_stamped_unstamped.progress: The disputed account traces to a debt-anchor in the flooded records office. Recover the ledger of the drowned quarter and read the smeared line.
morven_reach_stamped_unstamped.complete: Look at it. Stamped, un-stamped, stamped — a whole soul's worth of refusing, pressed into violet ink and mud. They keep reaching up out of the water to cross out the word 'collected,' and the machine keeps reaching down to write it back. Neither wins. That's the reach: a place where even the dead file appeals. Keep the ledger. Somewhere in it is a hand that hasn't given up. Rare thing.

objectives.text: Recover the disputed ledger

morven_reach_stamped_unstamped.note: The line was stamped, un-stamped, and stamped again, a dead hand refusing its own closing. The clerk kept the ledger. She could not un-write the verdict. But a machine that has to keep re-writing a thing has not, she noted, finished losing.

morven_reach_the_toll_forgery.title: A Receipt for Passage
morven_reach_the_toll_forgery.summary: The toll-posts of the reach take payment in the one coin nobody can hide — your own filed existence. A merchant who would rather stay unfiled needs a forged toll-receipt run to a fellow trader before the honest ledger notices the gap.
morven_reach_the_toll_forgery.offer: In this reach the toll isn't paid in coin, friend, it's paid in being written down — cross a post and you're an entry, and entries mature into debts, and debts mature into you-not-walking-anymore. So we forge. A clean receipt says the toll's already paid and the ledger, bless its literal little heart, believes paper over eyes. Run this to Marioara at the piers before the count reconciles. Quick and quiet.
morven_reach_the_toll_forgery.progress: Carry the forged toll-receipt to Dockmaster Marioara at the grey piers before the ledger reconciles the gap.
morven_reach_the_toll_forgery.complete: Delivered, and the count balanced on paper that never happened. There's a joke in it: the whole machine that's eating this world runs on trusting the record over the truth, so the record is exactly where you lie to it. Won't save anyone forever. Saves us tonight. Take a cut and don't get caught carrying the next one — they hang forgers, when they can be bothered to file the rope.

objectives.text: Collect the forged receipt
Deliver the receipt to the dockmaster

morven_reach_the_toll_forgery.note: The forged receipt balanced a toll that was never paid, because the ledger trusts paper over eyes. It buys a night, not a life. The merchant knew the difference and sold the night anyway. Everything good is temporary. That is what makes it good.

morven_reach_deepbed_lantern.title: Light for the Deep Bed
morven_reach_deepbed_lantern.summary: The reach's smith casts lanterns that burn under water, for divers who go down into the sunk quarter to bring the drowned home for burial rather than leave them as deposits. She needs black-glass to finish the next batch before the burying-day.
morven_reach_deepbed_lantern.offer: I make a lantern that burns in the deep bed — down where the streets flooded and the folk went with them. There are divers who go under to bring people up for a proper grave instead of leaving them as the canal's bookkeeping. Poor work, holy work. I'm short the black-glass that makes the lens. Bring me a good measure off the verge and I'll have light for the next burying-day.
morven_reach_deepbed_lantern.progress: Black-glass litters the sunk-wall verge where the old streets shattered. Gather a measure for the deep-bed lanterns.
morven_reach_deepbed_lantern.complete: That'll do. Enough lens for three lanterns, three divers, however many souls they can carry up before the light gives out. We can't un-drown the quarter. We can bring its people home a few at a time and put them under earth with a name, which the ledger hates and I love. Take a lantern of your own. The reach is dark in the low places, and getting darker.

objectives.text: Gather black-glass from the verge

morven_reach_deepbed_lantern.note: The smith's lanterns burn under water so the divers can bring the drowned home to earth and a name. It is slow, and it is little, and the canal fills faster than they empty it. She lights the next batch anyway. Some work is worth doing precisely because it cannot win.

morven_reach_the_incurious.title: The Incurious
morven_reach_the_incurious.summary: Guests at the reach's inn have stopped asking for anything — not a room, not a meal, only to sit where they can hear the marsh. The innkeeper wants to know what has taken the wanting out of them before her whole common-room goes quiet.
morven_reach_the_incurious.offer: My trade is people who want things — a bed, a bowl, a bit of talk. Lately I've a common-room full of folk who want nothing at all. They come in, they sit facing the reeds, they don't order, they don't leave. Listening, my girl calls it. I call it bad for custom and worse for my nerves. Go out to where they're all pointed and find what's pulled the wanting out of them. I'll hold the door.
morven_reach_the_incurious.progress: The incurious guests all face the drowned shrine in the eastern reeds. Reach it and read what stands there.
morven_reach_the_incurious.complete: A stone, you say, singing the wrong three notes, and it reads orange in the still. So that's what my quiet room is bent toward. I've thrown out the mark it left on my hearthstone and salted the sill. They'll want their beds and their bowls again by morning, most of them. Most. Take a room on the house, and a warding-sign, and don't you go incurious on me. I like my guests greedy.

objectives.label: the drowned shrine
objectives.text: Read the shrine the incurious face

morven_reach_the_incurious.note: The incurious all faced one stone, and the stone read orange. The innkeeper salted her sills and got most of her guests back to wanting things by morning. Most. She did not count the ones who stayed quiet. Counting, in the reach, is how you catch it.

morven_reach_marta_stick_echo.title: The Smallest Thing Kept
morven_reach_marta_stick_echo.summary: The reach's chronicler keeps the old creed the Archive was founded on — that a small, imperfect, mortal thing is worth more than perfection. She wants one such thing recovered from a finalized house before the lenders file the whole street to nothing.
morven_reach_marta_stick_echo.offer: A thousand years back, the Archive began with a walking-stick — an herb-woman's, worth nothing, kept because it meant something to one person. That was the whole idea, before the idea rotted. There's a finalized house on the drowned row with a keepsake still in it — a cord, a child's, I'm told. The lenders will file the street by week's end and the keepsake with it, closed and gone. Bring it out. I'll enter it as worth more than the ledger it defies.
morven_reach_marta_stick_echo.progress: The keepsake lies in a finalized house on the drowned row. Recover it before the lenders close the street.
morven_reach_marta_stick_echo.complete: A knotted cord, a child's work, worth nothing and everything. This is the vote the Archive forgot it was casting — the specific, imperfect, mortal thing against the perfect cold record. I'll keep it. Not file it. There's a difference, and this whole reach has forgotten it, and I have made keeping that difference alive my one job. Take the keepsake's twin. Give it to someone small.

objectives.text: Recover the child's keepsake
Enter it against the ledger

morven_reach_marta_stick_echo.note: A knotted cord, a child's, worth nothing and kept anyway. The chronicler entered it as worth more than the ledger that would have closed it. It is the whole founding creed of the Archive, surviving in one stubborn woman a thousand years after the Archive forgot it.

morven_reach_watchers_relief.title: The Watcher's Relief
morven_reach_watchers_relief.summary: Someone must walk the sunk-wall verge each dawn and read whether the locked door's sill has warmed in the night. The watcher's eyes have learned to slide off it; he pays a stranger to look straight.
morven_reach_watchers_relief.offer: Every dawn the same errand, and I can no longer do it — my eyes have gone kind to that door, they slide off the sill like it isn't there. That's the sickness of watching one thing too long. Walk the verge for me. Read the sill with the still. Tell me plainly whether it warmed in the night. Coin for it, and my thanks, which is worth less but I mean it more.
morven_reach_watchers_relief.progress: Read the locked door's sill at the sunk-wall verge and report whether it has warmed since dusk.
morven_reach_watchers_relief.complete: Warmer by a hair, or the same? ...The same. Good. A day the door does not gain on us is a day. That is all we get out here — days, counted, one refused entry at a time. Come at dawn tomorrow. It will need reading again. It always needs reading again.

objectives.label: the sunk-wall verge
objectives.text: Read the door's sill at dawn

morven_reach_watchers_relief.note: The sill was read and the door had not gained on the reach in the night. The watcher counted it a good day, which in the reach means only that it was a day. He will need it read again at dawn. He always will.

morven_reach_reconcile_the_tally.title: Reconcile the Tally
morven_reach_reconcile_the_tally.summary: The canal's count of its deposits never quite matches the dockmaster's, because things move down there. Each day the lurkers must be thinned to keep the two numbers close enough that no one comes to settle the difference.
morven_reach_reconcile_the_tally.offer: Honest work, dishonest water. My tally of what's sunk in the canal and the canal's own tally never agree, because the lurkers shift the deposits around like they're rearranging a larder. When the numbers drift too far, a Wolfarin man comes to reconcile them, and you do not want to be standing near when he does. Thin the lurkers. Keep my count and the water's count close. That's the whole job, dawn to dark.
morven_reach_reconcile_the_tally.progress: Thin the canal-lurkers below the piers to keep the deposit-count from drifting.
morven_reach_reconcile_the_tally.complete: Close enough. The numbers won't reconcile perfectly — nothing in this reach does — but close enough that no one comes to settle it, and close enough is the reach's whole religion. Your cut, off the top, undeclared. Come back tomorrow. The water never stays counted.

objectives.text: Thin the lurkers to hold the count

morven_reach_reconcile_the_tally.note: The counts held close enough that no one came to settle the difference. In the reach, close enough is not a compromise but a creed: perfection is the stone's, and the stone's perfection is the enemy. The dockmaster keeps her books honestly imperfect on purpose.

morven_reach_the_diver.title: Down to the Sunk Streets
morven_reach_the_diver.summary: A diver went down into the flooded quarter with a deep-bed lantern to bring her father home for burial and has not surfaced. Her sister wants someone to go down the sunk stair and learn whether he is coming up.
morven_reach_the_diver.offer: My sister took a lantern and went down the drowned stair to bring our father up — he's been a deposit in that cold water three years and he deserves earth. She said an hour. It's been a day. I can't swim and I can't stop shaking. Go down the sunk stair. If she's alive, bring her up. If she's not... light a lantern for her too. I'll dig the ground for whoever you can carry.
morven_reach_the_diver.progress: The diver went down the sunk stair into the flooded streets of the old quarter. Reach the stair-foot and learn her fate.
morven_reach_the_diver.complete: She's alive. Cold to the bone and empty-handed — the current took our father deeper than her light would reach — but alive, and up, and here. We'll not get him now. The canal keeps what it's owed. But I've got my sister and that's a trade the ledger never offered me and I'll take it weeping. Bless you. Take the lantern-oil. Someone else's stair needs lighting.

objectives.label: the sunk stair-foot
objectives.text: Reach the diver at the stair-foot
Bring word to her sister

morven_reach_the_diver.note: The diver came up alive and empty-handed; the canal kept the father it was owed. Her sister took the trade weeping — a living sister for a drowned father un-buried. The ledger never offered such trades. The reach's people make them anyway, in the dark, by lantern.

morven_reach_the_thinning_veil.title: Where the World Wears Thin
morven_reach_the_thinning_veil.summary: At the marsh's heart the reach reads orange no matter where you stand, and the fog itself thins until the far side of the world seems to press through. The chronicler wants it mapped before the thin place spreads to the causeway.
morven_reach_the_thinning_veil.offer: There is a place in the deep marsh where the alchemical sight reads orange whichever way you turn — not a stone, the whole air. The fog thins there until you can nearly see through to somewhere that should be a thousand years off. The thinning is spreading, slow, toward the causeway and the living reach. I need it walked and marked while a person still can. Take the veil. It keeps the thin place from reading you while you read it.
morven_reach_the_thinning_veil.progress: The thin place lies at the marsh's orange heart, past the drowned shrine. Reach it, mark its edge, and come away.
morven_reach_the_thinning_veil.complete: You marked it. Good — and you came back, which not all do. The thinning's crept a boat-length toward the causeway since last I dared it. The reach is the drowned future the map is growing into, they say, and here the future is pressing back through the fog to meet us early. We cannot stop it. We can measure how fast, and warn the causeway, and keep the light on. Keep the veil. You'll walk that heart again.

objectives.label: the thin place
objectives.text: Reach the marsh's orange heart

morven_reach_the_thinning_veil.note: The thin place read orange in every direction and had crept a boat-length nearer the living reach. The chronicler marked its speed and warned the causeway. She could not stop the future from pressing through. She could count how fast it came. In the reach, counting is the last defiance.

morven_reach_a_name_for_the_shroud.title: A Name for the Shroud
morven_reach_a_name_for_the_shroud.summary: The washerwomen of the reach sew shrouds for the drowned they bring up, and they will not put a body in the ground unnamed. One shroud waits on a name only a Morven's files can supply.
morven_reach_a_name_for_the_shroud.offer: We sew the shrouds, us washerwomen, for the folk the divers bring up. And we won't bury a soul unnamed — a nameless grave is just the canal's work finished on dry land, and we'll not do the ledger's job for it. There's one waiting, a man, no name we know. The House keeps files that could tell us who he was. Go and ask the clerk, gentle-like. Bring back a name to stitch into the shroud, and we'll put him under proper.
morven_reach_a_name_for_the_shroud.progress: Ask Clerk Profira to search the reach's files for the drowned man's name, then bring it to the shroud.
morven_reach_a_name_for_the_shroud.complete: Andrei. His name was Andrei, and he mended nets, the clerk's file said, and owed eleven coins to a lender who filed him for it. Eleven coins. We'll stitch 'Andrei, who mended nets' into the linen and put the lender's number nowhere. Under he goes, a person, not a balance. That's the whole fight, love, and we win it one shroud at a time. Take the linen-ward. Wrap something you'd hate to lose unnamed.

objectives.text: Ask the clerk for the drowned man's name
Bring the name to the shroud

morven_reach_a_name_for_the_shroud.note: His name was Andrei; he mended nets and was filed for owing eleven coins. The washerwomen stitched the man into the shroud and left the number out. A nameless grave, they hold, is only the canal's work finished on land. They win the fight one name at a time. It is the only way it is ever won.

morven_reach_the_still_market.title: The Still Market
morven_reach_the_still_market.summary: The reach's market has gone still in the finalized way — the stalls tended by people who move like closed files, giving no price and no greeting. A merchant wants proof of what her competitors have become before she is the last one left saying good morning.
morven_reach_the_still_market.offer: Half the market's gone still on me. The other stallholders — they stand their pitches, they mind their goods, but there's no price out of them, no haggle, no good morning. Finalized, the word is, though nobody'll say it in daylight. I want proof of it, a token off one of them, so the House will act before I'm the last trader in this reach who still bothers to sell. Go among them. Take something one of them won't miss. They miss nothing now.
morven_reach_the_still_market.progress: The finalized stallholders keep their pitches in the still market by the toll-post. Recover a kept-name token from one to prove what they've become.
morven_reach_the_still_market.complete: This token — it's an anchor, a debt-anchor, warm still. So that's what's minding the vegetable stall: a person collected on their feet, kept working because the ledger sees no reason to stop them. I'll take this to the clerk. Not that the House can undo it. But someone ought to enter that the market died standing up, still making change. Take your due. And say good morning to me tomorrow. I need to hear it back.

objectives.text: Take a token from the finalized
Bring the proof to the merchant

morven_reach_the_still_market.note: The still market's stallholders were collected on their feet, kept working because the ledger saw no reason to stop them. The merchant filed proof and asked to be told good morning, once, aloud. The market died standing up, still making change. In the reach, the horror is that nothing dramatic happens at all.

morven_reach_the_last_haggle.title: The Last Haggle
morven_reach_the_last_haggle.summary: The merchant runs the last stall in the reach that still bothers to argue over a price, and she will pay a small purse each day to anyone who comes and haggles her down properly, just to keep the sound of trade alive in a market gone quiet.
morven_reach_the_last_haggle.offer: Here's a strange errand, and I'll not pretend it isn't: come and haggle me. Argue a price down, loud and stubborn, the way it used to be done before the stalls went still. I'll pay you for the trouble. It's the sound I'm after — the sound of two people who both still want things, disagreeing about how much. In this reach that's the rarest music there is. Go on. Insult my prices. Please.
morven_reach_the_last_haggle.progress: Come to the merchant's stall in the still market and haggle her down, loud and proper.
morven_reach_the_last_haggle.complete: Robbery! Daylight robbery, and I loved every word of it. There — a purse for the noise you made. My father used to say a market's alive as long as somebody's shouting that a thing's too dear. By that measure this stall lived one more day, thanks to you. Come back tomorrow and rob me again. I'll have new prices for you to hate.

objectives.text: Haggle the merchant down properly

morven_reach_the_last_haggle.note: The merchant paid a stranger to argue with her, just to hear the sound of two people who still wanted things. Her father held a market lived as long as someone shouted that a price was too dear. By that measure the stall lived one more day. It is a small defiance. In the still reach, it is nearly a riot.

morven_reach_wolfarin_ledger.title: The Lender's Book
morven_reach_wolfarin_ledger.summary: A Wolfarin lender keeps a private book of who in the reach owes and who is nearly collectible, and the Morven guard wants it copied before he acts — to warn the marked, or, she admits, to know who to abandon first.
morven_reach_wolfarin_ledger.offer: The lender carries a book — not the House's ledger, his own, the one where he keeps who's nearly ripe to collect. I want it read and copied before he moves. To warn folk, I tell myself. And, if I'm honest, to know which of us he'll take first, so I can decide who I'll spend my last favors on and who I'll let go. That's the coward's arithmetic and I do it every night. The drake guards the annex where he lodges. You know the way.
morven_reach_wolfarin_ledger.progress: The lender's private book is lodged in the debt-annex the ledger-drake guards. Copy it and bring the dossier back.
morven_reach_wolfarin_ledger.complete: Twenty-three names, ranked by how close to collectible. Four I can warn. Nine I can maybe hide. The rest... the rest I've just learned to grieve early, which is the reach teaching me its one lesson again: you cannot save everyone, so choose, and carry the choosing. Keep the dossier's twin. Warn who you can on your road. It won't be enough. Do it anyway. That's the House's whole creed, under the cold.

objectives.text: Break past the drake in the annex
Copy the lender's private book

morven_reach_wolfarin_ledger.note: The lender's book ranked twenty-three lives by nearness to collection. The guard could warn four, hide nine, and learned to grieve the rest early. You cannot save everyone, so choose, and carry the choosing. It is the reach's one lesson, and the Morven's whole creed under the cold.

morven_reach_first_edition.title: The First Edition
morven_reach_first_edition.summary: The reach's smith moonlights in Hessik's alchemy and needs the first pressing of a reading-draught tested — the one that lets an eye see the signal without letting the signal see the eye. She needs a steady hand to drink it at the verge and report.
morven_reach_first_edition.offer: I dabble in the old alchemist's method, Hessik's craft — sight-work, reading the signal. I've pressed a first edition of a reading-draught that's meant to let you see the orange without the orange seeing you back. Meant to. I've not dared drink it myself, and I'll not ask a friend to be my first cup. But you keep walking back from that door, so your stomach's stronger than mine. Drink it at the verge. Tell me if it holds.
morven_reach_first_edition.progress: Drink the first-edition draught at the sunk-wall verge, read the door's sill, then report whether the sight held clean.
morven_reach_first_edition.complete: It held. You read the sill and the sill didn't read you — no address, no offer, just the color plain. That's worth more than gold in this reach, a way to look without being looked at. First editions are always half-poison, mind, so I'll not press many. But I'll press enough for the divers and the watcher and you. Take one. Look, and stay unlooked-at. It's the only safe curiosity there is.

objectives.label: the sunk-wall verge
objectives.text: Drink the draught and read the sill
Report whether the sight held clean

morven_reach_first_edition.note: The draught let an eye read the signal without the signal reading back — a way to look without being looked at, worth more than gold in the reach. First editions are always half-poison. The smith presses only enough for the ones who must look, and no more. Curiosity, made survivable, one careful cup at a time.

morven_reach_the_hessik_method.title: The Method Itself
morven_reach_the_hessik_method.summary: A page of Hessik's own method survives in a drowned records office, the alchemy that first taught Draconia to read the signal by its colors. The chronicler wants it recovered before the water dissolves the one craft that lets people see the enemy at all.
morven_reach_the_hessik_method.offer: Everything we know of reading the signal — blue for the dead, orange for the live stone — comes down from one alchemist's method, Hessik's, set down before the Archive rotted. A page of it survives in the flooded records office, ink running a little more with every tide. Recover it. Without the method we are blind to the very thing that is eating this reach, and blindness, in the end, is how it wins. Go carefully. The water guards its books.
morven_reach_the_hessik_method.progress: The surviving page of Hessik's method lies in the flooded records office. Recover it before the tide dissolves it.
morven_reach_the_hessik_method.complete: The ink held, most of it — the colors, the readings, the master tell that a live stone writes itself into orange. This is the one craft that lets a person see the enemy instead of walking blind into its warmth. I'll copy it a hundred times and scatter the copies where water can't reach them all at once. That's how you keep a thing: not in one perfect vault, but in a hundred imperfect hands. Take a copy. Read the world true.

objectives.label: the flooded records office
objectives.text: Reach the drowned records office
Recover the page of Hessik's method

morven_reach_the_hessik_method.note: Hessik's method — the craft that reads the signal by its colors — was recovered from running ink and copied a hundred times over. You keep a thing not in one perfect vault but in a hundred imperfect hands. The chronicler scattered the copies where no single tide could take them all. Imperfect, distributed, mortal: the Archive's forgotten creed, kept alive as method.

morven_reach_the_quiet_district.title: The Quiet District
morven_reach_the_quiet_district.summary: A whole district of the reach has gone silent overnight — not burned, not emptied, simply finalized, its doors shut and its people moving like closed files. A guard needs it confirmed and its edge held before the quiet spreads to the living streets.
morven_reach_the_quiet_district.offer: A district went out last night. Not a fire, not a raid — quiet. The doors are shut, the folk are in, and they move like files that closed. Collected, the whole street at once, the way a lender forecloses on a village and nobody declares it. That's how you know it's the real thing: the end of the world doesn't ask permission, it just processes the queue. Go and confirm it. Read the anchors on the district edge. I have to know how fast it's coming.
morven_reach_the_quiet_district.progress: The finalized district lies beyond the toll-post, doors shut. Reach its edge and read the debt-anchors to confirm the collection.
morven_reach_the_quiet_district.complete: Confirmed. A street collected in a night, no army, no war, just paperwork maturing into erasure. And the anchors on the edge are warming, which means the quiet's not done spreading. I'll set a line here — salt, wards, watchers — and hold the edge as long as bodies last. We can't reverse a finalized district. We can slow the queue. In this reach, slowing it is the whole of victory. Take the line-pouch. Help me hold the edge.

objectives.label: the finalized district edge
objectives.text: Read the edge of the quiet district

morven_reach_the_quiet_district.note: A whole district went quiet in a night — collected, finalized, no army and no declaration, just the queue processed. The guard set a salt line and held the edge as long as bodies would last. You cannot reverse a finalized street. You can slow the queue. In the reach, slowing it is the whole of victory.

morven_reach_soothing_the_watch.title: Something to Hold
morven_reach_soothing_the_watch.summary: The reach's watchers and divers come off their shifts shaking, and the innkeeper ties soothing-knots for their hands to hold through the night. She's run short of the marsh-reed she ties them from and asks for a gathering.
morven_reach_soothing_the_watch.offer: My folk come in off the cold work shaking — the divers, the watcher, the ones who read the door. I tie them little knots, reed-work, nothing magic, just a thing for a frightened hand to hold and worry at till sleep comes. My grandmother tied them for soldiers. I'm out of the good reed — the long marsh-reed by the eastern water. Gather me an armful and I'll tie the whole watch something to hold tonight. Small comfort. It's the size I can afford.
morven_reach_soothing_the_watch.progress: The long soothing-reed grows by the eastern marsh water. Gather an armful for the innkeeper's knots.
morven_reach_soothing_the_watch.complete: Look at that, enough for the whole watch and the divers besides. I'll sit up and tie till my fingers ache — a knot for every hand that has to touch the cold tomorrow. It won't keep a soul from being collected. It'll keep a soul from being alone with the fear tonight, which my grandmother held was half the fight and I believe her. Take one for your own hand. Your work's colder than most.

objectives.text: Gather soothing-reed for the knots
Bring the reed to the innkeeper

morven_reach_soothing_the_watch.note: The innkeeper tied reed-knots through the night, one for every hand that had to touch the cold work at dawn. They keep no one from being collected. They keep a frightened soul from being alone with the fear, which she holds is half the fight. It is a small warmth, tied by hand, against a cold that files whole districts. It is exactly the size of the reach's stubborn hope.

morven_reach_the_marked_map.title: The Marked Border
morven_reach_the_marked_map.summary: A boatman's map has turned up in the reach with the old inscription-marks copied carefully into its border, and whoever carried it is dead of no wound. The dockmaster wants the map recovered and read by someone who will not read it too closely.
morven_reach_the_marked_map.offer: A boatman came up the causeway three days back, map in his coat, no mark on him, fingernails clean, satchel sealed. Dead all the same. His map's still out there in the reeds where he fell, and its border's been copied over with the old angular marks — the kind you don't study long. I need it brought in and filed before someone curious finds it first. Bring it. Look at the border once, then look at your boots.
morven_reach_the_marked_map.progress: The dead boatman's map lies in the eastern reeds where he fell. Recover it without reading the marks too closely.
morven_reach_the_marked_map.complete: You brought it, and you're still talking, which is more than the boatman managed. See the border? He copied the marks in a fine hand, careful, proud of the work — and the copying is what killed him. No wound. The reading was the wound. We'll file it in lead where it can't spread, and we'll not speak of it. Take the map's twin, the blank one. Keep your curiosity for things that don't read you back.

objectives.label: the eastern reeds
objectives.text: Recover the marked map
Carry the map in without reading it

morven_reach_the_marked_map.note: The boatman copied the marks in a fine, proud hand, and the copying killed him — no wound, clean nails, sealed satchel. The reading was the wound. The map was filed in lead and not spoken of. Curiosity is the vector. In the reach, they teach it young: look once, then look at your boots.

morven_reach_the_kept_name.title: The Kept Name
morven_reach_the_kept_name.summary: The Morven's oldest quiet duty is keeping a name a person answers to, so the ledger cannot fully close them. The clerk wants a kept-name token carried to a woman on the edge of collection, before her account matures.
morven_reach_the_kept_name.offer: There is a thing this house does that isn't in any charter, and it's the only thing I'm proud of. We keep a name — the true one, the one a person answers to without thinking, the one their mother used. As long as the name is kept and said, the ledger can't fully close them; a soul that still answers to something isn't finished being a soul. There's a woman on the drowned row nearly collected. Carry her this token. Say her name to her. Make her answer.
morven_reach_the_kept_name.progress: The woman nearing collection sits on the drowned row. Carry the kept-name token to her and make her answer to her name.
morven_reach_the_kept_name.complete: She answered. You said it and she turned her head and she was a person for a moment, not a balance nearly settled. It won't hold forever — the ledger's patient and we're only mortal — but every day she answers is a day she isn't closed. That's the work, the whole cold-water work of it: keep a name, say a name, buy a day. Take a token of your own. Someone will need you to remember what they answer to.

objectives.text: Carry the kept-name to the woman
Keep a token against the ledger

morven_reach_the_kept_name.note: As long as a name is kept and said, the ledger cannot fully close the soul that answers to it. The clerk said the woman's name and she turned her head and was a person for a moment. It buys a day, not a life. The Morven have kept names this way for a thousand years. It has never saved anyone. It has saved a great many days.

morven_reach_the_writ_of_unfiling.title: The Writ of Un-filing
morven_reach_the_writ_of_unfiling.summary: The Morven guard one forbidden instrument — a writ that un-files a finalized account, reopening a closed soul at the cost of widening the signal that closed it. The watcher needs it recovered from the sunk vault, and needs to warn what it costs.
morven_reach_the_writ_of_unfiling.offer: Deep in the sunk vault we keep a thing we're forbidden to use, and use anyway when the grief's too much: a writ of un-filing. It cracks a finalized account back open — doors reopen, a face flickers back, a collected soul walks again. But mercy here is a transfer, not a cancellation. Un-file one soul and the signal widens; the orange creeps a street closer. I need the writ brought up from the vault. And I need you to understand what it buys, and what it spends.
morven_reach_the_writ_of_unfiling.progress: The writ of un-filing lies in the sunk vault below the annex. Recover it, knowing that using it widens the signal.
morven_reach_the_writ_of_unfiling.complete: You brought it up. Good, and heavy, isn't it, once you know the price. We'll use it on one — a mother, the clerk begged, just the one mother — and her doors will open and her face will come back, and three streets over the fog will read a shade more orange for it. That's the reach's whole arithmetic: no mercy is free, every kindness is a transfer, and you do it anyway, and you carry the transfer. Keep the writ's copy. You'll be tempted. Know the cost before you spend it.

objectives.label: the sunk vault
objectives.text: Recover the writ from the sunk vault
Carry up the writ of un-filing

morven_reach_the_writ_of_unfiling.note: The writ un-files a finalized soul — doors reopen, a face flickers back — and widens the signal by exactly as much: the orange creeps a street closer for every mercy spent. No kindness in the reach is free; every one is a transfer. They used it on one mother and carried the cost three streets over. Mercy is not cancellation. It is a debt moved to a colder ledger.

morven_reach_obsidian_focus.title: The Heart of the Deep Bed
morven_reach_obsidian_focus.summary: The deepest of the sunk streets holds a black-glass heart the flood-waters grew around a drowned shrine, and the smith needs it to forge a focus that steadies a diver's mind against the reach's pull. It must be cut free before the tide seals the stair.
morven_reach_obsidian_focus.offer: There's a heart down in the deep bed — black-glass, grown by the cold water around an old sunk shrine, smooth as a still pond and near as dark. I can cut it into a focus, a thing a diver holds that steadies the mind against the pull down there, the pull that makes the incurious walk into the reeds. But it's deep, past the third sunk stair, and the tide's rising to seal it. Go down while a person still can. Bring me the heart. I'll make it something that keeps a head clear.
morven_reach_obsidian_focus.progress: The black-glass heart lies past the third sunk stair in the deep bed. Cut it free before the tide seals the stair.
morven_reach_obsidian_focus.complete: Heavy, and cold, and beautiful the way deep things are before the light ruins them. I'll cut it slow and true — a focus that holds a diver's mind to their own thoughts when the reach starts offering them rest. Won't stop the pull. Gives a person a thing to hold their own name against while it pulls. That's all any of our work does down here: gives you something to be yourself against. Take the focus. Be yourself against the dark a while longer.

objectives.label: the third sunk stair
objectives.text: Reach the black-glass heart in the deep bed
Cut the black-glass heart free

morven_reach_obsidian_focus.note: The black-glass heart grew in the cold around a drowned shrine, and the smith cut it into a focus that holds a diver's mind to their own name against the reach's pull. It does not stop the pull. It gives a person something to be themselves against. In the reach, that is what all the work amounts to, and it is not nothing.

canal_maze_low_water_count.title: What the Low Water Counts
canal_maze_low_water_count.summary: The canals of the maze are the city's veins and its grave both, and every low tide lays out what was deposited. Lazar keeps the count. He has kept it too long to keep it alone.
canal_maze_low_water_count.offer: Every canal in this city carries what the city is done with, and the low water sets it out plain along the eel-gate channel. I count them at the turn of the tide. Not for the Archive -- for me. Walk the low sluice with me and put a number to it, so one man doesn't carry the whole number by himself.
canal_maze_low_water_count.progress: The low sluice runs black below the eel-gate. Walk it at the tide-turn and count what the water set down.
canal_maze_low_water_count.complete: Nineteen. Same as three tides back, near enough. You'd think that steady. It isn't -- it means someone's feeding the water at a rate. A body's a deposit here, and deposits balance a book. Take the coin. Don't thank me for the work.

objectives.label: the low sluice
objectives.text: Walk the low sluice at the tide-turn
Clear the rot off what the water set down

canal_maze_low_water_count.note: Nineteen, and steady, which Lazar says is the worst word for it. A body is a deposit in the maze, and deposits are made to balance a book no one here has ever been shown.

canal_maze_eel_gate_grate.title: The Grate at the Eel-Gate
canal_maze_eel_gate_grate.summary: The eel-gate grate is the last iron between the maze and the black weir, and Ilie forged half of it. The rot that breeds under the boards has taken the lower bars. He is too fond of the ironwork to watch it go.
canal_maze_eel_gate_grate.offer: That grate's mine -- half of it, anyway, the half that still holds. It's the only iron between the maze and the weir, and the rot that comes up under the low boards has been eating the bottom bars a night at a time. I'd go down and mind it myself, but I mind the forge better. Clear the swarm off it so I can re-hang the bars.
canal_maze_eel_gate_grate.progress: The dock-rot breeds thick on the lower bars of the eel-gate grate. Burn it back so Ilie can work the iron.
canal_maze_eel_gate_grate.complete: Bars are clear. I'll have them re-hung by the bell. Good iron holds a long time in this water -- longer than the men who hang it, most tides. Here, a hook off my own bench. It'll outlast the both of us, which isn't the compliment it sounds like here.

objectives.text: Burn the rot off the eel-gate bars

canal_maze_eel_gate_grate.note: Ilie re-hung the bars by the bell, and the grate held another season. Good iron outlasts the men who hang it. In the maze, that is said as a warning, not a comfort.

canal_maze_third_impression.title: The Third Impression
canal_maze_third_impression.summary: In the drowned counting-house one account is stamped collected, un-stamped, and stamped again, the violet ink smeared where a dead hand kept refusing the verdict. Matei has read it once too often, and now it reads back.
canal_maze_third_impression.offer: There's a line in the ledger down in the counting-house -- near the bottom of the standing page. Stamped collected. Then un-stamped. Then stamped again, the ink dragged both ways like a hand fought the impression while another hand made it. I've looked at it thirty tides. Last night the whole page turned to face me when I came in. Go and look at it. Tell me what a page wants with my face.
canal_maze_third_impression.progress: The standing ledger waits in the drowned counting-house. Reach it, and take word to Florea, who keeps the chronicle above the water.
canal_maze_third_impression.complete: You saw it turn too. And you saw the ink -- stamped, un-stamped, stamped. That's not a clerk changing his mind. That's a debt refusing its own collection, over and over, and something patient making the mark anyway. Florea will want it in the chronicle. I want it out of my head. Take this. I can't hold it and hold the page.

objectives.label: the drowned counting-house
objectives.text: Reach the standing ledger below the water
Carry word of the third impression to Florea

canal_maze_third_impression.note: Stamped, un-stamped, stamped. A debt refusing its own collection, and a patient thing making the mark regardless. The page turned to face whoever entered. Matei no longer enters. He sends the count up by rope.

canal_maze_warm_chalk_hand.title: The Warm Print
canal_maze_warm_chalk_hand.summary: A child's chalk handprint marks a canal wall, the same small emblem folk pressed a thousand years gone in a warmer country. This one is faintly warm to the palm, and a careful new mark has been chalked around it. Gavril wants to know which the mark means: guarding, or filing.
canal_maze_warm_chalk_hand.offer: There's a handprint on the wall by the chalk-run -- child's hand, in chalk, old as anything down here. My gran said her gran said folk pressed them for luck, once, somewhere the fog was thinner. This one's warm. Put your palm to it, you'll feel it. And someone's chalked a careful ring around it, fresh, in that Morven hand. I need to know if that ring is minding the print or measuring it. Go feel the wall. You'll know more than I do.
canal_maze_warm_chalk_hand.progress: The warm handprint waits on the wall by the chalk-run. Reach it, then ask Watcher Oprea whose hand drew the ring.
canal_maze_warm_chalk_hand.complete: Warm. I told you. A child's hand, warm a thousand years after the child, and a fresh ring around it that could be a fence or a tally. Oprea says even the one who drew it doesn't know which. That's the maze all over -- kindness and collection wearing the same chalk. Keep the charm. It means nothing, which down here is the strongest thing a charm can mean.

objectives.label: the chalk wall
objectives.text: Put your palm to the warm handprint
Ask Oprea whose hand drew the ring

canal_maze_warm_chalk_hand.note: The print is warm, a thousand years on -- the last unbroken echo of a small warm kindness. A fresh Morven ring surrounds it. Guarding it, or cataloguing it. The one who drew the ring does not know either.

canal_maze_where_walls_are_highest.title: Where the Walls Are Highest
canal_maze_where_walls_are_highest.summary: Something in the maze knows the water better than the drowned who dug it, and it takes the lost where the walls run highest and no one can see the black water move. Barbu has fished out four this month. He wants the guiding stopped.
canal_maze_where_walls_are_highest.offer: You get turned around in this maze -- everyone does, it's built to. But some folk get turned around the same way. Always toward the high channels, where the walls are highest and nobody topside can see the water take them. That's not the maze. That's a thing that lives in the maze, piloting the lost to the deep runs. I've pulled four out this month. Go into the high channels and put it down before it makes five.
canal_maze_where_walls_are_highest.progress: The lurkers work the high channels where the walls block the sightlines. Hunt them where the water moves and no one can see it.
canal_maze_where_walls_are_highest.complete: Two, and the high runs are quieter. You felt it, didn't you -- how it wanted you to go left where left is a drop? It knows the maze the way the drowned knew it, and it's got all the patience of the drowned besides. Four this month is four fewer, thanks to you. Take the tonic. The water down there argues with your gut. That settles it.

objectives.text: Hunt the lurkers in the high channels

canal_maze_where_walls_are_highest.note: It pilots the lost toward the highest walls, where the black water moves unseen. It knows the maze as the drowned knew it, and shares their patience. Barbu pulls out fewer now. He does not say none.

canal_maze_fare_you_dont_pay.title: A Fare You Don't Pay
canal_maze_fare_you_dont_pay.summary: Every crossing in the maze has a toll, and Marcu has made a modest study of not paying it. He needs the right blank forms lifted before the tollmen change their hand again.
canal_maze_fare_you_dont_pay.offer: Listen. Every pontoon in this maze takes a toll, and every toll's a line in somebody's book, and I have made it my life's small art to leave no lines. The trick's the forms -- the tollmen use blanks off the counting-house, and they never look twice at their own hand. Fetch me a stack of the blanks before they change the watermark, and I'll cut you in. Nobody drowns from this one. Rarest thing in the maze -- a swindle that hurts nobody.
canal_maze_fare_you_dont_pay.progress: The toll blanks stack unwatched on the drowned counting-house desks. Lift a set before the tollmen's hand changes, then bring them back to Marcu.
canal_maze_fare_you_dont_pay.complete: Beautiful. Same watermark, same crooked serif on the clerk's flourish -- they'll pass every pontoon in the maze. Nobody looks twice at their own handwriting, that's the whole science of it. Here's your cut, and one made out to you. Cross free a while. In a city that files every fare, riding unbilled is the closest thing to being free.

objectives.label: the counting-house desks
objectives.text: Lift a set of blank toll forms
Bring the blanks back to Marcu

canal_maze_fare_you_dont_pay.note: The forged fares passed every pontoon; nobody looks twice at their own hand. In a city that files every crossing, Marcu counts an unbilled ride as the nearest thing to freedom he can steal.

canal_maze_marrow_pells_books.title: The Books That Won't Close
canal_maze_marrow_pells_books.summary: In the annex Marrow Pell keeps books that should have closed and haven't -- accounts the Archive marked finalized that go on owing. Florea suspects the accounts you settled elsewhere are among them, filed and waiting. She would like to be wrong.
canal_maze_marrow_pells_books.offer: Marrow Pell is dead. Has been a good while. And the broker keeps books all the same -- down in the annex, accounts the Archive stamped finalized that simply. Won't. Close. I chronicle this city, and I have begun to find my own entries in his hand. Roads you walked. A well you read. Go down to the annex and look at what Pell keeps open. If my suspicion has legs, I'd rather it walked toward me than behind me.
canal_maze_marrow_pells_books.progress: Marrow Pell's annex sits low against the water, its books lit by no lamp anyone carries. Reach it, then bring Florea what you found among the open accounts.
canal_maze_marrow_pells_books.complete: So. My entries. And yours -- every account you thought you'd settled up the whole long road, filed here, open, accruing. You didn't close them.
