There was a bank of clean lichen by the cairn road, once, and a woman who brewed the old way from it. When both are gone, say there were.

objectives.text: Gather clean marsh-lichen from unturned ground
Bring the clean lichen to Iosif

salt_fens_brine_witch_reagents.note: The brine-witch brewed Hessik's method back to its uncorrupted first hand, one honest bottle, from the last bank of clean lichen on the cairn road -- ground the orange had not yet reached. Every season that clean ground shrinks and her reach shrinks with it. When the bank and the brewer are both gone, she asked only that someone say there were.

salt_fens_causeway_supper.title: Supper at the Last Hearth
salt_fens_causeway_supper.summary: The causeway host wants one good supper laid on for the fen folk before the long dark of the season -- proper meat, not brack-fish -- and needs brack-boar hunted off the high ground while there's still light to hunt by.
salt_fens_causeway_supper.offer: Once a season I put on a real supper. Not brack-fish, not grey salt on grey bread -- meat, and a fire built high, and every soul in the fens crowded into my one warm room pretending for a night the drowned city isn't downstream. But I need boar for it -- brack-boar, off the high ground, and they're mean as bailiffs this time of year. Bring me enough for a table of the lost and forgotten, and there's a seat at it for you. First seat. You've earned a warm one.
salt_fens_causeway_supper.progress: The brack-boar range the high ground, north and east, where the reeds thin. Hunt enough for a full table -- ten good beasts. Do it while there's light; the fens are no place to hunt in the dark.
salt_fens_causeway_supper.complete: That'll fill the pot and then some. Tonight the room'll be loud and warm and too crowded, and for a few hours nobody'll listen for anything on the causeway. That's the whole of what I sell out here -- a few hours of not listening. It's worth more than the salt. Sit. Eat. There's stew, and there's a girl who plays a pipe nobody taught her, and there's the door shut fast against the fog. Everything good is temporary. That's what makes it supper.

objectives.text: Hunt brack-boar for the season's supper

salt_fens_causeway_supper.note: Once a season the causeway host lays on a real supper -- meat, a high fire, every soul in the fens crowded warm into one room, pretending the drowned city is not downstream. For a few hours no one listens to the causeway. That is the whole of what Fane sells out here, and it is worth more than the salt. Everything good is temporary. That is what makes it supper.

salt_fens_daily_lamp_circuit.title: The Fog-Lamp Round
salt_fens_daily_lamp_circuit.summary: The fen-lights must be walked and trimmed every dusk, or the causeway loses its marks and a walker loses the road. The lamp-keeper cannot make the whole round on her knees anymore.
salt_fens_daily_lamp_circuit.offer: Every dusk the lights want walking -- trimmed, filled, re-lit where the damp's had them. Miss one and a soul on the causeway's got a gap of dark to cross with nothing to walk toward, and the fog fills a gap of dark with roads of its own. My knees make it to two lamps now, maybe three. Walk the round for me tonight. It's small work and it's the whole of what stands between the causeway and the brack.
salt_fens_daily_lamp_circuit.progress: Walk the fen-lights along the causeway, south to the low ground, and see each one lit and trimmed before full dark. Miss none.
salt_fens_daily_lamp_circuit.complete: All of them burning. Good. That's one more night the causeway's got its marks and the fog's got no gap to fill. Small work, I know. But it's the small work nobody does that kills folk out here, not the monsters. Come back tomorrow's dusk. The lamps don't keep. Nothing keeps. That's the fens.

objectives.label: the fen-lights along the causeway
objectives.text: Trim and light the fen-lamps before dark

salt_fens_daily_lamp_circuit.note: The fen-lights are walked and trimmed each dusk, or a gap of dark opens on the causeway and the fog fills it with roads of its own. It is the small work nobody does that kills folk in the fens, the keeper says, not the monsters. The lamps do not keep. Nothing does.

salt_fens_daily_brack_cull.title: Keeping the Count
salt_fens_daily_brack_cull.summary: The fen-lurkers breed back as fast as they are cut, and the causeway stays walkable only if someone keeps the daily count down. The hunter needs the near pans thinned again before dusk.
salt_fens_daily_brack_cull.offer: You cut them, they come back. That's the whole arrangement out here, and it's the honestest arrangement the fens offer. The lurkers are up in the near pans again already, thick by the causeway where folk walk. Thin them for me today. Not for good -- there's no for good in the fens -- just for today. Today's the only length of time this country lets you win.
salt_fens_daily_brack_cull.progress: The fen-lurkers work the near pans by the causeway. Cut them back -- a dozen keeps the road walkable till tomorrow. It's always till tomorrow.
salt_fens_daily_brack_cull.complete: Twelve. The road's walkable another day and the count's honest another dusk. Don't thank me and I won't thank you -- we both know it's back tomorrow, thicker for the culling, likely. But a walkable road today is a soul who makes the crossing today. You count the days you can save, out here. You don't count the ones you can't. That way lies standing in the reeds.

objectives.text: Thin the fen-lurkers by the causeway

salt_fens_daily_brack_cull.note: The lurkers breed back as fast as they are cut; the causeway stays walkable only day by day. You count the days you can save, the hunter says, not the ones you can't -- that way lies standing in the reeds. Today's crossing is a soul who crosses today. It is the only length of time the fens let you win.

ledger_roads_checkpoint_always.title: The Checkpoint That Was Always There
ledger_roads_checkpoint_always.summary: A toll-gate stands on the old post road that stood there yesterday under grass and no gate. Floarea keeps the road for the Accord. She would like it kept for people a while longer.
ledger_roads_checkpoint_always.offer: You walked the north post road this morning. Was there a gate on it? No. There was not. There is now, and a clerk in it, and a queue of the collected shuffling up to pay a toll nobody set. I keep this road for the living, or I did. Break the shades holding the line. Do it before the queue reaches the gate, because I have watched what the gate does to the ones at the front, and I would rather you didn't.
ledger_roads_checkpoint_always.progress: The new checkpoint stands on the north post road, past the milestone. The queue is short yet. Cut down the shades that keep it.
ledger_roads_checkpoint_always.complete: Gone, and the road plain grass again where the gate stood. You saw the ledger the clerk carried, open on the post. A page for every name in the queue. And one page, near the back, blank, waiting -- and warm to the hand. I burned it. Ash owes nothing. Take the coin and the writ; you'll want the writ before the week is out.

objectives.text: Break the queue at the new checkpoint

ledger_roads_checkpoint_always.note: A gate stood where no gate was, and is grass again. Floarea burned the blank page that ran warm. A page kept for a name not yet spoken. Somebody was expected. Somebody is always expected here.

ledger_roads_line_stamped_twice.title: A Line Stamped Twice
ledger_roads_line_stamped_twice.summary: Tinca has kept the road-ledger honest for eleven years by copying only what was already true. One line will not settle -- stamped collected, un-stamped, stamped again, the ink smeared where a hand refused the verdict. She wants it un-filed for good.
ledger_roads_line_stamped_twice.offer: Eleven years I have copied this ledger, and I have never once written a name that wasn't already owed. There is a line I cannot keep still. Stamped collected. Then un-stamped -- and I did not do it. Then stamped again, and the ink smeared, as if a hand kept refusing. Bring me a Writ of Un-Filing and stand at the line where the discrepancy walks, and we will settle it kindly, once, so it can stop being settled cruelly forever.
ledger_roads_line_stamped_twice.progress: Bring a Writ of Un-Filing to the smeared line. The discrepancy keeps to the crossroads at the second milestone, where the road cannot decide what it is.
ledger_roads_line_stamped_twice.complete: There. Un-filed, and it lay down. You heard it too -- the crossroads said it, in a voice with no throat behind it, flat as a stamp coming down. It named you. Not your name. The one who burned the warm page. It has an entry for you now, it said. Short. Entries grow. ...Keep the token. Choose whose it is out loud, the way the old warders did. It helps. It does not save you. It helps.

objectives.text: Bring a Writ of Un-Filing to the line
Settle the discrepancy at the crossroads

ledger_roads_line_stamped_twice.note: The line settled at last, un-filed and kind. In the settling the crossroads spoke -- not her name, her deed. It has an entry for her now. Short, it said. Entries grow. Tinca has stopped copying after dark.

ledger_roads_road_reads_back.title: The Road Reads Back
ledger_roads_road_reads_back.summary: Maricica keeps the road's history as it keeps its milestones -- one stone at a time, honestly. The milestones have begun answering her. She needs the reader-stones gathered from the shades that carry them, before she learns what the road has learned about her.
ledger_roads_road_reads_back.offer: A chronicler's whole trade is asking the road questions and writing down that it never answers. Mine answers now. I set a marker at the ford last spring -- a private thing, a date, my mother's -- and this morning the milestone beside it bore the same date, cut deeper, by no chisel I own. The shades carry reader-stones; that is how the road learns to read. Gather them off the queue at the ford. I want to know what it knows. I want to know before it tells me.
ledger_roads_road_reads_back.progress: The reader-stones are carried by the shades working the ford queue. Gather them, and meet Maricica at the ford where the milestones have begun to answer.
ledger_roads_road_reads_back.complete: You brought them, and I read them, and I should not have. Every marker I ever set is copied here -- my mother's date, and under it, un-cut yet, a space the width of a second date. Mine. The road was not remembering me. It was doing my arithmetic. This was arranged, all of it -- the honest work I did for the Accord was the road learning my hand so it could forge my ending in it. Take the lens. See it coming, if nothing else.

objectives.label: the answering milestones at the ford
objectives.text: Reach the answering milestones
Gather reader-stones from the ford queue

ledger_roads_road_reads_back.note: The milestones copied every private marker Maricica ever set, and left a space the width of a second date. The honest chronicle she kept for the Accord taught the road her hand. It was never reading her. It was learning to write as her.

ledger_roads_toll_of_standing_still.title: The Toll of Standing Still
ledger_roads_toll_of_standing_still.summary: The collected who reach a checkpoint and cannot pay do not leave. They stand. Zoe has counted a queue at the west post that has not moved in three days, and grows by one each night. Thin the shades before the road decides they are its.
ledger_roads_toll_of_standing_still.offer: You learn to watch the ones that don't move. A queue at the west post, standing three days now -- no shuffle, no complaint, patient the way only the collected are patient. And every night, one more. They're paying a toll the only way they can afford, which is with the standing itself. It costs them by the hour. Put them down before the count reaches whatever number the road is waiting for. I don't know the number. I know we're close to it.
ledger_roads_toll_of_standing_still.progress: The still queue keeps to the west post road. Break the shades before the nightly count completes.
ledger_roads_toll_of_standing_still.complete: Twelve was the number. You put them down at eleven, and the road went quiet like a held breath let go. It wanted a full row. Rows of twelve, always -- older than this road, older than the reckoning it serves. Take the charm. It means nothing, and that is the whole of what it's good for out here.

objectives.text: Break the still queue at the west post

ledger_roads_toll_of_standing_still.note: The queue wanted twelve; Zoe's count stopped it at eleven. Rows of twelve, older than the road that keeps them. She watches the west post still. She has started counting out loud, so the road can hear her disagree.

ledger_roads_accounts_receivable.title: Accounts Receivable
ledger_roads_accounts_receivable.summary: A crate of debt-slips has come loose from a foundered coach, and every slip names someone in the road-towns still walking. Tinca wants them gathered before a collector does the same and finds the living owe.
ledger_roads_accounts_receivable.offer: A coach went into the ford-mud last night and its strongbox burst. Debt-slips, hundreds, scattered down two leagues of road -- and here is the trouble: half of them name people who are still breathing. In this county, an unclosed name owing an open debt is an invitation. Gather the slips before a collector gathers them for me. What we hold, we can lose in a fire. What they hold, they file. Bring me the ledger-craft off the coach clerk too; it lists which names are which.
ledger_roads_accounts_receivable.progress: The debt-slips lie scattered along the ford road below the foundered coach. Gather the coach clerk's ledger-craft and the loose slips.
ledger_roads_accounts_receivable.complete: All of them, good. I'll burn the ones that name the living tonight, in the hearth, slow, so the wet paper argues with the fire and loses. The rest I'll file honest -- the dead can spare it. You did a kind thing today and it will cost the dead a little more so the living pay nothing. That is the best trade this road offers. I have stopped pretending there's a better one.

objectives.text: Recover the coach clerk's ledger-craft
Clear the shades gathering the slips

ledger_roads_accounts_receivable.note: Tinca burned the slips that named the living and filed the ones that named the dead. Mercy on this road is not cancellation. It is a transfer -- and the dead, who cannot object, carry it. She calls it the best trade going. She is right, and she hates being right.

ledger_roads_coach_runs_empty.title: The Coach That Runs Empty
ledger_roads_coach_runs_empty.summary: A mail-coach runs the south road on schedule, to the minute, with no driver and no passengers and its lamps lit. Safta has stopped booking fares on the south road. She wants the crossings cleared so honest coaches can run it again.
ledger_roads_coach_runs_empty.offer: I clear the coaches through, that's my post -- who rides, who pays, who's waiting at which crossing. There's a coach on the south road runs to the minute, dawn and dusk, lamps lit, and no one drives it and no one rides it and every crossing-shade steps aside to let it through. Empty, but they bow to it. Something's owed to that coach and it's coming to collect on time, the way the honest ones never manage. Clear the crossings it stops at. Let a coach with people in it run that road again.
ledger_roads_coach_runs_empty.progress: The empty coach stops at the crossings along the south road. Reach the third crossing and break the shades that keep its schedule.
ledger_roads_coach_runs_empty.complete: Ran late this morning, first time. Four minutes late, and four minutes is the loudest thing I've heard in a year. The crossing-shades didn't bow. You bought the south road four minutes of not being on time, and out here that's nearly freedom. Book what fares you can while it lasts. It won't last. Nothing that runs late on this road stays late for long.

objectives.label: the third south crossing
objectives.text: Reach the third south crossing
Break the shades that keep the coach on time

ledger_roads_coach_runs_empty.note: The empty coach ran four minutes late for the first time in a year, and the crossings did not bow. Safta counts it a victory in minutes. On the Ledger Roads that is the only currency victory comes in, and the road always has more time than she does.

ledger_roads_name_in_another_hand.title: Her Name in Someone Else's Hand
ledger_roads_name_in_another_hand.summary: Neaga found her dead sister's name on a fresh debt-slip, written in a hand she doesn't know, dated a week from now. She wants it un-filed before the date it names arrives.
ledger_roads_name_in_another_hand.offer: My sister died in the flood-spring, and I buried her myself, and I keep her name in my own hand in the book by my bed so I don't lose the letters of it. There's a slip come to the post with her name on it. Not my hand. A stranger's, neat as a clerk's, and dated -- next market-day. She's already collected. She's already gone. Why is there a bill coming due on her a week from now? Ask Tinca what it means. Bring me the token the old warders made, the one you say whose over. I want to say whose she was before that date does.
ledger_roads_name_in_another_hand.progress: Ask Clerk Tinca what a debt dated in the future means. Then bring Neaga a Kept-Name Token, so she can name her sister before the market-day the slip claims.
ledger_roads_name_in_another_hand.complete: Tinca says a future date means the road expects a body it hasn't got yet -- that it isn't billing my sister, it's billing whoever comes to answer for her. Which is me. Which was always going to be me. Give me the token. I'll say her name over it out loud, mine and hers both, so that when I stand at that gate on market-day there are two of us to un-file and only one to collect. She'd have done the same. She did do the same, once. That's the whole trouble with love out here -- it's the one debt that keeps you walking to the gate.

objectives.text: Ask Tinca about the future-dated slip
Bring Neaga a Kept-Name Token

ledger_roads_name_in_another_hand.note: The future-dated slip did not bill the dead sister. It billed whoever loves her enough to answer at the gate. Neaga will answer. She named them both over the token, out loud, so there would be two to un-file and one to collect. Love is the debt that keeps you walking to the gate.

ledger_roads_warm_bowl_cold_road.title: A Warm Bowl on a Cold Road
ledger_roads_warm_bowl_cold_road.summary: The road-house pot has run thin, and Zamfira means the carters and wardens who work the crossings to eat hot at least once this week. Gather what an honest kitchen needs, and let the taproom be loud a while.
ledger_roads_warm_bowl_cold_road.offer: Everyone who works this road comes through my door grey to the lips and colder inside than out, and I have exactly one cure for that and it lives in the big pot. Trouble is the pot's run to broth and rumor. The road-towns downhill still keep gardens that grow food and not debt -- bless them, there's a few left. Fetch me the makings, the honest kind, and I'll have the wardens fed hot and swearing at each other over cards by dark. One night this road can be a road with people on it. That's not nothing. Out here that's very nearly everything.
ledger_roads_warm_bowl_cold_road.progress: Gather honest makings from the road-town gardens downhill, and bring them to Zamfira's pot before the wardens come off the crossings.
ledger_roads_warm_bowl_cold_road.complete: Now listen to that. That is a taproom. That is Floarea losing at cards and Safta cheating badly and someone singing the toll-keeper's song wrong on purpose to make Zoe laugh, and for the length of one pot of stew nobody at that fire is counting anything. I'll take that. Here -- for the walk, and for the warmth you carried up a road that hates it. A stew and a stick and a stolen hour. Small things. They're the only ones that ever hold.

objectives.text: Gather honest makings from the road-towns
Bring the makings to Zamfira's pot

ledger_roads_warm_bowl_cold_road.note: For the length of one pot of stew the road-house was loud and warm and nobody counted anything. Zamfira called it very nearly everything. Small things -- a stew, a stick, a stolen hour. On this road they are the only ones that ever hold. That is what makes them worth the carrying.

ledger_roads_forged_fare.title: The Forged Fare
ledger_roads_forged_fare.summary: Draga sells the one thing the checkpoints can't tell from real: a toll receipt the clerk's own hand seems to have written. She needs blank stock recovered from a collector who took her last batch, and her method kept quiet.
ledger_roads_forged_fare.offer: A checkpoint reads two things -- the stamp and the hand that made it. Match the hand and the stamp forgives itself; a clerk never doubts his own writing. I make fares that do exactly that, and they've walked more living people past those gates than any warden's blade. A collector took my last stock of blank slips off a coach three nights back. Get it back. And here's the delicate part: I sell to anyone who pays, the desperate and the wicked both, and I don't ask which you are. Neither should you. That's how the trade stays open.
ledger_roads_forged_fare.progress: The stolen blank stock is on the shades that took the coach, along the east toll-road. Recover it, and bring Draga her method back.
ledger_roads_forged_fare.complete: All accounted for. You'll have noticed I don't ask what you'll do with the two I owe you. A clerk sold a forged fare to a mother last winter and to a man who'd sold three coaches of the collected the winter before, and both walked free through the same gate, and the clerk slept fine because the clerk didn't ask. I've made my peace with being the reason both got through. The road prices decency out of everyone eventually. This is just what mine costs.

objectives.text: Recover Draga's stolen fare-stock
Break the collector's shades on the east road

ledger_roads_forged_fare.note: Draga's forged fares walk the living and the wicked through the same gate, and she has stopped asking which is which. The road prices decency out of everyone in the end. She calls this what hers costs. She sleeps fine. She says the sleeping fine is the part that frightens her.

ledger_roads_nails_against_night.title: Nails Against the Night
ledger_roads_nails_against_night.summary: Anca shoes the wardens' horses and irons the crossing-posts, and the crows have learned that a horse thrown at the wrong crossing is a warden the road collects. She needs the flocks broken and good iron scavenged from a foundered post.
ledger_roads_nails_against_night.offer: A well-hammered nail beats a wish-coin, my grandmother's grandmother said, and out here it beats a prayer too. I shoe the wardens' mounts so they don't throw at a crossing, because a warden afoot at a crossing after dark is a name the road finds time for. The crows have got clever -- they spook the horses at the bad crossings, where the shades are thickest. Break the flocks. And there's a foundered iron post at the ford full of good cold iron; bring me what you can pull. I'll make nails of it. Nails are honest. Nails hold what they're driven into and ask nothing back.
ledger_roads_nails_against_night.progress: Break the crow-flocks working the bad crossings, and pull cold iron from the foundered post at the ford for Anca's forge.
ledger_roads_nails_against_night.complete: Good iron, and quieter crossings for the pulling of it. I'll drive these into the warden-posts myself, one to a beam, cold and deep. It won't stop the road. Nothing stops the road. But a nail says a beam belongs to somebody, and out here belonging to somebody is the whole of what a ward is. My grandmother knew that. She never once explained it. Some things you drive in deep and don't explain.

objectives.text: Break the crow-flocks at the bad crossings
Pull cold iron from the foundered post

ledger_roads_nails_against_night.note: Anca drove cold-iron nails into the warden-posts, one to a beam, and would not say why it helped. A nail says a beam belongs to somebody, and belonging to somebody is the whole of a ward. Her grandmother knew it and never explained it either. Some things you drive in deep and leave unexplained.

ledger_roads_crows_are_counting.title: What the Crows Are Counting
ledger_roads_crows_are_counting.summary: The carrion crows no longer scatter and settle. They land on the milestones in even rows and sit facing the same way, and Ioana swears they land one to a name on the toll-rolls. Break the flocks before the count they keep is finished.
ledger_roads_crows_are_counting.offer: Crows are honest birds. They come for the dead and they squabble and they go. These don't squabble. They land on the milestones, evens and rows, all facing north, and they sit. I counted them against the toll-roll one morning because I couldn't help it, and it came out the same number, crow for name, to the one. They're not scavenging. They're taking attendance. Break the flocks off the milestones. I don't want to be here the morning the count comes out complete.
ledger_roads_crows_are_counting.progress: The crow-flocks keep to the milestones along the north road, one to a name. Break them before the count is finished.
ledger_roads_crows_are_counting.complete: Scattered, and thank the small saints for it. But I'll tell you what I saw as they went up -- they lifted all at once, no straggler, like a hand closing a book, and they wheeled once and flew north, toward the fog. Toward the orange. They weren't counting for the road. They were carrying the count somewhere. Somewhere the road reports to. I've stopped looking north at dusk. It doesn't help. It's still there when I look back.

objectives.text: Break the counting flocks on the milestones

ledger_roads_crows_are_counting.note: The crows took attendance against the toll-roll, crow for name, and when they scattered they wheeled once and flew north toward the orange fog. Not counting for the road. Carrying the count to whatever the road reports to. Ioana has stopped looking north at dusk. It is still there when she looks back.

ledger_roads_discrepancy_of_ones_own.title: A Discrepancy of One's Own
ledger_roads_discrepancy_of_ones_own.summary: Tinca has heard of a broker in the drowned capital who kept an account the system could not close -- one discrepancy of his own -- and lived by it. She wants the writ-craft that does it, and someone to talk her out of, or into, learning it.
ledger_roads_discrepancy_of_ones_own.offer: There is a broker downriver, in the grey capital, who is said to keep one account no collector can settle -- his own, deliberately mis-filed, a discrepancy he made and guards, because a name the ledger can't close is a name the road can't collect. Marrow Pell, they call him. The dead broker who won't close. I want the writ-craft that does it. I want to make myself into a discrepancy and step off this road for good. Recover the writ-stock the coach clerks copied it onto. Then talk to me. Talk me into it or out of it. I have stopped being able to tell which is mercy.
ledger_roads_discrepancy_of_ones_own.progress: Recover the discrepancy-writ stock from the coach clerks along the ford road, then speak with Tinca about learning Pell's method.
ledger_roads_discrepancy_of_ones_own.complete: You've heard me out, and here's where I've come to rest. Pell keeps his discrepancy by never letting anyone love him -- an unclosed account with no one to answer for it can drift forever, free and cold and alone as a milestone. I have Zamfira's stew on a Thursday and Neaga's sister to un-file and a road full of names I've kept honest for eleven years. I can't drift. I'm anchored to people, and that's exactly the thing the road bills you for. So. I'll stay the discrepancy I already am -- one honest clerk in a crooked ledger -- and pay when it comes. Take the writ. You'll keep more names honest with it than I will.

objectives.text: Recover the discrepancy-writ stock
Talk with Tinca about Pell's method

ledger_roads_discrepancy_of_ones_own.note: Pell keeps his discrepancy by letting no one love him; an unclosed account with no one to answer for it drifts forever, free and cold and alone. Tinca is anchored to people, which is the very thing the road bills. She chose to stay anchored, and pay when it comes. She calls the anchor the debt. She would not cut it if she could.

ledger_roads_milestone_two_dates.title: The Milestone With Two Dates
ledger_roads_milestone_two_dates.summary: Every milestone on the north road carries the year it was cut. One now carries two years -- the second not yet arrived. Zoe wants it read before the later date comes, and the shades that guard it broken.
ledger_roads_milestone_two_dates.offer: A milestone tells you one thing honestly: how far, and when it was cut to say so. There's one at the high bend carries two dates now. The old one, worn. And a fresh one, deep -- a year that hasn't happened. The collected gather at it, patient, like they're waiting for a coach that's due. I need someone with a steady stomach to reach it, read what the second date sits above, and put down whatever's keeping the appointment. I'd go myself. I've read too many stones this month. You start hearing what they'd say if they had a mouth. I've heard enough.
ledger_roads_milestone_two_dates.progress: The two-dated milestone stands at the high bend of the north road. Reach it, and break the shades keeping the appointment.
ledger_roads_milestone_two_dates.complete: You read it. A name sits under the year that hasn't come, half-cut, waiting for the road to finish it. Not a name I know -- yet. That's the cruelty of the thing: it cuts the date first and the name at leisure, so you spend the year between wondering if the unfinished letters are the start of yours. Break enough of them and the road cuts slower, is all. Take the token. Say a name over it that you choose. Better the road hears you've already chosen than lets it choose for you.

objectives.label: the two-dated milestone at the high bend
objectives.text: Read the two-dated milestone
Break the shades keeping the appointment

ledger_roads_milestone_two_dates.note: The milestone cut the future year deep and left the name half-lettered, so the county spends the year between wondering whose start the letters are. It cuts the date first and the name at leisure. Zoe reads no more stones this month. You start hearing what they would say if they had a mouth. She has heard enough.

ledger_roads_debt_my_father_left.title: The Debt My Father Left
ledger_roads_debt_my_father_left.summary: Paraschiva's father paid off every debt he owed before he died, and got a receipt for it. A collector came anyway, with a slip that says the debt is her inheritance. She wants the receipt found and the truth of it asked.
ledger_roads_debt_my_father_left.offer: My father paid what he owed. All of it, every slip, and got a receipt with the toll-clerk's own stamp on it, and he kept it in the family book so no one could ever say otherwise. He's a year in the ground. Last night a collector stood at my door with a slip that says the debt passed to me. Inherited, it said. As if being his daughter is a thing you owe for. Find me his receipt -- it went into the flood-chest when the ford took our cellar. And ask Tinca how a paid debt gets un-paid. Ask her if love is a thing they can bill.
ledger_roads_debt_my_father_left.progress: Recover the father's paid receipt from the flood-chest by the drowned cellar, and ask Clerk Tinca how a settled debt is re-opened.
ledger_roads_debt_my_father_left.complete: You found it. His hand, the clerk's stamp, paid in full. And Tinca read the collector's slip against it and went quiet, and then she said the thing I already knew and couldn't stand to be true: they didn't un-pay his debt. They opened a new one, in my name, for the crime of remembering him. Grief is the debt, she said. The system can't file a man whose daughter still says his name warm. So it bills the saying. ...I'll keep saying it. Let them add it up. Some sums a person's proud to owe.

objectives.text: Recover the father's paid receipt from the flood-chest
Ask Tinca how a settled debt re-opens

ledger_roads_debt_my_father_left.note: They did not un-pay the father's debt. They opened a new one against the daughter for the crime of remembering him warm. Grief is the debt the system cannot file. So it bills the saying of his name. Paraschiva keeps saying it, and lets them add it up. Some sums a person is proud to owe.

ledger_roads_something_not_owed.title: Something to Sell That Isn't Owed
ledger_roads_something_not_owed.summary: Draga is sick of dealing in debts and wants, for one market-day, to sell a thing that is simply worth what it is. She needs marsh-cultured light gathered so the road-towns can hold a night market that owes no one.
ledger_roads_something_not_owed.offer: Everything I sell on this road is a debt dressed as a bargain -- fares against gates, dossiers against friends, forgeries against fate. Just once I want a stall that sells a thing worth what it is and nothing more. The road-towns want a night market -- proper, lamps and cider and somebody's daughter selling ribbon -- but they can't hold it, because the dark out here reports back and nobody'll stand in it after sundown. Gather me the marsh-light the culture-folk grow, the kind the dark doesn't mind. Lamps that owe nothing. Let these people buy a ribbon without buying a doom with it.
ledger_roads_something_not_owed.progress: Gather cultured marsh-light for the night market from the culture-troughs by the road-town gardens, and bring it to Draga's stall.
ledger_roads_something_not_owed.complete: Look at it. A whole street of lamps that owe nobody a thing, and under them people haggling over ribbon like the world isn't ending, which -- fine, it is, but not tonight, not on this street. A girl sold me a length of blue for more than it's worth and I paid it gladly and that is the first honest overcharge of my whole crooked life. Here. Take the extra light. Keep a lamp that owes nothing. On this road that's rarer than gold, and I should know -- I've priced both.

objectives.text: Gather cultured marsh-light for the night market

objectives.label: the road-town market square
objectives.text: Set the lamps at the market square

ledger_roads_something_not_owed.note: For one night the road-towns held a market lit by lamps that owed no one, and people bought ribbon under them as if the world were not ending. It is ending. Not on that street, not that night. Draga paid a girl over the odds for blue ribbon and called it the first honest overcharge of her crooked life.

ledger_roads_recently_called.title: The Roll of the Recently Called
ledger_roads_recently_called.summary: Maricica keeps a private roll of the road-town dead, honestly. Someone is copying it -- and the copy names people the day before they die. She wants the copyist's leaf found and its hand identified.
ledger_roads_recently_called.offer: A chronicler keeps the dead honestly: name, years, one true thing they did. I keep the road-towns' roll. Someone is keeping a second copy, and the second copy runs ahead of mine -- it names a soul the evening before the road calls them, in a hand almost mine but colder, drier, the loops squared off like a stamp trying to write. Find me the copyist's working leaf. It's out among the shades that carry the reader-stones; they must copy from something. I need to know whose hand learned mine well enough to run a day ahead of death.
ledger_roads_recently_called.progress: The copyist's working leaf is carried among the reader-stone shades on the ford road. Recover it and bring it to Maricica.
ledger_roads_recently_called.complete: It's my hand. That's the answer and it's the one I dreaded. Not forged by a person -- learned by the road, off eleven years of my honest copying, until it can write as me a day before it acts. Every true entry I ever made taught it the shape of truthful. Now it dresses its arithmetic in my letters so the road-towns will trust the roll that kills them. I'll keep writing. What else. But I date every leaf now in a cipher only I know, so that when the copy runs ahead, at least the false one wears the wrong day. A small wrongness. It's the only defense there is.

objectives.text: Recover the copyist's working leaf
Show Maricica whose hand it is

ledger_roads_recently_called.note: The copyist's hand was Maricica's own -- learned by the road off eleven years of honest copying until it could write as her a day ahead of death. Every true entry taught it the shape of truthful. She dates her leaves in a private cipher now, so the false copy wears the wrong day. A small deliberate wrongness. It is the only defense the road allows.

ledger_roads_last_honest_tollpost.title: The Last Honest Toll-Post
ledger_roads_last_honest_tollpost.summary: One toll-post on the whole road still takes copper and gives change, kept by an old warden who won't quit. The debt-checkpoints are closing on it from both ends. Safta wants the road cleared so relief can reach him before he's collected.
ledger_roads_last_honest_tollpost.offer: There's an old warden -- Grigore, worked the ford post forty years -- who still takes a copper toll and gives honest change and writes a receipt a living man can read. One post, on the whole cursed road, that bills coin instead of souls. The debt-checkpoints are closing on him from both ends of the ford like a book shutting, and he won't leave, because leaving is how they win and forty years has made him stubborn about the difference. Clear the road to his post. Let me get a coach of relief through before the checkpoints meet in the middle with him inside.
ledger_roads_last_honest_tollpost.progress: The old warden's post stands at the ford's honest crossing. Reach it and break the debt-checkpoints closing from both ends.
ledger_roads_last_honest_tollpost.complete: Relief's through, and Grigore's post still takes copper, and he gave me change and a receipt and told me to mind the ice like I was nine years old. He knows what's coming. Forty years on this road, he's not a fool. He said: they can close the road from both ends, but a man who gives honest change is a discrepancy they have to do by hand, and he intends to make them do it by hand, slow, one honest copper at a time, until they're sick of him. Take the map. He drew you the safe crossings himself. In copper ink. Of course in copper ink.

objectives.label: the old warden's honest post
objectives.text: Reach the old warden's post
Break the closing checkpoints at the ford

ledger_roads_last_honest_tollpost.note: One post on the whole road still takes copper and gives change, kept by a warden of forty years who will not leave. A man who gives honest change is a discrepancy the road must collect by hand. He means to make them do it by hand, slow, one honest copper at a time, until they sicken of him. He drew the safe crossings in copper ink. Of course he did.

ledger_roads_ones_who_walk_back.title: The Ones Who Walk Back In
ledger_roads_ones_who_walk_back.summary: When Floarea breaks a queue at a checkpoint, the shades reassemble by morning -- but a few come back changed, walking the wrong way, back toward the road-towns. She wants them found and put down before they reach a door.
ledger_roads_ones_who_walk_back.offer: You clear a checkpoint and it's back by morning; I've made my peace with that, the way you make peace with weather. Here's what I can't make peace with. A few of them come back wrong -- collected once, un-filed by us, and now they don't queue. They walk the wrong way. Off the road, toward the towns, toward doors, slow and certain, like they've an appointment with somebody's threshold. They were filed and un-filed and they remember the un-filing as an address. Find them before they find a door. Whatever we did to save them taught them the way home. That's the cost. There's always a cost. I just don't always get to see it walking.
ledger_roads_ones_who_walk_back.progress: The un-filed shades walk the wrong way, off the road toward the road-town doors. Find and put down the ones walking home.
ledger_roads_ones_who_walk_back.complete: Put down, all of them, short of a threshold -- barely. You saw where the last one was headed. Neaga's door. The sister we couldn't quite un-file, come home the only way the road left her. That's the thing about mercy out here: it doesn't cancel, it forwards. Un-file a name and you don't erase the debt, you just change the address it's collected at. I'll not tell Neaga which door it was walking to. Some kindnesses are just the mercy of not saying. Take the writ. Un-file the next one further from home.

objectives.text: Put down the shades walking toward the doors

ledger_roads_ones_who_walk_back.note: The un-filed do not queue -- they walk home, remembering the mercy as an address. Mercy on this road does not cancel; it forwards. Un-file a name and you only change the door the debt is collected at. Floarea will not tell Neaga which door the last one sought. Some kindnesses are only the mercy of not saying.

ledger_roads_buyers_bad_name.title: Buyers for a Bad Name
ledger_roads_buyers_bad_name.summary: Draga has come by a dossier on a toll-baron who sells the living to the checkpoints. Three parties want it: the baron, a Morven agent, and the road itself. She needs it recovered, and a buyer chosen with eyes open.
ledger_roads_buyers_bad_name.offer: I deal in habits, not assumptions. I've a dossier half-made on Iancu the toll-baron -- when he drinks, which crossing he skips his own toll at, and the count of living souls he's sold to the checkpoints to keep his own name off the roll. It's worth a fortune to three buyers. Iancu, who'll pay to bury it. A Morven agent downriver, who'll pay to use it, quietly, on the right neck. And -- I'll say it plain -- the road, which doesn't pay in coin but stops billing you a while, if you feed it a name it wants. Recover the working dossier off the shades that took my courier. Then help me choose. Every choice on this road sells somebody. I'd like, for once, to sell the right somebody.
ledger_roads_buyers_bad_name.progress: Recover the working dossier from the shades that took Draga's courier along the east road, then help her weigh the three buyers.
ledger_roads_buyers_bad_name.complete: We'll give it to the Morven. Not clean -- nothing's clean -- but the agent uses it to pull Iancu's own name onto the roll he's been selling others to, and there's a rough justice in a man being filed by his own trade. Iancu would've buried it and sold three more coaches by spring. The road would've eaten it and grown a mile. This way one wicked account closes and one honest agent owes us a favor and the living he'd have sold get another winter. That's not a clean win. There aren't any. It's the least filthy one on the table, and out here I've learned to reach for that fast, before a filthier one talks me round.

objectives.text: Recover the dossier on the toll-baron
Choose a buyer with Draga

ledger_roads_buyers_bad_name.note: The dossier went to the Morven, who used it to pull the toll-baron's own name onto the roll he had been selling others to. Rough justice: a man filed by his own trade. Not a clean win -- there are none. Draga called it the least filthy one on the table, and has learned to reach for that fast, before a filthier one talks her round.

ledger_roads_room_she_keeps_made.title: The Room She Keeps Made
ledger_roads_room_she_keeps_made.summary: Zamfira keeps a room made up for a son who took the road east years ago and never came back. A courier's slip has surfaced bearing his name, dated old. She wants the truth of it, however it lands, and someone to walk the crossing he was last seen at.
ledger_roads_room_she_keeps_made.offer: There's a room upstairs I keep made -- fresh linen, turned down, a candle set -- for my boy, who took the east road eleven years back to see the grey cities and send for me when he'd made his way. He never sent. I never stopped turning the bed. A slip's come to the post with his name, dated the spring he left, stamped collected, and I have not been able to make my hands unfold it. Ask Tinca what the date means. Then walk out to the east crossing where the carters last saw him, and tell me -- tell me true, I've earned true -- whether there's a bed I should stop making.
ledger_roads_room_she_keeps_made.progress: Ask Clerk Tinca what the old-dated slip means, then walk the east crossing where Zamfira's son was last seen.
ledger_roads_room_she_keeps_made.complete: You walked it. And you didn't lie to spare me, which is the only real kindness left on this road. He was collected the spring he left -- young, and curious, and he read something on a milestone that he shouldn't have, and the road closed his account before he'd finished being a boy. Eleven years I've made a bed for a name in a ledger. ...I'll keep making it. Not for him to come back -- I know now he can't. For me to have somewhere to sit each morning and say he was real, and mine, and warm once. The road can file him. It can't make me stop turning down the bed. Take this. He'd have liked you.

objectives.text: Ask Tinca what the old-dated slip means

objectives.label: the east crossing where he was last seen
objectives.text: Walk the east crossing

ledger_roads_room_she_keeps_made.note: The son was collected the spring he left -- a curious boy who read a milestone he shouldn't have, and the road closed his account before he had finished being young. Zamfira keeps making the bed. Not for his return, which she knows cannot come. For a place to sit each morning and say he was real, and warm once. The road can file him. It cannot stop her turning down the sheet.

ledger_roads_song_for_tollkeeper.title: A Song for the Toll-Keeper
ledger_roads_song_for_tollkeeper.summary: There is an old road-song the toll-keepers used to sing to pass a fare through kindly, and the last woman who knows all the verses is failing. Neaga wants it carried whole to the road-house so the tune outlives her.
ledger_roads_song_for_tollkeeper.offer: There's a song the old toll-keepers sang -- daft, long, a verse for every crossing between here and the sea, and if you sang a fare the right verse they'd wave you through smiling. Batrana Lina up the ford is the last who knows all of it, and her breath's going, and when it goes the song goes, and then there's nothing left on this road that a person made just to be kind. Go sit with her. Let her sing it to you, all the verses, however slow. Then carry it down to Zamfira's, where there's a fire and folk who'll learn it. A song can't be collected. It's got no name to file. That's why it matters that somebody carries it.
ledger_roads_song_for_tollkeeper.progress: Sit with old Lina at the ford and learn all the verses, then carry the road-song down to the road-house at Zamfira's.
ledger_roads_song_for_tollkeeper.complete: You learned it -- all of it, even the wrong-on-purpose verse about the dockmaster that makes Safta go red. Lina sang it through twice and then she laughed, which she hasn't done in a season, and said now she can stop holding her breath for it. And tonight the whole taproom sang it back, the verses out of order and the tune half-wrong and every soul in the place warm and off the roll for the length of it. A song's got no name to file. Lina made a thing the road can't collect. So few of us manage that. Sing it east someday. Let the crossings hear they were loved once.

objectives.text: Learn all the verses from old Lina

objectives.label: the road-house fire
objectives.text: Carry the song to the road-house

ledger_roads_song_for_tollkeeper.note: The road-song passed whole to the road-house fire, and the taproom sang it back off-tune and warm, every soul off the roll for the length of it. A song has no name to file. Old Lina made a thing the road cannot collect, and can stop holding her breath for it now. So few manage that much. It is worth carrying east, so the crossings hear they were loved once.

ledger_roads_milestones_are_moving.title: The Milestones Are Moving
ledger_roads_milestones_are_moving.summary: The milestones no longer measure the same distances. Ioana has paced the north road twice and the stones stand closer each time, as if the road is shortening itself. She wants two suspect stones reached and read before the road folds.
ledger_roads_milestones_are_moving.offer: I pace the road. It's how I keep sane -- so many strides between milestones, always the same, a thing you can count on. It's stopped being the same. I paced the north road Tuesday and again this morning and the stones stand nearer each time, like the road's drawing its own ends together. There's an inscription in the border-country that's the only one getting shorter, they say, the crossroads stone under a dead hand. This road's caught the habit. Reach the two stones that've moved the most and read what's left on them. I need to know if the road's shortening the distance -- or shortening the list of who gets to walk it.
ledger_roads_milestones_are_moving.progress: Reach the two milestones that have moved on the north road and read what remains cut on them.
ledger_roads_milestones_are_moving.complete: You read them. They're not measuring distance anymore. They're counting down -- each one a lower number than it should bear, and not in leagues. In names. The road's telling itself how many are left to collect before it can close, and every night the number's smaller, and the stones move in to match. When the count reaches nothing the road folds up and this whole county's a closed account. I've stopped pacing. Counting the strides was only teaching it to count me. Take the reading-draught. Read the stones blue before you trust a distance out here.

objectives.label: the first moved milestone
objectives.text: Read the first moved milestone

objectives.label: the second moved milestone
objectives.text: Read the second moved milestone

ledger_roads_milestones_are_moving.note: The milestones no longer measure distance. They count down -- not in leagues but in names left to collect before the road can close, drawing their ends together to match. When the count reaches nothing the county is a closed account. Ioana has stopped pacing. Counting her strides was only teaching the road to count her.

ledger_roads_unfiling_carefully.title: Un-Filing, Carefully
ledger_roads_unfiling_carefully.summary: Tinca has worked out that a name un-filed carelessly is only forwarded to worse. She wants writ-stock recovered and a method taught: un-file whole road-town rolls at once, so the road can't tell where the debt went.
ledger_roads_unfiling_carefully.offer: I've been un-filing names one at a time and I've watched where they go -- and Floarea's told you where, hasn't she, they walk home. Un-file a name careless and you just post the debt to a nearer door. But I've worked something out. Un-file a whole roll at once -- every name in a road-town in one stroke -- and the road can't tell which debt went where. It has to sort them by hand, name by name, and while it sorts, nobody's collected. Bring me the writ-stock off the courier the checkpoint took. I'll teach you the trick. It doesn't save them. It buys them time, in bulk, cheap. Time's the only thing that comes cheap here, and only if you steal it wholesale.
ledger_roads_unfiling_carefully.progress: Recover writ-stock from the checkpoint that took Tinca's courier, then learn the wholesale un-filing method from her.
ledger_roads_unfiling_carefully.complete: There. You can un-file a whole roll now, and set the road to sorting by hand for a season. It'll catch up. It always catches up -- it has nothing but time and it's patient the way only a ledger is patient. But a season is a season. A season is a harvest, a wedding, a child learning to walk before its name comes due. I used to think the trade was saving people. It isn't. The trade is stealing them time and making them spend it well. Take the writ. Steal wholesale. Make them spend it well.

objectives.text: Recover writ-stock from the checkpoint
Learn wholesale un-filing from Tinca

ledger_roads_unfiling_carefully.note: Un-file a whole roll at once and the road must sort the debts by hand, name by name, and while it sorts nobody is collected. It always catches up; it has nothing but time. But a season is a harvest, a wedding, a child learning to walk. The trade was never saving people. It is stealing them time and making them spend it well.

ledger_roads_nightly_clear_crossings.title: Nightly: Clear the Crossings
ledger_roads_nightly_clear_crossings.summary: The checkpoints reassemble every night, and every morning a road-town wakes to find a queue at its edge. Zoe keeps a standing bounty for whoever clears the fresh crossings before the first cart runs.
ledger_roads_nightly_clear_crossings.offer: Same as every dawn: they built back overnight. There's a fresh queue at the near crossings and the first carts want to run at light. Clear the shades before the carters reach them -- a carter who joins a queue by accident doesn't come off it. I keep coin aside for this because it never ends and pretending it might only gets people collected. Go on. Same work as yesterday. It's a road; roads are patient. So are we, or we don't last out here.
ledger_roads_nightly_clear_crossings.progress: The fresh crossings sit at the near edges of the road-towns. Clear the overnight shades before the first carts run.
ledger_roads_nightly_clear_crossings.complete: Clear till the carts got through. It'll build again by dark -- it always does -- but the morning run went clean and no carter joined a queue he can't leave. That's the whole of the job out here: not winning, just making it to the next dawn with the same number of people. Here's your coin. Come back tomorrow. There'll be a queue tomorrow. There's always a queue tomorrow. Sleep anyway.

objectives.text: Clear the overnight crossings before the carts run

ledger_roads_nightly_clear_crossings.note: The crossings reassemble every night and are cleared every dawn. Not winning -- only reaching the next dawn with the same number of people. Zoe keeps coin aside because it never ends, and pretending it might only gets people collected. There is always a queue tomorrow. She tells the wardens to sleep anyway.

ledger_roads_nightly_reshoe_wardens.title: Nightly: Re-Shoe the Wardens
ledger_roads_nightly_reshoe_wardens.summary: A warden's horse throwing a shoe at a night crossing is a warden the road finds time for, and the crows work the crossings to make it happen. Anca needs iron scavenged and the flocks thinned, every day, to keep the wardens mounted and whole.
ledger_roads_nightly_reshoe_wardens.offer: It's a daily thing and it'll be a daily thing till the road wins or I die, and I'll take those odds in that order. The wardens ride the night crossings and a thrown shoe puts one afoot in the worst place a person can stand, and the crows have learned it -- they work the horses at the bad crossings on purpose. Thin the flocks so the mounts don't spook, and pull me iron from wherever the road's foundered a post, so I've nails to shoe with come morning. Every day. I know. Honest work is mostly the same day done again until it counts.
ledger_roads_nightly_reshoe_wardens.progress: Thin the crow-flocks at the night crossings and pull cold iron from a foundered post for Anca's morning shoeing.
ledger_roads_nightly_reshoe_wardens.complete: Iron enough for a day's nails and the crossings quieter for a night. The wardens ride out whole tomorrow, which is the only measure of a good day I've got left. It'll all want doing again at dawn. That's not despair, mind -- that's just iron. Iron doesn't stay driven by wishing. You hammer it again and you hammer it again and one of these days the count of wardens who came home holds steady, and that's the day, and you don't know which day it'll be, so you hammer every one. Take your coin.

objectives.text: Thin the crow-flocks at the night crossings
Pull cold iron for the morning shoeing

ledger_roads_nightly_reshoe_wardens.note: Every dawn the wardens must be re-shod, because a thrown shoe at a night crossing is a warden the road finds time for. Anca hammers it again and again toward the day the count of wardens who came home holds steady. She does not know which day that is, so she hammers every one. Honest work is the same day done again until it counts.

ledger_roads_roads_own_ledger.title: The Road's Own Ledger
ledger_roads_roads_own_ledger.summary: Every private truth the road has forged into its milestones was copied from somewhere. Maricica has found where: a master ledger the road keeps at the county's high crossing, and means to read it whole -- the road's own account of itself -- whatever it costs to reach.
ledger_roads_roads_own_ledger.offer: I've followed the copying to its source. The road keeps a master book -- its own ledger, the account from which every forged milestone and future-dated slip is copied out. It's at the high crossing, past the last checkpoint, where the road-country runs up against the fog that reads orange. I mean to read it. Not to stop it -- I'm no fool, one chronicler can't un-write a road. To carry an honest copy of what it plans downriver, to the Reach, to people who might. Get me through the crossing to the master book. Whatever the road's written about this county, I'd rather we knew it than it surprise us name by name.
ledger_roads_roads_own_ledger.progress: Break through the last checkpoint to the master ledger at the high crossing, where the road-country meets the orange fog.
ledger_roads_roads_own_ledger.complete: I read it, and I copied it, and I've written down the one thing worth the walk: the road's ledger isn't finished. There are blank leaves at the back, dated forward, and they're not for this county. They're for the next one over -- the fog's moving, and the road grows toward it, and everything it's done to us it means to do there next, and sooner, having practiced on us. Carry this copy to the Morven at the Reach. Warn them while their leaves are still blank. That's the whole use of an honest record on a crooked road: it can't stop the next page. It can send word ahead of it.

objectives.label: the master ledger at the high crossing
objectives.text: Reach the road's master ledger
Break the last checkpoint before the high crossing

ledger_roads_roads_own_ledger.note: The road's master ledger has blank leaves at the back, dated forward -- not for this county but the next, toward the orange fog the road grows to meet, meaning to do sooner what it practiced here. Maricica carries an honest copy to the Morven at the Reach. A true record cannot stop the next page. It can send word ahead of it. She has gone east to try.

the_archive_the_unclosing_file.title: The File That Will Not Close
the_archive_the_unclosing_file.summary: In the deep stacks, one tablet has been stamped closed and un-closed for a thousand years, the violet ink smeared where a dead hand keeps refusing. The clerk cannot file it. He is beginning to suspect he is not meant to.
the_archive_the_unclosing_file.offer: You've a warm hand. Good. Cold hands make honest ledgers, but this one wants a warm one. Shelf nine, the low aisle -- there's a tablet down there that closes and opens, closes and opens, and has since before my grandmother's grandmother wore the grey. Every clerk who's tried to settle it has stopped sleeping. Read the aisle for me. Just the color of it. Not the name. Never the name.
the_archive_the_unclosing_file.progress: The refusing tablet lies in the deep stacks, shelf nine, the low aisle. Read what colour the ink has learned.
the_archive_the_unclosing_file.complete: Violet, you say, and orange underneath it. Of course. It's a debt the record cannot settle -- a life that keeps declining to be finished. I've catalogued forty years of finished things. This is the first one I've envied. Take the writ. It unfiles what should never have been filed. Use it once, and mean it.

objectives.label: shelf nine, the low aisle
objectives.text: Read the refusing tablet in the deep stacks
Ask the chronicler what a tablet that won't close means

the_archive_the_unclosing_file.note: One tablet in the Archive keeps refusing its own ending -- stamped, un-stamped, stamped again, for a thousand years. Nedelcu has stopped calling it an error. He has started calling it a survivor.

the_archive_the_enshrined_stick.title: A Small Warm Thing, Kept Wrong
the_archive_the_enshrined_stick.summary: In the Founders' Hall, under glass, an herb-woman's walking stick is enshrined with a caption that has forgotten what it was for. The chronicler wants the true telling set beside it -- before the last people who could tell it are collected.
the_archive_the_enshrined_stick.offer: There's a stick in the Founders' Hall, under glass, older than the debt itself. The caption calls it 'Relic, provenance uncertain.' It was a walking stick. It belonged to an herb-woman who fed strangers and asked one question, and it was kept here in the first years because it was small and warm and meant something to one person -- which was the whole idea, once, before we forgot the idea and kept only the keeping. Go to the sentinel-floor. Read the enshrinement. Then come back and tell me we still know why.
the_archive_the_enshrined_stick.progress: The enshrined stick stands under glass in the Founders' Hall, high on the sentinel-floor. Read the caption the Archive forgot.
the_archive_the_enshrined_stick.complete: 'Provenance uncertain.' A thousand years and that's what love matures into: a label that isn't sure. She asked one question, the herb-woman. Nobody wrote it down. That was the point of her and we filed the point away. Here -- a token of a kept name. Carry it. Some names are worth more unfinished than any ledger's worth closed.

objectives.label: the Founders' Hall
objectives.text: Read the enshrined stick's forgotten caption
Put down the sentinels guarding the glass

the_archive_the_enshrined_stick.note: The herb-woman fed six winters of strangers and asked one question. The Archive kept her stick and lost the question. Lupu wrote the true telling on a card. By morning it had been filed.

the_archive_the_orange_annex.title: The Colour Under the Annex
the_archive_the_orange_annex.summary: The apothecary's orange vial screams whenever she stands in the west annex. Something in the deep foundation is writing itself -- a live signal, matured. She needs eyes that will look at the colour and not at the words.
the_archive_the_orange_annex.offer: My reagent's gone loud. Blue is the dead talking, violet is the sub-earth, green is a stone waking -- I've read all of those, calm as tea. But in the west annex my vial reads shifting orange, and shifting orange is the signal in the act of copying itself down. It means a node is live under my feet, here, in the house built to be the counter to exactly that. Walk the annex. Read the depth of the colour. Do not read anything written -- comprehension is how the Courier died, no wound, clean nails, satchel sealed.
the_archive_the_orange_annex.progress: The live signal reads shifting orange in the west annex foundation. Read its depth. Read nothing written near it.
the_archive_the_orange_annex.complete: Deep, then. Deep and warm. The house that files the world to keep it safe has grown a piece of the world's own machinery in its cellar, and it's writing. I'll seal the annex and report the colour to the Accord -- the colour, the place, the depth, and not one word more. Take the counteragent. It quiets the pull. Nothing quiets the fact of it.

objectives.label: the west annex foundation
objectives.text: Read the shifting-orange signal in the annex
Break the golems standing over the live node

the_archive_the_orange_annex.note: The Archive was built as the counter to the stone. In its west annex, the stone is quietly writing itself into the foundation. Toma reported the colour and nothing else. That is all anyone can safely carry out.

the_archive_deadwoods_ledger.title: The Drowned Quarter's Book
the_archive_deadwoods_ledger.summary: A whole district's debt-ledger washed up the water-stair, still faintly warm, and the dockmaster wants it back in the stacks before a lender comes asking. Whoever holds that book holds every account-holder in the Drowned Quarter.
the_archive_deadwoods_ledger.offer: A book came up the water-stair on last night's tide -- the Drowned Quarter's own ledger, every soul in the district filed by what they owe. It's warm to the touch, which books aren't, and it's worth more than the lives in it, which is the whole rotten point of this town. A Wolfarin man will come for it by dusk. Get it into the deep stacks before he does, and let the Quarter keep breathing a little longer.
the_archive_deadwoods_ledger.progress: The warm ledger sits where the tide left it, at the water-stair. Carry it to the deep stacks before the lender comes.
the_archive_deadwoods_ledger.complete: Filed, and filed deep, where his kind need three seals and a magistrate to reach. He'll be furious. Let him. Those are people in that book, not balances -- Sorin's family's in there, and the widow on the third pier. A debt to Wolfarin isn't owed to a man, it's owed to the record, and the record never dies. Tonight it just got harder to read. Take this for the trouble.

objectives.text: Recover the warm ledger from the water-stair

objectives.label: the deep stacks
objectives.text: File the ledger deep, out of a lender's reach

the_archive_deadwoods_ledger.note: A district's whole debt washed up warm on the tide. Iancu buried it deep in the stacks. It bought the Drowned Quarter a season -- and told the tide exactly where the book is kept now.

the_archive_incurious_by_trade.title: Keeping the Reading-Clerks Dull
the_archive_incurious_by_trade.summary: The clerks who copy the oldest tablets are going too still, listening too well. The apothecary brews a draught that keeps a mind blessedly incurious. She needs the reagents before another copyist understands a line and stops being anyone.
the_archive_incurious_by_trade.offer: The copy-clerks read the old tablets all day, and the old tablets read back. Three this month have gone quiet -- properly quiet, the kind where the eyes track something you can't see and the hands lie flat and even on the desk. Comprehension is a wound that leaves no mark. I keep them dull on purpose: an incurious clerk is a living clerk. But my dulling-draught wants tomb-lichen and I'm out. Gather it from the golem-galleries. Quickly.
the_archive_incurious_by_trade.progress: Tomb-lichen grows where the golems walk, in the deep galleries. Gather enough to keep the copyists dull.
the_archive_incurious_by_trade.complete: That'll brew a month of blessed stupidity, saints keep it. You'll think it cruel, dosing bright people dull. It's the kindest thing in this building. The ones who understand the tablets don't scream. They just... finish. Take a charm off the batch -- it'll blunt your own curiosity when the walls start being interesting. They will start being interesting.

objectives.text: Gather tomb-lichen from the golem-galleries
Clear golems from the lichen shelves

the_archive_incurious_by_trade.note: The copy-clerks who understand the tablets do not die. They finish -- filed by their own reading. Toma keeps the rest dull with lichen-draught. Dullness, here, is mercy. Nobody in the Archive says so aloud.

the_archive_the_stamped_line.title: The Line Stamped Twice
the_archive_the_stamped_line.summary: A dignitary needs proof that a rival Morven agent forged a collection-stamp -- closing a living man's account for profit. The evidence is a habits-dossier the Morven keep on their own. Retrieve it, and choose whose file gets thicker.
the_archive_the_stamped_line.offer: Someone stamped a man collected who is demonstrably still walking, and did it to seize what he owed to the record. That is forgery of the highest order in a house where the stamp IS the truth. I want the proof: the Morven keep a dossier of habits on every operative -- their own included. Fetch me the one on the agent who wields that violet stamp too freely. What we do with it after, we'll discuss. Discussion is the whole of my trade.
the_archive_the_stamped_line.progress: The Morven habits-dossier is held in the watcher's reading-room. Retrieve the file on the forging agent.
the_archive_the_stamped_line.complete: There it is -- his hand, his hours, the pier he drinks on. Now: I can hand this to the Accord and end him by treaty, clean. Or I can keep it, and own a man who forges verdicts, which is a far more useful thing to own than a corpse. You look like you'd prefer the clean ending. I've filed enough clean endings to know they're the expensive ones. Here -- for the legwork. The choosing is mine.

objectives.text: Retrieve the Morven habits-dossier
Ask the watcher what the forged stamp really bought

the_archive_the_stamped_line.note: An agent stamped a living man closed, for profit. Ilie holds the proof now. He has not decided whether to spend it on justice or on ownership. In the Archive, that indecision is called leverage, and it is never wasted.

the_archive_bound_keepsake.title: The Keepsake Bound In
the_archive_bound_keepsake.summary: Sorin's mother was collected -- closed, filed, gone. But the Archive keeps every collected soul's one small kept thing, bound into an evidence-drawer. He cannot go into the stacks himself. He asks you to bring her keepsake home.
the_archive_bound_keepsake.offer: They collected my mother in the spring. Closed her account. There's no grave -- collection isn't death, it's worse, it's an ending with a receipt and no body to bury. But the house keeps a thing from everyone it files: one small warm object, bound into a drawer as evidence the life happened. Hers is down there. A cord she wound in her hair. I'd fetch it myself but the sentinels read grief as a debt. Bring me the cord. Let me have that much of her back.
the_archive_bound_keepsake.progress: The collected keep their one small thing in the bound evidence-drawers. Find the cord Sorin's mother wound in her hair.
the_archive_bound_keepsake.complete: That's it. That's hers. Sixty years she wore it and the Archive reduced her to a balance and this cord to 'exhibit.' ...But it kept it. That's the thing I can't hate them clean for. The machine that erased her couldn't quite throw away the one warm proof she'd been here. Take the keepsake she'd have wanted a kind stranger to have. She fed strangers. It ran in the family.

objectives.label: the bound evidence-drawers
objectives.text: Find the collected mother's kept cord
Slip past the sentinels reading grief as debt

the_archive_bound_keepsake.note: The Archive collected Sorin's mother -- closed her, filed her, kept no grave. But it kept the cord from her hair, bound as evidence she lived. He holds it now. The machine that erased her could not quite discard the warm proof of her. That is the cruellest mercy in the building.

the_archive_shroud_of_the_named.title: The Shroud of Kept Names
the_archive_shroud_of_the_named.summary: A shroud in the relic-vault is stitched with the names of everyone the founding Archive chose to keep unfinished -- and the stitches are coming undone, name by name, as the debt-system finalizes each one at last. The apothecary wants a rubbing before the last name pulls loose.
the_archive_shroud_of_the_named.offer: There's a burial-shroud in the relic-vault, older than the debt, embroidered edge to edge with names -- the founders' list of the ones they swore to keep OPEN, never to collect, because they were the proof of why the Archive was worth building. The stitches are letting go. One name a season the thread pulls loose, and that same season, somewhere, the person or their line is finalized. The shroud is a countdown and nobody's counting it. I need it read before it's blank. Copy the names still holding.
the_archive_shroud_of_the_named.progress: The unravelling shroud hangs in the relic-vault. Read the founders' kept names before the thread lets the last one go.
the_archive_shroud_of_the_named.complete: Forty-one still stitched. Last winter it was forty-four. The Archive vowed to keep these lives open forever, and the machine it became is quietly finishing them, one thread a season, and calling it good bookkeeping. Keep the shroud. Wear it if you must walk the deep stacks -- a garment of unfinished names confuses a house that only respects finished ones. It confused me for years.

objectives.label: the relic-vault
objectives.text: Read the shroud's remaining kept names
Take the shroud before the last thread pulls

the_archive_shroud_of_the_named.note: The founders stitched a shroud with the names they swore never to finish. The debt-system unpicks it one thread a season, finalizing each name it releases. Forty-one hold. Nobody in the Archive was counting. Toma counts now, which changes nothing, which is the whole grief of the place.

the_archive_first_edition.title: The First Edition Draught
the_archive_first_edition.summary: The oldest testimonies were taken by mouth, and the founders brewed a draught that let a listener hold a whole warm life without closing it. The recipe survives in one un-filed tablet. Recover it before a Collector's clerk indexes it into a weapon.
the_archive_first_edition.offer: Before the stamps, testimony was taken warm -- a listener drank a draught that let them HOLD a life whole, every messy detail, without the compulsion to define it, close it, finish it. It was mercy in a cup. The recipe's on one tablet the founders deliberately never filed, because filed things get used, and a clerk with that draught could hold a man's whole existence and settle it in an afternoon. It's surfaced in the reading-room. Get it to me before it's indexed. The difference between preserving and processing is one clerk's ambition.
the_archive_first_edition.progress: The un-filed founding recipe has surfaced in the reading-room. Recover it before a Collector's clerk indexes it.
the_archive_first_edition.complete: Ours, and unindexed, saints be thanked. I'll brew a batch the founders' way -- to HOLD, not to close. You've likely saved a hundred testimonies from being read to death by someone efficient. Take a first-edition draught. It'll let you carry a hard truth a while without it hardening you. Drink it the once, for something worth holding. Not for the Archive. For a person.

objectives.text: Recover the un-filed founding recipe
Warn the clerk before the recipe is indexed

the_archive_first_edition.note: The founders brewed a draught to hold a life whole and warm, never to close it -- and never filed the recipe, knowing an indexed mercy becomes a weapon. Toma has it now, unindexed. The line between preserving and processing was always just one clerk's ambition. It held one more day.

the_archive_the_chalk_ward.title: The Handprint on the Canal Wall
the_archive_the_chalk_ward.summary: A woman who feeds the Archive's underfed clerks presses a chalk handprint by her door -- the old kindness-emblem, a thousand years unbroken. She's out of the warding-chalk that keeps the finalized from crossing a fed threshold. Petru asks you to fetch more.
the_archive_the_chalk_ward.offer: There's a woman two stairs down who feeds the copy-clerks the house forgets to feed -- ladles them soup and asks nothing, same as her mother did, and hers. She presses a chalk hand by her door for luck, same little handprint you'll find on an orphanage wall a thousand years and a hundred leagues from here, wherever someone's fed a stranger's child. Runs in the whole warm world, that mark. She's out of the good chalk, the warding kind. Fetch her some from the apothecary's shelf. The finalized won't cross a fed threshold. Odd, that. Kindness holds a door.
the_archive_the_chalk_ward.progress: The soup-woman needs warding-chalk for her handprint. Fetch it and press it fresh by her door.
the_archive_the_chalk_ward.complete: Look at that -- fresh hand on the jamb, and she's already ladling you a bowl for the trouble, which is exactly the sort of thing that gets a person collected in this town and exactly why the door needs warding. Small and warm and stubborn, this whole street of them. A thousand years the machine's been filing everyone and it still can't file a woman with a soup ladle. Take her ward. Press it somewhere that needs holding.

objectives.text: Fetch warding-chalk from the apothecary's shelf

objectives.label: the soup-woman's door
objectives.text: Press the fresh handprint by her door

the_archive_the_chalk_ward.note: A woman feeds the forgotten clerks and presses a chalk hand by her door -- the same kindness-emblem the whole warm world keeps, unbroken a thousand years. The finalized will not cross a fed threshold. In the coldest house in Draconia, a soup ladle still wards a door. Petru counts that a victory. It is one.

the_archive_founders_soup.title: A Bowl for the Empty Chair
the_archive_founders_soup.summary: The Archive's one warm room is Mircea's taproom, and he keeps the founders' custom: the first bowl goes to the empty chair, for whoever the Archive collected that season. He's short on honest ingredients. Help him lay a table the record can't ledger.
the_archive_founders_soup.offer: This is the only room in the whole grey house where the fog stays outside, and I mean to keep it that way. I keep the old custom: first bowl of the night goes to the empty chair -- for whoever they collected this season, so someone still sets a place. But my larder's thin and I won't cook with anything traded off a lender's misery. Fetch me a proper cultured poultice-broth base from the apothecary and river-fish that were caught, not bought. Back by dusk and the second bowl's yours. First's spoken for. That's the rule.
the_archive_founders_soup.progress: Mircea needs an honest broth base and fresh-caught fish for the founders' table. Gather them and bring them warm.
the_archive_founders_soup.complete: Now that's a table. Steam on the windows, the empty chair laid proper, and a bowl for you that no ledger in this building will ever record, thank the saints. They can file a life. They can't file a supper. Every name they close, we set a place -- small thing, warm thing, done anyway. Sit. Eat. The record can wait outside with the fog where it belongs.

objectives.text: Fetch an honest broth base from the apothecary
Bring fresh-caught fish for the founders' table

the_archive_founders_soup.note: In the coldest house in Draconia, Mircea keeps a warm room and the founders' custom: the first bowl for the empty chair, for whoever was collected that season. The machine can file a life. It cannot file a supper. He sets the place anyway. That, and not the stacks, is what the Archive was founded to keep.

the_archive_sentinel_dust.title: What the Sentinels Are Guarding Now
the_archive_sentinel_dust.summary: The Archive's stone sentinels were made to guard the tablets. Lately they have turned to face inward, all of them, toward the deep foundation -- and the dust on the floor has aligned in the same direction. The captain wants them put down before he learns what they're listening to.
the_archive_sentinel_dust.offer: My sentinels have stood the same posts for four hundred years, facing the doors, guarding what's shelved. This month they turned. All of them, one night, to face the cellar -- and the dust on every floor's gone with them, lying in fine parallel lines pointing down and west. They're not guarding the tablets anymore.
