last_hearth_the_reader_who_refused.summary: A scholar came off the canal road copying inscriptions into a notebook -- and the marks have begun surfacing, unbidden, on its blank pages. It is spreading itself. Tinca needs the notebook found and the scholar warned before curiosity finishes him.
last_hearth_the_reader_who_refused.offer: A scholar's taken a room at the far end -- clever man, too clever, been copying stone-marks into a notebook up and down the canal. And I've had a look at that notebook while he slept, saints forgive the trespass, and the marks he copied are showing up on the blank facing pages. On their own. It's spreading itself through his book, and he thinks that's a discovery instead of a symptom. Find him. Get the book. Make him understand that understanding is the door, and he's got one foot through it. He won't want to hear it. The curious never do.
last_hearth_the_reader_who_refused.progress: Find the scholar's notebook at the far rooms and warn him before the marks finish spreading -- and finish him.
last_hearth_the_reader_who_refused.complete: He wouldn't burn it. Of course he wouldn't -- to a curious man, a spreading text is the find of a lifetime, not a death sentence with pages. I took it from him anyway, gently as I could, and it's in a lead box now with the warm tablet, and I'll not open either. Some knowledge you keep by refusing to hold it. Write him in my other ledger if you like -- the kept one -- while he's still a name and not an entry. And thank you. Curiosity's the deadliest thing anyone carries out here, and you carried none.

objectives.label: the scholar's rooms
objectives.text: Find the spreading notebook
Bring the notebook to Tinca

last_hearth_the_reader_who_refused.note: The scholar's copied marks were surfacing unbidden on the blank pages -- spreading itself through his book while he called it a discovery. Tinca took it into a lead box and will not open it. Some knowledge you keep by refusing to hold it. Curiosity is the deadliest thing anyone carries out here.

last_hearth_the_empty_place_set.title: The Empty Place Set
last_hearth_the_empty_place_set.summary: Every night Ioana sets one extra place at the common table -- for whoever the road hasn't given back yet, the way the western villages once set places for the ones walking east. She asks a stranger to fill it, just once, so the empty chair remembers what it's for.
last_hearth_the_empty_place_set.offer: You'll have seen the extra place I set. One plate, one stool, no name to it. The western folk did the same, my mother's stories say -- set a supper for the ones who walked east and didn't come back for it, kept the place warm on the chance. I keep mine for whoever the canal hasn't given back yet. Trouble is, an empty chair set too many nights forgets it's a chair and becomes a grave, and I'll not have a grave at my table. Sit in it tonight. Eat the extra plate. Remind the place what it's kept for.
last_hearth_the_empty_place_set.progress: Take the empty place Ioana sets each night, and fill it -- so the kept chair stays a chair, not a grave.
last_hearth_the_empty_place_set.complete: There. Someone in the chair, breathing, eating, being specific and warm and here. Now it's a place kept, not a grave dug. You'd think the difference is thin. It's the whole difference there is, out here -- between waiting for someone and mourning them, and some nights the only thing holding the first from the second is a stranger willing to sit down. Bless you for sitting. Take a keepsake-cord. Tie it to something you mean to come back to.

objectives.label: the empty place at the table
objectives.text: Fill the empty chair Ioana keeps
Let Ioana see the chair filled

last_hearth_the_empty_place_set.note: Ioana sets one empty place a night for whoever the canal has not given back -- the way the western villages once set suppers for those who walked east. An empty chair set too many nights becomes a grave. A stranger sat in it, and it stayed a chair. That is the whole difference there is.

last_hearth_the_wharf_chest_debt.title: The Wharf-Chest Debt
last_hearth_the_wharf_chest_debt.summary: A forged toll-receipt would clear the Last Hearth's grain-barge past an Archive checkpoint -- and Neaga hates that it has come to forgery, but the alternative is a hungry winter. She needs the papers run to the dockmaster at the wharf before the barge is stopped and its cargo finalized.
last_hearth_the_wharf_chest_debt.offer: I'll be honest with you, because lying wastes both our evenings: I've had a toll-receipt forged. The Archive's checkpoint up-canal would hold our grain-barge on a technicality and finalize the cargo into their stores while our children go thin, and I'll forge a hundred receipts before I let that happen. It's not honest. Honest got the Millrace district finalized. Run these papers to Casandra at the wharf before the barge casts off. If the checkpoint reads them true, we eat this winter. If not -- well. Run fast.
last_hearth_the_wharf_chest_debt.progress: Carry the forged toll-receipt to Casandra at the wharf before the grain-barge casts off for the checkpoint.
last_hearth_the_wharf_chest_debt.complete: It's aboard? Then we eat. I'll not pretend I feel clean about it -- somewhere a clerk will note a discrepancy that never was, and somewhere that discrepancy becomes a person's problem, that's how the ledger works, it never forgives, only relocates. But my discrepancy fed forty families and the clerk's is only ink. I'd forge it again. Take your cut and don't tell Tinca -- she keeps a cleaner ledger than mine, and I'd rather not be a line in it tonight.

objectives.text: Take up the forged receipt from Neaga
Run the receipt to Casandra before the barge sails

last_hearth_the_wharf_chest_debt.note: Neaga forged a toll-receipt to feed forty families past an Archive checkpoint, and does not feel clean about it. A false discrepancy fed children; a clerk's true one is only ink. The ledger never forgives -- it only relocates. She would forge it again.

last_hearth_names_for_the_kerbs.title: Names for the Kerbs
last_hearth_names_for_the_kerbs.summary: The kept graves need their names re-cut before the canal-damp eats them illegible -- for an unreadable name, Tinca holds, is a name the fog can finalize. She sends for fresh chalk-and-lead each day to keep the letters warm and readable.
last_hearth_names_for_the_kerbs.offer: The damp eats the kerbs faster than I can keep them. A name gone soft in the stone is a name half-erased, and a name half-erased is exactly the loose thread the fog pulls. So I re-cut them -- chalk the letters, run lead into the cuts, keep them readable one more season. It's endless. That's rather the point; endless keeping against endless taking, and the day I stop is the day the fog wins a name. Fetch me chalk-and-lead from Maricica's, would you? I've a kerb to keep before the light goes.
last_hearth_names_for_the_kerbs.progress: Bring fresh chalk-and-lead from the forge so Tinca can re-cut a fading kerb before the damp finishes it.
last_hearth_names_for_the_kerbs.complete: Readable another season. Good. You can put your finger in the cut and feel the name, and a name you can feel is a name that happened, and a thing that happened stays out of the never-was. Come again tomorrow -- there's always another kerb going soft, always another name the damp's decided it's tired of. The keeping doesn't finish. Neither do we, if we're stubborn. Stubborn's the whole counter, in the end.

objectives.text: Bring chalk-and-lead to re-cut the kerbs

last_hearth_names_for_the_kerbs.note: Tinca re-cuts the kept names before the damp eats them soft -- for an unreadable name is the loose thread the fog pulls. Endless keeping against endless taking. The day she stops is the day the fog wins a name. Stubborn is the whole counter, in the end.

last_hearth_the_barge_that_wouldnt_dock.title: The Barge That Wouldn't Dock
last_hearth_the_barge_that_wouldnt_dock.summary: A grain-barge drifts in from up-canal with no crew aboard, three bedrolls cold, footprints going down into the hold and none coming back. Casandra needs it boarded and read at the open piers before it's let near the warm town -- a finalized crew is a cargo, not a rescue.
last_hearth_the_barge_that_wouldnt_dock.offer: There's a barge come down the canal on the current alone, no hand on the pole, drifting for our wharf like it knows the way. I've held it off with lines. Three bedrolls aboard, gone cold. Footprints down into the hold. None coming back up. You know that pattern -- everyone out here knows that pattern now, and it never means anyone's hiding, it means the hold's got something and the crew went to understand it. Board her at the far piers. Read what's below under sight-light. Don't go curious, and for the love of every warm window, don't bring her cargo to my wharf.
last_hearth_the_barge_that_wouldnt_dock.progress: Board the crewless barge at the far piers and read the hold under sight-light -- without reading what took the crew.
last_hearth_the_barge_that_wouldnt_dock.complete: A stone in the hold, reading orange -- they were hauling a transmission-point down the canal and one of them got curious in the dark and then they all did, one after another, down the footprint-trail into understanding. I've cut the barge loose to burn on the ebb. Cruel to the grain, but grain doesn't listen and a live stone does. You did right coming back up those stairs. Most who go down after the curious don't. Take this. And put the hold out of your mind -- that's an order, and a kindness.

objectives.label: the drifting barge at the far piers
objectives.text: Board and read the crewless barge's hold
Report the hold to Casandra

last_hearth_the_barge_that_wouldnt_dock.note: A crewless barge drifted in with a live stone in its hold and a footprint-trail of the curious going down and not back up -- one got curious in the dark, and then they all did. Casandra burned the barge on the ebb. Grain does not listen. A live stone does.

last_hearth_the_deep_bed_light.title: The Deep-Bed Light
last_hearth_the_deep_bed_light.summary: Maricica forges deepbed-lanterns whose flame burns from oil drawn where the peat is coldest -- and the Hearth's old folk trust them over any Archive light. She wants to finish a batch for the winter but needs cold-peat oil hauled from the deepest cut.
last_hearth_the_deep_bed_light.offer: There's a light I make that the old ones won't do without -- deepbed-lanterns, flame drawn off oil from the coldest peat, the deep cuts where the ground never once went warm. Archive light's brighter, they'll tell you. Aye, and Archive light was made by folk who file people, so the old ones would sooner squint. I've the lantern-bodies forged and cooling. What I want is the oil -- hauled from the deepest cut, where cold means honest. Fetch me a good measure and I'll finish the batch before the frosts.
last_hearth_the_deep_bed_light.progress: Haul cold-peat oil from the deepest cut so Maricica can finish the winter's deepbed-lanterns.
last_hearth_the_deep_bed_light.complete: Cold oil, deep-drawn -- perfect. Feel how it doesn't warm your hand? That's the honesty of it. These'll burn all winter in the old folk's windows, and every one's a small stubborn no to the dark. Here, the first off the batch is yours -- carry a light that was made by warm hands out of cold-honest oil, in a town that files nobody. That's about as good as a lantern gets, this far down the tide.

objectives.text: Haul cold-peat oil from the deepest cut

last_hearth_the_deep_bed_light.note: Maricica's deepbed-lanterns burn on oil from the coldest peat, where cold means honest, and the old folk trust them over any brighter Archive light -- for Archive light was made by folk who file people. Each burning window is a small stubborn no to the dark.

last_hearth_the_watchers_two_asks.title: The Watcher's Two Asks
last_hearth_the_watchers_two_asks.summary: The fog has begun reading shifting orange wherever a stranger stands -- the signal has taken an interest in the one who carried the counter. Ilinca asks two things: put down the shamblers the interest is drawing, and hold one last watch in the middle of the orange to prove the interest can be refused.
last_hearth_the_watchers_two_asks.offer: Sit. This is the hard one. The fog's begun reading orange wherever you stand -- I've watched it happen, a ring of it, following you like a dog that's chosen its master. The signal's noticed you. It notices everyone who carries the counter, because you're exactly the material it wants: warm, stubborn, and now knowing how to fight it. So. Two asks. The shamblers are thick tonight, drawn to that ring the way moths are -- put them down. Then hold a watch in the middle of the orange and give it nothing. Prove to it, and to yourself, that being noticed isn't being taken.
last_hearth_the_watchers_two_asks.progress: Put down the shamblers the signal's interest is drawing, then hold the watch in the orange ring and refuse it.
last_hearth_the_watchers_two_asks.complete: You held in the middle of it and gave it nothing. Good. Better than good -- necessary. The signal will keep noticing you, all the way to wherever your road ends, and there's no charm for that, no salt-line, no ward. There's only what you just did: stand in the interest and stay yourself. Being noticed isn't being taken. Carry that when you leave the Hearth, because leave you will -- your road runs down to the Pit, I've seen the shape of it in you. Go warm at the fire. It may be a while before you find another.

objectives.text: Put down the shamblers the signal draws

objectives.label: the orange ring
objectives.text: Hold the watch in the orange -- give it nothing
Report the refused watch to Ilinca

last_hearth_the_watchers_two_asks.note: The fog reads orange wherever the counter-bearer stands now -- the signal has chosen its material. There is no ward for being noticed. There is only standing in the interest and staying yourself. Being noticed is not being taken. Ilinca has seen the road's shape: it runs down to the Pit.

last_hearth_last_warm_thing.title: The Last Warm Thing
last_hearth_last_warm_thing.summary: Before the road down to the Pit, Ioana asks the traveler to choose one small, specific, imperfect warm thing from all they've kept in the Hearth -- for the counter is not power but a particular memory, and it must be theirs, and it must be true. She sends them off named.
last_hearth_last_warm_thing.offer: So you're for the Pit. I've watched it come toward you since the night you first warmed at that fire, and there's no talking a person off a road the stone's already drawn. But before you go, sit with me one more time, because I've a thing to give you that isn't in any chest. The counter isn't a weapon, dear. It's a memory. A small, specific, imperfect, stubborn warm thing that happened and that you carry -- the trout Zoe finally ate, the chair someone sat in, a name read aloud in the yard. When you stand where the stone is, you'll need one that's truly yours. Choose it here, at the fire, where I can see you choose it. Then let me say your name once, so you carry that too.
last_hearth_last_warm_thing.progress: Sit one last time at Ioana's fire and choose the small warm thing you'll carry down to the Pit -- one that is truly yours.
last_hearth_last_warm_thing.complete: That's the one. I saw it settle in you. Small, and stupid, and yours, and true -- that's exactly what the perfect empty past can't stand, a thing too specific and too warm to file into never-was. Carry it down. When the stone offers you rest -- and it will, it offers everyone rest, that's its one trick -- you set that warm thing against the offer and you refuse. Now hold still. There. I've said your name, and Tinca's written it in the kept book, and whatever the tide does to the rest of us, you happened, and you were warm, and you were here. Go on. The fire will be lit when you're back. It's always lit. That's the whole of what we do.

objectives.label: Ioana's fire, one last time
objectives.text: Choose your warm thing at the fire
Let Ioana say your name and send you named

last_hearth_last_warm_thing.note: Before the road to the Pit, Ioana had the traveler choose one small, specific, imperfect warm thing to carry -- for the counter is not power but a memory too warm to file into never-was. She said their name once, Tinca wrote it in the kept book, and sent them down named. The fire, she said, is always lit. That is the whole of what they do.

orange_fog_the_thinning_line.title: Where the Map Stops Meaning
orange_fog_the_thinning_line.summary: The fog has a line it keeps, and the line has been moving west by a hand's width a season for longer than Lenuta has kept the count. She wants the newest edge walked and named, before the naming stops working.
orange_fog_the_thinning_line.offer: Everyone comes to the fog to forget where they were going. I stay to remember where it stops. There is a line out east where the ground first goes warm underfoot and the light turns the color of a bruise healing wrong -- the edge of the reach. I am too old to walk to it and too honest to pretend it hasn't moved again. Go to the marker-cairn at the thinning, stand where the last clean grass gives out, and come tell me what the fog is doing this year. Don't read anything. Whatever's chalked out there, look at your boots.
orange_fog_the_thinning_line.progress: The thinning line runs the eastern waste. The marker-cairn stands where the grass gives out. Walk to it -- and keep your eyes low.
orange_fog_the_thinning_line.complete: Warm ground, dust in lines, and the fog gone that shifting orange even a blind woman could feel on her teeth. A hand's width west again -- no. Two, this year. It's hungry lately. I'll cut the new line into the wall with the old ones. Someone should keep the count, even if the count is only how fast we're losing. Especially then.

objectives.label: the marker-cairn at the thinning
objectives.text: Walk to the thinning line
Clear the wisps drawn to the edge

orange_fog_the_thinning_line.note: Lenuta cut the new line beside the old ones -- a ladder of losses climbing west across her wall. The fog moved two hands this year. The count is honest. The count does not help.

orange_fog_kept_name.title: One Name Kept
orange_fog_kept_name.summary: Floarea watches the finalized walk their old routes through the fog -- closed files that forgot to lie down. She has learned the one thing that stops one: not a blade, but a name said with enough of the small, true weight only the living carry.
orange_fog_kept_name.offer: You've seen them by now. Men and women who were collected -- closed, filed, done -- walking their errands anyway, because a thousand years of habit is heavier than a verdict. Steel does nothing; you can't kill what's already stamped dead. But I found a trick, and it's a cruel one. Bring me a kept-name token from the ones that dissolve when the fog thins -- each holds a name the ledger never quite balanced. Say the name to the walker it belongs to, with everything you know about who it was, and the file can't close over that much stubborn truth. It comes apart. It rests. Four tokens. Four names given back.
orange_fog_kept_name.progress: The finalized walk the old lanes; the kept-name tokens fall from the wisps at the thinning. Gather them, and give each name back to the one it fits.
orange_fog_kept_name.complete: Four rested. Not saved -- there's no saving a closed account -- but rested, which is the most this place sells. You said the names right, I can tell; the ones you got wrong just keep walking. It's the specific that undoes them. Not the true. The specific. A wrong life said exactly is worth more here than a right one said in general. Remember that where you're going. You'll need it below.

objectives.text: Gather the names the ledger left unbalanced
Thin the wisps for the tokens they carry
Return the kept names to Floarea

orange_fog_kept_name.note: Four of the finalized came apart on their own names, said with all their small wrongness intact. Floarea keeps a list of the ones she still can't fit a name to. It is long. She reads it aloud sometimes, guessing, so the guessing at least is warm.

orange_fog_the_dead_touch.title: A Living Touch Receives
orange_fog_the_dead_touch.summary: A blind seer washed up on the black shingle speaking one sentence over and over: a living touch receives, a dead touch transmits. Anca thinks it is the only true thing said in the fog in a hundred years. She wants the seer's testimony carried to the last warm door before he finishes dissolving.
orange_fog_the_dead_touch.offer: A man came up out of the water three days back -- eyeless, but he sees something, and he won't stop saying it. A living touch receives; a dead touch transmits. He's coming apart the way they all do out here, slower because he keeps refusing to. He says he had it once already, a thousand years gone, and lost it, and it came round again the way everything here comes round. Take his testimony to the Last Hearth before there's nothing left to take. Don't read it on the way. If you understand it, you receive -- and this fog collects what it receives. Carry it dead. Deliver it warm.
orange_fog_the_dead_touch.progress: The seer fails on the black shingle. Take his sealed testimony west to the Last Hearth -- and do not read it, whatever the fog offers you at the rests.
orange_fog_the_dead_touch.complete: You carried it sealed. Good. He finished dissolving an hour after you left -- smiling, they say, which none of them do. The Hearth has the key now: what the machine hungers to receive, you refuse, and instead you push one small stubborn wrong-shaped true thing back into it. A living touch feeds it. A dead touch, holding tight to one imperfect day, can overwrite it. That's the whole map, in the end. That's the only errand left. Rest tonight. Tomorrow you go down.

objectives.text: Take the seer's testimony from Anca

objectives.label: the road to the Last Hearth
objectives.text: Carry the sealed testimony west
Keep the road clear of the drawn wisps

orange_fog_the_dead_touch.note: The seer came apart smiling, which the finalized never do. His sentence went west sealed and unread. A living touch receives; a dead touch transmits. The fog offered to explain it, at every fire. You looked at your boots.

orange_fog_the_last_ledger.title: The Ledger of the Drowned Quarter
orange_fog_the_last_ledger.summary: Draga was a clerk before the fog took her posting, and she has recovered a Drowned Quarter ledger from a wisp-haunted lane. One account in it is stamped collected, un-stamped, and stamped again -- a dead hand still arguing. She wants to know whose.
orange_fog_the_last_ledger.offer: Habit is the last thing to go. I still keep books out here, though the only debts left are owed by the fog to the people it ate. Look -- I pulled a ledger from the drowned lane, Quarter-issue, water to the spine. Every line stamped collected in violet. Every line but one, near the bottom: stamped, un-stamped, stamped again, the ink smeared where a hand kept refusing the verdict after the hand was cold. I want that line's name matched to a face before the fog thins the pages to nothing. Bring me kept-name tokens off the walkers in the lane -- one of them is the argument in that book.
orange_fog_the_last_ledger.progress: The drowned lane holds the walker the smeared line belongs to. Take kept-name tokens from the wisps there and bring them to Draga to match.
orange_fog_the_last_ledger.complete: There. The third token -- that's the hand. A tally-woman, small debt, paid twice by the record's own showing, collected anyway because the machine doesn't audit itself. She's been un-stamping her own line for a thousand years, cold, refusing. The ledger's a rotten thing and I hate that I love keeping it. But now the line has a face, and a face is one thing the fog can't quite finalize. Keep the book. It's proof the count was always wrong.

objectives.text: Take names from the drowned lane's walkers
Clear the wisps over the drowned lane
Match the smeared line to a face with Draga

orange_fog_the_last_ledger.note: The smeared line had a face at last -- a tally-woman, paid twice, collected once, refusing ever since. Draga keeps the ledger as evidence the machine miscounts. It miscounts by exactly one, over and over, and the one is always someone.

orange_fog_yeastless_bread.title: Bread That Will Not Rise
orange_fog_yeastless_bread.summary: Rada keeps the last inn this side of the fog and her starter died the day the reach reached her cellar. She has a wilder notion: a live culture from the Last Hearth road might hold, if someone fetches it before the yeast-cold gets it. Warm bread would mean something out here.
orange_fog_yeastless_bread.offer: I'll tell you the truth no traveler wants: my bread's been flat and grey a year now, and I stopped apologizing for it the way my grandmother stopped, because apologizing is a thing you do when it might get better. But I heard the Hearth folk keep a starter that's older than the fog and stubborner. If you fetch me a living culture off the west road and run -- run, mind, the yeast-cold's fast -- I'll have one warm loaf up before it dies. One loud warm night in the fog. That's not nothing. In here, that's nearly everything.
orange_fog_yeastless_bread.progress: A living culture waits with a west-road trader; the yeast-cold kills it slow if you hurry. Bring the culture back to Rada's oven while it still breathes.
orange_fog_yeastless_bread.complete: It rose. It ROSE -- look at it, the fool thing, puffed up proud like it doesn't know where it is. Sit. Eat it hot, that's the only way it's honest, tomorrow it'll be grey again and I'll not lie to you about that. But tonight the room's warm and the bread's alive and for a few hours nobody in here is listening for anything on the road. Bless you. Here's coin, and take a hearth-sign for your door, wherever your door ends up being.

objectives.text: Fetch a living culture off the west road
Bring the culture to Rada's oven

orange_fog_yeastless_bread.note: The bread rose for one night and the inn was warm and loud, the way an inn ought to be, once. By morning the starter was grey again. Rada did not apologize. She had already decided the one night was worth the fetching.

orange_fog_the_chalk_hand.title: The Small Warm Print
orange_fog_the_chalk_hand.summary: Otilia found a child's chalk handprint on a fog-wall, the twin of an emblem a thousand years and a hundred leagues gone. Something has begun cataloguing it. She wants it warded before the fog files it like everything else.
orange_fog_the_chalk_hand.offer: There's a wall out west where a child pressed a chalk hand, long ago, in a kinder place than this -- I know the mark; my grandmother's grandmother knew it, an orphanage sign from before the fog was a word. It's the last warm small thing for leagues. And something's been drawing a careful border round it in the same chalk. Protecting it, or cataloguing it -- I can't tell, and I don't think the thing drawing it can either. Take this ward. Set the salt-line and the folk-warding round the print before the fog decides which it's doing. Some things you keep by refusing to understand them.
orange_fog_the_chalk_hand.progress: The chalk hand is on the far fog-wall, half-bordered already. Ward it with the salt-line and folk-warding before the reach finishes cataloguing it.
orange_fog_the_chalk_hand.complete: You set the line. Good. The border it was drawing stopped -- just stopped, mid-curl, like whatever held the chalk lost the thread. Maybe it was protecting the hand all along and only needed to be told it was allowed. Maybe it was filing it and we've bought the print a season. I'll not pretend to know. A small warm hand on a cold wall, kept a little longer by people who wouldn't look too close. That's the whole trade we're in out here. Take the charm. Keep something small yourself.

objectives.label: the fog-wall with the chalk hand
objectives.text: Reach the chalk handprint on the wall
Drive off the wisps gathering at the wall

orange_fog_the_chalk_hand.note: The chalk hand kept, one more season, warded by people who took care not to understand what was drawing the border round it. It is the same hand a child pressed in Angel Wings, a thousand years upstream of everyone now standing near it. The fog has not filed it yet.

orange_fog_wisplight_harvest.title: Marsh of Cold Lanterns
orange_fog_wisplight_harvest.summary: Ruxandra sells light to people crossing the fog, and light is the one honest trade left out here. The wisps carry a cold marsh-glow she can bottle. It is grim work and she is cheerfully unbothered by that, which is how she has stayed sane.
orange_fog_wisplight_harvest.offer: Everyone out here's got a long face about the fog, and fair enough, but a woman's got to eat, and grief doesn't fill a lamp. The wisps -- the orange drifters -- carry a cold light in them, and folk crossing to the Hearth pay dear for a lantern that won't gutter in the reach. Grim harvest, aye. I've made peace with grim; grim and I are old married people, we bicker but we manage. Thin a good number of them and bring me their light. I'll cut you in, and I'll not once tell you it's noble. It isn't. It's just warm to hold, which around here I'll take.
orange_fog_wisplight_harvest.progress: The wisps drift the low marsh, cold light in each. Thin them and bring the marshlight back to Ruxandra's stall.
orange_fog_wisplight_harvest.complete: There's my stock. Grim as promised and every bit as bright. You'd be amazed what a body will pay for a light that holds in the reach -- or you wouldn't, if you'd watched them cross. Here, your cut, and take a jar of it for yourself; the fog's no place to be caught dark. Don't thank me, it spoils the arrangement. We're two people making a small hard living off a large soft catastrophe. Cheers to that, I suppose. Somebody's got to keep the lamps lit.

objectives.text: Thin the wisps for their cold light
Bottle the marshlight they drop

orange_fog_wisplight_harvest.note: Ruxandra bottles the wisps' cold light and sells it to the crossing folk, cheerfully and without one word of nobility. It is a small hard living off a large soft catastrophe. Her lamps hold in the reach when nothing else does. Somebody has to keep them lit.

orange_fog_dust_in_rows.title: The Dust Lies Listening
orange_fog_dust_in_rows.summary: Ecaterina walks the fog's inner posts and swears the dust has begun aligning in fine parallel lines pointing all one way -- the oldest symptom, this deep. She wants a second pair of eyes on the ground before she trusts her own.
orange_fog_dust_in_rows.offer: I've held posts in the fog eleven years and I know the difference between wind-drift and the other thing. The dust in the inner lanes has gone to lines -- fine, even, all pointing the one way, the way it does when a thing nearby is writing and the world lines up to be read. It's the first symptom, they teach you, the quietest. If I'm seeing it this deep it means the reach has a new mouth open somewhere near. Walk the three inner posts with me in mind, mark where the lines all point, and put down what's drawn to the writing. I need to not be imagining this. And I need to be.
orange_fog_dust_in_rows.progress: The inner posts hold the aligned dust. Walk them, note where the lines point, and clear the wisps drawn to whatever is writing.
orange_fog_dust_in_rows.complete: All three point the same way -- east and down, toward the black shingle. You saw it too. Good. Bad, but good. It means there's a stone waking near the water, a new mouth, and it means I'm not losing the last thing I trust, which is my own two eyes. I'll post the warning and move the fires back a lane. We don't fight it. We keep the count and we keep the distance. That's the whole of the guard's craft out here: knowing exactly how close the end is, and standing one step further off.

objectives.label: the inner posts
objectives.text: Read where the dust-lines point
Clear the wisps drawn to the new mouth

orange_fog_dust_in_rows.note: The dust pointed east and down, toward the black water -- a new mouth waking near the shingle. Ecaterina moved the fires back one lane. The guard's craft in the fog is knowing exactly how near the end is, and standing one step further off. Not fighting it. Counting it.

orange_fog_do_not_read.title: The Reading Still
orange_fog_do_not_read.summary: A dead alchemist's reading-still lies in a wisp-lane, the reagent inside gone the color that means a live signal is writing nearby. Draga wants it recovered and capped before some curious crosser drinks understanding and dies of it.
orange_fog_do_not_read.offer: There's a still out in the eastern lane -- alchemist's kit, good work, the owner face-down beside it with clean fingernails and no wound, which you'll have learned by now is how the fog kills the clever ones. He read something. The still's reagent has gone shifting orange -- it's reading too, on its own, transcribing whatever's near. Some fool crossing to the Hearth will find it, think it's a draught, and drink comprehension straight. Fetch it and cap it, and for the love of every warm thing, do not look into it. The color pulls. Look at your boots and carry it capped.
orange_fog_do_not_read.progress: The reading-still lies by its dead owner in the eastern lane. Recover it, cap it, and do not look into the reagent -- the wisps guard the lane.
orange_fog_do_not_read.complete: Capped and dead-blind, good. You didn't look -- I can tell, your eyes still have you behind them. That still would've taken the next curious soul the way it took its maker: no wound, clean hands, satchel sealed, gone. I'll bury it under salt where no crosser digs. The clever die first out here, always have. The trick to the fog isn't being clever. It's being clever enough to stop. Not many manage the second half. You did. Keep the striker -- you've the hands for careful work.

objectives.text: Recover and cap the reading-still, unread
Clear the wisps guarding the eastern lane
Bring the capped still to Draga

orange_fog_do_not_read.note: The still was capped unread and buried under salt where no crosser digs. Its maker died the fog's clean way -- no wound, clean hands, gone into what he understood. The trick to the fog is not being clever. It is being clever enough to stop. Draga notes how few manage the second half.

orange_fog_the_still_market.title: The Market That Kept Its Stalls
orange_fog_the_still_market.summary: A finalized crowd holds a market in the fog -- buyers and sellers frozen mid-trade, filed a thousand years ago, still going through the motions. Zamfira grew up nearby and wants the ones she knew given rest before the market becomes a landmark on someone's map.
orange_fog_the_still_market.offer: You'll come on a market out west that shouldn't be there -- stalls, folk, a whole trading-day held still, the buyers with coin half-out, the sellers with the scale half-tipped. Finalized, all of them, mid-haggle, a thousand years and they never finished the sale. My people came from that lane. I know some of those faces the way you know faces from a portrait. I can't give the whole market rest -- nobody could -- but the ones I knew, I can name. Take the tokens off the walkers there. Bring them, and I'll say who they were, and you'll give it back to them. It's the only errand I've got left that's mine.
orange_fog_the_still_market.progress: The still market holds Zamfira's people among the finalized. Take kept-name tokens from its walkers and bring them to her to name.
orange_fog_the_still_market.complete: That one -- the fishwife, loud as weather, my great-aunt swore by her eels. And that, the boy who swept for coppers. Say them back with the loud and the small intact and they come apart glad. There. Three of mine, rested, out of a market of hundreds I'll never reach. It isn't enough. Nothing out here is enough; enough was never on offer. But three faces I loved are done haggling, and the fog's got that much less of them. I'll take the not-enough. It's what there is.

objectives.text: Take names from the still market's walkers
Thin the wisps over the frozen stalls
Let Zamfira name her people

orange_fog_the_still_market.note: Three of the still market's hundreds came apart glad, named by a woman who loved the faces from her family's stories. It was not enough. Enough was never on offer in the fog. Zamfira took the not-enough, because it was what there was, and a fishwife she never met is done haggling.

orange_fog_forge_gone_cold.title: The Forge the Fog Forgot to Take
orange_fog_forge_gone_cold.summary: Stanca keeps the fog's last living forge and works it against the reach out of sheer contrariness. Her fire needs a stubborn fuel that will not go yeast-cold, and the wisps drift over a seam of it. She means to keep one forge ringing where the world went quiet.
orange_fog_forge_gone_cold.offer: The fog kills fire slow -- not a wind, a cold that comes up through the anvil and takes the heart out of the coals. Every other forge for leagues went dark and quiet, the way this place likes things. Mine rings. It rings because I'm too stubborn to let it stop, and stubborn is the one thing the reach can't file. But I need fuel with some fight in it -- there's a seam of blackglass out by the wisp-lanes that burns hot and cold-proof. Thin the drifters and bring me what you can prise loose. As long as one forge rings out here, the fog hasn't finished. I intend to be the last hammer it hears.
orange_fog_forge_gone_cold.progress: The blackglass seam lies among the wisp-lanes. Thin the drifters and bring the fuel to Stanca's forge before her coals go cold.
orange_fog_forge_gone_cold.complete: Hear that? Still ringing. Blackglass in the coals and they're hot to the heart again -- cold-proof, spiteful, perfect. I'll get another season of honest steel out of this, maybe two, and every strike of it is one the fog didn't get to keep quiet. They say stubbornness undoes the finalized. I say stubbornness is the whole of the craft: you keep the fire because it's yours, and you don't ask the dark's permission. Take the blade. I made it to outlast me, which out here isn't the boast it used to be.

objectives.text: Thin the wisps over the blackglass seam
Prise loose cold-proof fuel
Bring the blackglass to Stanca's forge

orange_fog_forge_gone_cold.note: Stanca's forge still rings where every other fire went quiet. She keeps it lit out of contrariness, which she names as craft: you keep the fire because it is yours, and you do not ask the dark's leave. She means to be the last hammer the fog ever hears. It is a small war. She is winning her corner of it.

orange_fog_wells_gone_copper.title: The Well That Learned the Taste
orange_fog_wells_gone_copper.summary: The fog's crossing-well has gone copper overnight -- the second symptom, arrived at the one water source travelers trust. Otilia needs the source found and a salt-brine cure carried before the next crossing party drinks the reach.
orange_fog_wells_gone_copper.offer: There's one well the crossing folk drink from -- the only clean water between here and the Hearth, and it went copper in the night. You know the taste; everyone learns it eventually. It means a stone woke near the source. If the crossers drink it they start listening, and listening is how the fog gets its hooks in before you've even felt afraid. I've a brine cure that'll bind the copper for a while -- a while, not forever, there's no forever out here. Find the source, pour the cure, and thin whatever's drawn to the fresh writing. Do it before the next party comes through thirsty and trusting.
orange_fog_wells_gone_copper.progress: The crossing-well's source lies up the fog-lane, newly coppered. Find it, pour the salt-brine cure, and clear the wisps drawn to the waking stone.
orange_fog_wells_gone_copper.complete: It'll run clean a season now -- the brine binds the copper, and the crossers can drink and not start listening, this year. Next year's not mine to promise. My great-grandmother had the saying: when the well goes copper, count your candles. She never once laughed saying it, and now I know why -- it's not a joke, it's an instruction. Count what warmth you've got, and mind how fast it's going. You bought this water a season. In the fog, a season is a gift. Take the tonic. Drink from the clean side.

objectives.label: the coppered well-source
objectives.text: Find the well's coppered source
Clear the wisps at the waking stone
Draw off the bound-copper cure

orange_fog_wells_gone_copper.note: The crossing-well runs clean a season more, the copper bound with brine that will not hold forever. When the well goes copper, count your candles -- Otilia's great-grandmother's instruction, said without laughing, because it was never a joke. The crossers may drink and not start listening. This year.

orange_fog_the_listeners.title: Those Who Stopped Finishing Sentences
orange_fog_the_listeners.summary: Three crossers who camped too near the reach have fallen to listening -- standing still, heads tilted, going quiet by degrees. Mira knows the counter is to keep them talking about small specific things, and needs them walked out before the fog finishes them.
orange_fog_the_listeners.offer: Three came through last week and camped a lane too far east, against my telling. Now they stand in the reach, heads tilted, not finishing their sentences. Listening. It's how it starts -- partial, the fog only half in them yet, and half is the last chance. You can pull a listener back if you make them say something small and exact: their mother's name, the color of their door, what they had for breakfast the day it all went wrong. Specific and warm and theirs. Go to them, get them talking about small true things, and walk them west of the line before the fog gets the other half. Talk fast. They're already quiet.
orange_fog_the_listeners.progress: The three listeners stand in the reach, half-taken. Reach their camp, keep them talking about small specific things, and clear the wisps drawn to their stillness.
orange_fog_the_listeners.complete: Two of the three came back -- blinking, cross, complaining about the cold, which is the loveliest sound the fog knows: a person annoyed enough to argue is a person still theirs. The third was too far; she wouldn't say her door's color, only tilted further. We walked the two out and left the third a candle, which is a lie we tell ourselves out here. Small and exact, that's the counter -- for listeners and for everything worse below. The general dies out here. The specific holds. Remember it going down.

objectives.label: the listeners' camp
objectives.text: Reach the half-taken crossers
Clear the wisps drawn to their stillness
Bring the pulled-back crossers to Mira

orange_fog_the_listeners.note: Two of three listeners came back annoyed at the cold -- the loveliest sound the fog knows, a person still theirs enough to complain. The third tilted further and got a candle, which is a lie the fog-folk tell themselves. Small and exact is the counter. The general dies out here. The specific holds.

orange_fog_toll_of_passage.title: The Toll Nobody Collects
orange_fog_toll_of_passage.summary: The fog's old toll-post still stands, and a finalized toll-keeper still stamps passage for the dead -- filing crossers into the ledger by reflex. Anca wants a forged receipt planted to jam the reflex before it stamps a living traveler collected.
orange_fog_toll_of_passage.offer: There's a toll-post on the black shingle, and a keeper still working it -- collected himself, long ago, but he stamps out of habit, files every crosser that passes into a ledger that reaches something below. Living folk have gone through and come out the far side quieter than they went in. Stamped. I've a forgery -- a receipt that reads paid in full, closed, no balance owed -- old clerk's trick. Slip it into his ledger and his reflex jams: nothing left to collect, so he stops reaching. Won't free him. Will stop him filing the next warm fool who trusts a toll-post. Mind the drifters round the post. They like the stamping.
orange_fog_toll_of_passage.progress: The finalized toll-keeper stamps on the black shingle. Plant the forged receipt in his ledger and clear the wisps drawn to the filing.
orange_fog_toll_of_passage.complete: It jammed. He's still there, stamp in hand, but the ledger reads closed now -- nothing owed, nothing to reach for -- and the reflex has nowhere to go. He'll stand and stamp air till the fog thins him. Cruel, in its way. But the next crosser goes through unfiled, and that's the trade. A forged receipt against a real erasure -- the whole late world in one small lie. I learned it from an old broker's books, Marrow Pell's kind of arithmetic: sometimes the honest move is a clean forgery, placed exactly right.

objectives.label: the toll-post on the black shingle
objectives.text: Reach the finalized toll-keeper
Plant the forged paid-in-full receipt
Clear the wisps drawn to the filing

orange_fog_toll_of_passage.note: The toll-keeper stamps air now, his ledger forged closed -- nothing owed, nothing to reach for. The next crosser passes unfiled. A forged receipt against a real erasure: the whole late world in one small lie, placed exactly right. Anca learned the arithmetic from a dead broker's books.

orange_fog_wardens_walk.title: The Warden's Nightly Round
orange_fog_wardens_walk.summary: The wisps drift thickest at night, drawn toward the fires where crossers sleep. Ecaterina walks a round each dusk and needs the drift thinned so the sleeping camps hold till morning. It never ends. That is the job.
orange_fog_wardens_walk.offer: Same ask, every dusk, and I'll not dress it up: the wisps come for the firelight when the crossers bed down, and a camp that goes dark in the reach doesn't wake. Walk the round with me. Thin the drift between the fires and the fog. It's not a victory, before you get ideas -- there's no winning a round you walk again tomorrow. It's a holding. The fog takes a hand's width; we hold the fires one more night; the count goes on. If you can stomach work that's never finished, I can always use the arm. Most can't. See if you're most.
orange_fog_wardens_walk.progress: The wisps drift toward the sleeping camps at dusk. Walk the round and thin them between the fires and the fog.
orange_fog_wardens_walk.complete: Camps held. Everyone in them wakes tomorrow, and tomorrow the drift comes back and we do it again. That's the fog: not a battle you win, a fire you keep. Some nights I think it's pointless, holding a line that only moves the one way. Then a child in one of those tents wakes up warm, not knowing how near it came, and I remember pointless and worth-doing were never the same word. Here's your coin. Same time tomorrow, if the arm's still willing.

objectives.text: Thin the drift between the fires and the fog

orange_fog_wardens_walk.note: The camps held one more night. The drift comes back at dusk; the round is walked again. It is not a battle to win but a fire to keep. Pointless and worth-doing were never the same word, Ecaterina says, and a child woke warm not knowing how near it came.

orange_fog_light_the_crossing.title: Lanterns for the Crossing
orange_fog_light_the_crossing.summary: Fresh crossers arrive each day needing lanterns that hold in the reach, and Ruxandra's stock runs dry each day selling them. She needs the wisps thinned for their light again -- a trade as endless as the fog and, she'll insist, a good deal more cheerful.
orange_fog_light_the_crossing.offer: Crossers in, lanterns out, stock empty by dusk -- it's the one dependable thing in the whole reach, which I choose to find comforting. They need light that holds, I've got wisps drifting free with light in them, and between those two facts sits a modest living and my continued good humor. Thin a batch and bottle the marshlight. I'll not tell you it saves the world. It sells the world a lantern, which the world seems to want rather more urgently. Grim harvest, cheerful clerk -- I contain multitudes. Off you go. The dark's not getting any friendlier.
orange_fog_light_the_crossing.progress: The crossers need lanterns; the stock's dry. Thin the wisps and bottle the marshlight for another day's light.
orange_fog_light_the_crossing.complete: Restocked, and just in time -- there's a party of six waiting who've heard what the reach does to a guttered lamp. Here's your cut, generous by my grim standards, which are the only standards that survive out here. You want the truth of the fog? Everyone's dying slowly and the lamps still need selling. Might as well sell them with a straight face and a fair price. Come back tomorrow. There's always tomorrow, and always somebody wanting light in it.

objectives.text: Thin the wisps for the day's light
Bottle marshlight for the crossers

orange_fog_light_the_crossing.note: The stock restocked, the crossers lit, the trade endless as the fog. Everyone is dying slowly and the lamps still need selling, Ruxandra says, so she sells them with a straight face and a fair price. There is always tomorrow, and always somebody wanting light in it.

orange_fog_the_incurious.title: The Man Who Learned Not to Look
orange_fog_the_incurious.summary: A crosser survived the fog by staring at his own feet the whole way through -- and now cannot stop, will not raise his eyes, half-mad with the discipline that saved him. Floarea wants him fetched to the inn and coaxed to look up at something small and warm.
orange_fog_the_incurious.offer: There's a man come out the far side of the reach alive, which almost nobody does, and he did it by never once raising his eyes -- boots the whole way, hours of it, refusing to look at anything the fog offered him. It worked. It also broke something. He won't look up now, can't, sits by the eastern stones with his chin on his chest saying don't look, don't look. He saved himself with the exact right instinct and it ate him. Fetch him toward the inn. Get him to look up at one small warm thing -- a lamp, a loaf, a kind face -- and maybe the instinct lets go its grip enough to leave him a man.
orange_fog_the_incurious.progress: The incurious man sits by the eastern stones, eyes down, half-broken by the discipline that saved him. Reach him and clear the wisps he won't look at.
orange_fog_the_incurious.complete: He looked up. Took an hour and Rada's warmest lamp held right at his eye-line, but he looked, and wept, and looked away, and looked again -- which is a man learning that not everything wants reading. He'll not be whole. Nobody who crosses the reach is. But he's a man who can raise his eyes to a lamp, and that's further back than most come. The instinct that saves you out here is the same one that ruins you. The trick nobody teaches: knowing when the danger's behind you, and letting your eyes go free.

objectives.label: the eastern stones
objectives.text: Reach the incurious man
Clear the wisps he refuses to look at
Bring word of him to Floarea

orange_fog_the_incurious.note: The man who crossed the fog by never looking up learned, an hour and a warm lamp later, to raise his eyes again. He will not be whole. The instinct that saves you in the reach is the same one that ruins you. Knowing when the danger is behind you is the trick nobody teaches.

orange_fog_map_that_lies.title: The Border of the Map
orange_fog_map_that_lies.summary: Lenuta's maps of the fog go wrong at the edges -- the reach eats the ink, and old landmarks migrate where no hand moved them. She wants the border re-walked and re-marked, and suspects the map itself has begun listening.
orange_fog_map_that_lies.offer: A map's a promise: here is a thing, and it will stay put. The fog breaks that promise. My charts go wrong at the borders -- ink fading where the reach touches the vellum, a marked cairn sitting a half-league from where I set it, as if the map were being edited by a hand that isn't mine. I've a marked border, good vellum, that resists the fading a while. Walk the true edge, set the marks fresh where the land actually is, and put down what's drawn to the vellum -- because I think the reach has started reading my maps, and a map that gets read is a map that starts telling the fog where we are. Careful work. The most careful you'll do up here.
orange_fog_map_that_lies.progress: The map's border has gone wrong at the reach's edge. Walk the true edge, set fresh marks, and clear the wisps drawn to the vellum.
orange_fog_map_that_lies.complete: There -- the border's honest again, for a season. You set the marks where the land is, not where the fog wants me to think it is. That's the whole fight, isn't it: keeping the record true when the record's what the enemy edits. The reach un-writes everything eventually -- ink, names, whole markets of people. A map that stays true one more season is a small defiance, and small defiance is all the craft I've got. Keep the border-marking. If you make it below and back, I'd like to know if my edges held.

objectives.label: the true edge of the reach
objectives.text: Re-walk and re-mark the fog border
Clear the wisps drawn to the vellum
Set the fresh border-marks

orange_fog_map_that_lies.note: The border is honest again for a season, marks set where the land is and not where the fog wants it believed. Keeping the record true when the record is what the enemy edits -- that is the whole fight. A map that stays true one more season is a small defiance. Small defiance is all the craft the fog leaves.

orange_fog_the_keepsake_cord.title: What the Dissolving Carry
orange_fog_the_keepsake_cord.summary: When the finalized come apart in the fog, small kept things fall from them -- a cord, a token, a keepsake the ledger never counted. Zamfira gathers these for the crossers' families, so that something warm makes it out where the person could not.
orange_fog_the_keepsake_cord.offer: When one of the walkers finally comes apart, it leaves things behind -- not the coin, the ledger takes the coin, but the small kept things no clerk ever thought to file. A braided cord. A child's carving. A keepsake worn smooth by a thumb that loved it. I gather them, and when a family comes crossing to the Hearth looking for someone the fog already ate, I've something warm to put in their hands. Not the person. Never the person. But proof they kept something, and being kept is the last thing the fog can't quite un-do. Gather what falls from the dissolving. Bring it to me. Let one small warm thing make it out.
orange_fog_the_keepsake_cord.progress: Small kept things fall from the finalized as they dissolve. Gather the keepsakes and bring them to Zamfira for the crossers' families.
orange_fog_the_keepsake_cord.complete: A cord, a carving, a keepsake or two -- look how worn they are, all of them, loved right down to the grain. I'll hold them for the families. There was a woman last month, crossing to find her brother; I put his whittling-knife in her hand and she wept like it was him, because in a way it was the last of him the fog didn't get. The person doesn't come out. The kept thing does. It's a poor trade and it's the only one on offer, and I'll keep making it till I'm one of the walkers myself. Take the cord. Keep something. Everyone should.

objectives.text: Gather keepsakes from the dissolving finalized
Thin the wisps over the fallen
Bring the keepsakes to Zamfira

orange_fog_the_keepsake_cord.note: The finalized shed small kept things as they dissolve -- cords, carvings, keepsakes loved down to the grain. Zamfira holds them for the families who come crossing to find the already-eaten. The person does not come out. The kept thing does. It is a poor trade and the only one on offer.

orange_fog_the_new_mouth.title: The Stone That Woke by the Water
orange_fog_the_new_mouth.summary: The dust pointed to a new transmission point waking near the black shingle -- a fresh stone writing itself orange. Mira cannot silence it, but she can salt-ward the approach so it draws crossers no further in. Delay is the only victory the fog sells.
orange_fog_the_new_mouth.offer: You helped Ecaterina read the dust; now here's the bill. There's a new mouth open by the shingle -- a stone gone shifting orange, writing itself into the world in real time, warming the ground, pulling the incurious toward it. We can't close it. Closing it means reading it, and reading it means being read; that's the whole cruelty of the thing. But I can salt the approach so it stops drawing folk deeper, cut the paths that lead to it, and thin the drifters thick around the fresh writing. It'll go on writing. It just won't go on recruiting. Delay. That's the coin the fog takes and the only one it gives back. Help me buy some.
orange_fog_the_new_mouth.progress: The new mouth writes itself orange by the black shingle. Salt the approach, thin the drift around the fresh writing, and do not read it.
orange_fog_the_new_mouth.complete: Salted and cut off. The stone writes on -- it'll write till it's a Deadheart or till something below calls it home -- but the paths in are broken and the drift's thinned, so no more listeners wander to it by accident. We didn't win. You don't win against a thing that's writing itself faster than you can walk away. We delayed. A season, maybe two, before the reach digests this corner. In the fog, delay is the whole art. Every season we steal is a season somebody crosses safe who wouldn't have. Take the ward. You've earned the salt on your hands.

objectives.label: the new mouth by the shingle
objectives.text: Salt-ward the waking stone's approach
Thin the drift around the fresh writing
Lay the salt-line across the paths in

orange_fog_the_new_mouth.note: The new mouth writes on by the black water, but the paths in are salted and the drift thinned -- it recruits no more accidental listeners. Nobody won. You do not win against a thing writing itself faster than you can walk away. You delay. In the fog, delay is the whole art, and every stolen season crosses somebody safe.

orange_fog_the_ash_chronicle.title: A True Accounting of the Fog
orange_fog_the_ash_chronicle.summary: Lenuta is writing the fog's true chronicle before the fog writes over her, and needs the deep-reach landmarks witnessed first-hand: the still market, the toll-post, the new mouth. A record kept by a living hand is one the machine has to un-write the hard way.
orange_fog_the_ash_chronicle.offer: My name is Cenusa -- ash. All of us out here are; the fog salts its keepers with the word for what it makes of us. Fitting. Before I'm ash in truth, I mean to leave a true accounting: where the reach is, what it took, who walked here when the writing was small enough to survive. I've the inner lanes. I need the deep landmarks witnessed by eyes I trust -- the still market, the shingle toll, the new mouth by the water. Go, look, come back and tell it plain, and I'll set it in the chronicle. A record a living hand keeps is one the machine can't just fade -- it has to come and un-write it, and coming takes time, and time is what we're stealing. Bear witness. It's the oldest craft there is.
orange_fog_the_ash_chronicle.progress: The deep landmarks want witnessing for the chronicle. Reach the shingle where the fog is thickest, see it plain, and clear the drift so you can look.
orange_fog_the_ash_chronicle.complete: Told plain, and set down plain -- the market that never finished its sale, the keeper stamping air, the mouth by the water writing its slow orange sentence. It's in the chronicle now, in a living hand, which means the reach has to send something to fade it, and sending takes time. That's the trick the whole late world turns on: make the record specific and stubborn and warm, and the machine that erases by generalizing chokes on it. You've helped me choke it a little. When you go below -- and you will -- carry one specific true thing down with you. It's the only weapon that works there. I've watched it work exactly once. Once was enough to write about.

objectives.label: the thickest reach by the shingle
objectives.text: Witness the deep landmarks for the chronicle
Clear the drift so you can look and live
Tell Lenuta what you witnessed

orange_fog_the_ash_chronicle.note: The deep fog is set down in a living hand -- the still market, the stamping keeper, the mouth writing its slow orange sentence. A record a living hand keeps must be un-written the hard way, and coming takes time, and time is what the keepers steal. Carry one specific true thing below. Lenuta has watched it work exactly once.

orange_fog_hearth_road_home.title: The Road to the Last Warm Door
orange_fog_hearth_road_home.summary: A crossing family is bound for the Last Hearth and daylight is failing in the reach. Rada asks that they be walked to the road's turn where the fog thins and the warm door is close -- so at least one party makes it out whole tonight.
orange_fog_hearth_road_home.offer: There's a family in my taproom -- mother, grandfather, two small ones -- bound for the Last Hearth, the one place west of here that's still all warm and no reach. They lingered over my flat bread too long and now the light's going, and the fog at dusk is no place for children who haven't learned to keep their eyes down. Walk them to the turn where the road climbs out and the Hearth-lamps show. It's not far. It's just far enough to matter. Get them to where the warmth starts and the fog stops, and let one family, tonight, cross this whole rotten reach and come out the other side entire. I'd count that a good day. I don't have many left to count.
orange_fog_hearth_road_home.progress: The family must reach the road's turn where the fog thins toward the Last Hearth. Walk them there before dark, and clear the dusk-drift from the road.
orange_fog_hearth_road_home.complete: They made it to the turn -- the little ones saw the Hearth-lamps and ran, actually ran, laughing, toward the warm, and the grandfather stood a moment just to watch them do it. A whole family, out of the fog, entire. Do you know how rare that is? You wouldn't, if you hadn't walked them yourself. It's the rarest thing the reach allows and it happened tonight because you were here. Sit. There's bread -- grey, but ours -- and it's on the house. Everything good is temporary. That's what makes it good. The children ran toward the light. Let that be the thing you remember about this place.

objectives.label: the turn toward the Last Hearth
objectives.text: Walk the family to the warm door
Clear the dusk-drift from the road

orange_fog_hearth_road_home.note: A whole family crossed the reach entire and the little ones ran laughing toward the Hearth-lamps. It is the rarest thing the fog allows. Everything good is temporary; that is what makes it good. The children ran toward the light. Rada asked that this be the thing remembered about her fog.

orange_fog_the_unfiled.title: The Account That Would Not Close
orange_fog_the_unfiled.summary: Word reaches the fog of a Collector -- a man stamped collected a thousand years gone who keeps showing up to work anyway, the one discrepancy the ledger cannot close. Draga wants a writ of unfiling drafted from the fog's own miscounts, to arm the discrepancy walking below.
orange_fog_the_unfiled.offer: Every ledger out here miscounts by one, always the same shape: an account marked closed that keeps moving. I used to think it was error. Now I think it's the same account, the same discrepancy, showing up in every book because it refuses to stay filed anywhere. There's a man they call the Collector -- collected himself, wearing the mark of it, and still walking, still working, a debt that won't clear. He's not the enemy. He's the flaw in the enemy. Gather me the fog's own un-stamped lines -- the tokens where the ink kept refusing -- and I'll draft a writ of unfiling from them: proof, in the machine's own hand, that closed and gone were never the same word. Put it where the discrepancy will find it. Arm the thing that keeps getting up.
orange_fog_the_unfiled.progress: The fog's ledgers hold un-stamped lines -- accounts that refused to close. Gather the refusing tokens and let Draga draft the writ of unfiling.
orange_fog_the_unfiled.complete: Drafted -- a writ of unfiling in the machine's own arithmetic, every line a place the ledger tried to close and a dead hand un-closed it. Proof that the system's most certain word is a lie it tells about one soul at a time. The discrepancy below can carry this: when a thing tries to finalize him, he shows it its own un-stamped lines and it can't balance. Specificity and stubbornness -- the man is both, a thousand years of both, a grave-debt that keeps showing up to work. He is what the whole map is really about, and he doesn't even know it yet. Neither did any of us, till the ink kept refusing.

objectives.text: Gather the ledger's refusing lines
Thin the wisps guarding the old accounts
Have Draga draft the writ of unfiling

orange_fog_the_unfiled.note: A writ of unfiling, drafted in the machine's own arithmetic -- proof that closed and gone were never the same word. It is left where the discrepancy will find it: a man collected a thousand years gone who keeps showing up to work. Specificity and stubbornness. He is what the whole map is about. He does not know it yet.

orange_fog_the_deepbed_lantern.title: Light for the Descent
orange_fog_the_deepbed_lantern.summary: The way down to Coldharbor Deep opens off the black shingle, and no ordinary flame lasts in the drowned dark below. Anca knows the making of a deepbed lantern -- marshlight bound in blackglass -- and needs its parts before anyone descends into the settled accounts.
orange_fog_the_deepbed_lantern.offer: You'll go down eventually -- they all do, and you more than most, you've the look of someone the fog can't keep. Below the shingle the under-docks open, Coldharbor, where the accounts get settled into black water and no honest flame holds a minute. But there's a lantern the old crossers made: marshlight off the wisps, bound in blackglass so the cold-dark can't drink it. I've the binding-craft, not the parts. Bring me marshlight and blackglass both and I'll build you a light that burns where the drowned dead file themselves. You'll want it. The dark down there isn't empty. It's full, and it's patient, and it's been waiting a thousand years to be read.
orange_fog_the_deepbed_lantern.progress: The deepbed lantern needs marshlight and blackglass. Gather both off the wisp-lanes and bring them to Anca's bench.
orange_fog_the_deepbed_lantern.complete: There -- a deepbed lantern, marshlight caged in blackglass, cold-proof and dark-proof and steady as a stubborn heart. It'll hold below where the water drinks other flame. Take it down with you, and mind the one rule that matters in the deep same as up here: the light's to see your footing by, not to read by. There'll be inscriptions on every drowned wall wanting your eyes. Look where you step. Don't look where it points. Come back up. Some of you always does, and I've stopped being surprised by which.

objectives.text: Gather marshlight for the lantern
Gather blackglass for the binding
Have Anca build the deepbed lantern

orange_fog_the_deepbed_lantern.note: A deepbed lantern -- marshlight caged in blackglass, cold-proof and steady as a stubborn heart -- burns where the drowned accounts file themselves. The light is to see your footing by, not to read by. The dark below is not empty. It is full, and patient, and has waited a thousand years to be read.

orange_fog_the_watchers_vigil.title: A Watch Kept for the Nameless
orange_fog_the_watchers_vigil.summary: Floarea keeps a nightly watch over the finalized she cannot yet name, guessing at them aloud so the guessing stays warm. She asks that the vigil-ground be kept clear of drift so the nameless are watched over one more night, un-filed a little longer.
orange_fog_the_watchers_vigil.offer: I keep a list of the ones I can't name -- the walkers whose tokens I've never matched to a face. Hundreds. I sit the watch over them each night and I guess: were you a baker, a boatman, somebody's mother? I get it wrong, mostly. But guessing at them keeps them people a little longer, and a person the fog hasn't quite finalized is a person it has to work to erase. The wisps come thick to the vigil-ground -- drawn to the nameless, I think, the way they're drawn to anything half-open. Keep them off tonight. Let me watch over my nameless in peace. It's not much of a mercy. It's the shape of the only one I've got.
orange_fog_the_watchers_vigil.progress: The vigil-ground draws the drift each night. Keep the wisps off so Floarea can watch over the nameless finalized one more night.
orange_fog_the_watchers_vigil.complete: Watched over, one more night, the whole nameless list of them. I guessed a dozen wrong and it doesn't matter -- the guessing's the point, the caring-enough-to-guess. A closed file forgotten is finalized twice; a closed file wondered about, out loud, in the dark, by someone who won't stop -- that's an account the machine can't quite settle. I'll keep the watch till I'm on the list myself, and then I hope some fool keeps guessing at me. Here. Thank you for the quiet. In the fog, quiet you can watch in is nearly the whole of peace.

objectives.text: Keep the drift off the vigil-ground

orange_fog_the_watchers_vigil.note: Floarea watched over the nameless one more night, guessing wrong a dozen times, because the caring-enough-to-guess is the point. A closed file forgotten is finalized twice; one wondered about aloud, by someone who will not stop, is an account the machine cannot quite settle.

orange_fog_the_last_warm_thing.title: The Keepsake and the Cord
orange_fog_the_last_warm_thing.summary: Otilia's grandmother crossed into the fog years ago and never came back. A keepsake matching hers has surfaced among the fallen. Otilia cannot bear to gather it herself, and asks that it be brought out -- proof the old woman kept something warm to the very end.
orange_fog_the_last_warm_thing.offer: My grandmother walked into the reach nine years gone, looking for her own mother, and the fog kept her the way it keeps everyone who goes looking. I've made my peace, or the shape of it. But Zamfira says a keepsake surfaced among the fallen that matches the cord my grandmother braided -- red and grey, a pattern only our line uses. If it's hers, it means she kept it to the end, held one warm small thing while the fog un-made the rest. I can't go gather it. My hands won't. But if you'd bring it out -- and a bound keepsake or two besides, for the other families that ask -- I'd know she wasn't nothing at the end. She was someone holding a cord.
orange_fog_the_last_warm_thing.progress: A keepsake matching Otilia's line has surfaced among the fallen. Gather it and a few bound keepsakes, and bring them out of the reach.
orange_fog_the_last_warm_thing.complete: Red and grey, our pattern, worn smooth -- it's hers. She kept it. Nine years I've told myself she went into the fog and became nothing, and here's the cord that says no: she went in and stayed someone, holding on, right to the end where holding on was all there was. That's not nothing. That's the whole of it, really -- staying someone, keeping one warm thing, while the cold does its filing. I'll wear it. And when the fog finally comes for me, I'll go in holding something too, so some grandchild of a grandchild knows I stayed a person the whole way down. Thank you. You brought her home. The cord did, anyway.

objectives.text: Thin the wisps over the fallen
Recover the red-and-grey cord
Gather bound keepsakes for the families

orange_fog_the_last_warm_thing.note: The red-and-grey cord was hers -- kept to the end, held while the fog un-made the rest. She went into the reach and stayed someone, which is not nothing; it is nearly the whole of it. Otilia wears the cord now, and means to go in holding something herself, so a grandchild of a grandchild will know she stayed a person the whole way down.

finalized_fields_the_last_open_account.title: The Last Open Account
finalized_fields_the_last_open_account.summary: Every account in the fields is stamped closed but one, and the clerk who found it cannot un-see the name. He asks a living hand to walk the rows and read what he is forbidden to.
finalized_fields_the_last_open_account.offer: I keep the intake ledger for the Fields, and in six years I have never seen an open line. There is one now, out among the grave-rows, and the name on it is mine. I cannot walk to it -- clerks who read their own account tend to settle it. You are alive and you are stubborn about it. Walk the rows. Tell me what the line says before it decides for me.
finalized_fields_the_last_open_account.progress: The open account waits out among the grave-rows, on the reading-post at the field's heart. Walk to it, and do not stay too long once you have read it.
finalized_fields_the_last_open_account.complete: Pending. My line still says pending. Six years and the ground never once said pending to anyone. It is not mercy, understand -- an open account is only a thing not yet gotten around to. But it is not closed. Take the writ. If they come to stamp me, put it on the desk before they do.

objectives.label: the reading-post at the field's heart
objectives.text: Read the open account in the rows

finalized_fields_the_last_open_account.note: One account in all the Fields still read pending. Iosif kept the writ in his coat and stopped signing his own name to anything. A line not yet closed is not the same as a life. He knew that. He kept the coat anyway.

finalized_fields_what_the_wardens_kept.title: What the Wardens Kept
finalized_fields_what_the_wardens_kept.summary: The grave-wardens walk the rows in patient order, filing the ones who wander. The watcher wants five put down and their satchels searched -- for a warden files nothing it has not first read.
finalized_fields_what_the_wardens_kept.offer: I watch the rows so the town does not have to. The wardens keep good order out there -- too good. They walk the same line every night, filing whoever drifts off theirs, and each carries a satchel of what it took off the last. I want five of them stopped and their bags turned out. Not for loot. I want to know what a thing keeps, when keeping is the only warmth it has left.
finalized_fields_what_the_wardens_kept.progress: The grave-wardens walk the filing-rows after dark. Put down five, and search what they carry.
finalized_fields_what_the_wardens_kept.complete: A cracked cup. A child's shoe, one. A copper coin worn smooth on one face from a thumb. Nothing owed, nothing filed -- the useless, particular leavings the ledger has no column for. They were not collecting. They were hiding these from the account. Even a warden keeps one thing it will not report. Take the shroud. It remembers what it covers.

objectives.text: Put down the grave-wardens on the rows

finalized_fields_what_the_wardens_kept.note: Each warden kept one useless thing -- a cup, a shoe, a worn coin -- unfiled, uncounted, warm from being held. The ledger has no column for what cannot be generalized. Manea buried the leavings unread. Some things are safest never understood.

finalized_fields_the_receipt_for_a_life.title: The Receipt for a Life
finalized_fields_the_receipt_for_a_life.summary: The chronicler has kept the intake dossiers dry and correct for years, and one of them is his brother's. He asks for the old counteragent, brewed where the symptom lives, so he can read the file without the file reading him.
finalized_fields_the_receipt_for_a_life.offer: I write the histories out here, which mostly means copying what the Archive already closed. Correct, dry, complete -- the way they like it. There is a dossier I have refused to open for a year because my brother's name is the title, and to read a finalized account in full is to be finalized reading it. There is an old draught for that. Brew it out here, where the wrongness is, so the cure keeps its grudge. Then I will read him. I owe him the reading.
finalized_fields_the_receipt_for_a_life.progress: The counteragent must be brewed where the symptom lives -- at the still-run among the filing-sheds, where the ground never grows cold. Bring it back before your nerve does.
finalized_fields_the_receipt_for_a_life.complete: I read the whole of him and I am still here. It does not save him -- collected is collected, a receipt does not refund. But the draught let me hold his account in my head without agreeing to it. That is the trick, they say. Read around it. Never resolve it. Take the still-run bottle. Drink it angry; that part matters.

objectives.label: the still-run among the filing-sheds
objectives.text: Brew the counteragent where the symptom lives
Draw off the counteragent of quiet

finalized_fields_the_receipt_for_a_life.note: Danciu read his brother's whole account and did not resolve it -- held the grief without letting it close. The Archive files what it understands. He kept his brother a question. It is the only tense the ground here cannot stamp.

finalized_fields_water_that_tastes_owed.title: Water That Tastes Owed
finalized_fields_water_that_tastes_owed.summary: The house-well has gone from sweet to a rust-taste the innkeeper calls owing. He wants the old salt-line laid at the field-wells, the way his grandmother would -- not to keep a thing out, but to remind it there is a door.
finalized_fields_water_that_tastes_owed.offer: My well ran sweet forty years and last month it turned. Not foul -- owed. That is the only word for it, the taste of water that thinks you are behind on something. My grandmother laid salt at every well when the taste changed. Salt keeps nothing out, she said. It only reminds the thing there is a door and a proper way through it. Lay the line at the three field-wells for me. Manners cost little.
finalized_fields_water_that_tastes_owed.progress: Three wells stand out in the filing-rows where the water turned. Lay the salt-line at each, the old careful way.
finalized_fields_water_that_tastes_owed.complete: Still tastes owed, if I am honest. Salt does not cure a node; nothing here does. But a door reminded of itself is a door not walked through, and my guests slept without dreaming of columns. That is a night bought. Out here you buy nights, not mornings. Take the pouch -- there is salt left, and there will be more wells.

objectives.label: the three turned field-wells
objectives.text: Lay the salt-line at the turned wells
Set the salt-line at each well

finalized_fields_water_that_tastes_owed.note: The wells still taste owed. Salt cures no node -- but a door reminded of itself is a door not yet walked through. Ursu's guests slept without dreaming of columns. Out here, a night is a thing you buy, and morning is never on offer.

finalized_fields_rows_of_twelve.title: Rows of Twelve
finalized_fields_rows_of_twelve.summary: The filing-barges run the drowned canals out to the Fields, and the dockmaster has counted the same wrong number twelve nights running. He wants the moor-wails on the water thinned before they finish spelling whatever they are spelling.
finalized_fields_rows_of_twelve.offer: I load the barges that carry the filed out to the rows, and I count everything twice -- a dockmaster who miscounts becomes cargo. Twelve nights the wails on the water have hung in rows of twelve. Not a flock. A ledger. They arrange themselves the way papers here arrange toward the Archive, and I do not want to read the sentence they are making. Thin them on the canal-edge. Break the count before the count breaks something.
